So if you store it, make sure it’s not attached to any metal. It’s not as strong as before and it’s falling apart. I plan to buy another and store it better this time.

This is a great magnet for the price, it's really strong so use caution around metal objects. I used mine for magnet fishing and it picked up plenty of old hooks and fishing lures. rating 3.0. the device is good, but the laser pointer is useless because it ... Just received this item about twenty minutes ago. Seems to do what it advertises; it takes the temperature accurately, as far as I can tell. The only problem I have is that the laser pointer is not centered. When I point the gun at a surface, the laser tends to be shining above and to the left of the actual gun. I double checked that the actual temperature sensor was not skewed to the left and verified that when I place a warm object where the laser is, the sensor still reads the cooler object.

Overall, the device is good, but the laser pointer is useless because it is misaligned.

rating 5.0. Wonderful multiple use tool. Limited only by your imagination. The perfect item to use for a variety of temperature needs. I have used this to determine the temperature of food on the grill, in the oven, on the stove and even checked the freezer temperature. In addition I use it to check to temperature of the pool. I also used it to check around the doors and windows in the house and discovered where I needed some extra insulation.

There are so many uses for this item that I could go on for hours, but suffice it to say this is a great and inexpensive temperature gauge with multiple uses, limited only by your imagination. I even used the laser light to aim at the floor and my dog chased the beam around the house, just don't aim the beam at little Fido, please!

When checking the temperature of food, it is important to remember the laser does not penetrate the food so its checking the exterior temperature, not the center temperature. Once you make some calculations and learnt to adjust for that this is a must have for all the grill/bbq/smoker fanatics amongst us.

rating 5.0. Great for taking the temp of wood burning stove, battery lasts forever I bought this to use on my wood burning stove so I could be sure it was burning at a good temp (between 400F and 600F). I just realized I've had it for almost two years and I've never had to change the battery. It works better if you position it a foot or two from what you're trying to read the temp of, rather than farther away. Think of it like a cone in front of the gun, the farther you are from the surface you're measuring, the wider the area it is going to take the temperature of. I point it at the top of my stove to take a reading. It's plenty accurate for what I'm using it for, but I wouldn't expect to point it at someone's forehead and get a medically accurate temp. If you want something like that you'll be paying a lot more money. It also can't really be used on reflective surfaces, so don't try using it on glass, as the beam will just bounce off and give you a reading of who knows what.

On a family meeting we found that a nephew wasn't playing with the other kids, so my wife sensed she was warm and she attempted to find the oral thermometer to no avail.
Here is where my gadgets come to play it's part, funny to say but: I turned off the laser pointer, asked her to look away then pointed in the forehead, about 7 to 10 inches away.

1st reading was 35.7 C and raising,
after 1 minute made a 2nd reading of 36.5 C,
a 3rd reading was 37.2 C, while I was shouting my wife, "bring the medicine and some ice in a towel, quick!"

Pros:
- senses immediately
- able turn off blue backlight
- able turn off laser pointer
- auto off
Cons:
- none so far

rating 5.0. Really Neat Device I bought this to help analyze the temperature distributions of my HVAC system. By 'shooting' it at the ceiling vents and noting the local airflow temperatures, I was able to quickly determine which vents needed partial close-off in order to balance out the system. I also used it to check the efficiency of my double-pane window installations vs. the insulated wall right next to each one, and as compared to the one remaining single-pane window in the house. (BIG difference). This is a 'fun' device that very closely matches both of my outdoor thermometers, but has a much higher range. I even used it last night to measure the temperature of a dinner cooked in the microwave (172.6 degrees F)

It is well made, and comes with a 9V battery, ready to use. I'm sure I'll find a few dozen more uses for it as well, in gathering information for evaluating and/or troubleshooting home appliances and automotive system performance.

rating 5.0. Accuracy Reviewed Overall this is an excellent product. The grip is nice, the display screen has a backlight and you can't beat the price.

A simple way to test the accuracy of this thermometer is to measure the temperature of a pot of boiling water. This will roughly give you a temperature of 212 F. Boiling water, in equilibrium (or the entire fluid being the same temperature) will maintain 212F no matter how quickly the water is evaporating. The reason this is a rough estimate is that the boiling temperature of water changes with salinity and altitude.

The thermometer I received was reading around 208-212 F. So how does this compare to the top end model? I am an engineer who provides safety related components to nuclear power plants. We use the $200+ model which is calibrated by a separate vendor. Our calibration standard is 1.5C which is 209.3F as our lower end tolerance. So this thermometer would NOT be suitable for qualifying safety related components for nuclear plants. Except you aren't trying to qualify safety related components.

In practice, these are used for taking a quick, general measurement of fluid temperature or identifying heat losses. I wouldn't expect many people to need to use these to get exact temperatures. I have used this at my home to identify what rooms are cooler then others, draughts around doors or windows and leaks in the HVAC system.

I would greatly recommend purchasing this over the higher end thermometers. Very solid product and definitely accurate.

rating 3.0. Finicky, but it works It's very sensitive to distance and lighting, but if you hold it within an inch or so of the tape, it will pick it up.  Make sure the surface the tape is mounted on is clean and uniform in color, or the tach will get confused.

rating 2.0. did not work in all fairness, the box it came in was slightly damaged on one corner, but it didn't have any visible damage inside.
tried to use it and readings, when I got any were all over the place.

rating 1.0. Cheap construction, short lived, non functional in daylight. Ordered two of these. First one worked fine for a while, but ate batteries like they were going out of style. 2nd unit never worked. Messaged CyberTech and never got a response.
rating 1.0. One Star Failed after 3 o 4 uses.
rating 1.0. Junk Accuracy off by over 50%. Total piece of garbage!

rating 5.0. Very good value Seems to be accurate. Did the flouresent light test ( should read 7200), spot on.  Tested against my vehicle's tach at idle accurate & steady. For way less than $20, it's a handy tool to have around. It came with ~18" of reflective tape, but you can also use aluminum foil and scotch tape if need be.  Best results are when the pulley or whatever is non reflective, with just one point being reflective.

rating 4.0. For the price, it's really tough to complain. I don't have anything else to measure RPM with to compare for accuracy, but the tachometer arrived promptly and in good shape.  Instructions are adequate (basically affix reflective tape to rotating object, then point tach laser at reflective tape while rotating).  Reflective tape supplied is generous (especially since you can apply to any, less permanent tape or surface and reuse one scrap a hundred times).  For really accurate measurements... well, you'd probably be in the market for something else anyway.  For the dirt cheap price, i'm happy.  I used this tachometer on two of my Dewalt cordless drills  with their RPM ranges printed on the tool body, each fitted with a 3" cutting disc, and with both of them this tach read within 100 rpm of the listed range. I'm sure a larger disc would provide greater accuracy.  Oh, and it did smell pretty strongly of cheap plastic when the package was first opened... but the smell fades.

rating 1.0. Ruined in less than 1 week of use The batteries the unit was supplied with did not work. But this was no problem. We bought new ones. It worked on three tests.  The fourth try, it only marked 0 RPM.  This unit has not lasted 1 week.  I do not suggest anyone buy this.

rating 5.0. Does what its supposed to I purchased this optical unit in order to verify my pulse tach readings on my boat motor.  It did what it was supposed to, and verified that I was receiving accurate readings on my pulse tach.  The unit is simple to use; point and press the button.  I've not had the need to use it for anything else, but it has become part of my motor troubleshooting kit.  an excellent product for the price.

rating 4.0. Neat little tool. This handheld laser tach works great. I used it on an outboard motor that could not support a standard tach. The only issue I had was that it did not work outdoors in direct sunlight. As the sun dropped down, then it was able to "see" the reflector and read the rpm's. Indoors it works perfectly. Aside from the sunlight issue this is a neat little tool.

rating 5.0. Great Value for Price, Numerous Uses I needed something to set the engine speed on lawnmowers, garden tractors, etc. Briggs & Stratton sells a little wheel with a wire that vibrates at a certain speed, but it's not usable unless you have a flat surface to lay it on, and it's hard to see the numbers--plus it costs 50% more than this little jewel.

This tach only require putting a reflective "spot" on the shaft or pulley you want to measure the speed on. Some reflective tape is provided--only 1/2 or so is needed. You could also use a spot of white paint, metallic tape, etc.

The only complaint I have about it is that it's cumbersome to press the button to take the measurement because of it's location on the front of the meter. I recommend putting it on the side near the top.

Steve in Utah

rating 1.0. Here's the video, you tell me if it's garbage (hint: yes, it is) [[VIDEOID:e31ab9cc7f299bd64960d080050d6cd4]] I wish I could say that I got my money's worth on an inexpensive tachometer. The description said "In wood and metal working it can be used to accurately measure the speed of a lathe or milling machine.", so I put the tape on the spindle of my lathe and took a video of the result.

As you can see, the meter never reads anything except zero RPM. I got some false hopes from looking at the contents of the memory (when I hit the "mem" button), but those numbers are clearly incorrect. They jumped from 59 RPM down to 27 RPM then 24 RPM, but 60 RPM is one revolution per second and you can see that the lathe is doing quite a bit more than that.

Even though the "low battery" indicator that was mentioned in the manual was not displayed, I changed the batteries anyway (just in case) and got the same result.

This unit is brand new. I received this evening. I didn't even get one successful use out of it, and I'm very inclined to believe the other reviewers who said that it only lasted once or twice.

rating 3.0. Inconsistent Reading Based on others reviews, I did not expect flawless operation. I first tried the tool in conjunction with an Innova 3160 code reader to observe live data rpm and it worked, read within 50 rpm which was good enough. While setting idle on a 75 hp jeep F134 engine, the machine read 4500 rpm at idle.... just no way thats close. Tried this reading from several different angles but it read impossibly high. Easy to read display and the laser is bright.
Reads better as close to a 90 degree angle as possible out of direct sunlight.
Apparently bright sunlight can have an undesirable effect and totally screw readings, the machine loses its mind. Checked again at dusk with no direct sunlight and it read correctly or close. So be aware of these limitations if you have incorrect readings.
Its either a cheap machine or a very expensive one, so I made the bet on this one based on reviews and expected frequency of use. There's apparently no in between cost wise.
It will usually work so far, but be aware of its limitations and know what to generally expect for a reading.
rating 1.0. One Star worked for one day then quit working

rating 2.0. Cheap, hard to use Affordable, but this thing sure is finicky! I realize this is a bargain basement price for a great tool, but this thing barely works! Or rather, it works under such a slight set of circumstances that it was useless to me. I'm not sure if it's just a cheap model, or if it only works under perfect conditions, but I had 4 or 5 jobs I needed to do with this one, and it barely handled one of them.

Maybe I needed a strobe tach or something high end, but this didn't cut it! Be careful what you buy it for as it will only work under the most basic tach conditions (like a spinning car tire or something similarly basic with direct access and very close proximity)!
rating 5.0. Awesome The Digital Photo Laser Tachometer performed great. I was very pleased and it tested to be very accurate. On an 18000 rpm spindle, the readout was 17997 rpm in just one second.

rating 4.0. Inexpensive, easy to use, spotty consistency If you're looking for a good averaged non-contact tach this will work.
if you're looking for a finely tuned, highly accurate tach keep looking.
Used if one half a dozen projects already and find it helpful but has issues being consistent in readings.
From reading to reading it will vary by 30-50%. Some may due to operator error (me) but not all.
Still, for the price, it does what I need it to do.

Updating as on 17 May 2014: The unit still works for the most part.
Still getting spotty results in back to back tests. Find it is VERY sensitive to direct light
whihc is an issue for me since 90% of my testing is outside. I have to create my own shade
to get good readings but creating my own shade isn't an issue according to my wife (yeah, she thinks she's funnny)

Also, I'd take a star off as it appears to eat batteries and at 4 AA per it's
going to cost me more in batteries than the unit itself. I store it in the soft case
standing upright so there is no pressure on the TEST button. Having to take the batts out
everytime will be a slight inconvenience. That and the clips in the batt compartment keep poppoing out.
Might be related?

rating 1.0. It fell apart before I could even get the batteries in. Disappointing tach. I took it out of the package and the window for the read-out fell out. I pointed it at a roll of masking tape sitting on my bench and it said it was spinning 7100 RPM. I pointed at other non-spinning things and got a wide variety of RPMs. I pointed it at my CNC machine's spindle , which already has a piece of reflective tape on it, and I could not get a consistent RPM read-out. The laser aiming beam is only about 1/3 there... It looks like a crescent moon.

I'm sending it back and getting the "AGPtek® Professional Digital Laser Photo Tachometer Non Contact RPM Tach". It looks to be made by a different company. Hopefully it's a much better unit.

I realize these are low-end tachs, but they shouldn't fall apart when you turn it over to put the batteries in.

rating 3.0. Should be sold in a better way I believe this should come with better expressed instructions on how to operate it! although it only has two buttons I have not been able to test it properly.
rating 5.0. works great I've had it for more than a year now and had no problems with it at all.and very accurate.you might want to buy more reflective stripe for it when you bye it.

rating 5.0. This is a Great Tool! I can't believe I got this for $15 with free shipping.  It took a while to arrive but it came all the way from China.  I had been wanting a tachometer to properly set the idle speed on several small engines but an accurate tach is very expensive.  This little gem is easy to use and works perfectly.  I won't even question its accuracy because electronic counting of pulses is simple and perfectly accurate.  The laser beam is bright so I had no trouble using it even in direct sunlight. The translated instructions leave a bit to be desired but it is so easy to use that's not a problem.  Anyone who would be using this tach frequently might want to look around for a source of reflective tape but there was enough tape with it to last me a lifetime.
rating 1.0. The unit display didn't even turn on. The whole ... The unit display didn't even turn on. The whole unit including the carry case had a cheap look and feel.  Junk.
rating 4.0. Pretty good for the price We used this for a school project and  it worked well. The unit records a low, high, and last reading, which is more than we expected.

rating 2.0. Don't get this, get the Neiko one ... This one uses 9V battery and seems to work only when the battery is very new.
Works for a few seconds then wait for a few minutes, possibly for the battery to recover.
Then works again for a few seconds.

The Neiko one uses AA batteries and seems much better although from the case and tape it seems that they come from the same manufacturer or supplier.

rating 2.0. Another DOA You will read about other units showing up DOA. My experience with this unit was the same. I applied
the reflective tape with some black tape backing it (to add contrast). I tried to measure the RPM
of my Sherline mill, and got exactly one reading out of the tachometer, then it refused to work
again. Always zero RPM. Pulled the batteries to try to reset it, but same result. Back to Amazon
this device goes. I'm going to look around to find alternatives. It's hard to complain about
something as inexpensive as this device is, but it is really cheaply constructed. Fail.
rating 2.0. Works... sorta... in a ... Works... sorta... in a darkened room, object has to be non reflective and dark except for the reflective tape... then maybe it will give a reading.

rating 4.0. Digital Tachometer Works perfectly as advertised. Of course I don't have a comparison of how accurate the displayed speed is. But I use it to adjust the speed of my router to be able to run larger bits.  To do that it doesn't matter if I am off a few RPMs. Important is to not run a large bit with 27000 rpm when it is only built for 18000. So I like it and it works. Very good price and it comes in this little blue pouch, just as the picture shows, so the device is a bit protected. I gave it 4 stars as I couldn't verify the accuracy of the device given by the manufacturer.
rating 1.0. Erratic display and unfocused light beam Erratic display and unfocused light beam.
rating 5.0. A very Good asset Really this is a very good RPM counter. After using non-reflecting tape on reflecting shaft its reading were more accurate at distance of 2' (feet).

rating 4.0. Great Product This tachometer works as advertised. It comes with enough reflective tape to last for some time. It is easy to use.
My only complaint is you need a small screwdriver to access the battery compartment. I usually remove the batteries from devices that don't get a lot of use and it is a bit of a pain to have to find a small screwdriver when removing/installing batteries. Other than that it is a great product with good accuracy.

rating 1.0. Fell apart as I removed it from the box. ... Fell apart as I removed it from the box.  Display cover fell off.  Had to press test button several times before it would function.  Returned same day.
rating 1.0. 100% JUNK Absolute junk. I have used both high and low quality tools in my many years as a tech but this one qualifies as USLESS JUNK!!! STAY AWAY
rating 2.0. Two Stars can't get a good reading
rating 1.0. One Star Worked out of the box ok. Second time I tried using it it would not work.
rating 1.0. Garbage Piece of junk, bought another one for have the price and through this in the trash.

rating 3.0. sometimes I get a reading. this is my first experience with this tool. I guess you can't beat the price. The only complaint I have is when I check small engines outdoors sometimes it's too bright. I find myself moving from side to side of the running engine trying to create shade.

rating 4.0. Good Product This product has done everything I wanted so far.  I purchased it so that I would know the rpm's turning on my mini mill.  I checked it against my lathe (which has a tach) and it's in the ballpark.  Amazon's shipping has certainly taken a turn for the worse though.  Items are taking longer and longer to ship and arrive and they are being routed ridiculously.

rating 1.0. No good Junk...plain and simple. I used it once and when I took it out a month later the thing no longer functioned. Even the display "glass" fell off. I threw it in the garbage. Oh well....Ya get what ya pay for!

rating 4.0. Cheap and Better. (Doesn't Comes with 9V Battery) I bought this to measure the RPM of one of my machine whose Odometer stopped working.
This meter just works fine, however it needs to put some reflextion tape to be glued on the pulley (or something like that) for which you want to measure RPM. Flex reflective strips are supplied. Also, this meter needs 9V battery not included.

rating 3.0. FIRST DIDN'T WORK, SECOND DIDN'T WORK... I bought this with the intention of using for setting the engine speed on a lawn tractor.  The first one arrived and I couldn't make sense of the display...turns out that the display was broken.  The seller immediately sent me another one.  The second one arrived and the display worked great...the buttons didn't.  To make a long story short...I took parts from each to make one that works.  I felt bad that the seller spent more in shipping than they got from me...quality of the product leaves much to be desired.  The one I finally have works well...In the end I got what I needed.

rating 2.0. Definite disappointment I was in total dis-belief of how poorly made this product looks and feels.  My disappointment deepened when I tried to use it.  Even using the reflective tape it came with, and painting the surrounding area black, it would not work outdoors.  Even on a VERY CLOUDY day.
rating 1.0. am not able as I  ,8 We did not purchase this product. This must be an error in amazon' ssystem

rating 5.0. Great tool for the price I am trying to have a "perfect control" for my home made CNC router and I got this device to have a good feedback of my router's speed. I combined with a speed controller and this gave me more precision in the final appearance of the jobs I perform. Since routers run very fast (about 33000 RPM), I needed slower speeds for plastics and metals and this combination gave me the chance to have a full control on speed and torque without the price of expensive spindles.
rating 4.0. Works as it should It is very accurate and easy to use.  Just be careful not to treat it roughly.  I dropped my first one and it was never the same.  It is a bit delicate.

rating 4.0. good gadget to have around. works good ,and is a very afforable price ,,insted of reflectic tape you can use any white reflectic marker or paint,on a clean surface,,also is great you dont have to get close to the fans or belts .

rating 3.0. It's ok but make sure you have a flat black ... It's ok but make sure you have a flat black surface behind the reflective tape or it won't work.  We used flat black spray paint because the gloss black background wasn't working at all.

rating 1.0. dirty flywheel, and after cleaning a small spot It seems you MUST use the reflective tape for this to work. I tried to measure the speed of a greasy, dirty flywheel, and after cleaning a small spot, the tape still wouldn't stick. So, I used a can of white spray paint to make a reflective spot. Even from a couple of inches away, the tachometer could not give a reading.

One star may be a bit harsh, as the device MAY work when used according to instructions, but it just seems too limited to be useful.

current_product_reviews: rating 3.0. perfect for Lathe RPM and CNC mill spindle RPM A bit hard to get it angled just right, but does the job with a small margin of error.  perfect for Lathe RPM and CNC mill spindle RPM.  Helps get the speeds and feeds just right.

input:
rating 5.0. The Best Canister I have ever bought! I have always bought canister vacuums for my bare floors and after buying at least 10 over the past 15 years this is the best I have
every bought! I love the Suction on this, you have a dial on the base to either get strong suction for the floors or lighter suction for vacuuming curtains and for me a rug hung on my wall. There is also another way to adjust the suction from the handle if you prefer. The bags are much bigger then any canister I have bought in the past too which is a money saver for me. The Vacuum is light weight and easy to store, the cord is longer then expected and I just love my new Bissell. I gave this a solid 5 star rating because you just can't get this kind of quality from any other brand that I have found and I have an upright Shark with detachable canister which I hate, I much prefer my Bissell Canister to my Shark and you can't beat the price either. I would recommend this product to Anyone looking for a Great, High quality Canister vacuum at a very low price. 5 Stars across the board from me :)
rating 5.0. Five Stars Great machine. Great price.  If you need a canister, get it.  You won't be disappointed.

rating 5.0. Great inexpensive vacuum Very pleased with this little vacuum - well made, effective unit. As others have noted, it's only flaws are a shortish cord and rather short tube, making vacuuming position a little uncomfortable for taller folks. Not that great on pile rugs, but is perfect for bare floors and low pile carpet.

Love that it is easy to maneuver, has reel-in cord and good sized bag for such a small unit. Highly recommended at $50 price point.

rating 5.0. Bissell Canister Vac My first canister vacuum - and I am a convert from the old upright. Seriously, it's only $50, so buy it and try it. If you hate it, return to Amazon at no cost to you. I typically research things to death before buying something, especially anything online, but I bought this pretty quickly after reading several positive reviews and the Q&As. I am about 5'5" and the length of the vacuum stick thing is just right for me - so this may be really uncomfortable if you're very tall. Maybe it comes with an extension pole or something, not sure. BUT, this vacuum has awesome suction and works so well on our very dark hardwood floors plus our kitchen tile. It is a pretty small vacuum, but the electrical cord is about 16' so I only had to move it once to a different outlet to reach the rest of my kitchen. I'd HIGHLY recommend this vacuum if you need something that performs well without breaking the bank.

rating 5.0. Bissell Zing. canister vacuum Love this vacuum so far. Used it a couple of times. Great suction.  O.k. on carpets. I have a dark green carpet that shows every piece of white fuzz possible. As I am a cleaning fanatic, it will probably fine for anyone else.

rating 5.0. Best little sweeper I ever had. Wonderful for hardwood and vinyl floors and for getting under beds, couches etc. Does great on stairs; light weight and very powerful suction. I plan to purchase another for my daughter.

rating 4.0. Light, small, powerful enough The canister vacuum  was as  described: light, small, powerful enough for our uses, in a rather small apartment with plenty of stairs. The automatic re-wind cord is a good feature, and the cord is much longer than that of our old vac. The attachments, as others have noted, are almost useless, they are so small and poorly designed. We will have to find a brush that fits the tube. The low price was most attractive.

rating 5.0. Terrific Vacuum Cleaner I wanted a canister vacuum and this one is perfect.  It is exactly what I wanted and hoped it would be.  The color is great too.  The suction is very good.  I have two cats and we have lots of cat hair around and it picks it all up.  I wanted it so I could get behind furniture and in hard to reach places and it is great for that.  I do use a duster attachment from my other vacuum on the Bissell Zing.  I like the name Zing, too.

rating 5.0. great! only lacks one thing Returned the Oreck $400+ for this vacuum and I'm not sorry! Have a bad back and needed a super-light-weight vacuum for daily dog hair p/u.  This one goes under ALL of my furniture; it swivels; it's light and maneuverable; great suction and stores well.  I am 5ft tall and this is the perfect height for me! The only thing I would change would be a beater brush for rugs.  I hope it continues to function just as well.
rating 5.0. Perfect Vacuum It is super-awesome! I have had for 1 year now. It is so light and maneuverable. Its vacuuming power is great. If possible, I would have given it 10 stars :)

rating 5.0. Great for My Needs After reading many reviews, I went with this canister.  I wanted something really light and maneuverable for the carpet on the curving stair case that is the first thing that greets you when you enter the house.  My carpet is really a light color (almost white) and very plush.  This vacuum has been perfect for what I wanted.  Really strong suction.  I use the small attachment, which I wish was a little larger, but it works really well and gets into all the corners and crevices. I still have a little problem with the black Pomeranian hair that embeds in the carpet, but I haven't found any vacuum that handles that.  I go over the stairs occasionally with a damp sponge that gets it up. It is perfect on upholstery and picks up the black Pomeranian hair there with ease.  This is the best vacuum I've used for upholstery.  I never use the large attachment because I have a couple of upright vacuums for the floors.  This is very light weight considering the power.  No problem for a 70 yr. old woman on the stairs.  It is well made and the retractable cord is a nice luxury.  All in all, it has been perfect for the stairs and upholstery.  I have used it for months, and it seems really well made.  No problems at all.

rating 2.0. Replacement Bags????? This is an awesome vacuum. Very strong, lightweight, I love it...when I have enough bags to use. Amazon does NOT sell the correct replacement bags and I feel like I wasted my money. The only place I was able to find bags was on the Bissell website. I tried to order more the other night and they were OUT OF STOCK. I called Bissell's 800 number and they confirmed that the only place to get the bags was on their website and they were out of stock. So I guess I'll leave my car, and couches and hard to reach places dusty until they restock the bags at Bissell...oh wait, even better, my husband will buy a NEW vacuum from someplace local that actually stocks ALL the accessories needed to consistently use it for what it was meant for. Cleaning.

I'm a huge fan of Amazon, but every once in awhile there's that one purchase you make and you end up regretting it.

rating 5.0. Best bang for your buck.  Great suction, fair price. I love this vacuum cleaner.  I have used it twice now and my husband has used it once.  His comment right after using it was, "Wow!  that thing's got some suction!".  It really sucks up way more than my old canister(s).  I have no complaints about the hose seeming to be cheap as some others have mentioned in their reviews.  The bags are also a typical size for a canister vacuum (some had complained they seemed small).  I wish the electric cord were longer, but that would not be a deciding factor for me and the auto rewind is a nice feature.  The attachments that come with it are quite small and I have not used them yet.  I will probably use my favorite brushes I kept from old vacuums.  Overall, this is one of the better canister vacuums I've owned with much more suction than others.  If deciding between vacuums, go with this one.

rating 1.0. Do Not Buy This is the worse vacuum cleaner I have ever purchased. While it is small and light weighted, it is extremely uncomfortable to use, the suction is ok, but the quality of the parts is so bad that you can't get any dirt off of a regular carpet....it flips non stop, so extremely unstable.
Please do not buy this product and I think that Amazon should reconsider who they are dealing w. Quality of a product one of the big reasons I shop on Amazon.
rating 4.0. Zing works well Bought this little vacuum for our weekend cabin.  It does a good job on the laminate and tile floors and is effective picking up dog hair.  Would recommend it.
rating 5.0. Five Stars The best vacuum for hardwood floors
rating 2.0. Two Stars does not vacuum very well.

rating 5.0. Best vacuum for dog hair I've bought many vacuums over many years, and this is the first one that doesn't spit out the hair behind it! The suction is great, it's lightweight and easy to maneuver. I like having a bagged machine, it's cleaner than the bagless ones, which never get really clean.
I'd recommend this to anyone whose dog sheds!

rating 5.0. One heck of a great Vacuum This is a little champ.  If you live in a smaller apartment, then this is the right machine for you.  Hard floors to low pile carpet , this little vacuum is a champ.  If you do not mind a bagged machine then please give this a chance.  If your a Dyson snob then it might come up a bit shy, but not a complete runaway.  I borrowed my mom's Dyson Pet and gave them a side by side.  This little bugger removed more pet hair than the Dyson.  Bissell has been at this for  over 100 years.  (1876) Replace the bag often, and do not buy bags from Amazon, they do not fit and are a cheap Chinese knock off.  Sorry Amazon but its true.  I love having clean floors again..  Remember to go to Bissell direct for bags, they are a few cents more but they make a huge difference.

rating 2.0. Zing Ding I bought this zing vacuum  for my new little all vinyl floor house, with just a few very low pile area rugs...and a long hair cat. It does the job on bare floor, has good suction with attachment/hose, and is lightweight and compact, handy. However it does an abysmal job on the area rugs...specifically the cat hair. What little it does try to pick up gets snagged on the fuzzy brushy strip on the floor head. No real brushes, and no beater bar, so it just plain doesn't work for the cat hair. Even on bare floor I have to keep stopping every few feet to dislodge dust & hair bunnies from the brushy strip. THEN I have to go around my rugs with a damp mop to dislodge & ball up the remaining cat hair & either pick it up or try to vacuum it again.  A poor use of energy. Otherwise I like it, the design, etc. I think I expected a rotating brush in the head. What was I thinking?

rating 2.0. Beware, the bags cost a fortune and they're impossible to find. Thumbs down. I like the vacuum cleaner well enough... but BEWARE of the cheap vacuum... they're clearly following the home printer route where the printer is cheap but they gouge you on the ink. The bags for this are impossible to find in stores (don't order the 'also bought with this' bags below, they don't work with this vacuum... made that mistake and didn't realize it until the return window had closed. And I ordered several packages.) You have to order the bags for this (model Y122 or whatever)... they cost $5 EACH with shipping. I have two dogs and two cats and I go through a lot of vacuum bags (they don't hold much). Really, reall disappointed. I just ordered six bags from another vendor. $30 with shipping. Nice, Bissell.
rating 5.0. Love it small and light, but powerful.  i am very tall and the extension handle pulls out to the right heighth for me.  Easy storage.  I recommend.
rating 5.0. What a dandy little vacuum Swivels easily.  Very light.  Step on cord retractor.  My house is a mix of tile and carpet.  This vacuum works great on tile dust bunnies.

rating 5.0. Great little vacuum I previously had a Miele vacuum, and was happy with that - but gave it to my daughter. I didn't really want to spend $500+ on a new vacuum! I read all kinds of reviews on vacuum cleaners, from Amazon to Consumer Reports, and the only consistently good report was on this vacuum.  It is a great little vacuum, easily as good as the Miele, and only $50. Definitely a perfect buy!

rating 1.0. Avoid it! [[VIDEOID:da5095802032000b14eb2878149e2554]] I did a lot of research before buying this vacuum. Practically all small,cheap vacuums had ambiguous reviews. This was no exception. I decided to buy this because it was the cheapest and I didn't want to spend more on another one that would probably be equally bad. It was exactly what I expected. It's ok for hard floors but utterly useless for carpets. All these people that say the opposite probably had never owned a good vacuum. Previously I was using either a Miele or a Siemens. Siemens was very good, Miele was exceptional. This piece of junk shouldn't be called a vacuum. I don't return it because it doesn't worth the effort. If you can't afford buying a good vacuum, you may as well buy this one, instead of another costing $80-$90 which will do the same work. But don't except anything. I wanted something cheap, small, for a short period of time that I won't regret  leaving it behind. If this describes your needs also, you know the deal. However, if you are looking for a vacuum that actually cleans your carpet, look somewhere else.
rating 4.0. Excellent for Laminate Floors Easy to use and does an excellent job on my laminate floors.  I bought this because I replaced my carpeting with laminate floors.
rating 4.0. o.k vacuum This vacuum works well on floors but not so much on carpets .

rating 1.0. Good on hardwood, Hard on back First I'd like to say I should have listened to some of the reviews on this vacuum. The suction was great. It was not good for carpeting there is no sliding it on the carpet. The wand is great that it is telescoping but it is too short it needs to be at least a foot longer. I returned this vacuum because of these reasons but the #1 reason it kept giving me zaps as I was vacuuming. I've used may vacuums and that was a first for me. Amazon made this a very easy return transaction!

rating 3.0. Powerful but has drawbacks. Let's face it, this a great value at $50, which is less than the price of a Miele Parquet floor brush attachment. But there are issues. We have hard surface floors, and short pile oriental rugs and thought this vacuum would be a good choice. We thought it might be a good upstairs vacuum so we don't have to lug the Miele up the stairs, but we wound up returning it.

Pros
Very powerful, great suction, probably more than the newer Mieles which we just replaced it with.
Adjustable motor control.
Lightweight, about 10 lbs., good for carrying while vacuuming stairs, or drapes etc.
Has rubber coated wheels, like the Miele, to prevent scratching hardwood floors.

Cons
Noisy, has a high pitch whine when cranked up to maximum power. Gets tolerable if you turn the motor down to about half, but you've sacrificed a lot of suction
The telescoping wand is short as others have reported. Compared to the Miele, it's about 10" shorter to the handle. At 5'11", I found it uncomfortable having to stoop a bit.
Lightweight, has a tendency to tip over. Not as balanced as the Miele, which is a couple of pounds heavier. The Bissel has a narrower stance and sits a little higher.
The standard tool has hard plastic wheels, which was not a deal breaker with us. There are generic natural bristle brush tools with 2 rubber wheels that we would have gotten to replace it had we decided to keep it.
No HEPA filter.
Bags feel a little thin and cheap.

If you are on a tight budget with mostly hard floors, and short pile rugs, this will probably work for you if you aren't bothered by the short wand. The cheapest Miele, the Olympus, if you can find it at a discount with the optional Parquet 3 brush, usually free HEPA filter, is the much better route to go again if you have mostly hard floors and short pile rugs.

rating 5.0. Good price, good product I read other reviews and I admit I was a bit concerned about ordering this vacuum, however, it is small, very lightweight (truly- I could carry it in one hand while going up and down vacuuming the stairs) and pretty powerful! It vacuums my area rugs with ease. I do go over them twice as I have long hair and my rugs tend to collect a lot of it, but two passes and the vacuum doesn't pull up any more.

I like that you can adjust the length of the handle, but I agree with other reviewers, the attachments are a major flaw. They are a joke and the very first time that I used the vacuum, it did smell terrible. I made sure to open all the windows before because others have also commented on the horrible smell it makes when you first get it. Otherwise, I really like this vacuum!

rating 2.0. This is not a vacuum to use for whole rooms ... This is not a vacuum to use for whole rooms of carpet.  It works well for throw rugs and small areas, such as carpeted stairs.

rating 5.0. LOVE IT This Bissell works better than some of my more expensive, comparable vacuums!  I clean houses, so I am very hard on vacuums and go through them pretty quickly.  I was looking for an inexpensive one just to last me for a month until I could get a better one...  Guess what?  I'm keeping this one!!  I works great and very light weight! I can go from hard wood to carpet and then on to laminate with such ease.  Thank you Bissell and thank you Amazon!

rating 5.0. Great little vacuum How is it possible that a 43 y/o male union pipefitter can be so psyched about a sub $40 vacuum? Yes, I do most of the cleaning and cooking in this castle. So there.

I ordered this vac based on the reviews here and will be put to use at the cabin 'up north' which mainly has wood floors,a few rugs and small carpeted areas. I did a quick test around the house to give the vacuum a proper shakedown Assembly was a breeze (who looks at the manual anyways?) and it took a total of 5 minutes from opening the box to plugging in the power cord for some serious dust bunny busting.

This little vac really sucks up the floor. I cleaned 4 rooms in a matter of minutes compared to using a swiffer type floor duster thingy which I normally use, then suck up the piles with the hose attachment from the upright vacuum, which really is a pain and not an efficient use of time. I giggled like a little girl cleaning the linoleum bathroom floor of all the Chewbacca dander the wife and daughter leave behind after a 2 hour session of styling and blow drying. Seriously, the amount of hair that fall off their heads everyday could choke a billy goat. So I was happy that the little purple vac cleared the floor in no time.

Next was to tackle the lint and dust on the hardwood floors under the beds. Piece of cake. I think I heard the little purple vac mutter, "is that all you got?" Fine then, let's hit the dining room and get rid of all the crumbs, dust and whatever the dog dragged in that I somehow managed to miss when I supposedly cleaned the other day. No problem. Next was the living room where it still baffles me how much scuzz manages to accumulate under the sofa and chairs. Debris sucked up in seconds. And the little purple vac did a fairly decent job picking up rawhide bone bits that the dog left behind on the area rug. I evened vacuumed the walls where dust had built up due to the constantly running ceiling fan in the upper landing.

I'm pretty impressed with this vacuum. It's light, not too noisy (about the same as all the other upright vac's I've owned) and does a really nice job cleaning hard surfaces. I personally probably would not count on this vacuum to do regular carpet cleaning duties, but for quick crumb cleanups it'll do just fine. You really need a rotating brush and a beater bar for carpets. Plus a steam cleaner/ extractor if you have pets. Don't ask...I also found the cord rewinder worked well.

If I can find one fault it's not with the vacuum itself but the attachments- where's the dust brush? No big deal for me as I have a few that I kept when the other previous vacuums I've owned 'bit the dust'. Yes pun intended. Not everyone is going to have a drawer full of leftover attachments. I do like that Bissell included 3 spare dirt bags in the box.

In Summary:
1) Cheap (I mean inexpensive)
2) Cleans/ dusts/ sucks dirt on hard surfaces without a hiccup.
3) Light and maneuverable. I haven't used a canister vac in over 25 years (last one my parents owned).
4) Will do a quick cleanup on carpet (crumbs, confetti, hair, etc). This isn't a shop-vac so for wet spills use something else. Like a shop-vac or an extractor. My house is 50/50 carpet and hard surfaced floors and I use an upright vac for carpets.
5) I like it. A lot. Which means squat to 99.9999995% of the people reading this review.

I have no idea how long this vacuum will last. I used it for a total of 30 minutes so far and I didn't see anything wrong with it. In fact, I'm so impressed with this little purple vac that I'm considering getting another one for most of the cleaning duties and just use the upright for the carpets and rugs. I also read some of the negative reviews here and I'm a little puzzled on what people expected of this vacuum. No- it won't thoroughly clean carpet. I'm 6'1" and found the telescoping wand to be a decent length for me. I never had it flip or tip over, and I'm not the nicest on equipment. And if the hose rips I'll break out the electrical tape and patch it like I've done numerous times for the other vacuums I've had.

And that's about it.

rating 5.0. Great Vacuum for the price !!! I have several vacuum cleaners Including a $2000.00 Rainbow vacuum. Beside the Rainbow non have worked like I thought they should the Rainbow is great but messy to deal with. This little cleaner SURPRISED me with the impressive amount of power which is adjustable by a knob on the top of the unit, it controls the motor speed which is a nice touch because when trying to vacuum a low nap rug with it at maximum speed it will try and suck the rug up.The unit has a very well made feel to it, nice metal extension wand which feels nice and sturdy. A bag indicator that lets you know when the bag needs to be changed, which by the way is very simple and easy to do. The cleaner rolls very easy and does not tip over. This vacuum cleaner is small and light weight. It is just right for cleaning your entire house it is not just for quick pick ups. Surprisingly quiet at full blast and gets even quieter when you turn the motor speed down. The only thing I found odd and it does not effect my opinion of this product it comes with the most tiny toy like little crevice tool and upholstery nozzle. Which luckily  have some that are the regular size that came from other cleaners that fit and function fine with this unit.  For $49.00 you can not go wrong, take it from a man who is OCD about keeping a clean house and vacuums it everyday. It does come with 4 bags counting the one that is pre-installed in the vacuum. Has a nice auto cord retract that works nice and smooth. Bissell Has come along way and proving it with this quality product. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND THIS PRODUCT AND SELLER. Oh and it is a nice purple color that looks great.

rating 4.0. Great for the price but only for small homes I've waited before writing a review to see how this vacuum cleaner managed after 6 months and it's still going strong. It has a powerful suction on both wood flooring and carpet and it's easy to get into all the corners. Someone else mentioned that on carpet you can only pull the head towards you but the brushes prevent you pushing it away and that is true. Takes more time to vacuum carpets because of this but it doesn't affect the overall clean. The hose is far too short however and I have a bad back after vacuuming my apartment because I'm bent over the whole time. For the price this is a great little vacuum cleaner and is small enough to be very easily stored in a closet, but avoid vacuuming for long periods of time.

rating 5.0. Great little vacuum This vacuum is my favorite. We live full-time in an RV, and we first purchased a lightweight Eureka stick vac that was more like a toy than anything else. After that, we did a lot more comparison shopping and landed on this one.

In retrospect, I think I would prefer the cordless version, but oh well. This thing works great. It's more powerful than I expected, and it seems to work great on both carpet and hard floors. It even came with a couple replacement bags.

The downsides: First of all, the cord retract does tend to get stuck. If the cord gets caught on anything, it will stop retracting, and sometimes you have to pull it all the way out again to get it to start up again. Not ideal. And there's no real way to manually roll it back up. Second, it's not the most compact when it comes to storage due to the hose. You may not be able to tell from the pics (I couldn't), but the metal handle piece at the end of the hose is curved rather than straight. I like the design when it comes to using the vacuum, but again it's not great for storage.

The upsides: Everything else. Powerful, does its job well, small enough to reach all the hard-to-reach places in our RV, lightweight, etc.

Tip: If you have a lot of hair on the floor (we do, with 3 animals and 2 long-haired people), you will need to pick it up and clear the hair off the brushes pretty frequently. It's easy to do.
rating 3.0. Ok for the money Nothing to write about, not too expensive, works just ok...Ok for the money, light and easy to store. WIsh it was slightly more powerful.

rating 4.0. Great little machine I bought this Bissell Zing vacuum cleaner to replace a similar Sears model that died after only a few yrs. My 1st Sears canister vacuum had lasted 20-25 yrs, so I was most unhappy when its replacement quit working, which at the time of purchase cost more than 4x the price of my present Bissell Zing. I was unwilling to spend a lot of money if a new machine might not last long. I wanted to try something different. I have been as complete as possible in this review, because I had a lot of questions when I did my research for which I could not find answers.

I like this machine much better than what I had. The Bissell is smaller, much easier to maneuver & incredibly lightweight. I did not get tired at all dusting & vacuuming several rooms, 1 after another. The machine is noisy, but the sound is dependent on how strong the suction is, which can be easily controlled with a knob on the top of the unit. Most of the time, it was not necessary to use it at full suction, esp. on bare floors or while dusting, & it did a good job at both.

The on-off switch is 1 of the black "buttons" on the top side of the canister, a much better design than the Sears unit, which was on the rod & didn't work after only a short while. It was always "on" when it was plugged in; not good, & another reason why I would not go back to a Sears model. The other black "button" controls the cord retraction, 1 of my requirements.

The attachments are made out of plastic & I hope they last, which is why I gave the Bissell 4 stars instead of 5. They look cheap after having had a much sturdier machine. The set doesn't come with a dusting brush (but it can be purchased separately). However, the one I had from my Sears unit fit perfectly & works fine. I prefer dusting with a vacuum cleaner, so having a good dusting brush & a canister machine are important to me.

The floor/carpet attachment is not very wide, only 10", & is narrow, which aided in the improved maneuverability. I was able to get in places impossible with my old machine, like behind the toilet. The height of that attachment is less, too, so that it easily fit under a bookcase which had short legs. The end of it where it attaches to the hose swivels 360 degrees, which helps. That end also has a molded piece on its side which fits in a groove on the bottom of the canister for more compact storage.

There is a lever on top of the floor attachment which allows a bar to come down, on which is a row of non-continuous brushes. The set doesn't come with instructions other than on the side of the box, so I took it that the brushes are to be used on my hardwood & linoleum floors to prevent dragging the plastic bottom over them. There are also 2 small wheels at the back of the floor attachment. The brushes quickly pick up hair & dust, which I ended up removing manually.

I only have a couple area rugs & throw rugs. On the bottom of the floor attachment are 2 tiny strips, just under 3" long, on either side of the suction hole of another kind of textured plastic. I think these are the only contact points for vacuuming rugs. I had to turn the suction to the highest point to vacuum an area rug, but needed less suction for a small bathroom throw rug. It did an adequate job, although I do have an upright Eureka vacuum cleaner that I could use instead.

The rod is made out of a lightweight metal & the upper half telescopes into the bottom half, which is an interesting design. With the floor attachment on, the length to the top of the metal rod is almost 3 ft. The end of the hose has a curved hard plastic section which is the handle & it adds about another 9" to the height from the floor. I am 5'3" & had no trouble using this unit. I did not have to lean over & my back did not hurt afterwards. Toward the end of the "handle" there is a sliding piece that can be pulled back to expose a small hole to lessen the suction even more if desired.

The hose is 4'9" long in the flexible part (between the attachment to the canister & the handle section which is part of the hose) & easily swivels 360 degrees, much better than my former vacuum cleaner. The bottom attaches to the canister with a simple locking twist. All the other pieces push together & are tight enough that they don't fall off. In fact, I once pushed the end of the hose into the top of the rod with enough force that I needed my husband to detach it, but he had no problem.

There are some other small attachments. One is a brush for cleaning upholstery, which I haven't used yet. It is about 3" long & has 2 thin strips of very shallow soft bristles on either side of the suction hole. The other attachment is a little over 4" long & is for narrow confined spaces. It works well. On the end of it is a piece that can swivel out and has a U-shaped brush, 1 1/4" long & 3/4" wide, with bristles slightly longer than 1/2". I'm not sure what it is for. Again, I wish this vacuum cleaner came with complete instructions. There is another plastic piece (shaped like 2 C's back to back). One side fits over the bottom of the metal rod & the other side holds the narrow space attachment so that it is always close at hand while vacuuming.

One thing I found surprising is that the vent holes are on the top back of the canister & they emit quite a bit of heat, which might be uncomfortable in tight quarters.

Inside the cleaner bag slips on a plastic clip. It seems like the compartment space may be smaller than other models. I am not sure how quickly the bags will fill up, since I have only begun to use my vacuum cleaner. There is a small window on the top of the canister which indicates when the bag is full.

So far, I have been pleased for the ease of use at such a great price. It is a cute little machine & I even like the purple color.

current_product_reviews: rating 3.0. Good, but expensive bags. OK, this is just a quick note on this vacuum. It is good, it sucks hard and seems well built and very affordable. However, the correct bags for this vacuum are difficult to find and expensive. The bags that Amazon suggests you buy with the Bissell Zing 4122 are bags that say 7100 and 7100L series. These bags are not like the ones that come with the unit, they are significantly smaller and the cardboard that attaches is wider and very difficult to fit. They do work though, just not correctly. The correct bags are, I believe: Bissell Dust Bag 3pk 4122 Series #2138425 (if you search Amazon) At the time of writing this they cost 14.70 for 3 bags.

current_product_reviews: rating 2.0. Don't buy multiples hoping the colors will match..... I bought 2 packages of these trying to make a rainbow stacker toy for my kids without having to buy full spools of every color of the rainbow. Each color in this bundle is about 17-18g and I needed double that for some of the colors. When I received them, some of the colors weren't the same shade between the 2 bundles, so they definitely aren't consistent with colors. The 2 lime greens were completely different, there was no way I could combine them to make one piece without seeing a huge color difference. The quality of the filament is fine, works great in my Ender 5 Plus with no issues, but if you're hoping to buy more than one and color match, I wouldn't count on it, unfortunately.

input:
rating 5.0. great little case for a clown I bought this case to store my balloon/facepainting supplies since I am a volenteer clown and planned to modify it to meet all my needs. In the past I had to carry my large makeup case and another bag with all of my balloon supplies, not to mention my purse and lunch bag. This case allows me to combine almost everthing into one. Now I just carry this case and my lunch bag.

The only fault I can find with this case is the slightly strange odor. The odor went away after I aired it out for a couple of days.

The carry strap is super convenient and the latches fit tightly. Be advised that the dividers do not go all the way to the top of the case so some materials may spill into other areas. I have made new dividers that go all the way to the top out of balsa wood and it works great with the slits in the side of the case and didn't cost a lot. The sides use a type of foam with slits in it to provide the adjustable compartments so it might wear out quickly if you are storing heavy materials in the spaces.

I use the top portion for my face painting supplies, but I had to make a a cover that fit over the existing tool holder to accomplish this. All in all I am very pleased and the case worked the way I hoped it would with the modification I knew I would have to make. This case can take a beating and can be used for many years.

rating 2.0. Cheap I should have listened to the other reviews when purchasing this product. The outside looks alright, but the inside is pathetic. It's very poorly put together with glue coming apart on arrival. The foam that sits on the inside lid was off-center so you could see the aluminum. The bottom of the case is made with the same cheap plastic foam that is visibly glued to the case. The separators are just cardboard pieces with the same plastic foam glued over them. They don't line up with the others and side of case so you have to put them at an angle. Very poor quality.

rating 1.0. Not a Serious Tool Box I rarely write reviews, but this "tool case" angered me so much, I decided to share my feelings. This is not a real tool box. The quality of the case is terrible. Like other reviewers mentioned, the interior is made of cheap foam and and fiberboard. The adjustable panels our so cheap and flimsy, I was afraid they would break when I tried to put them in place. They also don't always align properly. My biggest gripe with the box is that when you carry it, the smaller tools are thrown from their compartments.
It is obvious that the manufacturer cut corners and that their goal was to make the case as cheap as possible. And they suceeded. I think the case might make a nice carry-on suitcase for flying. But as a serious tool box, it is terrible. Good tools should be put in a good tool box. This ain't it.

rating 4.0. Decent Carrying Case The dividers do not hold well depending on how heavy the objects are within the case. Overall a good decent build. Worth the buy. I use this for my hair care equipment. (clippers,scissors,essentials when it comes to cutting hair)
rating 4.0. Purchased for its cool looks. This is not a particularly rugged product, but looks cool, and works for me as a way to store extra tech stuff inside, or for light transport.
rating 5.0. case was as advertised case was as advertised and was perfect for my needs. good price also. I would reccomend this case to anyone who needs a fairly secure case.
rating 5.0. case was as advertised case was as advertised and was perfect for my needs. good price also. I would reccomend this case to anyone who needs a fairly secure case.

rating 3.0. It's a keeper I bought this to replace a similar case that I've had for almost 20 years that finally began to disintegrate. I like that this case has rounded corners, and the locks seem strong enough to repel a 4 year old child. The case shipped with two keys appropriately tied down inside with zip ties. The case is lightweight, but I don't feel like they've sacrificed much durability in keeping the weight down. It also looks nice and the price was definitely right. I'd have happily spent more for a more professional, more durable case that I'd never have to replace in my lifetime, but I just can't seem to find that case.

Now the bad stuff. My biggest complaint is that the inner panel with the 13 pockets is just inadequate and poorly designed. The pockets are just small enough to fit absolutely nothing. I'd rather have 5 pockets that actually fit something, vs. 13 pockets that only fit 5 items. Strangely enough, the pockets are in the same layout and almost the same size as the one from the old case I'm replacing, but the subtle differences undermine the functionality completely. The latch system that holds this panel to the lid of the case is completely backwards in my opinion. The locking pin should be on the panel, and the flap to secure it should be a grommet that fits over the locking pin. Instead, this case has the locking pin strangely attached to the "security flap", and the panel has a grommet/hole that awkwardly fits over the security pin. It's more difficult to secure, requiring two hands, and it doesn't seem to be the most durable option. Lastly, regarding this panel, every time I open the case, the panel falls out of its "holder" brackets, which makes me absolutely crazy. I'm now drilling holes in the brackets to extend them or tie down the panel in a manner that will just hold the stupid panel in place. Here's an idea for manufacturers: Try your product before shipping it.

I could also complain about the handle feeling like it's going to break soon. I carry a lot of heavy tools in this case, and I can't imagine that handle is going to last too long. When it breaks, I'll have to create a solution, but until then, I'm trying to be as gentle as possible.
rating 5.0. Awesome I love the case. I really don't feel like writing a long review but what i like the best is the dividers.

rating 5.0. Tested the case, it is aluminum panels I had read that this case was not aluminum so was real nervous about buying it. It's intended use was as a mild faraday cage for sensitive electronics.

The report seemed odd to me as harbor freight sells an externally identical case which they also claim is aluminum.

I performed 2 tests.

Cell phone test: Dropped my cell phone in the case. Tmobile in NY metro area. In the case with the lid open 2 bars. Close the lid,and calls no longer go through. Not a panacea though. I performed the same test outdoors with 3 bars and the case no longer blocked the strongest 2 antennas. This is NOT something plastic that thin could do, except perhaps polyethylene (shopping bags, garbage cans), except paint doesn't stick to that so it would be obvious plastic.

Scratch test: Picked a inconspicuous area next to a latch and intentionally scratched the paint off the panel with tool steel. Not only did it scratch the underlying metal but I could flatten one of the textured bumps with minimal effort, and the scratch was blatantly metal underneath. Shiny and grooved like guess what, brushed aluminum.

Yes the panels have at least a thin layer of aluminum. (maybe more)

Only disappointment was the insert tool slots are not only not elastic, but not big enough to be useful for anything other than a screwdriver. I knew this from other reviews, so fair game. Kept that panel in place and just turned it around as it helps keep the base contents in place. It does that pretty well.

Rugged, functional, aluminum case. Win.

rating 3.0. Dirt-Yes; Grease-not so much 15 year old concrete floor in 3 car garage.  1600 psi pressure washer.  dirt is gone; grease, oil, unknown stains- not so much. BTW, don't put the cleaner in the pressure washer soap/cleaner compartment.  Wet floor, pour cleaner onto floor and scrub with short bristle brush and let cleaner sit.  Spraying the cleaner through pressure washer was not effective.  Burned thru the cleaner at a high rate.  Higher p[i pressure washers will undoubtedly do a better job, but that would not be because of the cleaner, strictly the higher pressure getting into the stains!

input: rating 5.0. Good PLA. This is fairly high quality PLA for the price.  It is worth noting that, despite what it says, I've had the best luck printing at 230 degrees.

rating 5.0. Print at 230° Printing at 230° worked for me. I usually use Hatchbox filament at 210° but it was underextruding with this filament/ not sticking to the bed. Per an earlier review, I upped the temperature to 230° and I'm now good to go. 🥳

rating 3.0. Hard to recommend for the price Amazon basics PLA is a decent filament but is hard to recommend. This needs lots of fine tuning that isn't needed in higher quality PLA it has some difficulty with adhesion and is somewhat stringy but most of these problems can be solved with hotter extrusion temps. I had good prints at 215 on my extuder and 65 on the bed. All in all a decent filliment but for the price you can get something alot better.

rating 2.0. Really finicky filament Super difficult for me to dial in the best settings to use for the white PLA. Spent hours. And even when I was confident I had the best settings I was going to get, it's just not a good filament. I figured for the price Amazon was charging they must have a decent PLA. Didn't like it. Reminds me of latex. It wants to stretch with the nozzle as it moves away and I don't mean in the normal way you'd expect stringing to behave. It's more of an elastic stretch of what was already deposited versus a pulling of ooze out of the nozzle.

rating 3.0. Not packaged properly The items did not come vacuum packed (which can allow moisture into the filament which can cause errors in the print), and the filament was not threaded through the hole so there is the potential it is twisted on the spool.  I will update the review after using the filament and let you know if there were any issues.

rating 2.0. Needs some work. Prints have stringing, filament is brittle and hygroscopic, prints appear inconsistent with obvious layer lines. I think having slight opacity is a good way to mask layer lines, they may actually be using too much dye. The other thing that might be causing the pronounced layer lines would be varying diameter or volume along the line of filament which might be allayed with finer tolerances. I appreciate the value and the ability to buy bulk quantities, but the result is unfortunately not up to par with some of the other suppliers who have figured out how to better optimize filament for FDM.

rating 1.0. Tangles as it unwinds off spool, doesn't stick well either. I have printed hundred of items and have multiple 3d printers. On my MK3S it kept peeling up as I printed, had to run higher bed temp like 67 on 1st layer to get it to stop. Using hairspray is darn near a must with this filament. Also, It would keep getting tangled as it unwound off the printer spool, 1st time I have ever had that happen and it did it on all 3 spools I bought. Get another brand and save yourself headaches..

rating 3.0. Most average filament I've tried Bought this in bulk due to dwindling supplies of filament during all the Covid supply chain issues. This is certainly the cheapest filament I've tried on a cost basis. Though it's very finicky. For the longest time, dozens of Benchy's and cal_cubes, I couldn't find something that worked. Once dialed in it is decent, but not great. Def a you get what you pay for territory.

rating 2.0. Might wanna pass this one Rarely do I ever have enough problems with filaments that I have to write a review about it but gonna have to do it for this one. Considering as of right now May 2020 there is a worldwide shortage of filaments, I had to resort to getting this one. But it’s just not worth the headache. I’ve used many types of filaments with varying results but this one takes the cake on giving me the most problems. So we here we go :

1. Typical poor packaging that all Amazon filaments have. This is already a bad sign.
2. Read the other reviews. They’re not kidding. You gonna have to crank that nozzle temp up to at least 230 for this to remotely get proper adhesion to print decently (or rather as well as it could).
3. But even with the temps high enough, your gonna get warpings no matter how much you tune the settings.
4. But say you somehow manage to get it to an acceptable result. Then there’s the suddenly clogging that will occur over and over again.

After messing with this thing for nearly 20 hours trying to salvage any use for it, I had to return. I could technically live with the minor warpings and occasional loss adhesions. But the clogging got to me and I couldn’t take it anymore. Had to return this one.

To sum it up, avoid this one. I get there’s a shortage of PLA filaments now, but spend that extra money to get something higher quality.

rating 5.0. some good some bad + UPDATE First roll was amazing I made many prints I was proud of. They looked professional. Second roll I can't make a single print to the end. I have even reloaded file I previously printed and they are horrible. the material strings and is brittle. I measured the diameter and it is all over the place. I can't speak for the first roll because it was great so I didn't measure. During the first roll I was so impressed I ordered a spool of black. I measured it as well and it is all over the place. from .17 to .18 within an inch. NOT IMPRESSED. Of course this all happened towards the end of a long print. Very unhappy. I feel roll 2 cost me money. Roll 1 was awesome so good luck.

Update...
2-1-2021
I have learned alot. I have inspected the rolls more carefully when I receive them. Amazon Basics has actually became my favorite GO TO filament. They have arrived pretty darn consistent. The others must have been a fluke. I print on an Anet ET4 with a temp of 210C and Bed 55C and WOW. I know longer worry about the filament. I have uprated my stars. Go FOR IT!!!
rating 2.0. Not for my creality's This filament keeps clogging, no matter what I do. I dryed it for 6 hours, I increased nozzle Temps... You name it. I don't recommend.

rating 2.0. Poor Quality 2/3 rolls were spooled so when printing it would snag.  Never made it to the 3rd roll before returning.  Filament prints poorly too.  It is elastic and doesn't flow through the nozzle well.  Overall not worth committing to 3 kg of.
rating 2.0. Tangles What you save in the price you pay for in tangles. 6 of my last prints have failed the moment I leave the room due to tangles in the spool. Will not buy again. Recommend Hatchbox.

rating 3.0. Good Price, poor spool winding, leading to binding and print failures. I have purchased this product 3 or 4 times now, so we are talking 9 to 12 spools over the year.

While it is a good deal for 3 spools of filament I found that the spools weren't wound very uniform and tend to bind up often causing print failures. This filament tends to need to be babysat.

So while it is cheap, quality control could be better! I have wasted probably 2 spools worth of this filament over the year.

Unless you are doing small short prints, it's not worth it. Even a short print can bind.

current_product_reviews: rating 2.0. Rough print quality I've been a 3D printer for over two years and and have tried numerous types of filament.  I made the mistake of bying a triple pack without testing the product first.  Sadly, the print quality isn't the best.  The filament is very dark.  If you print lithophanes, like me, this is not the product you want.  Due to the dark color, only a small amount of light passes through the filament.  Additionally, the overal print quality was substandard.  I will eventually use this role, probably on test prints, but this experince has given me enough information to never buy it again.  If your prints don't require light, this will work omay, but know it's really not the best, or even cheapest for that matter, on the market.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. As of now, the only downside is I can't get more. Finally, a few companies have started to address the issue of the never-ending spool hoard that comes with being a 3D print operator.  After two years, I have amassed dozens of the empty spools and it's worsened by the fact they're not recyclable.  So, when I saw Sunlu's refillable spools I was pretty darn excited about putting an end to the waste.  Originally, I purchased the 5-refill pack that comes with one spool.  Everything worked out perfectly.  I was able to load the spools with zero issues and my prints are excellent.  The only downside is that as soon as I knew I wanted to keep moving in this direction, as I checked back I discovered sunlu no longer keeps this item in stock.  99% of my printing is in white and their 5kg bundle was perfect for what I am wanting.  However, I am certain that as soon as it is returned to Amazon, I will purchase more refills.

rating 5.0. Most of the tools you need to get started in 3D printing By the time I found this kit through one of their videos, I had already assembled about half of what came in this kit. rating 5.0. Jack performed very well and was delivered in excellent condition The jack was delivered very carefully by the Amazon driver (I watched him set the package down gently). There was an outer Amazon box and the boxed jack inside, with bubble packaging to keep it from banging around. The jack box was new and undamaged, and the jack inside was perfect, no oil leaks, no missing or damaged parts. And it worked straight out of the box - I was able to use it immediately. I do appreciate the other reviews indicating that shipping damage can happen so I carefully unboxed it, and was pleased at the shipment quality.

The jack worked flawlessly. I used it to jack up a sagging deck to install piers. I had very little clearance (less than 12") and was able to easily operate the jack handle (using only two of the three handle parts) and effortlessly raise the deck 4".

As other reviews note, there is force required to push the plunger back in the bottle, it was more than I could muster while lying down under a deck with very small clearance. I was able to maneuver it enough to get it out after supporting the weight with piers, but it took some wiggling and leverage. Once the jack was pulled out from other the deck, I was able to retract the plunger by standing on it with one foot - it did not take excessive force or jumping, just a patient lean.

Overall this jack arrived in great condition, performed excellently for what I needed, and exceeded my expectations after reading the reviews here. I am happy with this product and Amazon's delivery.

rating 4.0. Tiny scoops! I'm sure that in the scientific world these are awesome scoops that are high on the "must-have" list. If however, you are like me and have no concept of how incredibly miniscule 8-12 mg really is and were merely looking to replace the small scoop that came in your powdered stevia bottle, then this is not the scoop you are looking for! On another note, these make terrific coffee stirrers and fun toys for plastic-addicted maniac cats.  I'm also thinking that they could be used for a rousing game of spoons for your lego mini-figures or a really long game of pick-up sticks. So many uses...so little time.

rating 5.0. Great to use for reloading These scoops are great for the purpose of weighing out smokeless powder for reloading. The small scoop size allows for easy handling of tiny amounts of smokeless powder.

rating 1.0. 3 mg per spoon No where does it say the volume of the spoon which is insanely annoying if you want to figure out the actual weight of what you are measuring. So after scoop after scoop I painfully determined it to be about 2/1000th of a tsp. And the weight of my particular substance was 3 mg. Ridiculously inaccurate and worthless. A spoon has a volume not a weight. 8-12 mg is only for liquid. For anything else you will be way off.

3 mg is too little for my purposes - too many spoons and too slow - so now I need to search again.

input: rating 5.0. Must have for people with hard water Best stuff ever to keep your DW running clean.  The Sears repairman recommended it for our hard water problems and I've used it faithfully ever since.

rating 4.0. What is my dishwasher growing? This stuff helps to keep whatever grows in my dishwasher to a minimum.  For whatever reason, newer dishwashers tend to grow some sort of bio-terroristic slime that will transfer onto your flatware and bake to it.  This product helps to blast off whatever bacteria this is and make your dishwasher look presentable for when your mother-in-law comes to visit.

rating 5.0. Works better than any competing product This is the only product I will use for my dishwasher, and have tried many others.  When washer gets loud or has a smell to it, this is my go-to solution.  Took the advice of another reviewer and interrupt the cleaning cycle mid-way for about 45 minutes to let the solution sit in the pipes/tubes as well as the washer itself (I suspected the tubing had build-up causing the noise level).  Worked fantastic.  Some have said the wax plug does not dissolve - have never had an issue, just use your hottest setting and run the hot water in the adjacent sink prior to starting the cycle, and should not be an issue at all (assuming your hot water heater has a normally high setting).  Some may think the price is high, but considering you only use it periodically and it removes smells and sounds, I'd rather buy this than call in a repair-person or replace the dishwasher.  Do not hesitate to buy  It works great.

rating 5.0. Overall great job My repair person insisted I use Glisten for the dishwasher. He said it was best out there.  He was right. If Glisten to add a chemical to melt or have the grease safely go down the drain that would make the product 1000 times better.
