rating 5.0. Great investment in your quality dishwasher Love this product.  After years of suffering with an inferior quality dishwasher, my wife and I finally found an outstanding one.  To keep it in tip-top shape, we use Dishwasher Magic every two months.  We live in the mid-west where there is a lot of lime in the water which causes scaling in dishwashers.  We've been using this product over the past year and I can't see any indication of lime build-up in the dishwasher.  It also smells good too.  Our dishwasher works as well as the first day it was installed.  Dishwasher Magic is incredibly easy to use, is inexpensive and works great!
rating 5.0. Great product for dishwasher maintenance. Great product to keep dishwasher absolutely pristine.

rating 5.0. I will never go without Dishwasher Magic again, I love the stuff! I've been a loyal user of Dishwasher Magic Dishwasher Cleaner ever since I had a nasty buildup in my dishwasher. I cleaned and cleaned my dishwasher by hand, but the hoses were still dirty so my dishes were not getting clean. I had a handyman come to the house to fix my machine, but he suggested Dishwasher Magic. He suggested I use the cleaner, and then turn off my dishwasher in the middle of the cycle for a few hours to let the cleaner sit in the hoses and inside parts of the machine. This not only cleans the parts you can see, but it cleans the inside parts better. I did what he suggested and I have never gone back! It worked like a charm! I use the cleaner every couple of months to keep my dishwasher clean and free of buildup. I buy this 6 pack of cleaner because it is a much better deal and it is so worth it to keep the dishwasher clean, rather than wait till it gets yucky again. LOVE this stuff!

rating 5.0. Descales well After one use all visible limescale buildup in the tub is gone. The steel is shiny, and it smells a little bit like a freshly sanitized hospital ward. I hope that the cleaner ran through the machine and descaled more than only the tub.

rating 2.0. Does not work on E-coli and other bacteria The item description is very misleading. The 3rd bullet point states that it protects you from E-coli, salmonella and other bacteria. rating 5.0. It really works! We have a Bosch dishwasher that is only a few years old. This past year our dishes, especially the glassware, developed a musty smell after coming out of the washer. We tried everything from tech support to internet searches to troubleshoot and find a fix. Sometimes a possible fix would work for a while only to have it come back. We were just about to buy a new dishwasher, but decided to try the Glisten, even though we had our doubts.

It worked! We did need a second application within 2 weeks as the smell started to return, but so far no further problems. We do use it proactively once a month. Dishes smell great and it saved us the cost of a new dishwasher. Can definitely recommend.

rating 5.0. Dishwasher sparkles! It's a great product. Just unscrew the cap (leave the wax plug alone), pop it face down on the bottom rack, and turn the dishwasher to a high heat wash cycle. Once it's done, the dishwasher and traps will be cleaned up and the unit will run like new.

We had a problem on some dishwasher pipes and used this. It did the trick and saved us $250 on a repair bill.

rating 5.0. After reading some reviews I decided to try Glisten and I have to say the results are excellent. After 3 bottles over the course I have hard well water that created a white coating around the heating element in my dishwasher. After reading some reviews I decided to try Glisten and I have to say the results are excellent. After 3 bottles over the course of a month the white scale on the heating element is 90% gone but what is most surprising is how much quieter the machine runs, how clean it is inside and most importantly how much cleaner our dishes look. I guess over the course of 10 years scale built up in the pumps and although I did think our machine was loud I had forgotten how quiet it was when new. It's at least 50% quieter. Additionally I didn't think our machine was dirty but after using Glisten the inside shines. Same for the dishes, we didn't think they were dirty but they didn't shine like they do now. All in all excellent stuff. After the scale is completely gone from the heating element I'll use a bottle every couple of months to keep is running well. We were actually thinking about replacing our machine but now it's like new.

I also tried Ball citric acid used for canning http://www.amazon.com/Ball-Jar-Citric-Acid-7-5-Ounce/dp/B007CRHD4U/ref=sr_1_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1451664426&sr=8-3&keywords=citric+acid on the heating element. It worked very well also. It seemed to work best when the element is warm and damp. You can see the scale bubbling off. If your machine is bad I'd suggest pouring the citric acid directly on the element along with the Gllisten then after the machine is clean use Glisten a few times a year to keep it clean.

input: rating 5.0. Beautiful print quality. I used the phrozen abs like matte grey. Had an issue initially cause the build plate wasn't z calibrated low enough at first. Actually I didn't even use a paper. Just loosened the screws, lowered the build plate myself, then calibrated and tightened the screws again.  Just putting this out there in case anyone has a similar issue of their test print not sticking to the build plate. Other than that, it's not very noisy and prints in high quality.

rating 2.0. Too many layer lines due to mechanical issues I wanted to start the review by saying, I have 8 years experience printing in FDM, and 1 year in resin. I own 5 printers (4 Elegoo and 1 Creality). This Phrozen is a very well designed and manufactured printer but issues with layer lines due to mechanical problems (possibly caused by packaging or assembly).

I spent 2 days troubleshooting the first Mini 4k printer I got. Even spoke with tech support and printed all the files with the settings they suggested. Ultimately after tons of research and reading through forums, nothing fixed the layer lines I was getting. So, I requested a replacement.

The replacement had similar issues, but not as pronounced. With this one, I spent another 2 days troubleshooting and printing all the suggested files and used the settings the Phrozen tech asked me to from before. The issue continued, and after speaking with dozens of people on forums and printer groups, I came to a conclusion the issue was most likely due to the lead screw or rail.

At this point, my patience has run out. I do not think it's reasonable for me to further work or try to fix this, i'm packaging to return AGAIN... Im hoping to buy one more in a few months when this issue is resolved or I have more time to troubleshoot. Or.. maybe I just got 2 bad printers in a row. Not sure, but lost a lot of time and resin trying to get this printer to work.

rating 5.0. Great design and great prints Phrozen really did a great job with this unit. The 4k mono screen cranks out crisp, highly detailed miniatures. I liked it so much that I opted to pick up their mid sized Mighty printer as well.

rating 5.0. Pretty Fast My first resin printer, so I felt like it would be daunting. After setup and getting used to the post-curing process, this thing spits out nice looking prints(I mostly just print at 0.05mm or 50um).

I was more worried I wouldn't level the bed very well especially with how it kept feeling like it wasn't leveled enough to keep the test paper snug. Turns out this one is notorious for being a little difficult to initially level your bed. I definitely recommend patience and perseverance. I ended up doing a weird method of holding it all down evenly and setting the screws, but then releasing spots that the paper was able to slip and tightening those down further.

Side note: looks like some other reviews are for the regular Sonic Mini. The 4k version has a aluminum resin vat(instead of the plastic) and also the bed has a bit of a slope now. So they did some QoL updates. I wish the vat had a pour spout though that other brands have, but I make do.

rating 1.0. Sonic Mini 4k failure I really wanted to like this printer. The prints were nice and crisp, the printer easy to use.
After about a month, the USB port stopped reading any drive I plugged into it.
Phrozen was great here and shipped me out another unit within days.
The new unit seemed to be great. Printed out several test prints to fine tune the Aqua 4k grey resin and was able to get one good print.
Now, I have had 3 successive failures in a row, where almost all the supports looked like they were just chopped off. Between each print the vat was cleaned, the resin was well shaken, and the build plate was leveled.
When working, this printer is amazing. Unfortunately, it won't work.

rating 1.0. Yeah, I'm sticking with Elegoo... 12 hours of failed prints, multiple FEP changes, bed levelings, setting changes, resin changes... the Mini 4K just doesn't work. I kept getting strange failures almost as if the LCD was cutting out periodically although the LCD test looked fine.

The build plate wasn't actually flat when I got it. The bottom left was slightly higher than the rest so following Phrozen's advice, I sanded it down with the included sandpaper on a sheet of glass... this made no difference and my prints still failed.

I tried leveling on the LCD in which the paper would have minimal friction. Luckily my build plate had good tolerances so it wasn't super tight but after failing to level on the LCD, I leveled in the vat as per suggestions... same failures.

I heard quite a few good things about this printer and was aware of Phrozen's quality control going into it but I didn't think it would be this bad. I've owned multiple printers throughout the years and by far this was my worst experience.

It's going back to Amazon and I'm picking up another Elegoo. All my Elegoo printers have been simply plug and play... they work right out of the box with no fiddling around.

rating 1.0. Faulty product Parts stick to vat. Can’t even print out the included test print. Tried some very basic print and they all failed. Black spots were also found in the lcd. This is very disappointing.

rating 5.0. This thing is awesome! This is my first 3d printer. I watched a ton of youtube before I decided on this one. Let me tell you, I have not stopped printing files since I got it. I play Flames of War (15mm miniatures). A single house is $20 +s/h. With this I been making an entire city, farm, windmill, walls etc. And it isn't $20 a building.

I also downloaded some bigger 28mm miniatures and made some for fun. The detailing in this is amazing. Take it from an old gamer (pewter models) this thing will rock your ass off.

rating 5.0. Amazing For Miniatures The printer arrived with a broken LCD screen. Seeing as this is not my first printer I emailed phrozen support with pictures and results of the usually requested information companies ask for in regards to broken lcd screen claims. I had a quick no hassle reply with them saying they are shipping me a new screen that same day.

With the new screen put in, it works amazing. It blows my photon out of the water and even shames my friends X1. The level of detail it retains is insane.

If the goal is for detailed miniatures, definitely go with this printer. I wouldn't settle for anything less.

I would like to see a double rod z axis as standard in future versions.
rating 1.0. would not recomend Not capable of 4k out of the box and tech support sucks. I have 2 resin printers the lcd on this one is less sturdy and they want 110 to replace it.

rating 4.0. Getting parts can be pricey I have a few printers, fdm, and now this dlp. Using the 4k resin by phrozen, I've managed to print at .025 resolution, I print pretty slow but, they models come out amazing. The detail I can get on something a third of the size of a glue stick is shocking.

The not so great. They give you sandpaper-dont use it.
It's for scuffing the build plate. Buy a tile from as hardware store, 12x12, stock up on some 220 grit full sheet. Rubbing alcohol and water in a spray bottle. I use that to lap my build surface flat, but not scratch it deeply.

If your prints fail, clean vat feature is fast and very effective on getting stuck goop off the fep.

Lcd screens right now you have to go through phrozen to get them, shipping is kinda high.

All in all, this is an amazing printer. Maybe I'm the lucky minority but I've had zero issues that weren't user error in getting near flawless prints. I will be buying a second machine.

rating 3.0. "POTENTIAL" is the key word for this printer. I am really enjoying using this thing. However, there are two CRITICAL design failures that should be remedied by Phrozen as soon as they're able:

1) The build plate arm will flex on layer changes (both on pull-up and push-down). This leads to layer shifts along the Z axis, ie. banding. I designed and built a diagonal strut to reinforce the build plate arm, and now that force is being transferred to the Z axis gantry, which is still flexing significantly - though as the flexing has been relocated, it seems to affect print quality dramatically less. This NEEDS to be improved and is not acceptable on a $400 printer.

2) The tape bezel around the LCD display is a fantastic idea - it keeps the printer safe in the event of a vat failure. But it's so thick (0.12-0.16mm measured at different sections) that it can cause layer adhesion issues. After removing this tape bezel and replacing it with kapton tape (0.06mm thick), the layer adhesion issues have disappeared.

After addressing these two issues myself, I am getting FANTASTIC quality prints. But why should you have to do this on a $400 printer? I really hope Phrozen is able to address this on future runs of this printer, because otherwise, it's a fantastic machine and I love using it. But having to modify it like that to get reliable overnight and at-work prints was really unfortunate.

Now for the positives.

The 4k display absolutely comes in clutch for super fine details. The printer's high-intensity UV array leads to incredibly rapid layer curing times (I'm running 3.5s with Elegoo gray, which is recommended to have 8s layer cure times - with no related issues). There's no issues with UV leaking from the enclosure lid (though some does come out of the USB and cooling fan ports). Aside from the build plate arm, the printer is built incredibly tough, and I have no doubts the printer will last a very long time with proper care and maintenance. The touch screen display is very responsive and the onboard firmware is quick enough that I don't notice an annoying amount of delay while navigating menus on the printer. Also, having the vat cleaning setting is awesome if you've just had a print fail but don't want to spend a few minutes filtering out your UV resin, scraping the vat, and then pouring resin back in - but remember to leave a piece of old support in the corner of the print area before you start the vat cleaning process so you have something to pull it out by!

In conclusion, this is a great printer (with some work and critical thought), but I don't think I'd recommend it to people as their first resin printer. Am I happy with it? Yes. Do I think it's perfect? No. But with some elbow grease and thought, this printer does have the potential to really be something special.

rating 5.0. Amazing results out of the box The included test print (a pre-sliced rook torture test from Thingiverse) was perfect.

The next prints - my first ever prints on my first SLA printer - were flawless. I used cheap Elegoo resins. I used an Elegoo Mercury Plus with diluted Simple Green to wash and cure the models. I used roughly the same settings as recommended in the Phrozen Sonic Mini 4k fb community Google Docs spreadsheet.

Only got a few layer lines on a single large model, but that's definitely from me pushing the speed to about 33% above the recommended settings for that one model. All other prints are perfect.

The Phrozen Sonic Mini 4k has sold me on SLA printing. I have 5 other FDM printers that churn out PLA, TPU, ABS, ASA, and PC. I can get ABS to print so well it almost looks injection molded. None of these FDM printers can do what SLA printers do, even with perfect settings. All of my FDM printers required mods and tweaks just to get passable prints. This SLA printer worked out of the box. The only downsides to SLA printing seem to be (a) the special handling of toxic resins, and (b) the cost per unit of build volume versus FDM.

rating 5.0. Best Printer Ever This printer is absolutely amazing. rating 5.0. Awesome printer. No regret purchase. This is my first 3D printer and I’m obsessed. The detail I get on my miniatures is phenomenal and the printer is very easy to use. Highly recommend.

Learnings:
- sand the baseplate right out of the box. I had a couple failed prints since base layers weren’t sticking to my baseplate. Sanding with included sandpaper solved this.
- use good resin. I was originally using the Phrozen Aqua Gray 4K resin but it was very smelly and prints came out brittle. Switched to the Anycubic Eco UV Resin and have had great success with build quality (at the slight cost of build time)
- use in a ventilated space. The fumes are toxic. I moved this to my bathroom as it’s the only place in my apartment that has an intake vent that vents outside. I also got a respirator with active carbon filters ($25 on Amazon) which made a big difference.
- the baseplate is a little tough to adjust. Mine was ok and calibrated fine once I loosened the hex bolts, but it does stick a bit. Wasn’t an issue for me though I’ve heard others have had more issues.

rating 2.0. High Potential Limited by Cheap Build Quality This printer is a vexing conundrum. It's theoretically capable of producing the highest resolution prints on the market right now, but it rarely achieves it because it's held back by mechanical issues stemming from its build quality. The flexibility of both the entire Z axis and the build plate arm makes for some really unpredictable printing. You can get a flawless print on one go and detached supports the next with the exact same file and the exact same resin in the exact same room at the exact same temperature. And this is even using Phrozen's own resin at exactly their recommended settings for the SM4k. It's infuriatingly frustrating and has resulted in a *lot* of wasted resin.

The build quality is overall frustrating. If you compare this printer to other 6" SLA printers, this one has by far the lowest build quality. It feels like a cheap toy compared to its competitors. The plastic body, the janky screen that can't sit flush, the vat that doesn't have a pour spout, etc. all just add up to making a product that feels like it should be less than half its price. Not to mention the fact that when I took it apart to replace the LCD, it had a breakout board held in place by tape. Tape. No screws. No frame. Just tape holding a loose breakout board to the inside of the body.

Some serious corners were cut with this printer, and I'm not sure why given that we're starting to see the competition offer 4K screens without the horribly detrimental build quality issues. It's a shame because this printer *should* be incredible, but it's held back by some seriously questionable decisions and quality control. The end result is that you often get "4K" prints that are lower quality than "2K" prints from the competition because the competition has its mechanical house in order.

I'd say avoid this printer unless you absolutely need the 35 micron resolution and want to find yourself frequently praying to the 3D printing gods. The print quality when it works (which again isn't often) is the only reason I'm giving this printer two stars instead of one.

rating 5.0. Excellent Print Quality and Easy to Use This is my second resin printer. First was a Wanhao D7 2k (which still works great) but doesn't have the ability to produce super high quality miniatures (just good ones.) This unit is great. As long as you dial in your slicer (Use Lychee, ditch Phrozen's, sorry Phrozen) your results will be amazing. Top cover is a little fragile as the corner cracked from a drop from a few inches onto the counter, but it doesn't affect the unit. Considering buying a second to churn minis out or going up to the Might 4k for large scale prints.

rating 1.0. Not what's advertised I got those switches because they said they were 20 amp rated, looks like the seller has changed the description already but pictures still show they are 20amps (as of 08/01/2021). These switches definitely can't handle 20 amps. They are 5-10 amp max. The original switch I was replacing was a 15amp and that looked much beefier than these. After 2 weeks of using this switch it started sticking in ON position, which is an indication that it can't handle the current load and is about to fail. Original switch lasted over 10 years before it started sticking.
Also the size is smaller that original and it wobbles in the cutout of the panel.
So if you are looking for a switch to handle low current, feel free to use this, but not for big power tools or power supplies that need a bigger draw.

rating 4.0. It's a cheap lighted rocker switch. It works okay.  Yes, it's a neon light and the light only works on 110VAC but that's what I expected.  It's a cheap switch and I would not switch it under full load and expect it to last.  It's a little smaller than the switches I replaced which was kind of annoying but I had trouble finding an exact replacement.  Like others I used hot-melt adhesive to help mount it.  That worked just fine.  Price was good and I have a few spares if one fails.  Wiring is odd but it matched the switches I replaced.  The neutral connection (silver) is just to complete the circuit for the light.  The center terminal (brass) is your load and the hot side of the light.  The end terminal (brass) is the hot wire.  Good luck.

input: rating 5.0. Highly recommended This cement has a great "peanut butter-like" consistency which makes it very easy to work with. It sets up relatively hard in about an hour; I used it to cement some fire bricks in my backyard fire pit and it worked great. Definitely easier than mixing up your own!

rating 3.0. Not as advertised Deceptive.  This item is NOT the item displayed in the image.  The picture clearly shows a "1 Gallon" product, but the actual item you'll receive is 1/2 Gallon.  The 1 Gallon version of this product is titled "MEECO'S RED DEVIL 611 Refractory Cement" and is understandably more expensive.  Don't get duped by the deceiving picture or the fact that the written description states weight rather then volume.

rating 5.0. Goes on easy and dries hard. This is a very smooth mix that spreads like butter and sticks very well. It covers a large area for the volume.  I would like to give my fire box a second coat, but one tub covered almost everything in sight.

rating 5.0. Used this to re brick my Rumsford fire place We used this cement to rebrick our fireplace interior. We set firebrick and used this as the mortar. It set up wonderfully and was as hard as the firebrick after our first hot fire. It's been a few weeks since the job and it looks terrific. Easy to work with, will recommend it and use it again!

rating 5.0. Works very well. I bought this refractory cement to use for constructing a home made gas fired smelting oven.

The cement is a dark gray color and has the consistency of warm peanut butter. It spreads nicely onto fire bricks and is easy to work with. When it dries, it has the same gray color, but slightly lighter.

The cement air dries in 1/2 hour or so, good enough to handle something that was cemented together, but to really CURE the cement needs to be heated.

For example, cementing one fire brick to another, after an hour of drying time, the two bricks can be (with considerable force) pulled apart. However, if the same setup is heated (I baked mine in the home oven at 500F. for a few hours) the same bricks will crack elsewhere before the joint fails.

In summary, this cement works great, it's easy to use, easy to apply and cleans up with water. It dries "well enough" to handle assembled items, but for a full strength cure, the cement must be heated.

(Added info 23 September 2014):

One important thing to know: Do not "scrape off" excess, partially dried cement from a trowel back into the container. The scraped off pieces will cure even with the cover on the tub, resulting in hard chunks mixed with good cement.

I suggest using a sheet of plywood or some other inexpensive, disposable item and scoop out the cement you need, then immediately cover the tub of cement. When you need more, scoop out more and re-close the tub.

Never put any unused cement back into the tub, or it will ruin the whole batch.

Another important thing: When this cement is not used for a while, the solids and liquids will partially separate, leaving tiny puddles of brown liquid on top of the gray cement. Do not dump this liquid out! Thoroughly re-mix up the cement and it will be fine.

Yet another important thing: This cement is highly alkaline (caustic - like lye or drain opener) when wet (uncured). If you get some on your hands and it stays there for a few minutes, you will know it because it will start to sting. This will not cause any injury, it just hurts a bit. So if you do get any on your skin, immediately wash it off with a lot of cool running water (or better yet wear gloves when working with the cement).

Note that the cement is NOT caustic and it is COMPLETELY safe once it is cured (dried). The fact that wet cement is caustic is not unique to this brand or item. Almost all "cement" type products (grout, concrete, etc...) are caustic when wet.

rating 5.0. Great stuff Great stuff.  A little expensive, but a little goes a long way.  This material is not castable, and needs to be used in thin coats.  I put about 1/16 inch on each block and stuck them together.  I then coated the resulting firebox with a thin coat as well, and it was very strong.  If you want a castable material to fill a large volume, use something else.

Product is thick and required some water to thin out for easy application. Once applied it sets up fairly quick. I used a duct tape to hold pieces together until set up. Once set I cured in Kamado.  Fixed my fire box as if it never broke.

rating 2.0. Here's what I learned. I got this refractory cement hoping to completely redo the cement on my home forge. I ended up getting this cement after looking around some and deciding it was a good choice considering my options. After getting it I found to my dismay after starting the job that I drastically underestimated the amount of cement I would need. I ended up trying to mix in some broken up high temperature chimney blocks and ended up with a slopping mess that I can't use and will need to restart. To be honest this product Might Work Very Well if you use it correctly and the thing I am unhappy about is the difficulty of finding a high temperature cement that you can apply in one thick application. So to help all of you here is what I learned:
1. This is a pre-mixed, ready-to-use cement. You do NOT need to add water, so the volume you buy is the volume you have PERIOD.
2. Recommends to use a thin layer just to seal cracks in firebrick/masonry cement.
3. Does not mix well with extra ingredients like pebbles or other cement/mortar or broken up firebrick pebbles.

rating 5.0. Great repair paste for our Pizza Oven. Fantastic gear. Slowly built up layer after layer to repair a 3 by 2 inch gap in the opening of our clay Pizza oven. A bit of stippling and some HT paint and it is like new again. Advice:- build up slowly and apply some heat between each layer until it sets hard. I used a gentle blow torch. Worked like a charm.

rating 3.0. very dried out compared to any mortar/cement i've ever used ... very dried out compared to any mortar/cement i've ever used before. not sure if this is "normal" for refractory cement. doesn't offer much support, and definitely wouldn't hold with any weight dependent upon it... could only ever be used as joint mortar.

rating 5.0. I proved it handles 3000°F This stuff is great.  It went on smooth and creamy, and adhered great.  And it handles at least 3000°F as advertised because I overheated the system and the steel melted onto the cement. Oops.  Steel only melts above 2800°F.  The cement did not get damaged :)

rating 5.0. Works Great on Large Cracks Great stuff.  Worked great on my fireplace.  I used it to replaced cement that was in a large crack where the fireplace and the house come together.  The fireplace is huge....will take 36 inch logs easily.  The crack was 1/3 inch....this cement worked better than anything I have used over the last 38 years.  I built a small fire after doing the cementing to start the cure.  Then I let it sit till the next day when temperature outside had dropped to below freezing and built a huge very hot fire with oak and hickory logs.  The cement dried completely as directions indicated.  Will remember the next time to buy this product.

rating 3.0. NOT FOR OUTDOOR USE. I didn't see any reviews ... NOT FOR OUTDOOR USE. I didn't see any reviews stating this. In fact, I read reviews that stated how well it worked in outdoor fire pits. rating 2.0. UPDATE: Deteriorates over time! UPDATE: Downgrade from 5 to 2 Stars!!!

Had stored it properly per directions. I open the bottle today and see that the glue has turned into a useless gel! I bought the 2 bottles on the same day. The Green bottle is the squeeze on type. I have opened it more often than the Yellow bottle.I needed the Yellow bottle for my 3D printing projects and so I kept it aside to save it.

In one of the pictures you will see that the gel is so thick, that the bottle can be lifted off the table with the unscrewed applicator still stuck to it!.

Do not use the brush applicator unless you plan to expend the glue within a finite amount of time. You are better off with the Green squeeze on applicator that works even to this day!

Original Review:
Excellent and easy to use. I use it to glue 3D printed parts. And it has served this purpose well. The brush on applicator makes it easy and manageable to work with when compared to a tube applicator. And not a whole lot of glue is required to hold the parts together.

But I would be mindful of the fumes.

rating 5.0. Fast and efficient tool for soldering. Make sure you pull the brush through the device to clean outside pipe/fitting. This tool works well to clean copper inside and out--all in one tool. Also, it helps reduce wear on your joints from repetitive stress. I haven't used it long enough to discuss longevity, but it's similar to other manual brushes we all use and replace periodically for best results.

As far as instructions, there aren't any. So to clean inside fittings, make sure the narrow brush is extended just as pictured on Amazon. To clean outside pipe (or street fittings), pull the narrow brush all the way through the drum brush inside the red plastic. This exposes the round, drum brush inside. You should NOT be running the brush inside the pipe (or street fitting) while attempting to clean the outside. This is wrong. You need to draw the brush into the red plastic part and through the drum brush entirely then clean the outside of a pipe or street fitting. Conclusively, you don't just leave the tool as pictured and merely clean inside and outside. You need to make an adjustment depending on inside or outside.

rating 5.0. I'm a general contractor and have used many other versions ... I'm a general contractor and have used many other versions of this tool. The stainless steel hold up a lot longer and speeds up the whole process.

rating 3.0. This will save your wrists and speed up polishing the ... This will save your wrists and speed up polishing the copper tubing but be ready to make some field fixes.

The inside brush always breaks off even if I'm spinning with spiral running the drill below 1000 RPM. You might get it to last longer if you can avoid having the drill clutch kick in.

Eventually the entire brush assembly will break off the shaft, a pipe clamp fixes this.

rating 4.0. Works great, saves a ton of time! This thing works great.  Saves a ton of time.  The bad reviews are most likely from people who do not know how to use it properly.  The red part of the tool slides forward, (completely burying the inner wire brush in the middle section of the red part) to clean the outside of pipe, then slides back(to expose the inner wire brush) to clean the inside of fittings.  I'm betting the people who broke theirs were accidentally cleaning the inside of the pipe, big no no.  One star taken off because the red outside of pipe cleaner can be difficult to slide causing you to force it and scrape up your fingers or hand on the "inside of fitting" cleaner but I'm not sure if it would work as good if it was looser.

rating 1.0. I HATE IT DO NOT BUY!!! Absolute garbage of a product. Not only are the materials used in this product flawed, its also flawed in its functional design. If you try to clean the outside of a copper pipe with the barrel extended forward and the inside brush in the rear,, there is no locking the device in this position, so as soon as you put the smallest amount of forward pressure, as in just to try to slide the barrel over the pipe, the barrel pushes forward again over the inner brush and you haven't gotten anywhere. I also got the inner brush stuck inside a pipe fitting,,, such a TERRIBLE DESIGN, i cannot express how much I hate this product. Whoever designed this obviously never cleaned a piece of copper in his life.

rating 5.0. You also have to treat it like a scientific instrument because it is a scientific instrument I've been using this for over a year reliably. Replaced the batteries one time. Dropped it in the water many times and it keeps working. I saved the calibration fluid in mason jars and have been re-using them for over a year. I see a lot of negative reviews by people who don't understand that this is a temperature-calibrated pH meter and so it takes some time for the probe to reach equilibrium temperature with the solution. Yes, you may have to wait 5 min to get an accurate reading if you take it out of the fridge and put it into hot water and you will not have to wait more than a few seconds if your probe is at room temp and so is your solution. You also have to treat it like a scientific instrument because it is a scientific instrument. It requires that the tip stay moist so just do it. Make sure the probe is touching the sponge and the sponge is wet. You will also need to clean it periodically. I use alcohol when it gets gummed up and then I recalibrate. If your batteries are dead or dying it will become inaccurate.

I gave it 5 stars because it's the same as those $200 meters at the hydro shops but for $50.

rating 5.0. Awesome This product is beyond my expectations after the poor experience of trying to purchase one of those 4 in 1 stick PH meters. I ordered the stick PH meter first only to find out after I hit purchase that it was a drop shipment and it would take 3 weeks for delivery. The listing on Amazon stated shipment within 2 days, but nothing about 3 weeks. The package arrived in a plastic garbage bag shipped from China, it wasn't even padded or wrapped in bubble wrap. The package was crushed and mind you, it has a long thin probe. Three days ago, I ordered this meter and both meters arrived today.

One crushed in a plastic garbage bag shipped from China and one shipped by Amazon in box designed for shipping. Now for the product review. This meter not only came in a shipping box, it came with a sturdy and secure storage case. I mention the sturdy case because the meter is secured in brackets inside the case, so it's not rattling around in the case (rattling not good for sensitive lab equipment). It came with the correct batteries. You know the kind of batteries that the average person would not have in the kitchen drawer (meaning take a trip to wally world). It takes 4 button style batteries and it even came with an extra package of 4. Meaning, I could use it right then and there. Unlike the meter placed on a slow boat from China in a garbage bag. I inserted the batteries, calibrated it and finished a project that should have been done two weeks ago.

The only criticism, if your not familiar with this type of lab equipment and must use the instructions, be prepared to read the instructions several times. The product is made in China and the instructions are written in English. But the Chinese don't think like Americans and reading American Chinese is yet another language we are forced to learn. I can't wait to be able to buy American products again made by Americans with instructions written in American English. All that aside, this meter is a good buy. Leave the stick meters in the garbage bag where they belong.

Please pardon my rant. To make up for it, for those who need a soil tester and are just as frustrated with the stick meters, here is how you use this meter to test your soil. Simply add your soil and equal amount of distilled water above and beyond what gets soaked up into a glass jar and shake it thoroughly. Let it sit for about thirty minutes and shake the extract again. Pour the liquid (extract) into another glass jar and test with this meter. Yes, it takes longer, but if you want an accurate PH reading, this is the EPA method ( I read that somewhere).

One last tid bit. Beware, now that every big box outlet is allowing drop shippers. If you need to return your purchase, they won't offer you the prepaid return sticker. And often the return shipping to China cost more than the purchase price. I learned my costly lesson, if not shipped directly by the big box, I ain't buying it.

rating 4.0. Cost Effective Solution for Accurate pH Reads for Aquarium Water Changes Tried many pH reading mediums including strips, liquid solution and the throw-away pens. The liquid solutions came close but are not completely accurate. With proper calibration (takes some effort to read and thoroughly understand the instructions and process), this little meter gives by far the most accurate pH reads. As a bonus, the meter reads the water temperature. For the price, this little model is a steal and calculates close to the $150+ models. Personally, I use to calibrate pH and temps for water changes in my aquariums. Prior to purchasing this meter, I was guesstimating or running many liquid tests to match the new water to the existing tank water being removed. This little meter pays for itself quickly in time and solution costs. I simply add buffering and hot water to the tap water and test as I'm adding. Get an exact match each and every time. My fish are happier and less stressed for the extra effort and patience required while the meter reads stabilize.

For those who gave the meter less than 3 stars, I would venture to guess their initial calibration was improper and/or they lack the patience for the reads to stabilize.

NOTES on this model:

Calibration MUST be followed exactly as the instructions state. There is indeed a 9.1 solution rather than a 10.0 but the meter will calibrate exactly.

The pH reads do take a minute or more to reach stability thus patience is required. Swirling around will help the unit to read quicker.

The tip MUST be kept moist at all times to avoid having to recalibrate. I simply washed the sponge that came with the unit, keep it wet and over the sensors then place the cap on tightly. Presto, stays calibrated and gives accurate reads each and every time.

rating 1.0. This is a very good pH tester! (Please see Update for major change in viewpoint.) We make home-brewed Kombucha beverage and had need of a good pH tester. Kombucha tastes best when bottled within a certain pH range. Originally, we purchased a cheaper pH tester of a different brand that took much patience to use, long waits to derive the pH level and constant upkeep, never knowing if it was giving us an accurate read of the correct pH or not. We finally decided we needed something different because the cheaper model was not working. I began to research pH testers and reading reviews online at Amazon. To be honest, we didn't really want to put more money into another pH tester nor buy a more expensive unit because we didn't know if any of them worked good. However, we knew we were not getting good results and didn't want to brew bad Kombucha either, so a new meter became of paramount importance. After thorough research, I decided the best one to try would be Dr. Meter pH 100 Pocket Sized Meter. Listen folks, I'm here to tell you, having tested this product in comparison to our former meter, the old adage stands true, "you get what you pay for"! Dr. Meter pH 100 Pocket Sized Meter is worth the price paid as it gives excellent results quickly, is easy to use, comes very well packaged with all you need to begin pH testing! What could be better!

Dr. Meter pH 100 Pocket Sized Meter comes in a well constructed plastic box with a good solid latch on it and contains everything you need including extra supplies! The container not only stores the manual and the extra supplies, but has a place for the pH meter to snap into so it won't jostle around, but stays in place securely until needed. It comes with extra batteries, and three different ranges of pH solution powders (2 packets of each, six in total). You choose the appropriate pH solution powder, mix with distilled water, place in clean jar with a lid to use each time until it's gone and a new batch is needed. The User Manual is easy to read and instructs on how to use the unit, as well as how to set it up the first time, changing from Centigrade to Fahrenheit if you choose, for the temperature. You set it up so the pH matches your prepared solution. The Meter has a cap that fits on the end of the probe in which resides a type of sponge cloth that you place several drops of solution into so it will keep the probe damp. Then you snap it on tightly and put in the case until you are ready to use. When you are ready to test, you take it out, rinse with distilled (or pure water), wipe off and then perform your testing. When finished, you repeat the procedure of rinsing off with distilled (or pure) water, drying off, making sure there is enough solution on the sponge in the cap or adding more to it, fitting that on securely and snap back into the case for the next use. Then simply close the box tightly and it stays safe and protected for its next use. It's simple and quick. It takes less than a minute to do the actual testing to find what the pH is. We will soon have a pool that will require pH testing and this same tester will work for that as well. It was worth taking the time to do the research and read the reviews and invest in this meter so we could be sure of accuracy for the things we will use it for. I would recommend this product to anyone needing a good pH tester.

UPDATE: Unfortunately, I have to say this pH tester was not what it appeared to be after all. We used it and it did work fine for a number of months, but then it died. This was not a cheap pH tester for us so we were disappointed that it did not even last one year. I'm sorry to those who may have gone on my recommendations and if you did so, I hope you have had a better outcome. It did work well when it worked but then it was dead way before it should have been. We were disappointed and again I apologize, but of course, I didn't know when I wrote the original review the outcome would be as it was.

rating 1.0. Possibly a Fraud - Do not buy I ordered this pH meter for brewing/pickling. After repeated testing and running through all of the buffer packets, it COULD NOT calibrate. After a google, it looks like this product may be a fraud and some of the positive reviews on here are for another product entirely. I returned it and thankfully Amazon refunded me after they could not get in touch with the manufacturer.

All in all, pH meters are just pricey and cutting the corner isn't worth it. You obviously want a more accurate reading than using strips, so just spend the extra money on a decent one.

rating 3.0. Off to a rough start, but optimistic I will update this review as I continue to play with the meter.

The good: very sleek design and incredibly portable. comes with a case for travel usage.

The bad: Received this a few days ago to get pH of streams while working in remote areas. As I opened the package, the travel case lid was not sealed and the components were all over the package. Shipping happens so I wasn't too upset. Began to calibrate it today and kept on getting an error message h----. Having calibrated pH meters consistently over the past few years, I was a little worried and figured something on the inside was going on. So I took the meter apart to discover one of the prongs was bent and would not fit into the necessary frame. Since my pH calibration solution had been sitting out for a while, I decided to mix up a new batch. While testing it I knocked over the beaker (100% my fault) and it spilled so I just ordered new calibration kits. If this is your first time using a digital pH meter, I would recommend doing some video watching on how the calibration works. Previous reviewers complain about the directions, and I will admit they are not the best, but in this day in age there is no excuse for failing to calibrate properly. Once I get the new mix in I will attempt to calibrate again and update this review. Optimistic about the product overall but slightly disappointed I had to take it apart and reassemble on day 1.

rating 5.0. Rvised..... I would have given five stars but the temp setting would not change from c to f, but as far as PH goes it was great for one second, one calibration. I will use my finger to test water temps for my green babies, however the temps in Fahrenheit would have been nice. It tested right the first time with General Hydroponics 7.0 and off with the rest. The meter took a fast down turn and would not drop when adding PH down, it is going back and I've resorted to the old $13 one that I saved. I may give another one a chance, but too much riding on plants to play low quality games, maybe it was a bad apple in the bottom of the barrel, damn!

I will start a new from fresh since they sent me a new one. The new one was just opened and was dried out with crystals on the bulb, I washed with distilled water and bought probe solution that it is soaking in as I type. I will rinse with distilled water, blow dry with my mouth and use probe solution in cap, every time! Well, that is if it calibrates properly this time. If this one does not stay stable or work right, it will be no fault of my own. I think/hope it will be a good one, I'm giving it a second chance even though the first one caused mass damage to my grow. I will update again on the new one as I have the last one, seems to have big potential but the next update will tell ''A Tale of Two Meters.'' LOL!

rating 5.0. If you have a small rotary tool get these! If you have a small rotary tool these things work great. They'll cut most things that you'll consider cutting (carbide is pretty hard), do it faster than a sanding drum or grinding stone, and so far have been virtually indestructible (I'm sure someone is going to do something stupid with one and post "well I broke it" ).

I have been using larger die grinders for automotive porting work and I thought someone was joking when they suggested using a small dremel type tool and 1/8" shank burrs for porting... well, I wouldn't want to use them for larger areas but they really are the deal for really fine work around a valve guide in the pocket.

rating 5.0. Very good tools Let me give a bit of a disclaimer here, I am NOT a tool & die guy, or an expert on metal working tools. That being said, I do all kinds of DIY projects and crafts. My rotary tool is one of the most used tools I have. Everything from guitar making to sharpening yard tools to shaping wood and metal, these tools seem to be very high quality and very HARD. My specific reason for purchasing this particular set was for working and machining kydex for custom sheaths and holsters. These cutters are AWESOME for that. I have also cut some wood and some sheet steel. Cutters have done well on all that with almost no sign of wear. They are still SHARP. Did I say these things are HARD? They are. Also, there weren't any real measurements of sizes given when I purchased. I was hoping the biggest cutter was 1/4 inch, and it is. So if you look at the biggest cylinder shaped cutter, it is 1/4 inch almost exactly. Everything else is just a little smaller than that. I am VERY HAPPY with this set. Even thought it is Chinese, it seems to be real hard tungsten carbide, and even if it isn't, it is plenty for the tasks I need. Also, arrived in 3 business days, which is fine by me.

My biggest issue with this product is the accuracy. I have always used test strips and I use two different brands that match up almost perfectly. Testing water for PH, ammonia, and all that is very important for the health of your aquarium and if the readings are even off a couple of points it can be detrimental. This was showing 7.6 for my PH which is worrisome, whereas my test strips showed 7.2. Just that .4 difference could have made things very complicated and put my aquariums at risk.

Easy to calibrate.

rating 1.0. Miserable fail of product This product is terrible. Heat resistant my a**. I spent $30.00 for 2 rolls of this junk. Followed directions to the T. Installation of product was easy and looked really good. After wrapping the exhaust pipe and letting it cure overnight I took the car out to run an errand. This product started smoking so bad that I had to pull over to make sure there was not a fire.I had other drivers blowing their horns and pointing out the smoke. I decide to turn around and drive back home without running my errands. By the time I got back home I had only driven about 8 miles over a time period of about 20 minutes, and the exhaust had blown a hole through the fiber fix "HIGH HEAT' wrap. I would advise consumers to save their money on this over priced product and search for a better solution. $30.00 for 2 rolls. 2 inches wide by 70 inches long.

I even work in the auto industry. Fiber fix I'm sure works great near your tail pipe or on your furnace but they need to be more careful with their description on where it can be applied. I would put a warning on the wrapper not to use it near the exhaust manifold. Oh well though, money wasted but lesson learned.

rating 5.0. Exhaust leak is gone! Easy to install. Exhaust leak is gone! I am so happy. It has been 1 month. I will update later if it stops working. ha ha.
I had a leak at a join on pipes which was harder to wrap, so I used tailpipe sealer, and two roles of this fiber fix heat wrap tape. . . It was fairly easy to put on. the tape that comes with it is very dinky, so I used large aluminum heat tape to cover instead of tape provided. I highly recommend getting your own tape. Also, the sand paper that comes with it is so small it is there only to remind you to sand. I used a wire brush to sand. Overall, I saved hundreds of dollars using this, and am happy so far.

Update:. Two years later and no leak. I wish I could give more than 5 stars.

rating 5.0. Worked perfect for car exhaust. I had a rusted and broken exhaust flange near my car’s muffler causing the pipes to become completely disconnected. The instructions were simple and easy to follow. After it cured, which only took a short while with the car running, the repair dried hard as steel. Also, the check engine light went off so no more exhaust leak. Highly recommend!!

rating 5.0. Perfect for small jobs I had a small hole (about half the diameter of a dime) in my car's flex pipe and this worked perfectly at patching it up. It's been a month now and it is still holding up just fine. For extra security, I added a few screw clamps while it was still curing. I also used a heat gun on the area to help with the initial curing and then drove around for about 15 minutes. I never had an issue with smoke as others have stated so I can't speak to that problem.

Just a few tips:
- I underestimated the length of a single roll. It was just enough to patch up my job but I almost wish I had bought two rolls. If you aren't sure, buy extra!
- Clean and/or do a light sand on the area before applying-- it'll give you better adhesion.
- Wrap it TIGHT. It wasn't very stretchy so you need to put some muscle into wrapping it.
- Take your gloves off or put on clean ones before adding the foil tape at the end. The entire length of tape has an adhesive backing so I got portions of it wet while trying to peel the paper off so it didn't end up sticking very well. Wasn't a huge deal though because I added some screw clamps as I stated initially.

input: rating 5.0. This is the Only penetrating oil to ever buy Don't waste your time with any other supposed creeping oils. Buy the best the first time.

I read that Kroil works where others fail so I gave it a try.  I was able to remove all three of the badly rusted caps with a little Kroil and some taps from a small mallet on a pipe wrench.

This stuff helped me beat the odds.  With Aerokroil, patience, and persistence I was able to remove every plug without breaking one of them.

rating 5.0. Best penetrating oil around, period This is the best penetraiting oil around, period.  It does take time to work as do all products like this, but it does work great.  It has worked better for me than PB Blaster.

rating 5.0. On Time - As advertized I received the Penetrating Oil within the time specified, and it was exactly as advertized. Good Job. I would purchase from this source again. This product frees parts which have been bound together over time, and is a great product.

rating 5.0. The best penetrating oil This is great penetrating oil. I have a gear box on a rotary cutter that has a pipe plug (screwed in) that needs to be removed to check the proper oil level. I've never been able to get it off. In fact I usually just stick a piece of wire thru the fill hole to make an estimate of the proper level. Today, I was looking over the rotary mower and decided to check the gear oil level. I remembered I bought the Kano Aerokroil oil some time ago (this past July) and decided to give it a try. I squirted a small amount on. It just so happened nature called , so I let it penetrate for several minutes. When I came back, I applied my vise-grips and the plug screwed off like it had never been stuck. In previous attempts I had used other popular penetrating oils but they did not work. Like I said, I had given up all hope of getting this plug unscrewed. So I'm very happy.

This oil seems expensive, but when a rusted on bolt comes off, you realize it is cheap.

Update (Sep 1, 2012). I just finished changing my O2 sensor. (If you ever changed one on V6 Camry you know how tough these can be because of the limited space.) It was frozen in place. Squirted some Kano on three times over the course of an hour and half. Took off to do some errands and came back after about 2 hours. Then the O2 sensor broke free with minimum effort. This is great stuff. Just give it a chance to work. I need to use it more often.

rating 5.0. Best penetrating spray I've used Originally bought this to use on the sparkplugs in a 99 F150.  It loosened them right up.  I've also used it on rusted bolts and it hasn't failed to penetrate yet.  I like to spray it on the bolt at least a day before I want to remove the bolt, then spray it a couple more times the day I'm removing the bolt.

rating 3.0. Helps clean lead fouling from a revolver Lead fouling is a common problem with revolvers, especially "dirty" .22 caliber revolvers, and there are a variety of products recommended for removing it. Aerokroil is one of them. It certainly helps and I'm glad I bought the product for this purpose, but I find that I need to use other products in addition to Aerokroil to complete the job.

Evidently, the product works well in penetrating oil applications such as those you would use something like WD40 for, but I have not used the product for this type of application.

rating 5.0. The good stuff I have been using Aerokroil for more years than I care to think about. The bottom line is, this stuff really works. There are a lot of penetrating oils out there that do "work", but if you want the best of the bunch, this is it.
The only problem is that Kroil is harder to find than the usual brands seen at the auto supply and hardware stores. So it is nice to know that it is available here on Amazon. Keep a can of in your work shop for those difficult stuck bolt problems and use the other common store brands for normal stuck bolt problems.

rating 5.0. Hubby thought he had it all... My husband thought he knew every product available. When I bought this as a gift, he was scepticle.  This is now his product of choice to maintain, loosen and fix garden equipment, engines, lawn mowers, kids toys etc.  GREAT product.

rating 5.0. SOLD ON KROIL(AEROKROIL) I've tried numerous penetrating solvents, but after hitting the wall trying to free a 1995 Camry passenger side drive shaft from its' motor mount housing, Aerokroil did the job with little effort other than patience. After spraying and light tapping, the parts broke loose after 3 applications covering about 30 minutes. For any tough jobs, Aerokroil gets my vote. The only unfortunate thing is that I can't get it locally. It has to be shipped to me.

rating 5.0. Amazing stuff, miracle in a can. I cannot believe how great this stuff works. I've tried them all, PB, LiquidWrench, WD-40, all of them pale in comparison. When they claim it's the oil that creeps believe it. I recently acquired a 28 year old car that was stored in a moist area and was used on roads that were salted. I broke a bolt off flush to the intake manifold and had to get the easy outs on it. After I drilled into what remained of that bolt, I was struggling to get it out so I grabbed this, hit it once with a little spritz, started tapping on the tap with the easy out and it backed it right out. I had a frozen rear brake adjuster, same deal, spritz, whacked it a few times and it freed up. I looked inside and saw it had crept all the way down to the bottom of that adjuster. It is a miracle in a can. If you can't get something loose with this stuff, you will probably have to get the torches and start melting metal. I did have an injector line get stuck on an injector (Diesel) and I couldn't get it to come loose with this stuff, the only time it didn't work, however, I could tell the line had been overtightened. Had to cut that one off. Out of 8 injectors though, considering the condition of the car, I didn't do too badly.

rating 5.0. works great, forget all other oils i recently had to change my primary O2 sensor and had trouble removing it from the header of my car (seized). i doused it with pb blaster and went at it with my socket wrench and O2 sensor tool remover for a good hour but it just didn't budge. did some research online and found reviews for kano kroil to be quite positive for these kind of situations so i purchased the aero version from amazon. after spraying some kroil on the bolt, 10 minutes of waiting and 2 really big pulls on my 22mm wrench, it finally gave. going forward i'm not even going to bother with pb blaster, wd-40, etc...kano kroil is the way to go for rusted/seized bolts, short of using a torch.
rating 5.0. Geat product Great price Simply the best penetrating oil available. Great price and shipped on time. If to have a tool box than you should have a can of Aerokroil!

rating 5.0. Best Penetrating Oil on the market I purchased this oil after reading about it on a firearm cleaning site. Users have been applying this to a cotton barrel swab and leaning the carbon out of their firearms. It works great on preparing to remove copper fouling also.
I run it through the barrel on a swab and let it sit for an hour, then run a brass brush through and it is amazing how clean it gets. Ran a clean swab through. Then run some extreme copper solvent. I also recently used it on some frozen rusted bolts on the exhaust pipe of a tractor. Sprayed it on, let it sit overnight. Although they didnt break loose as easy as I had hoped, I did end up heating them with a torch and then spraying again, they did eventually come loose., I was afraid I was going to snap one off.
This is a great oil and has an added feature, it smells great too!

rating 5.0. Bolts corroded in place and nothing works? This does. What are called penetrating oils are used to free up metal parts that are supposed to come apart, but don't, likely because of corrosion and the passage of many years. The standard "penetrating oils" are WD-40 and PB-5. They have worked pretty well for me.

Until I hit the bracket holding a spare tire under a 1984 RV. I used lots of PB-5, and eventually a 3/4 impact wrench. Simply said nothing was working at all. I started to plan cutting the thing out and welding up some sort of replacement. A diesel mechanic friend asked if I had used Kroil. They used it working on large buses, and she mentioned that some airline mechanics swore by the stuff.

I used it, waited a few minutes and gave the bolt another shot. After a minute with the impact wrench the nut began to move slowly! Slowly the movement picked up. The nut actually came leaving the threaded rod pretty clean. Kroil is worth every penny when you have the tough ones to deal with.

NOTE - I don't own any stock nor received any samples. This stuff really works.

rating 5.0. A well-built device that works well for a good price. This is a really nice little converter. I use it to drive powered speakers from my TV (instead of the low-quality TV speakers). I would expect it to work well with any other device that has a digital optical output.

This rather small, well-built metal box came with the digital optical cable (TOSLINK), and the power supply. (Also, a coax cable, which I didn't need or use.) It was very easy to plug the optical cable into the back of the TV, and the other end into the converter. Then connect the power supply, and the 3.5 mm stereo plug for powered speakers and we were up and running.

Please note that the TV no longer controls the volume levels. That is now controlled by the audio device that is being driven by the converter. In my case, that's a medium quality set of computer speakers.

I expected something like this to cost a lot more, but I got it for about $13 with Prime. The quality at that price is excellent.

rating 1.0. Verify the digital output format of your device before purchasing! New review: CHECK THE MANUAL OF YOUR DEVICE BEFORE BUYING.
My TV auto-switches between two audio signals, based on the input signal. If input is NTSC, output is PCM. If input is ATSC, output is Digital Dolby. NTSC (National Television System Committee, or "Never The Same Color") is pretty much dead. Make sure the device you are connecting to supports PCM, and if need be, the user can select the appropriate output format. There are other, similar devices which support multiple formats. Check before you buy! For reference, I am retaining my original review:

Old review:Hooked up as specified: Digital input to TV's digital audio output, aux output to external speakers or headphones. Got nothing but a clicking noise.

rating 4.0. Works well so far, maybe very tiny added delay I don't yet notice a difference in sound quality between this device and running the optical output straight to my amp. I may notice a slight bit of added latency but it's almost too small to notice, even for someone who is very sensitive to that sort of thing.

I needed this device for a specific purpose. I wanted to use a bluetooth transmitter simultaneously with my amp's speaker output. The transmitter could take input from the headphone jack of my amp, but it shuts off the speakers. The amp has an analog line-level outputs, but it only outputs when the amp is receiving analog input. So to piece things together, I have the TV optical output lead into this device, which outputs to the amp's analog input, then the analog out from the amp goes to my bluetooth transmitter. Thus, I can have speakers on and the headphones on, or any combination of on/off. I also did this because I didn't want to wear out the headphone jack on my amp, which would happen with repeated plugging/unplugging. The latency that I mentioned happens on the speakers as well, so it's not a bluetooth thing. Again, though, it's virtually unnoticeable.

rating 5.0. Works great, but Please read ! Works great with my (2016) LG_50LF6100 TV and my 1985 Technics receiver.
Comes with optical (TOSLINK) cable and power supply....which I don't think other adapters do.
No hisses or pops so far. I had an issue which the supplier corrected very quickly. (Yes, would buy again.)

The product is made in China so instructions are limited and written in poor English ... SO PLEASE READ:
1. This audio converter only supports LPCM and PCM audio format.
You will have to select optical output and PCM on your TV audio settings.
2. PLEASE remove the plastic caps on the tips of the optical cable (see attached)
3. PLEASE insert the optical cable carefully, IT ONLY GOES IN ONE WAY. DO NOT FORCE.
Examine the cable end after taking the plastic cap off.
Note the shape of the cable end and match/align with the corresponding receptacle on the device.
One side is squared. One side has chamfers.
4. Optical out on my TV is considered "external speaker" so I adjust the volume on my receiver and not on my
TV. This converter itself does not have audio volume control.

Remember that optical cable ends are somewhat delicate.
Dirt or scratches can cause them not to work, so don't mess with them.

rating 5.0. The missing link in my home theater system. Nicely manufactured case, extruded and machined aluminum housing. LED to indicate power. Simple to set up -- wall wart power supply that I plugged into a switched outlet on the back of my old surround sound receiver, an optical cable from the new TV, and RCA jack stereo wiring to the audio inputs on the receiver. I needed this because my receiver did not have HDMI or optical inputs -- too old for those -- only RCA jacks.  I was set up and had audio in 5 minutes. The MUCH harder part was programming my DirecTV remote to "punch thru" the volume control for the receiver so that it worked elegantly. Also programming the TV to turn off its' speakers and output PCM audio on the optical port.... I do think that the complexity level has reached a breaking point on home theater....

input:
rating 2.0. Great navigation, terrible mop, tiny dustbin Dustbin is tiny and requires daily attention, the mop function is a joke. Remove the mop, expand the dustbin, and this would be a really great robot vacuum...for hard floors.

Does about as well cleaning carpets as a child with a broom.

Its best feature is the lidar navigation which works superbly after some firmware updates. App is slick and intuitive and scheduled cleaning is easy to program, got it vacuuming my house daily. 1800sf in the mountains with two dogs is a lot of floor filth to maintain. Love having consistently clean floors for once in my life, though it looks like I still need to mop the old fashioned way.

rating 5.0. 2 year review and love it! I’ve now had the S5 max for 2 years now and it is running great. We absolutely love it. Because of how well it works we bought the S4 max for my parent which is similar without the mop feature. Roborock is one of the best if not number one brands.

rating 5.0. Great robot vacuum-mop combo!! The Roborock S5 max is a major upgrade over my Neato D5 connected that died last month! It has better suction while being quieter than my old botvac. More important are the features. The ability to create rooms AND customize vacuum and mop settings for each ( no mop zone for carpeted rooms ) is amazing. And the battery life is phenomenal! My whole apartment gets vacuumed and mopped on one charge ( 1.5 hours ). While the mopping function may not handle tough stains on one pass, it will definitely keep your floors looking great provided that you remove the heavy stains by hand prior to running the vacuum. If you are on the fence about getting a vacuum / mop combo bot, do not hesitate and purchase this guy!!

Update: 03.29.2020

Even more to love about the Roborock S5 Max

Both the dustbin filter ( have to let dry for 24 hours prior to next use ) and the mopping pad are washable! Yes, you save money with the S5 Max! My prior robot was a Neato D5 which the filters had to be replaced. Admittedly, I change filters out each month as I have 2 cats, so it got expensive. With the S5 Max, when the filter or mopping pad starts to look dingy, I just detach them and soak them in a bleach water solution for a while, scrub, and when dry, they’re like new again. Get this robot and improve your life!

rating 5.0. Well worth the money! [[VIDEOID:42072ab5511bbd7dfb885242e378b570]] We have dark wood floors all throughout our home, so let me tell you that when I use our upright vacuum, within 30 minutes we already see dust on the floor again (just part of the deal of dark floors). We don't have any kids or pets, so it's not like there's a good reason for it. After breaking my back vacuuming and mopping on a very, very regular basis - I told my wife we need some kind of robot vacuum.

I did a LOT of research on all different brands and models. Everyone knows Roomba...their branding has completely taken over the market. Anyone I know that has a robot vacuum, has a Roomba. It's like Kleenex.

Seeing how expensive Roombas are, I did my homework and discovered Roborock, among other brands. For the amount of features you get with the Roborock S5 Max, versus ANY of the Roombas, for a significantly lower price...well it's not even a comparison. Don't be fooled by a brand name...the S5 Max blows any competitor out of the water.

We've only had it for a few days, but I've run it a lot already to test it and honestly, play around as well. This is by far a lifesaver, and I can truthfully say that our floors have never been cleaner with such low maintenance.

*** Pros ***
1. You can run this thing whenever (even when you're not home), in any place, room, zone, etc. - you don't have to do an entire floor when you just want one room or spot.

2. My floors have never been cleaner. Simple as that - the suction on this thing is incredible. And it's not very loud at all, especially on "balanced" mode.

3. The Roborock app is extremely intuitive and feature-filled. Lots of different options and controls. Takes some getting used to at first, but its great.

4. It goes under my bed, nightstands, and dresser - which are places that are a nightmare to vacuum regularly. I didn't even think it would when I first bought it (kind of an afterthought), but I saw it do with my own eyes and that earned a star or two itself. AND it went under my dining tables, in between chairs, to get every spot. As long as the space is wide enough for the robot to get through, it'll clean it. Again - wasn't imaging that it would do that!

5. They update the firmware, so you know they are supporting and improving their products. Spending this much from a brand that isn't as well known is a risk, but that part at least made me more comfortable. Also, there is a subreddit for Roborock, where you can get some support and learn more.

6. I have a roughly 3/4-inch room transition between my mud room and kitchen...and I was worried the robot wouldn't climb it. Silly me, this thing goes right over it! Doesn't mean it will do it with any transition, maybe it's the shape of mine...no idea, but it does it with ease!

*** Cons *** (Mind you I still gave a 5-star rating, so these aren't deal breakers by any means, more of an FYI)
1. I can echo what some of the reviewers said about how this isn't truly "mopping". You cannot use any cleaner, polish, or disinfectant with it (good idea for the future for them though, along with an additional revenue stream) - it's just running a damp cloth over the floor. So you will still have to do old fashioned mopping, or Swiffering, or something once in a while to really clean the floors. But it's fine for maintenance. AND I knew that before I bought it - know one should buy this thinking that it's going to truly mop.

2. You'll see in the photo I uploaded, that when it mops, it doesn't get within 4-6 inches of the edge. Not a huge deal, but something to know prior to buying.

3. Although it did a great job mapping my different floors (yes, multi-floor mapping is available now on this model), when I tried to run it on a different floor a second time...it kept getting lost. It took a few tries to get it to clean that room. But maybe that will get better as it maps the floors more - time will tell.

4. You have to take the mop attachment off when you just want to vacuum, especially on carpet, unless the attachment is 100% dry.

5. The more often you run it, the sooner you'll have to change out parts like filters or the brushes. There is a "maintenance" section of the app that tells you the remaining life expectancy of different parts (which is a pro).

*** Final Thoughts ***
I would buy this again without hesitation, it's just that good. Nothing feels better than walking barefoot on always "just cleaned" floors. If you don't need the mopping function, definitely look at the Roborock S4. That was my runner up.

rating 1.0. Don't buy if you have a bright home/apartment If you have a bright home or floor to ceiling windows this unit is beyond useless. Again if you have windows or your home is bright this thing will not accurately navigate your home. not even close.

Without a firmware update the thing knocked over its own charging station...really?

Not near as loud as a regular vacuum. It is nice to be able to run daily with just a couple button pushes from my phone.

rating 1.0. after a couple of weeks the robot constantly gets lost cant find its way to the base and it keeps re-creating the map.  also the no-go zones disappear and i have to keep recreating them.  now i have to manually place it areas i want it to clean

rating 1.0. Don't buy Roborock products *update after 10 months of use. [[VIDEOID:7da277d4416863c007378d17e4d9daca]] ** 10 month update: I have uses this 2-5 days a week for the past 10 months. It has drastically lost suction, it will just stop and delete the maps, I have to re-map all the time and using this vacuum has becoming increasingly annoying.
I have had to replace the filters so often, it makes loud buzzing noises and my most recent big issue is the brush roll keep randomly popping off which opens the bottom and tracks dirt, sand, ect all over my house.

There is no reason for any of this to happen, I maintance this vacuum more often that it requires it in the app, always cleaning the brush roll, wiping sensors, ect..ect.. it is so draining to take care of it and still have it not work properly.
I have reached out to customer support MANY times and they refused to replace it, they told me I could have a refurbished one that has "60%-70% use" whatever that is supposed to mean, probably more junk with the same issues that other people have gotten sick of and returned.
I'd check out ecovacs or another reputable brand.

Alright, I am changing my review after just about a full month of using daily, I have spoken to Roborock customer support several times and I will include their "helpful" suggestions in this review.

The "pros" I thought this robot vacuum has have been disproved, I thought it was doing a great job cleaning, I thought it had great suction..

The dust bin is full before it can finish a room and the filter is absolutely useless. I have one shorter hair dog and the dust bin is full after one room and the filter needs to be cleaned DAILY. Seriously, daily, which renders it useless while it dries fully for 24 hours.
*Customer support told me to put it outside in the sun so it will fully dry in -only- 12 hours. I live in Minnesota, where we have 3 months of sunny days, this isn't great advice, plus a filter shouldn't need to be cleaned daily they didn't comment on that.
*Customer service then recommended I buy more filters. No thanks.

The mop is useless, it is very cheaply made, as in thin and will get soaked and dirty after a few minutes. It also doesn't have fit securely so it flops over, making it totally useless.
*Customer service recommended taping the mop pad on. Uh, no. This is totally ridiculous!

The mapping is a mess. It is hard to section off rooms and they seem to change slightly after use, the first time I attempted to use the mop I took.the time to set it up perfectly so it wouldn't mop the carpet.. then trying it a few times after the rooms had kind of merged together, but only by about 6 inches and it was trying to mop the carpet. I deleted the map and did the process over, the same thing happened. It was like the no mop zones wouldn't stay where I put them.

Suction is decent, when I say decent, I mean along the line of a lightweight vacuum you can pick up at Walmart for $50. Yeah, it kicks up dirt/allergens too, since I have been using it instead of my amazing upright Shark, my allergies have been out of control..eyes burning, itchy everywhere, asthma kicking my butt. Just like one of those crappy vacuums, but it moves by itself, which apparently warrants a hefty price tag. I also have a Bissel Spinwave vacmop and out of curiositylady night I ran it right after the Roborock and it was PACKED with dog hair and dust, what was the Roborock doing?

If you're looking for deep cleaning, don't bother. It may do okay on hard floors if you turn suction all the way up, but I have watched it glide right over a little hunk of lint and didn't pick it up on our tile floor, so not certain of that either.

A few nights ago I watched it wander around aimlessly and turn around in circles several times it wasn't establishing location either. It is like it just stopped working as well as when I first got it.

TLDR: Poor suction, mop 100% useless, mapping memory degrades after a few weeks. Dust bin is tiny, no hepa filter= Bad for pet owners and allergies.
Awful Customer support.

We are returning this tomorrow. It stinks!

rating 3.0. Mopping issue [[VIDEOID:6a8a5c15b44b55dcc4a95017b896b746]] So I love all of the features related to vacuuming, the app, scheduling, etc. My problem is the mopping.
