rating 2.0. Cheapo Base Model Shark Ion Beats This I bought from this brand because of Vacuum Wars, but either I received a defective vacuum or the YouTube Channel receives kickbacks from this brand. It has little to no suction power on the 3 carpeted bedrooms. In 2/3 rooms, we have a very low-pile carpet, to the point where my mini hardwood floor vacuum can suck up things on it. Even on max power, this thing consistently misses things that are sitting on TOP of the carpet- I’ve dropped a dried tiny piece of play dog in front of it and it mowed over it; it runs over the feathers from our duvet comforter without even blowing them away. Further, the app is riddled with bugs. The sequencing option is ignored by the vacuum (see photo where it went from section 1 to 4), and you can’t section off certain rooms, like my laundry room… for whatever reason, it refuses to allow me to create anything unless the doorway is super small. Well, any new-build with an open floor plan is going to have large openings. Another frustrating thing is that it is supposed to clean the perimeter of the room and then move in rows - it rarely does that. It won’t go close to the baseboards, and it takes weird pathways (no rows). I am not even confident that the laser system actually works on this thing because it has to run into the furniture to know something is there. I have a $199 Shark Ion vacuum that I ran after this one was done and it was FULL of dust. I gave it 2 stars because I like that it creates a map and does a decent job on hardwood floors. Unfortunately, I’m not in the business of spending money on something that doesn’t work half as much as it should.

rating 4.0. Good vacuum- New design disappointing I will start by saying this vacuum is probably the best things I’ve ever bought. rating 5.0. This thing does a great job! As a former owner of two 1st generation Roomba's I can only say that the technology has caught up to the mission. rating 5.0. Having two dogs, hair everywhere... This is great! So far so good, have had this running about a month now. We have a ~2000 sqfoot home, and our 2 full grown boxers leave hair everywhere. Sometimes I don't see it til it's on my socks or clothes.

We had to get a robotic vacuum out of desperation... The S5 Max had the most compelling reviews. So far, so great.

Tips: pick up every little thing in each room that could get caught in the brush roller. If it can get caught, it will get caught: loose wires (USB chargers), loose rugs, legos (we have a 6 year old), beads, leaves, and tons of dog hair. Beware tough objects--like USB charger wires--they will end up cutting the roller scrubbers on the main brush, if leave the S5 Max to clean on its own. It happens...
After cleaning and scanning the whole house, we had a whole house map on the S5 Max. I eventually divided up the map into rooms--I'm able to give deep cleanups to our most trafficked areas. Map editing is very cool.
We basically clean every other day, to minimize the amount of manual pickup. It's a great way to keep the clutter and mess in check.

rating 5.0. Awesome Vacuum This is a very awesome robot vacuum cleaner to have around the house! It’s very easy to set up if you read the instructions. I really like having it moving about around the house too. It has really good suction power. After it cleans my carpet, you can literally see how clean it is because of the carpet sticking up. 😁

rating 4.0. Great vacuum but... First, the good:

The S5-Max is an excellent vacuum cleaner. It is easy to set up and use. The ap is easy to use, and there are many YouTube videos showing how to use the more esoteric features. The vacuum is quiet and doesn't damage the furniture. Its mapping feature allows the user to place areas off-limits so that the vacuum doesn't get stuck under cabinets or furniture. All-in-all, this is an excellent VACUUM cleaner.

Now the so-so:

At this price, it could have come with two filters and two mopping pads. The mopping pads are washable, so that's not as big an issue as the filters. The filters are sturdy and reusable. They are also washable, but they take at least two days to dry -- during which time, the vacuum cleaner is unusable. The instructions tell you to wash the filter when you empty the bin. That is not necessary -- a bang or two on the inside of a trashcan gets it pretty clean. I wash mine once a week now. I also bought extra filters. They are very reasonably priced and available on Amazon.

The instructions about how to empty the bin and take off the filter are not very good. They do not tell you that there are release buttons to push to remove the bin, and it takes some time to figure out how to open the bin and to replace the filter. This is not a show stopper since you will be able to figure it out, but the instructions could have been more understandable.

Now the bad:

The S5-Max is billed as a vacuum/mopper. It is specially designed to enhance its mopping ability. I paid extra for this feature. Unfortunately, the S5-Max does not mop -- or at least it doesn't do anything I would call mopping. It drags a slightly damp (and I mean SLIGHTLY damp) cloth across the floor. It doesn't mop the floor in any meaningful sense. At best, it can be described as dusting the floor. It picks up what little dust the vacuum doesn't get. And since it cannot use any mopping solution, it is not even as good as a Swiffer. (Use of anything but water -- preferably distilled water will corrode the machine.)

If you are looking for a mopping robot that will clean dirty floors, you will have to look elsewhere. Other than that, it is a great vacuum robot, and I would recommend looking at other robots in the company's lineup. For instance, the S4 is supposed to have all the vacuuming features of this robot, but without the mopping or the high price.

rating 3.0. Impressive robot vacuum, with a few hiccups The short version;

This is a terrific vacuum. My floors have never been cleaner. I am very satisfied with my purchase and could confidently recommend it. I really feel bad for giving it only 3 stars, however, a few minor issues detract from making it the perfect product. At this price point, there should be no compromises. If these issues are corrected in future firmware and app updates, I absolutely will give it 5 stars.

If you are looking at the S6, save a few bucks and get this model. If you don't need the mopping feature, save a few more bucks and get the S4.

The longer version;

I have owned two random navigation robot vacuums in the past (Roomba and Ecovacs), but I really wanted one with smart navigation and single room cleaning. I spent a fair amount of time comparing different options by reading Amazon reviews, comparing models on manufactures websites and watching YouTube reviews. I had finally decided to get the Roborocks S6. Just before making my purchase, I noticed that Roborocks had a brand new S5 Max model. At the time there were no Amazon reviews or ratings, and no YouTube videos. The Roborocks product comparison showed that the S5 Max had all of the same features as the S6, at a lower price. Sure, the S5 Max is slightly louder and has a slightly smaller dust bin, but it adds being able to control the water level per room through the app. I took a gamble on the S5 Max and I am happy with my purchase.

The Good:

- The mapping feature is very good. I love that it maps in real-time, on the first run. It doesn't need to dock to generate the map like other robots. And it doesn't need to run several times to generate a usable map like other robots. It can work with no map, or a partial map. The map is also updated in real time on each cleaning, so if you move a piece of furniture, you don't need to create a new map. It is very addicting to watch the map updating in the app as the robot runs through the house.

- The smart navigation is very good. Although it seems to take a few odd patterns here and there, and it does go over a few of the same spots multiple times, it efficiently cleans the whole house, and makes it back to the dock. I only two small complaints which I will address below.

- Great battery life. I have a 2000 sq. ft., 2 bed, 2 bath home and it can clean the whole house in a single charge. The very first time I ran it, it did have to return to its dock to top up before continuing, however, now that it has generated a full map of the house it can do a full clean on a single charge. A typical cleaning for me is 75 minutes and it will finish with just under 50% battery remaining. At 20% battery remaining, it will automatically return to the dock and charge to 80% and continue cleaning where it left off. This allows it to do larger homes. However, it does charge slowly, so if recharging during cleaning is necessary, it's going to take a while.

- Dust bin is sufficient for me. The dustbin is smaller than most competing robots. With daily runs on weekdays, I only need to empty the bin once a week. My house is mostly hard wood and I have no pets, so that helps. People with larger homes, more carpet, or pets may need to empty it more often.

- Great suction and cleaning. Most competing robots have two side brushes, this only has one so I expected to have to manually sweep up corners. Amazingly, with only a single side brush, it is very effective at cleaning the whole floor, including corners.

- No-go / No-mop zones. This was an essential feature for me. Other robots require physical barriers, magnetic tape, or expensive virtual barriers. I don't want to have to constantly put up and remove barriers, place unsightly magnetic tape all over the house, or clutter the house with expensive laser towers. Also, since my house is mostly hard wood, I have rugs in all of the high traffic areas. With no-go zones set in the app, I don't have to worry about cables under my entertainment center being sucked up, and with no-mop zones I don't have to worry about soggy rugs. You are limited to 10 no-go zones and 10 no-mop zones in the app. You can add additional virtual walls if you hit the limit.

- Cleaning. The robot is remarkably easy to clean. The dustbin comes out easily and the area surrounding it is spotless. The filter is washable and easy to clean with the supplied brush. The main roller comes out easy and the ends of the roller come off for simple hair removal. Very little hair gets trapped in the main roller and the wheels are spotless. The silicon side brush stays clean and will last a long time. Cleaning the sensors just takes a quick wipe with a soft microfiber towel. The mopping pad is washable and reusable and you don't have to empty the mopping water tank when you are just vacuuming. With how simple cleaning and servicing this robot is, I hope it will last for years to come.

The Meh:

Mopping - It just drags a wet microfiber cloth across the floor. Although you can adjust the level per room (heavy on the bathroom tile, light on the hard wood everywhere else), it doesn't clean worth a damn. I wish you could add some dish soap, vinegar or Bona to the tank, but it runs the risk of clogging or corroding the vacuum. I still use it to mop weekly, but I have to get the spots it leaves behind. Had I known, I would have just got the S4 which has all the same vacuum features, but no mop.

No-go, No-mop zones - While I love the virtual zones, and it is easy to add a zone in the app, even at the highest zoom level it is difficult to place them accurately. When you finally get them to your liking and save the map, the zones will shift a few pixels, so you need to end up creating a larger zone than you intend.

Setup - The pairing started without a hitch, but because I was still connected to my VPN at the time of pairing, it failed to confirm the WiFi configuration. After disconnecting from my VPN it couldn't communicate with the vacuum at all because it already had a saved WiFi configuration. After several failed attempts, I reset the device and repairing worked flawlessly. This was mostly my mistake so I can't fault the vacuum, but the app could have been more clear about the issue. I had to spend time debugging to figure it out and how to reset it. One word of caution is that the vacuum requires 2.5ghz. This is good for longer range, but not if you don't have a dual band router. I use Google WiFi, so I have strong dual band signal all through the house. A lot of routers only work at a single frequency and newer top end robot vacuums do support 5ghz.

App - For the most part the Roborock app is good, but the app only supports portrait mode (only tested on Android). Sometimes the map disappears for a few seconds and sometimes the app stops tracking the vacuum. Exiting and re-entering the robot reloads the map and starts tracking again. They have 2 apps which both support different features, so to make full use of it, you need two apps and two accounts. I opted not to create an Xiaomi account and deleted the Mi Home app. As far as I know the only features I am missing are room naming and Alexa integration.

Size - It is small enough to get just about anywhere, but I wish it were just a little bit smaller and shorter. It is larger than my prior vacuums and cannot fit in places others could, like under one of my couches, between my office chairs or beside the toilets, etc. The mopping hardware and lidar both add to the size so it's excusable given the features.

The bad:

Navigation - While it generally does a good job navigating the house with a few questionable patterns, it is completely fooled by my dark bedroom rug pattern. I open the curtains in the morning and have scheduled my bedroom lights to come on full brightness at the same time the vacuum runs and it helps a bit, but it still runs around in circles and sometimes eventually gets lost or stuck under the king size bed. This isn't supposed to happen with Lidar. Setting no-go zones means there is a huge part of the bedroom it can't clean and with the furniture placement and vacuum size, it can't navigate around the foot of the bed to clean the other side of the hard wood. Setting a large no go zone also will make it get trapped sometimes. The only thing that seems to work even a little bit is using virtual walls to guide it in a straight line, but this is far from foolproof. Roborocks has suggested that I cover the cliff sensors, which is fine in my single story home, and I will update after I have tried it. However this solution won't work for everyone.

No room naming- I believe technically you can name a room if you use the Mi Home app, but the Mi Home app doesn't have all the features of the Roborock app and requires setting up a different account. This feature is promised later, but this is a new company I have no experience with so I have no idea how much faith to put in them to keep their promises.

No Google Home integration - For privacy and security, I do no have any smart home apps installed on my primary phone. I have to hunt down a secondary device, boot it up, open an app, and select a room to clean instead of just saying 'OK Google, vacuum the living room'. This is another promised feature, so we'll see if it ever arrives. At least in the meantime scheduled cleanings work for me.

Object detection - This robot wants to climb up into my shoe rack, scratching the paint in the process. Even though it knows it has mapped out a large piece of furniture. It also wants to climb my speaker stands. My wife likes to leave slippers laying around that it will suck up and drag all around the house. I have tried using magnetic tape on the shoe rack and speaker stands, but the sensor is under the vacuum, so it needs to climb the objects before it's detected. Again the only solution is no-go zones which are nearly impossible to set accurately around a small object like a speaker stand and there is a 10 no-go zone limit.

Conclusion:

A smart robot vacuum with a few annoyances. Despite only a 3-star rating, I could confidently recommend this product. When Xoami / Roborock figure out the app situation and finally release a single app with the promised features, it will get a star back. Even then though, it is a 4-star product because of it's issues with object avoidance and inability to clean in dark spaces. If a later firmware updated resolves these issues, I will gladly give it 5 stars.

Update 1:

I have uploaded a picture of the map of my bedroom showing the crazy pattern it takes through the room. I have a large no-go zone so that it can only take straight lines under the bed by the headboard and foot of the bed. There is also a picture of the rug which confuses it and a picture of it getting stuck under the bed even with the no-go zone and plenty of light and a strong wifi signal.

rating 5.0. works pretty well! To start off, I'd never purchased a robot vacuum before. I always thought they were gimmicky wastes of time (some probably still are). And while this to me doesn't replace a good vacuuming and mopping, it sure comes about 80% of the way there in my opinion.

Pros:
1) holds up well to daily cleanings and sometimes multiple times per day
2) gets a ton of (in our house) dog hair, dust, dirt and random specks of stuff
3) has a good battery life and can run for an hour + (I'm not sure where the manufacturer is getting 180 mins from... perhaps they are running it at the lowest suction power... I run mine on the turbo setting (max))
4) saves me from having to vacuum so often
- I honestly haven't vacuumed since getting this about a month or so ago
- I'm a little OCD, so if something cleans 80% of what a regular vacuum would... I like to go over it about once a week or so.
5) Well worth the $500+ price tag for peace of mind and vacuuming reduction
6) Floor mopping
- does a decent, but not great job of this. but of course you can't really put much other than water in it. Roborock's manual frowns upon any cleaning solutions in the water tank.
7) Much cheaper than Roomba's and to me it works just as well. Don't be turned off by the Chinese manufacturing, it's a good product.

Cons:
1) not to be run during movies or at bedtime
- little noisy, but not like an airplane taking off or anything
2) doesn't pick up everything
- again though, for what it does and for the price, this item is still a steal
3) Initial high price of $450-$550 (depending on if it's on sale)
- however, that con is quickly paid off in about a month worth of cleaning. you'll thank me later if this review swayed you to purchasing this for your home :)

4) sucks with cords and sometimes getting stuck on rugs
- it's hit or miss sometimes. do not send the robot into areas where you have cords, it will try to suck them up. if you do, try and lift the cords off the grounds or put something that the robot will bump into before it gets to the cords.

All in all, I would say perhaps 4.5 stars. Just half a star off for noise level at night and not vacuuming as well as a legit full-blown vacuum. That being said, 4.5 stars is not an option, so I'll give it 5.
- Travis

rating 2.0. Not worth it unless you just want a robot floor duster The vacuum worked well for the first couple weeks before becoming less efficient with each use. rating 4.0. Very nice upgrade over our last Roomba, Mopping feature not very useful [[VIDEOID:e260e1ed97810478db879a94e8afd1f4]] I have had an 800 series Roomba for the last 5 yrs and while I love it to keep our dog and cat hair fluff down, I really wanted an improvement. I also went in to this investment after doing A LOT of research, and I knew full well the mopping feature on the S5 Max was not much to write home about. If I could have gotten this model without mopping and a bigger bin I would have (I considered the S4 but needed a couple of the features on the S5 Max and no better bin).

Initial review after a week of testing in our home:

Battery Life: One problem with our older bot is that although it had great suction, it's battery life did not do well on the extra thick carpet in our bedrooms. The s5 max out of the box can do 95% of our 2100 sq feet ranch on one charge (half hardwood half thick carpet/rugs). I don't need it to do the entire house (closets included) every day, so it manages our daily needs on one charge.

Intelligence/Capabilities: I have been impressed at the s5 max's ability to work out problems, map and maneuver over a very thick rugs edge. It sometimes has to take a couple tries to get on our living room rug, but it figures it out. It also knows what is a rug and what is hard floor, and for thicker rugs, it will do the hard wood around it, THEN, get up on the rug and clean just the rug. This is definitely some advanced programming that I appreciate. It sometimes gets a little confused around some chair legs, but it figures it out and I have yet to have to get it unstuck from anything and it manages the stair edge no problem. One SMALL knock, it goes around the room initially on a cleaning making a quick map, so if you move a chair or dog bed mid cleaning, it doesn't adjust and clean the newly opened space later on. It does seem a LITTLE off on our map, like it tracks at a very slight angle to the walls, but the laundry room it starts from is at a 45 degree angle from the rest of the house, so I am probably going to redo the map based on a more square starting point (you can see its a bit askew in the map photo).

Dustbin: This would be one knock on the S5max over our old roomba, the size of the bin is about HALF that of the roomba. That said, it has two things going for it even with a small bin. It compacts the fluff better then the roomba did (even super full it hasn't asked me to empty it), second, because it works better then my roomba in mapping and battery life, I am able to clean the carpeted areas more often. SO, after it ran a couple full circuits of the house, now I don't have to empty the bin mid cycle anymore. We have a yellow lab, and the hair that comes off that dog is insane, but as long as I stay up on the cycles it doesn't overflow.

Cleaning Accuracy/suction: The mapping program and the cleaning ability is awesome. I am always impressed every time it runs at just how clean and thorough it was (although to be fair, my previous roomba did not have laser mapping and just bounced around the room for awhile hoping to cover most of it, so my expectations were low). The amount of dust and hair it gets out of our carpet is BETTER then my house cleaner's vacuum, I am definitely impressed. Just running this thing daily has made my house feel SO much cleaner with 4 animals about.

Mopping: I had pretty low expectations on this option going in to it, had hoped it wasn't warranted, but alas, its pretty pitiful. I could probably tie a wet rag to the back of my ankle while I walk around and clean about as good. The pad doesn't make sense, the soft part doesn't cover the full width of the bot, and its not very 'plush', so it doesn't absorb the water well and disperse it over the pad, so it just puts wet stripes on the floor rather then a true full width section of pad. I will keep testing it, but this might be something I don't use much (or for a very small area where I tell it to run 3-4 times over). Small Update: After washing the pad it seems to absorb a bit better and streak less, but still not super useful.

App: The app is fairly easy to use. It could use a few extra features, would love to be able to program say room cleaning in a particular order rather then just one at a time. But I do love the no go zones and such, less picking up for me to have to do. I am still learning some of the features, but overall its okay.

Noise: Noise wise, it might be a hair better then my roomba, but it is still a vacuum. I don't run it at night, but we have 4 animals and a kid, so I prefer to walk the house and pick up before hitting the go button.

Maintenance: It definitely tangles up WAY more of my long hair in the rollers then the roomba rubber ones, but the little cleaning tool built in is handy. So expect to have to clean that up quite often. I cannot speak to the filter endurance yet.

Overall, I am VERY happy. If the mopping feature had any benefit it would be 5 stars. I will update if anything changes!

(ps....my cat is very unhappy he cannot ride this one like the roomba. The center height sensor and lasers do NOT like him sitting on top for a ride so it usually tells me to come rescue it or just moves back and forth trying to figure out where to go)

rating 3.0. Love / Hate Relationship with this Vacuum So I've been wanting to have a Robot Vacuum for quite some time now. After doing a ton of research these vacuum's, I settled on the Roborock S5 Max. The biggest reason is that it's comparable to the top brands of vacuums and the reviews were really really good.

I've had the vacuum now for a month and here is my love and absolute frustration with the vacuum.

I'll start with the good:

The suction is really really impressive. I'm truly amazed how much this vacuum picks up, it's almost disgusting to see everything it does get. We do have 2 dogs, 1 sheds so much that I could make sweaters and Rugs out of the loss of hair. This Vacuum is simply amazing for dog hair. LOVE IT!

I also love the tracking and mapping ability of the device. It knows where it is and I love how uniform it is in it's vacuum ability. I love the app that allow you to track where it's been.

The mopping ability of the vacuum is kind of meh... yes it mops... it's.... ok-ish I guess. Honestly I'm not going to mop with it probably at all.

Here is my biggest and most frustrating issue with this vacuum... it's stupid. So I have a threshold that goes from hardwood to tile, it's rounded incline that does reach to 1 inch above the tile. Now before Roborock replies to this saying that their vacuum can only climb 2cm... it actually can handle my threshold. The first 2 weeks of owning this vacuum, it went over the threshold with zero issues at all, easy climb, went over it almost like it wasn't even there. Then after 2 weeks it seemed to start to struggle... eventually it would find it's way over. The last week and a half though... the vacuum has gone full on stupid. It basically seems to only be able to go over the threshold, mind you with an absolute breeze, when it's heading back to the dock.. but if it's vacuuming... the vacuum with start to climb the threshold, turn around and go back off of it. It will do this like 10 times and then alert that the vacuum is stuck or it will skip the room entirely. I've literally watched this stupid thing be able to climb the threshold... stop, turn around, go back off the threshold and then say it's stuck. Again, this issue just started a just over a week ago. I've cleaned the sensors, I do that weekly, I clean the filters, and the brush weekly. I clean the wheels... weekly.

So here is my issue... I think this is a great vacuum if you have absolutely zero thresholds... it will do great for you. However I think they have a motor issue that begins to slip on the drive transmission, and it's very weak. I also think it has a programming issue where the vacuum won't take the thresholds at and angle when vacuuming, when going back to the dock it takes it at an angle every single time and no issue. And as I'm typing this... the dang thing just notified me that it's stuck over a threshold it literally just climbed 2 minutes ago.

Unfortunately I can see myself selling this thing here soon.

rating 3.0. Works great while it last but the company is a joke Update: support actually offered free return shipping even before this review but I missed it. Turns on within 6 months, they provide free return shipping and their system cannot open a repair ticket without tracking number (lame). Original:
Used it for less the 6 months and only 2 - 3 times a month (less than 1000 sq ft). The wheel starts acting up. One of the wheel will randomly stops turning. While it does fix it, it comes back randomly again and I have to do this 5 - 6 times to finish one cleaning. Contact support again and they ask me to reset the vacuum! This is obviously a hardware problem, how does resetting the software fix a hardware problem I ask but they don't give me any reason, so I asked to get it repair under warranty. rating 4.0. Just get one, and this is the best! Two months in,
Gets floors 90-95% clean. Cleans places you don’t (under furniture) doesn’t clean places you do (moving things out of the way), and hits everything else perfectly.
The way it cleans is gratifying with straight vacuum / mop lines. If you know, you know.
Does not reliably get fuzz off of carpet very well. Picks up some, but my Dyson struggled with this too so I wouldn’t be too critical in this regard.
Maintenance is a new thing for me. It cleans 500-600 sq ft before needing to be emptied and have the roller cleared of hair. I have a wife, and a three year old with long hair, plus for some reasons the old builder grade carpet in my house extrudes fuzz like you wouldn’t believe. Pretty much like to check/ clean this stuff after every use now. That said, it’s very quick and simple, I can do it in 2 min now.
The way it maps and moves is very satisfying to watch, and I found the app to be great and intuitive. Top notch for all of the software and hardware that makes this thing work.

Overall, beats the heck out of pushing a vacuum, and I love it, but I won’t be giving away my Dyson anytime soon.

rating 5.0. Excellent Vacuum, Solid Mop The Roborock S5 Max has been an excellent addition to our cleaning process at home. We have a dog that sheds constantly and drags mud in from the yard, and being able to automatically schedule cleanups has made our house much cleaner. The vacuum has no issues going from the hardwood floor to rugs or carpet, and the LIDAR sensor prevents it from knocking into furniture to find barriers. The no-mop zones are a huge plus so we can mark exactly where our wood floors end and the carpets begin to avoid getting water where it doesn't belong.

The e-tank mopping features is a nice addition. However, our dog is fairly heavy and the mud he tracks in gets surprisingly stuck. While the S5 Max can handle most of the lighter pieces of mud, it doesn't quite scrub hard enough to get all of it every time. It's still a marked improvement over where it started, and generally leaves our floors looking excellent.

rating 4.0. Really good compact shop vac I've used this vac for a few different reasons, and gotten different experiences.
1. at home, we were putting in new floors and had to vacuums the concrete base several times before laying the new floor down, and had to vacuum as we layed, to make sure there were no bumps caused by little particles left behind. This little guy did really well, getting every bit up, and even going under the moulding in some places to get small chunks of drywall the builders stuffed in the wall when they laid the carpet.
2. on the couch to pick up dog hair. This went OK, but not great. It took longer than I expected, I used the attachment with the brush. I found a pet hair remover tool from petsmart that we use now instead, to save time and hassle of taking this vac out of the closet and all that.
3. at my store. We take our pets to work with us, which is nice until all the hair and dust accumulates under racks and in corners and places that the broom doesn't want to reach. We sweep every day but we notice that after sweeping we're still finding dust bunnies that show up after we moved a fixture back into place. I took this little shopvac with me to the store and it did amazing. We have concrete floors so it had no problem rolling around, and the long cord was nice to have too.

Overall, the product is great for the price. I just wish that storing it would be easier, as it doesn't have a place for the cord or hose to roll up and secure, or for all the attachments, which are just in a plastic bag hanging in the closet I keep the vac. It's nice because it's small, so it will fit in the hall closet, but it's kind of shoved in there because the hose and cord just have to fall around it.

rating 5.0. Holy Pink Vacuum Batman. I did a lot of research before choosing this vacuum, despite its "pinkness". I'm happy to say the color is still my biggest gripe. The vacuum DOES come with 1 filter bag for dry use. After that you need to buy either bags or filters, and replacement filters come with retaining rings.

I chose this vac for all the extras it has compared to some others I looked at. A long cord, various attachments, 2 extension handles for high cobwebs and so you don't have to be bent over or on your knees all the time, plus a nice floor attachment which is perfect for my small wood floor. It's also lightweight, has great suction (more than my upright), a nice long hose, and will certainly replace my upright for many chores. Other little things include a wall mounting bracket, filter info displayed clearly on the vac itself, a clear rubber switch guard and easy bag replacement. OK, I'll stop now, but I am very glad I chose this one.

So far it's lived up to the shop-vac name and gets 5 stars easily, especially when you consider the exceptional value. If I have any problems soon I'll edit this review.

rating 5.0. Wonderful and Versatile This vacuum works great whether I am using around my basement workshop area to clean up sawdust, metal shavings, etc or upstairs in my living area. I find it particularly handy when using the cup brush to clean the occasional dust webs that accumlate around the ceiling and corners joints, it is small and light enough to hold while in use, and the wheels allow it to easily pull around.

I noticed a couple of negative comments here that I feel need addressing. One concerns the attachments, complaining they are cheap and brittle plastic. I cannot agree with this. The attachments I received are sturdy and durable, the material seems to be like a combination of plasic and rubber that holds up well, and believe me I cannot be and am not overly cautious when I am using them in the basement. They are
durable and do not show other than the normal wear one would expect.

Another comment regarded the recommended use of filter discs for picking up dry materials. This vacuum allows you two options, you can opt to use the filter discs - which are optional and not included - or use a normal type vacuum bag - one of which is included. Either option will work equally as well but you need to use only one or the other. I have used Shop Vacs for many years and personally I prefer the filter discs. The filter bags are designed for use in dry material pick-up and the only issue I have with these it that they seem to reduce the suction capabilities a bit and don't allow for the volume of pick-up the filter discs do. When using the bag the volume of material picked-up is limited by the size of the bag, but the volume of the filter discs is restricted only by the volume of the entire container. The filter discs need to be kept clean, especially if you are cleaning up fine dust like from drywall sanding, but they are easy enough to remove and dust off. I find the discs available in many places at around $5 for a pack of 3 that includes the mounting ring and depending on how frequently or under what conditions I am using this vac each filter will last a good 2-3 months (sometimes even longer)- just be sure to keep it clean.

Fortunately, I have not had any floods, leaks, overflowing toilets etc to have to use the wet pick-up function so I cannot comment of how it will work for wet-use. Yet I am sure if this does occur I will not be disappointed.

This little vacuum is a quality Shop Vac product that I anticipate will give me many years of dependable service.

*** Update *** Well, I got to chance to find out how well the wet pick-up function works when the pressure release valve on my hot water heater let loose. I used the smaller nozzle, 6" wide, and kept the rear of the nozzle only slightly elevated to drag it over the standing water; the suction was strong enough to leave an almost dry area on the concrete where I dragged the nozzle.

A couple of tips for wet usage - When inserting the hose into the body of the vacuum be sure the open part of the plastic fitting on the end of the hose is pointing downward, so that when the water is sucked in thru the hose it is directed to the bottom of the container. Also, remove any bags or paper discs/sleeves and use only the foam sleeve. When done I would suggest removing the foam sleeve to be fully rinsed out under water and letting it dry before putting it back on. I also rinsed out the canister, hose, wands and nozzle when I was done - though that is just the way I do things and may not have been absolutely necessary.

It worked very well and I have no reason to find any fault with the wet pick-up function If there was any at all it would be the necessity to repeatedly stop and empty the canister when it became full. But considering this unit is only a 2.5 gallon capacity that is to be expected and not a slight against the overall performance of this Shop Vac wet/dry vacuum.

I've been forcing myself to use it for the two years I've had it--didn't realize how much I disliked it til I used one where I w I bought this because my old shop vac finally needed replaced. I've been forcing myself to use it for the two years I've had it--didn't realize how much I disliked it til I used one where I work recently. Bissell Zing. I immediately ordered the Bissell ($50) and am moving this shop vac out to the garage today. The hose is hard plastic--tore at the base and difficult to maneuver. Also, they added a *blow* option, but when you've got the hose attached in the vacuum hole, it is BLOWING dust and dog hair all over the place (I have all wood floors). The Bissell is easier to pick up for stairs, etc, has a much higher quality hose, doesn't tip over so easily, and doesn't blow like a tornado out the back end. Happy now!

rating 5.0. Strong Little Vac!! I was in the market for a new vacuum and this time around, decided on a Shop-Vac if only because I'm soooo tired of having to untangle the beater brush on my upright (my fellow ladies with long hair understand)

I liked this model the most since it came with all the accessories I was looking for, had wheels to follow me around and because it's pink :). What I didn't expect was that it would also be very light weight! I love that I can easily pick it up while cleaning the stairs; something I could never do very well with my upright.

The suction on this vac is strong and I feel as if my house has never been cleaner! Of course, this may be difficult to use if your entire house is carpeted... Most of my floors are bare and I have a few area rugs that slow me down a bit but not so much for me to ever consider going back to a traditional upright. The floor brush works fine on rugs with the bristles out; but the bristles will occassionaly flip down on its own and the brush will then 'stick' to the floor.

I love that I can easily clean under difficult to reach areas with the crevice tool and it really does pick up everything. I've used this to clean out my floor air vent in the foyer with ease (which was buried in a heavy thick dust that my last vacuum hose could hardly put a dent in); and it also cleans out all the dust bunnies from the upstairs bathroom ceiling fan, which previously had to be cleaned ala q-tip since my last vacuum wasn't strong enough to pull out the dust and dirt.

There is a limit however in what it can actually take in size wise. I noted that my unit was getting very hot and suddenly wasn't picking up debris as well; the filter was still clean (that's all the inclosed manual had to say about suction power) and the disposable bag was hardly full. Basically, it just turned out to be a clog in the hose. Things like a long tootsie roll will easily be sucked up into the hose, but will jam at the end of the hose which has a small L shaped opening. Once you clear the opening; reattach, turn it back on and if the suction hasn't returned in full force, repeat the process. I had to do this two or three times in order to completely clear out several clogs that had developed before I was back in business. Not a real hassle and now I'm just more mindful about what goes into the vac (no more tootsie rolls) :).

It is light, small, maneuverable, and the attachments are great !  I have dust on the floor molding in my house that my expensive Dyson COULD handle if it didn't come with a shockingly poor tool.  Dyson Frankenstein-merged a cup brush attachment and an angled nozzle tool so the brush is unusable.  So much for Dyson design.  : /  This Shop vac has a great cup brush attachment and all of the suction power and portability for this job.  I rated this five stars because 6 stars is not an option.

rating 4.0. Good replacement to use on my central AC units... These seem to be high quality caps. We have two AC units and I replaced the cap in the first one two years ago and the other this year. I keep an extra one on hand now just in case. The tell tale signs of a bad start/run capcitor is that the fan and compressor try to start and immediately stop. If this is the case, take a look at the old capacitor for bulges in the top or oil leaking out. If you see anything like this you probably have a bad capacitor. These are so easy to replace, anyone with any DIY skills can do it, just make sure to pull the electrical disconnect before you remove the control panel from your AC unit. Also, the chances are this new cap will not fit in the bracket that holds your current start/run capacitor. No problem, just get some metal duct strapping from your local home improvement center and place a piece around the cap and use a sheet metal screw to attach it inside the unit where the old bracket was. Replace the wires from the old to the new, the terminals are labeled, and you should be back in business. If you are still worried it may be too much just search you tube, there are a bunch of good video that show you how. You can save yourself a ton of cash changing these out yourself...If you have an HVAC company come and do this it will probably run you anywhere from $150 and up.

rating 5.0. Good enough for me I have not used it yet, but today I measured it at 44.9uF & 4.9uF. Good enough for me!

Update:
First off I bought a Smart-C multimeter from Amazon so that I could test capacitance on these HVAC capacitors. It is a good investment in my opinion as it was only about $13 and can let you know if your capacitor is within tolerance spec. My 10 year old GE 97F9851 was still in spec, but just barely. It is a 45+5 but it was actually reading 43+4.9. I tested this new capacitor that I purchased and as you can see by the pictures it is reading 44.9+5...that is good enough for me. The main reason I purchased this was to have a spare. The reason I specifically picked this one is that it matched the circumference of the current GE 97F9851 capacitor that I have. It is a little bit shorter, but that did not affect my installation as the only thing that mattered for installation into my uint is the circumference. You can see by the pictures that it matches perfectly.

rating 5.0. Although supposedly slightly larger than the oem capacitor it fit into oem strap/holder better than the too skinny one that was Fast and fit! This dual 45 and 5 microfarad 370v 440v volt condensor run capacitor fit my 2008 Goodman condesnor well with high end of 440V rating fits lower voltages as well. especially as motor gets older and draws more. Installed on Goodman split system Heat Pump Condenser GSZ130361AB  Air handler ARUF363616BA. Wire ties can make almost any size capacitor work in most situations regardless. Although supposedly slightly larger than the oem capacitor it fit into oem strap/holder better than the too skinny one that was inside my unit dangling loose just above the contactor! Wire ties can make almost any size capacitor work in most situations. Works on many other manufacturers systems and applications. To check to see if a capacitor is bad, use a capacitance tester to measure the microfarad rating on the capacitor. Compare it with the manufacturers rating. If it is more than ten percent off the microfarad rating on the capacitor replace it. With the Microfarad ratings, it is important to follow the 10 percent rule: that is stay within plus or minus 10 percent of the microfarad rating. With the voltage rating, it is okay to go up on the voltage with the replacement but never down. In other words of you have a capacitor rated for 370 volts that is 25 microfarads if you do not have an exact replacement but one that is 25 microfarads and 440 volts that is an acceptable replacement.

rating 5.0. This $8 part saved me a $350 service call. I have a very large home (7300sf) with dual phase Carrier AC system for the first and lower level, and a second system (heat pump) for the 2nd floor and loft level. Last summer the upper level unit stopped working. Called an AC tech who took roughly two hours to figure out what was wrong. The outside unit stopped cutting on. Finally he replaced the capacitor and $350 later the AC was working again. Fast forward 8 months later. The same thing began to happen with the unit for the first and lower level. I was standing out front and could hear a whining sound coming from the side of the house. I went to investigate. The fan on the compressor appeared as if it was stuck. I pulled the breaker and stuck a rod through the top grill to push the blade to see if the fan motor had seized up. I was able to move it and pushed it until it made a single revolution. I put the breaker back in and the unit kicked on with the fan running. 2 months later I noticed that the first floor of house was warm although the AC was running. I checked the temp of the air coming out of the register and found it to be warm. I checked the unit outside and sure enough not only was the fan not spinning, the was not running at all. Immediately I'm thinking the entire compressor is gone, which I had already thought would eventually happen but simply monitored it listening for the whining and humming sound I heard before.

I contacted an AC tech that I used in the past since I knew he'd give me a far price, but for some reason the guy couldn't make it that day. But then he never came when he said he would nor did he return any of our calls. We had a friend reach out to someone who knew a guy, who knew a guy yada, yada... At this point the outside temperature was getting milder due to the amount and frequency of rain here on the east coast so there was no rush to spend upwards of $3000 to have the unit replaced. I just so happened to google the symptoms trying to determine if the entire unit needed to be replaced or if there was a way to replace the fan/motor assembly for less than the cost of a replacement compressor. Low and behold the very first YouTube video I came across, there's a guys describing the exact same set of symptoms, first the fan didn't turn on, he pushed it like I did and it ran for about another month, then the entire unit stopped working. He replaced the capacitor while videoing the repair and within ten minutes he had the unit running. Needless to say, I did the exact same thing and had my system running in 5. The biggest issue was finding the right one and then deciding which brand to trust. I called Carrier to try to find the OEM part number without having to open the unit up until I was ready to fix it. They refused to give it to me due to liability (such BS, my warranty is over), they obviously did not want to take business away from their dealers. I was able to get the part number from a distributor and also found out that Carrier doesn't make their own parts anyway and typically uses multiple Chinese made brands. I chose this one due to it's popularity among HVAC companies. Works like a charm.. I bought two so I'd have a spare at the ready in the event the same thing happens again.. 4 screws and less than 10 minutes of work and I saved myself $300+ on this repair. I won't know the reliability of the part until the unit is running until the temperature changes. but for now, the problem has been resolved with a $6 part ($8 with Prime Delivery).

As for the guy who came to fix it the last time this happened, he obviously did not know what he was doing. Screwed up my Smart Thermostat and ended installing the old Honeywell unit thinking it was the stat that was causing the unit not to kick on. I guy I will be doing my own HVAC repairs from now on unless the unit need to be replaced or it's too hot to go into the attack (but then I'd just at night when it's cooler).

rating 5.0. cheap fix for common AC issue Living in South Florida means you have to run your AC on and off almost year-round. This, of course, translates in to regular upkeep such as switching out your air filter monthly as well as other common problems you may face throughout the year. Issue:  the fan blows out warm air but the fan is running fine. Firstly, shut the AC off and look inside the unit to see that all the ice has melted before proceeding. The issue is a common one: the capacitor. We had a "friend" who worked a side business as a licensed AC repairman who works for  large company. If we called him on an emergency, he would stop by after his shift and change the part out for 100 bucks a pop. We were happy to pay it as we were more worried about getting ripped off by another AC guy that would tell us we needed a new condenser or worse, a whole new unit, so to us we were happy to pay the 100 bucks. After watching him do the capacitor change several times as this had been the repeated issue, I decided to see if Amazon had the part. Sure enough they did and it was delivered to our door within two days for under 10 bucks! The issue happened again only two weeks back and we had the part on standby. We were able to quickly change it out and just like that the issue was resolved! There are plenty of YouTube videos that show you how easy it is to change this part so I suggest you trying to see if that is in fact the issue befor calling an AC repair man for an over-inflated price to resolve an an otherwise easy repair.Buy 2 or more at a time to have in an emergency.

rating 5.0. Worked perfectly in a pinch I used this to replace a 45uF, 440VAC capacitor in my HVAC that had failed after 10 years.
I couldn't find a single-run capacitor with the right specs to replace the broken one, so I bought this capacitor.

Amazon shipped it within 24 hours, so I was able to get my AC back up and running again before the 90-degrees heat hit this week. I'm going to continue looking for a single-run capacitor with the right specs, but for now, with the expected heat wave coming our way, I'm glad I was able to order this with next-day shipping from Amazon.

rating 3.0. Easy and Inexpensive Fix, but capacitor is short lived UPDATE: The Titan brand capacitor I installed in May 2020 failed after only 1 year. I live in New England, so the capacitor is only used during the summer to start the outdoor unit, so technically I didn't even receive a year of service from the Titan brand capacitor. That capacitor replaced a Titan capacitor that failed after 5 years of service in my American Standard Allegiance 12 Air Conditioner.

The fan on the outdoor unit stopped spinning, and when it should have turned on, it didn't, although the contactor was pulled in (a sign that it is receiving power and a signal from the thermostat to cool), and the fan for the indoor unit was blowing. I was able to get the fan to spin by using a stick, but it was running slow and eventually stopped. After narrowing down the potential problems, and determining the culprit was likely the capacitor, I replaced it and the problem was solved. In my case, the capacitors failed, but were not corroded, bulging or leaking. The capacitors install very easily, and in only a few minutes time, my Air Conditioner was operating properly. Making my own repair saved me money but because I didn't have a spare capacitor on hand, my family and I suffered several days without air conditioning. These Titan capacitors do not seem to last long, but they are inexpensive, so its a good idea to have an extra one on-hand, just in case.

That’s the main point and definitely use this with a mask for double protection. It looks so clean and is barely even there. Careful in holding to avoid denting the cover. Good material used and will definitely last plenty of uses if handled properly. Clean and disinfect for next use. If you dislike the foam kind, which is good for kids to avoid poking their eyes, this eye gkass type is a good option. It slips in easily even if you wear eye glasses.

input: rating 5.0. Excellent PETG filament. This PETG is strong, layers bond great and is solid black. Printed great on my ROBO 3D printer. Size is consistent and as usual the ESUN spool is always a nice touch. I'll be switching out from PLA to PETG from now on.  I have attached a close up photo to show the detail and layer bonding.

rating 5.0. Great Stuff Another great PET product from eSUN. I previously ordered the blue, which was a great first time experience. This solid black PETG looks amazing, looks like PLA (not see through at all,) prints in a very nice glossy jet black finish, and is very good quality filament. The spool I had was all within +/- 5mm and had an average diameter of 1.70.

rating 3.0. Too black I love the natural, yellow is terribly ugly and black is too black and too shiny. The attached picture is of a PETG(left) part and ABS(right) part - both the same model. Its so dark and so shiny its hard to see detail - pictures don't do justice.

rating 5.0. This PETG filament just rocks! I'm really start of thinking change pla to PETG for some special and more oriented prints, this kind of material should be a must have in your makerspace, I highly recommended it. Looking forward to try some new and exciting filaments! ;)

rating 1.0. it's stringy and does not handle fine details well it warps alot. it's stringy and does not handle fine details well. it smells a little.

rating 5.0. PETG is a great material! PETG is a great material!

It took me a while to get things really looking good with it but once I figure out a few things.. everything else fell into place.
I'm using a Robo3D R1+ with a E3Dv6 hotend.

* Yes it DOES print better with a heated bed and it IS SO much better than ABS in my opinion. I like 70c for bed
* YES the warping is MUCH lower than ABS and even PLA (in my view)
* Yes it does string a bit more until you get the right temperature.
* Yes it IS stronger than PLA.

What I learned...
It seems to print better (for me) without the fan.
Start at the recommended high and keep lowering until you get good results.
PETG seems to have a "dull" color when you below the optimal temperature.
as soon as I reach the right temp (for me) it starts to take a bit of a glossy shine.
the difference between "dull" and "shine" seems to be about 2c I found my eSUN Black PETG perfect temp to be at 249c.
I was able to actually change the look of a printed item to get a "dull" look or a shine by the temp of the entire print job.
For me this actually makes it preform as two different materials making it more versatile. The dull looking object was also just as strong as the glossy item.
Black was the easiest to find the right temp because my temp trick works the best with that color.
The green does the same but harder to see with the flashlight due to the color.

rating 4.0. Love PETG I use an E3D V6 hotend and no heated bed. I have Buildtak on the glass, with a 50-50 mix of white glue and water. The latter actually is more for a release agent. There is a two layer raft, the first layer goes down at 245 C and all layers after that 240.

Depending on what is being printed (smaller stuff need cooling larger do not) sometimes I have a fan that creates a breeze across the bed. Layer adhesion is great, but you will need to tweek the retract and feed to cut down on stringing. The surface is nice and bright. At 245 the nozzle dribbles, so wipe it between prints, and lay down a couple of perimeters to wipe the nozzle before it starts printing.

I am only printing PETG on my Kossel and this brand of PETG is about the same as the three other brands I have used. I build radio controlled model airplanes and print ribs for the wings and formers for the fuselage and it gets too hot in the sun and car for PLA and I won't use ABS. I have no problem with water uptake like PLA.

Edit: I am still printing with PETG on my Kossel, still love it, but have been printing all layers at 247 for awhile now. I put a new boot on the hotend and have eliminated the buildup problem I use to have.

Edit: The boot would never quit stinking so I got rid of it.

rating 5.0. Another winner from eSun, highly recommended Another winner from eSun.  I really liked the eSun PLA Pro and got great prints from it on my Wanhao i3.  I tried Taulman TechG and it was OK but I couldnt get it dialed in.  So, I tried the eSun PETG and it is fantastic, just like its PLA+ brother.  WOW, very nice glossy black prints and very strong.  I print the PLA+ at 215/60 45mm/sec and I print this PETG at 250C/70 45mm/sec.  I'm not seeing a stringing problem or anything unusual (except a little crackling occasionally from the moisture the PETG must be picking up??)  I had to print it a little hotter at 250C without fan to ensure layer bonding.  Other than that, I really see no need to experiment with any other filaments.  Between the eSun PLA+ and the PETG I am extremely pleased.

rating 5.0. Favorite filament to date! This stuff has really blown me away. I've been printing more and more parts for my quadcopters and have been struggling with pieces shattering on hard impacts, but apparently those days are gone!

I print with a 240c hot end and 80c glass bed and this stuff prints just as easy as PLA, has a great look to it, and is incredibly strong! I honestly don't see myself using anything else for the majority of my prints.

If you've been printing PLA/ABS and are feeling the need for more strength this should definitely be your next stop. And it's hydrophobic so you don't have to worry about keeping it away from moisture and then baking it when you forget! Does it get any better?
rating 1.0. One Star Started bubbling and popping out of the box. not recommended

rating 3.0. and great prints. Because eSun has different colors, I wanted to try them out. Unfortunately, but I was getting frequent jams in my E3D V6 extruder. The thickness variability is all over the map and was having difficulty in dialing in the settings as well as the preload on the feed on my Printrbot Simple Metal. I purchased clear PETG from another supplier on Amazon and low and behold, the filament thickness was fairly consistent, no jams, and great prints.

rating 5.0. This opaque black PETG seems to print better than the semi-transparent colors also offered by eSUN This opaque black PETG seems to print better than the semi-transparent colors also offered by eSUN.  Once you get your settings dialed, it prints as good as PLA, with much more strength and flexibility.  No smell whatsoever when printing and very little warping except on very large flat pieces.  About the only con is that support material is sometimes difficult to remove. This is definitely my go-to filament for parts that need strength.  There's a great write-up in the Simplify 3D forums that will help you get your slicer settings dialed in.

rating 5.0. I really like this filament I really like this filament, it's never given me any real issues. It arrived quickly in a vacuum sealed bag with a desiccant. I printed this filament on a prusa i3 WITHOUT a full metal extruder from 255-257c with few issues. This is a stringy filament but the strings can be removed with a few passes over the flame of a lighter so I preferred using that method instead of trying to dial in the retraction properly. Please be aware if you run this filament on an extruder with a Teflon tube liner eventually you will need to replace the tube. At around 260 the Teflon liners begin to fail rapidly which will mainly cause filament jamming. I think I printed for around 40 hours before I needed to replace my teflon extruder tube so just be aware and order some extra extruder tubes if you intend on using this with a conventional hot end.

rating 1.0. PLEASE DON'T BUY!!!!!!!!!!!! I tried everything that is listed from esun on the FAQ page.  I have tried 4 to 5 rolls of this stuff and it is the worst thing I have ever bought in my life. I wanted it to work so bad hence the 5 rolls.  I have been trying for 8 months to get this to work. YES I have tried everything not only from esun but from all ends of the internet to get a successful print with nothing that worked. I know how to print and have been printing for a couple of years now. I don't usually write bad reviews but I cannot stress the how much I despise this filament.

rating 5.0. My favorite filament This has become my favorite filament for printing. I have a Flashforge Creator Pro which I use daily to print various parts for photography and engineering projects. I used ABS filaments mostly before finding this eSUN PETG.

The problem I found with ABS is that it tends to be somewhat brittle and I had a few parts that had problems with the layers separating under stress. There is also a problem with warpage on larger parts.

When I changed to this filament, I found it to be much stronger and a bit more flexible. This helped a lot when assembling parts.The layer adhesion is amazing. I have yet to make a print that failed to stay together. I have printed parts that are about as large as my printer can handle (about 5-3/4 x 9 inches) and had no warping at all. This would have been very difficult when using ABS.

My general print settings are: Extruder Temp = 230C; Bed Temp = 75C; Travel Speed = 150mm/s and the bed is covered with Kapton tape.
I have replaced the stock extruder with a conversion to use a metal tube instead of the Teflon tube and it seems to be much better in general and it allows higher extruder temperatures.

The bed adhesion to the Kapton tape is very good. It was difficult to remove the printed parts at first (using higher bed temperatures) but I discovered that it much easier when allowing the bed to cool more before attempting it. I also removed the top cover of the printer (as you would to print with PLA) to allow the bed to cool more as it prints with much better results.I found that using this removal tool was perfect.(www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00VB1U886/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o09_s02?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
After allowing the print to cool for just 5 minutes and using this tool allows even the biggest parts to pop off the bed with no damage to the Kapton tape at all.

The slight flex that this filament gives to the finished parts is my reason for using this as much as I do. I tend to build more complex parts in sections and then screw them together using threaded inserts or tap bearings into recesses printed into the parts. When using ABS I found that when fitting the inserts into the part occasionally split the part. Since using the eSUN PETG, I have yet to encounter that problem and I get very tight fitting inserts.
I have included some photos of a Focus Rail Slider that I designed and printed using this filament. The prints are .30mm layer thickness from a low poly count model used as a prototype so the surfaces are rougher than is possible with this filament. Also included is a photo of small locking clips printed at .20mm layer thickness to show the resolution of small parts printed using this PETG.

I highly recommend this filament for printing mechanical parts. I have used probably 20 to 25 different filaments over the last 3 years and this is by far my favorite for quality, cost and ease of use.
rating 5.0. Great filament! As strong as ABS with much less warping and much better layer adhesion.

rating 5.0. Simple Printing of High Strength Parts This stuff is great. I printed a few PLA prints and the stuff was a pain to clean off. PLA was very sticky and once it got into my hot end, was impossible to clear. This stuff prints pretty easily and has great layer adhesion. The parts feel extremely strong and I am able to get pretty fine details. It also is able to bridge with the fan turned up during bridging.

I will say that the temperature range I have found is pretty picky. If I go down to 225C, I will get some clicking (skipping) in the extruder gear. If I go above 233C, it starts to ooze a bit when not printing. I am running this at 230C and 0.2mm layer height with 90C bed temperature. I have also dialed back the retraction to prevent the oozing. You will need to tune your ideal temperatures to match your printer setup, but once you have it dialed in, the prints are great.

rating 2.0. eSUN PETG filament is just like any other PETG eSUN PETG filament is just like any other PETG, once you configure your printer to handle the special needs, it works fine. All PETG are very viscous and requires more cleaning and more refined setting on infill and first layer. I print on glass, at 95ºc, Hotend first layer 255ºc and 236ºc for the others, this way I don't need any dressings or treatments on my glass. First layer width, 175% and hight 175%. Infill Extrusion width at 140%. My extruder is .40mm, Ext multi .99 and Width .48mm. This will get most of you started if you never worked with PETG before.
The eSUN PETG is as good as any other materials, the tolerance is not the best, but it has never jammed my extruder because of tolerances. What I do find extremely frustrating is that I have to babysit the spool all the time, for the way it is wound, jams on the spool. Un-jamming takes some skills as not to kink it enough to become an issue, jamming the hothead. If I was to purchase another spool, I would re-spool it to reduce jamming by entanglement. Making the price per spool less then desirable, do to the extra labor. I think I will stick with HatchBox PETG.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Very clean and consistent results, no issues with stringiness. Will definitely buy again.

rating 5.0. Solid performer, love the clear spools. I have had excellent results with this eSUN PETG filament on my Prusa i3 MK2. The strength of this filament Is excellent, tougher than ABS although not as heat resistant. This PETG filament is stringer than PLA or ABS but with the correct retraction settings strings were minimal and otherwise it printed as easily as PLA without the odor of ABS. I received this filament quickly and it was packaged very well, for these reasons I would highly recommend this filament.

rating 2.0. Just stick to PLA I have a lot of trouble setting the print setting to match this filament. It is NOT as easy to print as PLA like other says.

Furthermore, filament cooling fan would cool the filament too fast, leaving weak bond between layers. That weak bond makes the model weaker than PLA.

In conclusion, I do not recommend using this filament if you're not willing to experiment.

rating 1.0. or even PLA and get better results. I wanted to give eSun a try but compared to my other PETG rolls from Hatchbox and Maker Geek this stuff is stringy and surprisingly very brittle. However the resulting brittleness is just not acceptable for PETG which is usually more flexible and stronger than PLA.  This stuff is actually more brittle and weaker than any PLA I've ever used.  It snaps with relatively little pressure rather than bending as PETG should.  I will not be using the rest of this roll or purchase any more eSun rolls, as I'd rather use other PETG, or even PLA and get better results.

rating 5.0. Excellent PETG Filament!! I have had my printer for almost two months, and it was only last week i started trying new filaments. eSun's PETG black was my first filament other than PLA. rating 5.0. Good PETG print at higher temperature than Hatch Box PETG Good PETG print at higher temperature than Hatch Box PETG.
Very shinny surface and good layer bonding.
My setting on Prusa MK2 with PEI bed.
Slic3r
Extruder temperature: 240 c, part cooling fan off for the first layers then increase to 247 with 40- 70% fan speed.
Bed temperature : 65 c Easy parts remove.
75-80 c parts stick to bed very good. (difficult to remove).
Extrusion multiplier : 0.98

rating 4.0. Excellent filament for your money! This filament is great! Full disclosure, it's much trickier to dial in the settings than any PLA I've ever used. But once you get it dialed in the results are absolutely fantastic, even without a cooling fan. I personally use a 240 degree nozzle temp and a 60 degree bed temp, with a speed of 40mm/sec and a retraction of 4.5mm, which is higher than I can use for PLA with good results. The filament also needs a much higher nozzle height than PLA does, if the nozzle is too close to the bed you get lots of stringing. The only reason I give 4 stars instead of 5 is there is very little information about the proper settings for this filament, my first several prints were very frustrating, and the company puts out almost no information on proper print settings. But as I said once it's dialed in it works great, and does overhangs better than most materials I've used.

rating 5.0. PETG hasn't let me down yet! I have been very happy with eSUN PETG filament.  I purchased a second roll of the black filament to help my girlfriend with a kids craft history teaching event.  I printed nearly 75% of the spool in the first couple of weeks and had no issues with tangling or a single failed print.  The eSun reels feel much more durable than some of the other brands I have tried.  The material printed very well and was very durable.  PETG does tend to string more than ABS but the prints are usually easy to clean up.  The material adheres and releases well from the bed build tape.  If you have issues releasing the prints from the durable build tapes, heat the bed slightly and it should come off easier.  I have used this material successfully on a Flashforge Creator Pro and an Anet A8 printer.  I have had great results with both printers.  The canon(s) in the photos was printed in three parts and the treasure chest in two parts.
rating 2.0. Usually a great product. Not this one Full of moisture, not printable. Missed return windows by 1 day. Usually a great product. Not this one.

rating 2.0. Tangle in 3 different parts of the spool The filament itself is fine. But quality control over the spool is bad. This is the first spool I’ve purchased from eSun and my positive experience was short lived to find my CR10 control unit flipped over because a tangle in the spool. I cut off that portion and decided nothing is perfect and went on using the spool again. Now towards the end of a 3 day print I come home to find the same thing happen two more times, basically ruining my print. I had to edit the GCode to try and pick up where the print failed but it left an ugly seam which will have to be manually filled and sanded. Being that I don’t see a whole lot of reviews like mine I’m willing to think this is just a bad spool but I hope someone from eSun will contact me and replace it.

rating 5.0. Best 3D Printing Filament Favorite filament thus far! This PETG has been awesome and has printed very well. The prints are super strong, yet more flexible and forgiving than PLA. I already went through a roll of this and just ordered another. I get the best results in Cura at 230C and 70C bed with a pretty high retraction setting of 90mm/s and 4.5mm. Stringing is actually pretty good and nothing a flame or wire cutters cant solve. Warping is a non existent problem even with huge parts measuring 250mm+ and bed adhesion is almost to good.

rating 5.0. Works amazingly well if you follow the guidelines I've used about 1/4 of the spool of the black PETG, and found it to be consistent and reliable on my Prusa i3 MK2S. I'm sitting right at the edge of recommended print speed (60mm/s) and getting amazing results. The finished prints are extremely tough and durable. If you're used to PLA, you may be surprised by how shiny this material is.

I was glad to see that the package includes specific instructions from eSun about what to expect with this material and how to go about using it with your printer. My personal recommendation is to study those guidelines carefully before you even load the spool up on your printer.

For those that may have issues with this or are new to PETG and use Simplify3D, I use an initial retraction of 0.54mm before the print starts, then 0.8mm retraction thereafter. No cooling fan or keeping it at 50% speed. Printing at 240C for the hot end and 90C for the heated bed and not had a single problem with adhesion or bad layers.

Please note that this stuff can be really tough to get off the bed. Do not start a print with this and let it finish sometime overnight while you're sleeping! You'll have to tear it off the print bed with a prybar at that point.

rating 2.0. The only thing I like about this is the price The only thing I like about this is the price. But I guess that's why the filament is such low quality. It works decent, but compared to other brands, it's just not a quality product. Also, the spool unwinds and gets tangled easily. I'm waiting for Hatchbox to be prime eligible again so I don't ever have to use this stuff again.

rating 3.0. Filament diameter not consistent enough. In general, I've had pretty good luck with this product. rating 1.0. Horrible- diameter is very inconsistent. Filament prints well for ... Horrible- diameter is very inconsistent. rating 1.0. Won’t stick Can’t get a good print, won’t stick to the bed. I have been successful with other manufacturers PETG so it isn’t my printer.

rating 5.0. Good petg Good petg filament so far just starting on my first roll and it is printing great! I am starting to love petg it is way better than abs, and pla. The only thin* that sucks about pet g is bridging, and supports. Supports are a nightmare to get off and if you use fans to try and get futher bridging then layer adhesion falls off. However this is not the fault of the manufacturer it is the material itself. The strength and durability of petg is great! It will take some test cubes to dial in your settings if your new to petg but once you get them dialed in this is a great filament. ESun petg is almost as good as talumam Tglase, but at a quarter to half the cost. My first roll is dry, and that is the key to good petg just like other 3d hydroscopic filaments. Not nearly as bad as nylon but not quite as forgiving as pla.

rating 1.0. Milky water and black spots, and TERRIBLE customer service I bought this pan to use as a water pan for my dogs. After using it only for a few days, I noticed that the water would get "milky" after it sat for awhile in the pan. So, I washed it down again using nothing more than some mild dish soap and a sponge. Again, the water looked milky after sitting awhile. Then I started to notice black spots appearing on the finish. At that point, I didn't want to risk my dogs' health further so I stopped using the pan. I sent Behrens and email asking about the problems, but got no response. Then I tried calling (after having to search around Behrens' website for the number), and got a voice mail. No return call by someone from Behrens. Owning a $10 pan shouldn't be this much trouble, so it's going back to Amazon or it's going to get tossed into the garage and used there. My dogs will be getting stainless steel pans!

rating 5.0. Strong enough to resist shape deformation even with lots of oil After a plastic pan flexes on you and spills oil everywhere, you will wish you bought this pan. It is strong and will not deform when filled with fluid. Plus it will never get soft or degrade the way plastic ones do.

The only downside is that since it is metal, it does get hot and will transfer that heat to the hands. So either wait for it to cool down if you drain hot oil into it, or use gloves to pick it up.

rating 5.0. Solid utility pan for oil or use with a Weber kettle grill 18" in diameter, nicely made out of steel, fairly substantial. You need one of these for your basic Weber kettle grill to replace the useless metal tray that they ship it with, the thing that's supposed to hang on by three spring clips. Get this pan instead and discard the metal tray, put this on the lower support frame. It'll catch most of the ashes, which means you don't have to spend $150 to get a Weber kettle grill with an ash catcher.

rating 3.0. Oil pan bird drinker. I use these for bird drinkers.  I first etch the surface with vinegar, then spray with a zinc chromate primer.  Then I spray with black cooler sealant.  Next, I drill a one-half inch hole in the side and fix in a evaporative cooler float.  Hook all up to a water supply and you have your bird drinker…lasts only for about one season, however, since there will still be rust in spite of all the prep work.

rating 5.0. Large, sturdy, just as described. This pan is so sturdy and large.  I bought it to hold favors at a child's birthday party and of course it worked for that very well.  I am impressed with how sturdy it is and am sure it has many other uses.

rating 5.0. Perfect for oil changes or any type of auto fluid draining This is so much better to drain fluids than one of those small oil containers with the lid. Yesterday I had an oil change with an impossibly tight filter and I dripped oil everywhere getting it off - no problem, this pan caught every drop.

Afterward, I pour the used oil back into the 5 quart oil jug to recycle using a funnel. The pan wipes clean for storage for next time. Perfect system and way better than an all in one pan/container that ends up leaking everywhere.

rating 5.0. Great Toothpicks Loved these toothpicks.  But the price is getting out of control, I use to be in Dentistry for 33 years.  And after I retired I could still get 1000 for $25.00.  Now they want so much for them and you can't get them in the bag of 1000.  And when you use them once to twice a day and there is 2 of you, they go pretty fast. rating 4.0. Effective for Cleaning off Fingerprints and Coffee Stains! I've been trying different towels from this brand for several months now and I've loved everything I've found so far. These towels seem dry when you get them, but they are definitely filled with a cleaner which is apparent when you start to use them. I find it is best to clean the stainless steel, say on a coffee maker, and then wipe it down with a clean cloth to polish and shine. I could see these being used for refrigerators and other stainless steel items around your house. Just polish with a dry cloth afterwards and you won't have an issue with residues.

rating 5.0. Less Dust I very rarely buy vacuum bags at the store where I bought my vacuum. Why? Because it is just easier to order them online and I feel like I get more for my money.
