The bags are made so they prevent dust from leaving the bag. So less dust around the house to clean up too. I just find I love these better than any other bags I've tried in the past few years. I'm always happy when I put in a new bag and go about my housecleaning duties. I've used enough vacuums in my life to know Panasonic are the best and these bags are better than any I've tried.

~The Rebecca Review

rating 5.0. Microfiltration I will admit to enjoying cleaning my own house as it not only burns calories, it allows me to take a break from thinking intensely for hours at a time. Housecleaning can almost be meditative if you find the right cleaners. What is especially enjoyable about the Panasonic U6 bags is the microfiltration.

Over the years I've had bad experiences with vacuum bags that either broke, allowed the dust and dirt to return into the air or that simply could not contain more than ¼ of a bag before needing to be changed.

The quality of your vacuuming endeavors is most definitely determined by changing the bag often enough to not cause the motor strain, but you don't want to have to change the bag each week as this gets expensive.

After trying a variety of "alternative" selections for the U6 vacuum bag size, I've come back to the genuine article, which has yet to be duplicated. The Panasonic vacuums also last for years and are very durable and do a good job of picking up cat hair.

~The Rebecca Review

rating 5.0. Great product if used properly This has been the sixth type of slow cure resin I have purchased and it will now be my standard. I have used it for dice making, micarta, jewelry and as a seal coat for large pieces of wood. It mixes very well with acrylics, mica powders and even poorly cleaned metal shavings. The key to mixing is that it changes the reaction cure time especially dependant on how much you put in, typically making the cure time shorter. I highly recommend getting pumps for the jugs that distribute an exact amount per pump, I am using syrup pumps for mine as they handle the thicker A part. Also, using the pumps, I have had great success in mixing by pumping the B curing agent into my mixing container, then pumping the A resin right on top in the same amount. The weight of the resin sinks down and starts a thorough mix right away. Using this method (plus mixing by hand) I have not had any resin not fully cure.

rating 5.0. Beginner Resin Crafter Only resin I've used so far and wanted it for crafting. It came in a nice size box and both bottles had a seal. Both bottles were crystal clear in color. The bottles are slightly bulky but it's a lot of resin for a great price. Measuring is a little tricky since the hardner is thicker but I took 2 of the same size silicone cups and measured to the same line. I set a timer for 4 minutes and stirred slower, scrapping sides at times. You can see that it goes from cloudy with striations to crystal clear again. I let is sit for 5 minutes (setting a timer again) to let the bubbles rise to the top. It works perfectly! I divide it by the colors I decide and add mica powder and glitter. Put it in my silicone molds. It gets hard within 12ish hours but it is better to let it sit for 24 hours. So, if you have a thicker mold, demold it and then let it sit on a flat surface (on a silicone mat) to stay straight until rock hard. I am very pleased with how my pieces look. They all turned out great and once I get a little better, I plan on selling some of the items.

It comes with no instructions, other than, mix equal volume, not really great on details for someone  who has never mixed epoxy before, does not tell you the maximum heat product hold, even though  it is a casting epoxy,  agian, people wouldn't  know if they could put a hot plate on a surface  that they maybe  have used this product  instead of this,  other than lack of knowledge given about this epoxy,  it has a good shine, like glass Epoxy A is super thicker than other brands and Epoxy  B is almost  like water. But the steps are still the same. I have waited after  mixing to get a good thick pour,  seems a bit runny. even holding  a hold pint after mixed for at least 15 mins, and still no thickening to it.. great for casting,  maybe not for a project pour like a huge table, it left runs down rhw edges, it did seem to get very messy.  but thw shine was amazinf, Like i said, not my first rodeo with epoxy, but i really liked the fact, the micro bubbles were easy to pop..so that is a plus, so without actually knowing  the history of this epoxy.  i would not recommend it for tumbler making.

rating 2.0. Dries Tacky I have tried using this epoxy now on several occasions and each time it fails to completely dry into a hard solid layer. Its like it almost dries, but not all the way so the surface is left with this tacky feel to it. The price is great, but it is useless if the product doesn't completely dry. I am mixing equal parts resin and hardener by weight so I don't think it is user error on my part. Either way when it didn't completely set up I had to sand it back off which was a real pain. Overall not worth the money or time I put into giving this brand a try.

input: rating 5.0. Classic Caliper was delivered quickly. Yea! It is easy to read and use. I like it because it has a classic dial on it (as opposed to new digital ones). I don't really need the fractions on the dial but it is convienent for me when I need a quick and dirty measurement. (its not really that dirty ;) I have to admit that it gets me a little respect to have it at work. ( I am a female engineer and I think the men here think it is cool I have one.)

rating 5.0. Oshlun MTDCT-06 6 inch stainless steel fractional dail caliper http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0012YMV50/ref=cm_cr_rev_prod_title
I like the accuracy of this product. It has a variety of functions such as depth gauge, inside measurements, outside measurements. The dial is easy to read, calibrated in 64ths of an inch. I do a lot of wood turning and this caliper makes my work more accurate. It is truly a fine tool. It locks in position making assuring accuracy in use.

rating 4.0. Oshlun Caliper The dial is very easy to read.  It is not as smooth to operate as I was hoping, but for the price it is decent.  I would put it on the same level of quality as those found at Harbor Freight.

rating 5.0. Excellent Purchase I purchased this item because I am tired of using a caliper to measure something then having to convert the results from decimal to a fraction. I also wanted a non-digital caliper because every time I use a digital caliper the battery seems to be dead when I need it.

This has a scale showing fractions of an inch in 64ths which is close enough for me. It also shows the usual decimal scales we have seen before. It is made of stainless steel so it won't rust and it appears to be well made. The scales are easy to read.

You can zero the tool by loosening a thumb screw and turning the scale. Tightening the thumb screw again keeps your adjustment from moving. The tension on the moving head as you take a measurement is adjusted with a different thumb screw. You can tighten it a little to make the movement a little stiffer as you take a measurement or you can tighten the thumb screw a lot so a measurement you have set will not change.

If you buy this tool I doubt you will be disappointed.

rating 1.0. DISAPPOINTING I was really looking forward to getting these calipers, because I really liked the design of the dial. Still do actually.

Unfortunately, the rough operation and the fact that it wouldn't hold zero even ONCE in a row meant that I had to return it.

Problem list:

1. Poor machining leading to rough rack & pinion engagement.
2. A "gravelly" sensation while operating.
3. The sensation of a "hump" that the gear needed to get over as calipers were opened.
4. Operation: Close caliper & zero. Open and measure item. Remove item and return jaws to starting point. At this time the needle should be pointing exactly at the spot you "zeroed" it to originally. Unfortunately, no matter how many times I operated it and went through the procedure, the needle never returned to zero.

I'm afraid that I will have to give this tool a single star. I still like the design, but a measuring device is supposed to accurately measure. Aesthetics are supposed to be a bonus, not a sole redeeming value.

I blame myself really. I should stop being a cheapskate and just buy a Starrett.

rating 4.0. Does the job well I am a professional woodworker. My pieces fit together with dry fit precision. My regular dial caliper failed. I used a spare to get me through the pinch; not great but it worked in a pinch. I ordered this caliper because it has both fractional and decimal identifiers. It is solid and easy to read and to work with. I recommend it.

rating 2.0. Low grade Low grade caliper. Plastic bezel, rough movement. It's not terrible, just low grade.
Buy a used, high quality caliper and you'll be better served.
rating 3.0. You can tell This caliper is ok to use in a pinch, but compared to a Mitutoyo or Starrett, this one is much more cheaply made
rating 4.0. Cheap but good I like it, it's cheap and works very well. You'll need to sand down the sharp edges and loctite the thumb wheel screw.

rating 2.0. Seems to made cheap. The middle part of the slide pops up and it just seems cheaply made.  I think that it can be used as advertised but I feel for the price there should be better quality put into this item.  I have a 19 dollar digital that feels better than this one.

rating 4.0. Very good caliper I own a 12" Brown & Sharpe caliper in thousandths of an inch, which looks and feels like the sort of instrument a company like Apple or Mercedes would produce. A great deal of thought went into its design, and you can see it in the details.

This caliper by Oshlun is not of that caliber, but it is extremely good and works without flaw. Its accuracy is absolute.

More than one reviewer commented that the feel was not right. One said it was gravely, and another said there was a "hump" in the action. I experience neither. The action is silky smooth without any rough spots over its entire range. The needle always goes back to zero. (the dial is adjustable if for some reason it needs it) In fact, the instrument seems to be an exact copy of one by Fowler (in .02 mm markings), but this one is in fractions of an inch. It copies the better instrument very closely, with only a few differences, but those differences are enough to feel that it's not in the same class as those more expensive instruments. It's about half the price, too.

So, what we have is an instrument that looks and almost feels like those costing twice as much to 5 times as much, and is really just as accurate as those. It has sharper corners, more likely to snag something, and maybe ever so slightly less "tight" in its action, but it is still very smooth, very well-machined, and does not disappoint. If I did not have the more expensive instruments with which to compare it, I would never know that it was possible to make one better.

What I'm saying is that this is a fine measuring tool that will never wear out, always will measure accurately, and will not disappoint. It is impressive to see, to hold, and to use. There is no reason to avoid it; it's a fine tool for a good price. It requires no batteries, and its dial is always easy to read. It also has tenths of an inch on its scale, so it's a dual-scale device. It also has millimeters on the shaft, but not on the dial.

I use this a lot, in spite of the finer calipers I own. I'm not afraid to drop it or use it where it could get dirty, because it's NOT the more expensive one. And... the results are exactly the same as if I were using the Brown & Sharpe. I guess the Brown & Sharpe reads more accurately, since its scale is 10 times the sensitivity of this one, but this is still a great instrument.
rating 1.0. Waste of money Fell apart right out of the package.  Poorly made. I would not waste your money on this. There are other calipers for around the same price much better quality.

rating 5.0. Fantastic for the price I already owned an inexpensive digital caliper but if I leave thee button battery in it when not in use the battery discharges over time. Also the return to zero does drift slightly during use. When I found I needed a fractional caliper I decided to try a dial type.

I bought the Oshlum Dial Caliper over 2 years ago from Amazon for about $31; but I waited to use it on several projects to determine how good it was for my need and how well it held up before I reviewed it. I use it for DIY projects so I did not want to spend the price for a professional grade tool.That considered I found the build quality to be very good and it returned to zero every time. The action was very smooth and was as accurate as I could determine against an extrapolation caliper I know is accurate to .0005 inch. The dial is large enough to easily view and displays both fractional measures on the outside of a drawn ring and decimal measures on inside of the ring. I did not abuse it but it did hit a cement floor more than once and has not shown any damage. I found it excellent for my needs so it deserves the 5 stars.

rating 5.0. For Woodworking Accuracy Had mine about 3 years now. Use it all the time in my woodshop. Never had any problems with it. Works smooth, zeros in and keeps zero. I have noticed that occasionally the zero is off by a tiny, tiny bit, say half the width of the needle. I read in the company's description above that accuracy is +/- .001, so when I see it off zero, that tiny bit probably is .001. So mine is as accurate as described. The metal is nothing fancy, but it is smooth and machined well. Everything fits well and works. I am an avid woodworker. I use it for thickness planing, some joint work , measure the depth of a hole or groove, width of a groove, inside measurements, etc... The dial is easy to read and is in fractions, which I use. So no math involved. The fraction marks are in 64th's, you can also easily read between the marks to 128th's. If I cut a joint to 128th's of an inch you know it is a very good day in the shop indeed !!!  Realistically if you are looking to buy one for very fine tolerances for fitting metal parts together or building machinery then you might want to go way up in price range. It's great for wood, the wood probably will move a 128th mark on a humid day anyway. I prefer a dial then a digital. I've read that a digital can round a measurement up if it falls between digits, that it uses energy when off, so batteries get used up. A dial lets you see the measurement when between digits or markings, so I know if just one more swipe of the plane will put it where I want it.  I don't think you can buy more accuracy at this price range. For working wood I don't know why you would need expensive accuracy as the wood will move fine increments across it's grain overnight, just laying in your shop. Many woodworkers work down to a 32th of an inch in accuracy. So to be able to see a 128th is very close measuring for woodwork.

rating 5.0. I love this tool I love this tool! I do woodworking as a hobby and this is my go to for measuring everything small: board thicknesses, offsets, hole depths, pilot hole size for screws, bit sizes (easier than finding the tiny markings on bit sides), and really anything I need to measure less than 6". I use the locking function and a pencil or scribe to mark lines. It comes with a case, but it is always out and close by on my bench.
The little retaining bar at end that keeps the depth bar in its groove almost immediately came loose. The tiny screws that hold it are pretty worthless. But that didn't affect the tool too much. I just hold the bar in its groove with my thumb when needed. This is my second fractional caliper - I wore the first one out after 12 years.
(Of course this review is U.S. centric - it is a fractional measurement tool - no good for metric uses.)
rating 1.0. Sent me defective used item I took a chance and bought a used item.  Dial totally didn't work.

rating 1.0. Spend more money on something better!!! Not what I expected. Razor sharp edges, piece of junk case, rough operation and generally poor QC. Buy something more expensive as you will be disappointed in this.

rating 5.0. Year of use, still going strong I've been using these for over a year nearly every day. Great product for price, I use it for woodworking and general measurements, so up to 64th (128ths if you read between the lines) and hundredths of an inch is perfect. The mechanism works really well so if I need to adjust it by as little as a thousand the of an inch I can and it won't scoot over or under.

Feels really well made, even after a year none of the components have any signs of damage or rust and no threads have been stripped by careless over tightening, which has happened on more expensive calipers than these. It's also fairly easy to disassemble and remove the dust that builds up, the only real complaint I have is the foam in the case started to separate, fortunately a little glue fixed it.

However, If you're doing machining work I would recommend something g that reads thousands of an inch, I have a more expensive calipers I keep exclusively for machining, but this is my favorite one, I am not scared if it drops, or someone steals it at a job sight as I can easily afford another.

rating 3.0. Sloppy Assembly - wrong size screw As delivered, the thumb wheel, its support fitting, and the associated screw were loose in the box.  On attempt to assemble, I discovered that the screw was undersized and not correct.  It should be #4-48, but is instead #3-56.  I will find a suitable screw locally, but this is a disappointment, as I am otherwise satisfied with the caliper.

rating 5.0. I wish I had bought this long ago I don't require a caliper very often and, with a few exceptions, don't require much accuracy from it. For years I have been using a plastic caliper which works fine but it is calibrated in .64" or tenths of an inch. So I would use it on an item and the scale would read "24". OK, so now I have to do the math: 24/64" = 12/32" = 6/16"= 3/8"! With this new caliper I just look at the dial and read 3/8", it couldn't be easier!

The construction is solid, after I locked down the dial it consistently returned to zero each time. This is going to make my life in the workshop much easier. For those of you who routinely work in fractions of an inch, buy this caliper, you won't go wrong.

rating 5.0. Perfect calipers for woodworkers! I have two sets of calipers: this one and a digital electronic caliper by iGaging that reads in fractions, decimals, and metric. If I really need to dial in a measurement precisely, I use the iGaging. But 98% of the time the Oshlun is my go-to! It's the one I carry with me in my pocket, my tool bag, or even when I go to the store and need to measure a board or a bolt. It's so much easier to intuit the dimension of an object when I'm seeing a visual representation of where that dimension lies on the fractional "clock face." So many times when I use the other one - the digitial one - it will give me an answer of, say, 21/64ths and then I have to stop and spend two minutes doing math in my head and figuring out what that precise number means in practical terms. As a woodworker, I technically work to the nearest 32nd, but I THINK in terms of 16ths and 16ths "strong" or "weak". The Oshlun gives me that knowledge at a glance, without mental math and without hesitation. A machinist may need an instrument that can give them accuracy to the thousands of an inch, but for the work I do, the Oshlun Fractional Dial Caliper is the perfect tool for measuring when I need something more precise than my tape measure, alone. Highly recommended.

rating 2.0. much too stiff the slide is very stiff and from about 3 inches to the end, there is a distinct gritty feel to it. I've tried 3 in 1 oil, dry lube, and wd40. noting helps. won't purchase again. very disappointing since my other Oshlun products are well made.

rating 1.0. Do not purchase. Sadly, I can open the calipers an inch, close it again, and it will be off of zero. It has skipped over so many teeth that the bezel is now turned almost 90 degrees. These are calipers. If they are not precise, what’s the point? I’ve adjusted the set screws in order to keep it better aligned on the geared track, and it helped, but it still will not give the same results for consecutive measurements. Do not purchase.

rating 5.0. really does dry in one minute Wonderful for binding large surfaces, and faster.

Not sure if it is more cost effective, though, since a quart size of the weldwood contact cement gel is much less expensive.

If you are in a hurry this product is the best. It also may be less toxic.
rating 5.0. Great for Automobile Headliners I got it to adhere new material to the headliner in my '83 GTI.  The stuff worked perfectly.  I highly recommend it.

rating 5.0. Expensive, but great glue I've bought a few cans of this and for spray on glue it's very good. Works a bit like contact cement- spray on, leave for a few minuets until it goes tacky and then stick both surfaces together. I originally bought it because I needed to glue some rubber flooring down, but it didn't work fantastically well (mainly because the rubber has a high oil content), but it certainly worked better than any other glue I've tried. On most surfaces it works really well- this is a great go to glue for quick repairs and sticking pretty much anything (as long as it doesn't need to weight bear).

rating 5.0. Surprisingly effective so far I've never tried a spray on contact cement - always used the roll-on can of goop. But I saw this used and recommended in a wood working video, so I decided to give it a try. I'm happy with it so far. I haven't used it long enough to talk about longevity of the bond. But I will say the application was super easy, worked well, and gave me a very strong bond.

I was concerned about getting a flat seal between laminate and substrate, particularly with making multiple overlapping passes to cover a panel. But the woodworking video showed that even with what looks like uneven (almost silly string pattern) spray coat and obviously thicker overlaps, the stuff flows and produces a smooth bond when joined.

I will suggest that it's better to put two or three thinner coats on each piece instead of trying to get a "typical" thick coat you'd get from rolled or brushed glue. It only takes one or two minutes for each coat to dry to tacky. (A thick coat took much longer.). And you must abide by the instructions not to brush or roll the sprayed material. Just spray it and leave it be.
rating 4.0. I like it This is a good price. This is to be used for fixing the loose heading in the car. I don't believe it will last very long, but a good temporary fix.

rating 4.0. It glued polyethylene plastic sheeting I used 90-24 to patch thick (10 mil) plastic sheeting and it is the only product I have found so far that works. It patched a rip in my sheet plastic green house cover and in a fish pond I lined with the thick plastic.  By the time I sprayed both the patch and the ripped area and got it thick enough to hold, there was not much left in the can but it did stick and hold and it was permanent in ten minutes.  90-24 is so similar to common contact cement, I ran a test to see if the more readily available product would hold.  It seemed to stick but when it had dried for a week the ccc peeled easily from the patch and the ripped area.  So 90-24 is the best I have found to glue polyethylene sheeting.

rating 4.0. Awesome for hard plastics Works like contact cement, apply to both parts, let each dry to touch, 1-2 mins. then press together. Full set takes up to 10 min. It has an adjustable nozzle for the spray pattern that works well.

I used this to hold cracks in my car's bumper by spaying this into a cup and used a small brush to apply into the cracks. (Gotta work fast or in small sections!) I then reinforced the backside with fiberglass cloth and soaked that with Devcon epoxy for hard plastics. It's working, but am pessimistic about long term success.

Probably need to reapply but overall we are happy with the performance.

input: rating 5.0. Great way to save your fencepost I have a post and rail fence, and have needed to replace several leaning posts over the last couple of years, especially right after the winter. This can be very expensive when a professional does the repair. Then I found out about this item, the EZ Mender, which allows you to easily reinforce an existing fencepost, even one that has begun to rot below the surface. The two steel frames are hammered into place on either side of the post, and then securely screwed to the post. Your post is now upright again and firmly reinforced at the base of the post below ground. I can't believe how easy and inexpensive this repair is.

rating 4.0. Good produt but be aware I installed this item and works well. However be aware while the picture shows 2 units the package actually arrives with one. What is confusing is that other offers have pictures with 1 unit. Usually you need 2 for proper installation. I managed to get the job done with one.

rating 5.0. These E-Z Menders saved me time and money..... Lately in Oklahoma, we've experienced some extremely windy conditions during the month of June.  My wood post stockade fence took a beating and soon I had several fence posts rocking back and forth.  The fence contractor for my home builder did not set the posts correctly and so my 1 year old fence was hanging by a thread.  I took a chance with these things and came away very pleased.  I bought 6 as you are supposed to use 2 per post.  Installation was a snap with a 10 pound sledge hammer.  No more rocking and swaying.  I figure I bought at least 2-3 more years before I have to replace my crappy fence.

rating 5.0. Actually works! As noted by others, you need 2 of these for them to be useful.

My situation was a 6' privacy fence that rotted out at the cement base due to the footing being covered in soil.
When I received the items and looked at the instructions, I was cynical that they'd work since I'd be hammering them into the concrete footing and they start off by going in at a bit of an angle, but the wedge part half way up straightens them up once you get them in deep enough. Took about an hour, but your milage will vary depending on the material you are hammering into.

PRO* TIP: Use 2 sledges! Place one sledge on the unit's target area and strike it with the other. Much easier, quicker and less damaging than trying to hit the E-Z Mender directly. I recommend the smaller hand sledges, not the big boy. EDIT: It has been brought to my attention that striking one hardened surface on another is not safe. While the Mythbusters beg to differ, I think it is safe to say that you should exercise caution when swinging a sledgehammer at anything. /EDIT

Also, the instructions don't mention using the larger hole towards the top, but when I had pounded them in as far as I could they were still a bit separated from the post. Drilling a hole through the post, from one hole to the other (be sure they are aligned) and tightening the E-Z Menders to the post with a 5" bolt took care of that.

* I am by no means a "Pro"

rating 5.0. Great Product I wasn't sure about these when I ordered them, but they saved me a lot of work.  They were easy to install, and look like they will last much longer than the original post did.
rating 5.0. Does what it says it does. Used 4 of these things to shore up my fence so that I could put off rebuilding it.  Really made a good difference.

rating 5.0. It works! I had a couple of fence posts that need reinforcement.  I thought I'd have to take the fence apart, dig up and pull the fence posts, and replace them.  Before doing that, I tried a couple of these Simpson FPBM44E E-Z Menders.  They worked!  Compared to rebuilding, the price is minimal; and they're easy to install.  I'd definitely buy these again.
rating 5.0. Great product I loved the product.  I knocked them into the ground to fix a rotting fence post and they worked like a charm.  I placed some concrete just in case.

rating 5.0. Excellent in every way Had a leaning fence post that was causing two sections of my privacy fence to sag.  I kept putting the repairs off because of cost, the fence is really still in good condition and I'm not much of a carpenter.  Anyway, saw this product through Youtube demos and they came highly recommended in my online research.  I can't begin to say how great these EZ fence menders are...they were exactly as described and installation was the easiest part of fixing the fence.  My post was indeed rotted a bit at the bottom when I dug around it.  I took a small sledge hammer and hammered them in the ground on the front and back of the post.  A simple screw on each side to secure it and then I was pouring quik-crete in that hole and I was done.  There are several Youtube video clips that demonstrate better than I can write about it.  The quik-crete hardened in less than an hour and I proceeded to put the fence back together.  If, like myself, you just want to perform a quick repair of a fence post and save yourself some cash, I can highly recommend these.  My local retailer had a similar product, but it wasn't as long and they were twice the price.
rating 4.0. Worked Very Well They worked as expected and saved me from having to replace post in the near future.  Would buy them again ween needed.
rating 4.0. Fence post mender worked! I had one fencepost that was weaving in a strong wind.  I put the mender in on only one side, and that firmed it up.
rating 5.0. Worked great Worked great to stabilize some wood 4x4 posts that were rotted off. It beats trying to dig out the old cement around the posts.

rating 5.0. Made me think of "wack a mole"! I'm a 68yr old granny and I whacked this thing into place with  a 3# sledge hammer. Took me 3 days because it was hot out, so I whacked a few inches a day....but I did it!.....and that fence post is not going anywhere anytime soon! I love it when a product does what it supposed to!

rating 4.0. 3000 Blows from Thors Hammer Ok let's talk frankly. Any of the videos they show you doing this with less than 3000 hammer strikes per mender is absolute nonsense. It does work but the problem is this - the tolerances between any existing concrete and your fence post is small enough to make ramming a metal frame into the gap nearly impossible. Be prepared for a long and tiring ordeal.

rating 5.0. Strong Support THese supports are very strong & have the necessary spring to support my old arbor for years to come. My arbor is over 20 years old that support many large rooted plants. These added much needed support. You do have to put your back into driving them into the ground.

rating 3.0. Didn't work for me... but appears to be high quality Wanted to repair a broken 4x4 PT post previously set in concrete. This may have use to set a new temporary post or repair a post that is set in ground without concrete.  Description says "Allows easy repair of rotted or damaged 4x4 wood posts installed in concrete or dirt", since it did not work for me (in concrete), my rating is 3 star "its okay" for some uses.  The item does appear to have significant strength.

rating 5.0. Great product -- better pricing available I got three of these for the $17 price, and can't find that deal any longer. Now I can only find 1 for $17. rating 5.0. Best Tape Ever For the past three months, I have had long strips of this tape holding down the weather seal on my garage floor. rating 5.0. Excellent sticking power, even on porous surfaces. I used this stuff to tape down carpets, and on unpainted, porous wood. It is amazing how well it sticks. It is quite thick and strong also, but you can tear it like duct tape. Only thing I couldn't stick it to was a dusty metal surface, and I didn't expect to anyway: it just picks up the dust. Once clean, that metal surface gave ultimate adhesion. I just bought 2 more rolls.
rating 5.0. Super Strong Tape! The best tape you can buy for packing. It won't come off your boxes and is super strong! Cant go wrong with this brand.

rating 5.0. THE BEAST standard of tapes! Will secure just about anything and highly weather resistant too! The name says it ALL - GORILLA STRONG... Not much else to say aside from, stick and forget about it. Weather has little if any effect, the sun might as well be a lamp shade bulb, this stuff does not peel away like cheapo duck/duct/etc tape.

The only annoyance is the smell of the whole large bundle. The adhesive is gorilla strong and so is the smell on the roll! I keep it in a zip-lock bag. But what do you expect from a SERIOUS, near industrial grade tape... After applying it, the smell subsides though. If you NEED a serious business - survive a ripped jacket or tarp accident - this stuff will DO IT!

Want to go even more GUERRILLA? Scuff surface of intended application, wipe with iso-91% alcohol, apply tape, then SOW the tape with a needle and thread = PERM sealant = no fraying/peeling edges (all tape suffers from this weakness of design)!

GO GORILLA!

rating 4.0. Good for some unusual uses I use a lot of duct tape for halloween stuff. Gorilla tape is no fooling strong but some other heavy duty tapes are a little sticker. I'm taping together plastic from milk jugs and I need stuff to stay taped in place. There is a 3m product that is sticker, about as strong, and a little more flexible that i prefer. (However, 3M didn't put a product number on the inside tube, so I can't provide a link until I find another roll at Home Depot.) One very nice thing about the Gorilla tape is that it makes a very smooth shiny black surface.

I used the tape to repair (well, hold together) a large trash big that had some substantial cracks. The tape stood up for about 2 years. I think that mostly the sun did it in. Now using it to cover a nasty dent in the car doors (see pix)
rating 5.0. Five Stars Give me 20 rolls or so of this stuff and I could repair the space shuttle

rating 5.0. Excellent Product this tape is amazing. after trying other tapes that didnt work i decised to go with this one - i got it to hold a bumper that was falling off on my car and its been over a week and that bumper is not moving. i love this tape

rating 5.0. Tape with a Kung fu grip This is some sticky tape it is like crazy glue tape. It has a lot of adhesive on it and really grips. I am not sure if it just better adhesive or just more adhesive that makes it so good. The con is it leaves a lot of residue when removed. Works on car windows and wood for sure.

rating 5.0. Can't live without this around the house I find so many uses for this tape that it would be hard to enumerate them. I don't like the ugly gray of duct tape, so this is my choice. It is very strong tape.

My one caution would be to let you know not to use it in strong direct heat or sunlight as the adhesive gets soft and melts. I found this to be a problem with the Handicap Parking hanger for my car. I purchased a plastic clip for the hanger that allows you to keep it clipped to your rear view mirror at all times, then flip it up when driving. The plastic wasn't strong and it cracked, so I fixed it with strips of gorilla tape. Since it is always in the windshield and exposed to high temps in the summer, I would find that the two pieces were always sliding apart because the adhesive had liquefied. Any other use has been a dream, though.

I use this tape on my cheap shelving units that have a black color to the veneer. I tape it across the back to hold that cheap cardboard part that is supposed to nail on the back and hold it all together (hmm, right). I tape it from the sides, all across the back to the other side, where all of the shelves are situated. I also run one strip from the sides across the top of the unit. I've had those large units pull apart when you try to move them, but not with the Gorilla tape holding them together. The black tape is discreet against the color of the shelving unit also. The one that I have is 6 feet tall by almost 4 ft wide, and that pressed board is very heavy. This unit, which cost about $28, has been moved twice and still looks as good as it did when I first put it together. That would never be the case, in my past experiences anyway, if I had not strengthened it with the Gorilla Tape.

I consider this one of the indispensable items to have on hand around the house at all times.

rating 2.0. Did not work in clean dry smooth wood Disappointed with the lack of adhesion. Was trying to cover a few wasp holes in the bare wood frame that I made for grape vines. The wood frame is bare, dry and smooth and thought that it would be good enough to attach a few pieces of the tape over the offending holes. rating 5.0. The BEST tape ever This stuff is strong. My husband built, what was suppose to be a hot house, out of shower glass panes.  He put baby chicks in the hot house, and they converted it into a chicken coop.  However, the structure was not exactly air tight, until I framed all the windows with this tape. The tape is still secure, and has held up against snow, rain, wind and hail. Looks good, too.  If you like duck tape, you'll love Gorilla tape.

rating 5.0. This review is for thous of you who expect the best tape for repair jobs you could ask for What ever you put this tape on be sure you want it to stay there. The bond is so strong dont expect to repair something that with tape that has a super bond and then want it to come of easily. Its made to stay where you put it. If you put it on paint common sence tells you if you take it off its going to take the paint off, why wouldnt it? Every repair I have made with this tape has gone beyond expectations.
I moved into a house that had really cheap floor tile in the kitchen and was installed badly, I simply cant afford to replace the floor. I noticed that some of the tile was comeing loose. I used this tape on the seams. This is black tape did I expect it to look like a professional repaire job? No. I expected it to keep the tile just where it was, and thats on the floor. After 4 months of people walking on it, 2 large dogs running over it washing the floor (using some common sence when gong over the tape), not one single part of the tape has begun to lift off.
The screws holding the front cover of my diswasher had broken off and the only repair available was to replace the door. Well there was another option. I used the gorilla tape along the seam of the door and that was over 6 months ago and its still there and my dishwasther works perfectly.

I have used this tape on tile, enamaled stainles steel, aluminum, plastic, and wood. There has not been a single project I hae used this tape on where the tape came off.

If you want to do a temporary repair on something until you go to the hardware store just use something else made to be temparary. Don't use Gorilla tape and then complain because there were some problems taking it off. No product works on 100% of everything. Why would you expect this tape to do so.

This tape is made to stay, period. Out of all the tapes there are how many would work being used on something that is exposed to direct sunlight for extended periods of time.

When I need to make a repair the first thing I think about is how permanent do I want this repair. Would you use a sledge hammer to pound in a finishing nail for a base board.
I've just used it to secure a bookcase. The kit was given to me and it is the worst design I have ever seen. I would move it slightly and the whole thing would flex and the connections would come undone. I secured all the connections with gorilla tape. There is no give at all and the connections do not come apart anymore and so when i move the bookcase it doesnt fall apart. What started out as a piece of crap bookcase, actuall it shelving. it now a totally usefull set of shelves.

Update: 10/14/12 bookcase is still together, dishwasher door is still being held together by this tape, and my floor tiles are still on the floor thanks to this tape. No additional repairs on any of these items been necessary. Had to use a large cooler in my kitchen to keep something cool that I couldnt fit in my refridgerator. 3 10lb bags of ice were in it. Silly me, I didnt check the valve on the cooler to be sure it was closed. I was gone for about 5 hours and came home to a whole lot of water on the kitchen floor. The tape that was holding down the tiles and covered with water to this day are still secure. None of it peeled up and its still keeping the tiles secure to the floor.

rating 5.0. If there is something better, I have yet to find it. Best duct tape I have ever used. If there is something better, I have yet the find it. The shell of this stuff is bullet proof, the body has mesh so strong I could rappel with it, and the adhesive is literally twice as thick as the standard silver tape. I have had it on a boat cover hole (That's right - I'm cheap!) for 6 weeks, and shell has yet to even wrinkle. It has taped targets to pine trees, clear plastic sheeting to brick and "chalky" aluminium siding, and I used it to seal a rain leak on an EZ-up canopy. This latest roll I bought says "Improved!" Improved?! It's now better than all I stated?! What is this witchcraft?!?!? Well, I drink no Kool-Aid.... I know that sometimes by way of improvement, sometimes thing take a step back. I will update if need be, as I do all my reviews. 9.10.15

***UPDATE*** Not sure what the "Improved!" part is or was, but this product still performs as good as it ever did. I am still stuck on Gorilla Tape, nothing more for me! 10.20.15

***UPDATE*** 9.22.2020 Still use Gorilla exclusively. Best performance to date was sealing the sun roof on a beat up Jeep Grand Cherokee. Survived 90+ summer sun and -15 winters. After 1 year it was still not leaking up to the day the Jeep was totaled. I keep a 1" black roll in my BOB, a 2" black is always in the shop AND in the back of the current Jeep, and a 3" white stays with the camping gear. Buy with confidence - it is worth the extra $$$

input: rating 5.0. Such a great item for leg/knee-surgery patients! After knee-replacement, my husband needed to ice his leg a lot. He also needed to 'wrap' the leg to protect the staples and incision. In the hospital, they used ace bandages over the skin, then the 'ice pad' from the ice-water circulating machine, then ANOTHER ace bandage to keep that in place. With all those elastic bandages, the wrinkles they were causing his skin where they bunched up got really painfully annoying, and putting them on and taking them off was a two-person job. When he got home, I purchased this 'sleeve', which was long enough to cover his whole leg, and then double back over, creating a 'pocket' in which to put the ice pad. Easy-peasy, and no wrinkles, no binding, no double-wrapping or discomfort at all. The tubular elastic bandage slipped on like a stocking and fit his leg securely yet comfortably. He used this bandage for a couple of weeks straight with no loss of stretch and very minimum of raveling, even though the ends are scissors-cut. I'm so happy I found this bandage!

rating 5.0. Super Tubies! Tubie-grips are amazing. I first encountered them when I had to go to wound care for a non-healing ulcer. They used tubies over the gauze-wrap bandage on my leg. It served two purposes: 1) the gauze bandage stayed where it had been put, which if you've ever tried to wrap something in gauze, you know it slips, and 2) it served to gently compress the entire area of the leg that it covered, preventing it from ballooning up and actually reducing the existing swelling.
Tubies come in one long tube, so you cut the length you need. They come in many sizes, from super tiny meant for your fingers, all the way up to quite large tubes meant for an abdominal or chest wrap. They are washable and dryable, but they do last longer if you air-dry them. Over time, they become looser, but you can sort of compensate for that by using 2 layers.
If you are trying to heal a wound, especially one that has been hard to heal, I recommend using the tubies to cover the gauze wrap, so that you have moderate compression. This will keep your gauze in place and provide support and encourage good blood flow to your wounded body part.
I also recommend tubies for helping tired and swollen legs. I have personally used a large tubie from my thigh to my knee, overlapping it with a tubie from my knee to my foot. Between the two of them, my leg swelling was greatly reduced, and my legs' tiredness and constant pain was much improved.

input: rating 5.0. Five Stars Perfect. Soft metal like butter me now there are threads. Just as promised

rating 1.0. Made over like holes with no threads on aluminum Did not work for me and my drill press. Made over like holes with no threads on aluminum. Watched videos to see if I was doing it wrong and I was not.
rating 5.0. Quality plus Good quality taps.  I use regularly with no worry.  I will buy again.  I have paid more for a lot less!

rating 5.0. Great name brand taps Great name brand taps. They're made by Yamawa, one of the largest tap makers in Tokyo Japan. These in particular seem to be the titanium coated high speed steel taps. Though i can't pin point them in their catalog. These are made in Japan. Much better than the box store taps. They're capable of tapping stainless steel! I use it for aluminum and it cuts like butter! And you get a a range of M3 to M8 taps. (No M7) Perfect for me! I am incredibly pleased with these.

rating 5.0. Works great. These are good quality taps. No complaints.

To the 1-star reviewer that mentioned not having any threads when using this with a drill press: you can't tap with a drill press. The tap needs to be able to pull the drill down as it threads the hole. Otherwise, you'll just get a hole with no threads.
rating 4.0. cheap taps good for chasing threads, not good for cutting original threads
rating 1.0. One Star It snaps at first try. Not worthy of 1 star.

rating 1.0. Buyer beware! I am unhappy with what arrived. The photo for the product clearly showed manufactured in Japan Yamawa HSS bits. What showed up was Made in China knockoffs. I am stuck with them because I need them right away for a project.

rating 4.0. Did the Job on Soft Metal I had one of those stupid little headache jobs ... I had to shave about 1.5mm from the head of a M6 socket bolt, but had to do it accurately and repeat it several times. So my first step was to make a little fitting for my lathe that would allow me to chuck up the M6 bolt. That’s where these taps came into play. It was pretty easy work to drill a pilot hole in a piece of brass rod, then use the M6 tap to enlarge the hole and cut threads. The design evacuates chips fairly well, though the tap did clog up when I used too much cutting fluid. Overall, these taps seem to work pretty well for softer metals — for a harder metal, I think you’d need pretty much the same size pilot hole that you’d use on any other tap. For the cost, though, I’ve got no complaints.
rating 2.0. Cheap Not so great.  Quality of the cut threads is so-so.  Probably fine for soft plastics like HDPE or similar. Would not use on metals.
rating 1.0. Junk Junk. All broke but one.
rating 1.0. Garbage Drilled the proper pilot hole and lubricated and the bit snapped as if it was made of plastic. Junk... don't waste your money.

rating 5.0. Oh Baby Do I Love These Things I deal with a lot of tempered steel. Having to run a tap through it is a chore and I usually break the smaller taps. On this occasion I was using a traditional M5-.08 tap. Cutting oil and all, the flutes fill with metal and jam the tap either gong in or coming out. On the way out, it snapped. I wanted to get the job done ASAP and the only set that could be delivered that day was this spiral set. I will never go back to the old straight flutes again. These things cut and eject the cuttings quickly. No sticking and they cut easily. For once being in a hurry paid off. Otherwise I would have never learned how nice these things worked.

rating 5.0. Spiral taps you can power tap with I bought these so that I could power tap with a drill.  Hand tapping with straight flute taps is tiresome and time consuming.  I chucked up the M6, and bam.  Tapped hole. Ran it pretty hard and it didn't snap.  Still sharp after several holes in who-knows-what steel.  Good value so far.

rating 1.0. Snapped M4 in aluminum Snapped M4 tapping threads into aluminum. Not my first time tapping threads. Used cutting oil and drill press unplugged for perfect alignment. Not happy. Cant imagine people using these on steel of any kind if it snapped in aluminum.
rating 2.0. Brittle They are rather flimsy and broke the m3 on the 3rd hole and i even drilled the hole oversized
rating 1.0. Fragile I went from beyond excited to upset pretty quickly... did 3 holes fine 4th hole this happened now it ruined a long extrusion with it.
rating 1.0. m3 tap failed very easily... maybe the larger diameters will hold up better? lets hope so, although this was the only size I really needed.

rating 5.0. Used in aluminum, nice threads and no issues! I bought these for tapping the ends of some 8020 (30mm and 20mm). This was my first time tapping and I used a hand tap. The taps worked quite well and cut very easily. I didn't break a single one, and I was quite sloppy at times. I mostly used the M8 tap, but also the M5 tap at times. I've tapped about 30 or so holes with them, and they're still going strong.
rating 1.0. No happy. Item work not well .i return always. I don’t like these.

rating 3.0. Ok as a backup, not for big jobs I purchased these because I needed to tap some aluminum extrusions a 3D printer. All in all, I needed to tap about 32 M6 holes about 15 mm deep into relatively soft aluminum.

This tap made it about halfway through the job before binding and snapping, despite taking it slow and using liberal amounts of tapping lube. Despite the spiral flute design, it also didn't like just going straight ahead, and need to reverse every few turns to break and clear chips. After this one broke I went and bought a decent through hole spiral flute tap from McMaster for $15, and the difference was night and day—it cut smoothly with very little resistance, and not a hint of binding.

If you might need to tap an M3-M8 hole or two at some point, get these. If you *know* you'll need to tap a bunch of them, buy the real deal.

current_product_reviews: rating 1.0. M5 Snapped on 4 hole Don't buy, unless you like wasting your money....
other_product_reviews: rating 1.0. Made of Cheese-Grade Brass (See picture) Nozzle bore increased in size about 15 over the course of about 15hrs worth of printing. Basically, every nozzle from this pack has started to fall apart instantly up use. Do not buy. A genuine nozzle will last infinity longer and be cheaper in the long run.
rating 1.0. 50% Dead on Arrival I have bought 4 of these. Half of them were dead on arrival. One just completely didn't work and one was permanently enabled causing thermal runaway. Do not buy.

input:
rating 5.0. Stop researching. Just buy this one!! I definitely never thought I would be the type of woman to fall in love with a household appliance, but here I am, in LOVE with my new vacuum!! We previously owned the regular Hoover Windtunnel, and used it without incident for 7 years... we literally used it until the wheels fell off! During that 7 years we got a dog and had two babies, and decided that it was probably time to "upgrade" to a Dyson Pet Vacuum. Worst. Mistake. EVER. Less than two years in, the Dyson broke, and it was going to take way more time to repair than you want to wait for, with two toddlers and a dog and lots of messes.

Cue my impulse buy to go back to the Windtunnel (1/4 the price of the Dyson!). We've only had it for about a month, but I absolutely love this thing! It is a little harder to push and maneuver than the Dyson (I would not recommend it to someone with mobility issues), but if you can get past that, this thing is amazing!
rating 1.0. One Star Was broke when I received and sent it back.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Works better than my Dyson!!

rating 1.0. More dust out than in. Very disappointed in this vacuum.
I owned the previous model for 5 years before the suction gave out. Figured no problem I'll just order the newest model. I didn't finish my living room before it started blowing dust on everything, covering even the baby swing and top of the couch. The dogs ran for cover because it was so thick in the air.
I'm persistent so I sent the vacuum back assuming it was a manufacturers defect. The replacement vacuum had the same problem.

I spent the extra money to get a Dyson, and it was worth it. Not only did the Dyson clean up the mess the hoover left behind it also cleaned the extra mess of two dogs and being without a vacuum for a month.

rating 1.0. Worked for all of 30 seconds. Used the vacuum for the first time today and it smelled like an electrical fire was taking place. Machine worked for all of 30 seconds and then the light on the unit started flickering and it lost it's suction. Very disappointed. Will be returning for a refund and purchasing another brand.

rating 5.0. Great for Pet Owners! This vacuum is amazing!! I use it multiple times a week and have a really big dog that loves to shed hair. This vacuum is great on hardwood floors, rugs, furniture and doggie furniture!! I even clean my mud room with it! Usually I see Christmas Tree pine needles until the summer, but with this vacuum, I've only seen one!

rating 4.0. So in love with this vacuum So in love with this vacuum. Best one I've ever had, that's for sure.

I bought it to clean up pet hair on carpet and couches, and was impressed (and disgusted) by how much DIRT it pulled from my carpet!

Works well on getting hair off my micro-suede couch, but did not do as well getting hair out of my seat covers in the car. So it just must work better on some fabrics.

Warning: the vacuum brush means business! I probably had it on the wrong setting for how short the pile on my rug is, and it kind of "fluffed" the material. Not sure how to explain it... Still looks fine, but if you have a fancy rug that you're concerned about, I would test a small area of it and make sure you've got your settings correct for it. You can also just turn off the brush!

rating 1.0. Gets hot fast & doesn't keep suction. Good vacuum. Mine gets hot really fast...after about a minute of being on. I have a 1000 sq ft apartment, with one short haired cat, and I have to empty it 3-4 times each time I vacuum. Not sure if I'd consider it worth it.
Edit 1.30.19- This vacuum officially lost all suction. I tried to vacuum a rug yesterday with pet hair and I went over the same spot 4 times and it didn't pick anything up...I just gave up.

rating 1.0. A high maintenance choice Do NOT buy this product! I am updating my review because I've now had this vacuum for many months. It is junk. The clip that hold the cord in place, allowing you to clean the floor without holding the cord, has broken off -- through no fault of any user. The cord retractor barely works, with two feet of cord that won't wind back into the cleaner. It requires frequent messy cleaning. I'm already thinking of replacing this -- never to buy another Hoover. .... Below is my original review, unaltered: I bought this product about five months ago. I can't recommend it. And I wanted to share why, as I wish I'd read a review pointing out this problem: High maintenance. The vacuum works well, as long as it's very clean. In these five months, I've had to take apart the filter and empty the dirt cup twice -- and it's a messy, dusty process that also requires me to run a filthy permanent filter under running water for two minutes. Today, in fact, the vacuum shut down with a dirt cup less than 1/4 full. That resulted from some kind of thermal protector, though the vacuum wasn't hot as far as I could tell. It's on the heavy side too, as other reviewers have pointed out. That's not a huge issue for me. However I've now had another problem as well: The cord rewind feature doesn't work properly on mine. It's too bad. Before this purchase, I owned a Hoover vacuum for 18 years, replacing the bag inside when needed, which was rarely. It was a gem and cost about $100 back then. This new model is a pain -- and I won't buy another Hoover for a long time because of it. Hope this may help you as you consider options. Good luck!
rating 5.0. Great :) Came in perfect condition and works great, thank you :)

rating 2.0. Engine Died After Three Months I had the vacuum from 09 January to 03 April and it just died. The engine stopped working. After going through and cleaning out every filter, checking connections and belt all was great after being less than 3 months old and the engine just died.

rating 1.0. This vacuum worked fine for a few months then suddenly the brush would ... This vacuum worked fine for a few months then suddenly the brush would stop moving.  I tried cleaning the brush, empty the filter, checked the belt.  I got the brush to eventually work but it then required a lot of effort to push the vacuum across my carpet.  My wife could barely move it.  I would not recommend this product.

rating 5.0. Love it. It cleans better then any vacuum that ... Love it. It cleans better then any vacuum that I have owned. It cleans easily and after every use I empty the dirt and clean the top filter. No clogging when it is cleaned each time.

rating 5.0. Very nice vacuum for the money! So far it has been working great. No issues with funny smells or belts breaking. It really picks up the dirt and pet hair (golden retriever)! It is pretty amazing what it picks up. We just had some newer, softer fiber carpeting installed (Carress by Shaw, I believe), so I wanted something that would work well but not damage it, and this seems to be fitting the bill very well so far.

I was happy we went with this model that can raise and lower the height of the deck. Since it has so much suction power it would get "stuck" on wood floors and even be really hard to push on carpeting if the deck was too low. Just raise it up a notch or two and things work great... lots of suction without having to get a workout. Easy to clean. Easy to put together. The tools work great, too!

The hand vacuum tool works great on stairs and spins very fast, keeping it from stopping like an older version we had (unless you really mash it into the carpeting). It works using the airflow from the suction (it is not driven with a motor), so you have to keep from pressing down too hard, but it is easy to use and works well.

The vacuum seems very sturdy and is not that heavy. The suction swath is pretty wide, too. The suction hose seems very well made... not made out of cheap plastic/rubber tubing... it is sturdy and well made. I did a lot of research and looked long and hard at various Shark vacuums. They seemed very good, but they did not raise up and down and the suction swath seemed very small. I'm sure they are very good, too... but not sure they are worth $100 more. I looked at other brands/models, too but kept coming back to the Hoover because of it's various options, versatility, good reviews, and price. I think we made the right decision.

We have only had it for a month or so, but we use it a lot and it has not disappointed us. Hopefully that will continue!

rating 1.0. This Vaccum started to work fine intially. After a couple of weeks it started ... This Vaccum started to work fine intially. After a couple of weeks it started to make weird loud noise, and its not functional anymore.
Would not recommend this product. Do not waste money on buying this, look for other vacuum instead.

rating 1.0. it now turns off after 5 mins of running and need to let it cool down before it will work After a month of use , it now turns off after 5 mins of running and need to let it cool down before it will work again

rating 1.0. Hoover vacuum no longer reputable. Worst vacuum ever. After just a few months, the vacuum would stop after running just a few minutes. I called customer service and they said the vacuum was overheating and that I needed to dis-assemble it and clean all of the parts in order for it to work again. (That sounds like the old blue-screen-of-death for the Windows operating system.)

After six months of use, the brushes stopped spinning. Aparently one of the shift parts broke. Ugh.

At that point I thru away the vacuum and bought a bissell. It works perfectly, doesn't stop, and the parts are not breaking.

I have owned Hover Vacuum cleaners for years, but not more.

rating 4.0. Good pet vacuum! This product was not my first choice in a pet vacuum but I am definitely glad I ended up getting it! After doing hours of research I had narrowed my search down to a couple different vacuums, this one was the cheapest by far and so i went with it.

It has excellent power to pick up hair off the floor. I have both hardwoods and carpet in my house and this vacuum tackles both without a problem. The main issue I have always had is pet hair on the sofa. My dog sheds like crazy . . . and is white . . . and we have a brown sofa. So you KNOW when she has been on the sofa. Well, not any more cause this vacuum pics it all up without a problem. The attachments are great for getting into various places, but I really love the crevice tool and the turbo pet attachments. They do an excellent job of getting the dog hair off the couch!

The cord is nice and long, so I can go anywhere I need without constantly changing plugs. I wish the hose was a little longer, but that is not too big of an issue since the power cord is so long. I also love the ability to retract the cord when finished instead of having to wind it around the side of the vacuum. It means if I have only one area to vacuum, I only pull out a little cord and then back it goes without any problem I have a feeling this function may not last because there are already times it takes a little effort to get the cord to go back in. I will push the pedal and it will pull most of it in, then I will have to pull some back out and push the pedal again for it to take the rest of the cord. This is really my only serious complaint. I just hope the vacuum and especially the cord retractor can last a while for me!

rating 5.0. If you need a great vacuum look no further. this is the best vacuum cleaner for the money by far. I had one of these for 15 years and gave it to my oldest daughter and it still works like new. I bought this one to replace my other one and I just love every thing about it. The cord re-tractor  works so good and never have  problem. It cleans the carpet like new every time. Picks up stuff so deep it cleans the carpet great every time I use it. the canister is so easy to empty for someone like me that is disabled and cant bend much. It is not heavy at all and if you learn how to set the height on te vacuum it pushes very easily over the carpet or hardwood floors. It cleans my furniture with the small brushes and picks up all the dog hair that my dog leaves behind.  I just love this vacuum it makes cleaning my house so much easier,  I read these comments and I have no idea what they are talking about as far as over heating and smelling hot etc. I would guess they need to learn to set the vacuum at the right level so it doesn't drag on the carpet and that would fix their problem. Because I have used one just like this for 15 years and love it and this new one is even better than the last. And it cleans my truck better than the car wash vacuum does.

rating 5.0. The Pet "Tools" Really Work... surprisingly. So... this is the fourth vacuum I've owned as an adult (second hand store upright that took the old school bags, little walmart dirt-devil cyclone-y vacuum, pull-behind cannister, and uber-manly shop-vac). Until my last roommate moved in, I was vacuuming the house with the shop vac. This was: 1) Loud (it's the 6hp that doubles as a leaf blower), 2) Dirty (it left a fair amount of dust blowing around... the dog would sneeze), 3) Dangerous (it ate a tennis ball... was actually sorta rad). But yeah, roommate moved in and brought her [brand and model removed because I don't want to put anyone down] "Pet" model vacuum. And it was pretty awesome. Between us we had two cats and a Saint Bernard. Come spring shedding... that's a lot of butt-fluff.
I was actually looking to find the exact same model as hers when I found this one on sale. SO GLAD. On to the actual review...

The little rubberized "pet" tools? They look stupid. I didn't think they'd do anything. I can't believe how wrong I was. The dog hair in the back of the car (under the seats, in small nooks and crannies) and even just the ground-in / matted down stuff that the vacuum at the car wash couldn't get up... this destroys. I can vacuum out the back of my ride and not be embarrassed to have people see it. It's phenomenal.
I know it's silly... but the auto-cord-winder is one of those little touches that really put this into five-star range. It's s silly, simple thing. But not having to wind up the cord after cleaning is super nice.
The ability to turn the brush on and off on the fly is outstanding (I've got hardwood floors, deep carpet, and thin rugs to deal with) and that's just super nice. Also, the fact that it shuts off when you have the handle in the upright / locked position is nice too.
The canister is a little clunky to empty, but it empties fully and easily. Let me explain. With the dirt (and dog hair) collection canister removed, you empty it by pressing a lever that unhinges the bottom (swings free, but doesn't detach). So I recommend pulling up the sides of the trash bag around it a little before letting loose. In contrast, my roommate's vacuum would empty through the top past the filter insert which would require a whole lot of shaking and ketchup-bottle-style thwacking to get the clump of animal fur and dirt out. This is easier, but it's all coming at once.

Overall? Five stars, man. Expectations fully met. And what it does to my car... that's freakin' beautiful.
Oh, also, the purple color... very manly. :-)

rating 2.0. Omg! This is one heavy vaccume! Its so ... Omg! This is one heavy vaccume! Its so heavy it's hard to manuever it around.  My cleaning lady said the same and with eight foster dogs its impossible to use it daily as it's also too big to go under furniture. It's strong as far as suction but too darn heavy unfortunately.

rating 1.0. Cord retractor broke after two uses. Suction suffering after ... Cord retractor broke after two uses. Suction suffering after 2 months. Cleared filter and all notable area that may have been clogged, no improvement.
rating 1.0. It's VERY heavy, does not float, is hard ... It's VERY heavy, does not float, is hard to push, and it doesn't pick up well.  I wanted to return it but it was too late.

rating 1.0. Piece of crap. Received it today Piece of crap.  Received it today, put it together, turned on.  With in 2 seconds its was smoking.  Defective piece of crap.  Please save yourself the money and buy a different vacuum.

rating 4.0. Great price and fast shipping were the selling points for ... Wife loves this, seems to get all the pet hair op in the first pass. Great price and fast shipping were the selling points for me but I must say I'm surprised at how quite this unit is. Pretty compact it fits( rather easily) in a hall closet and is fairly easy to clean.

rating 2.0. Needs to go to the shop after 4 uses! I LOVED this vacuum when I got it!  It was PERFECT to clean up my dark carpet with yellow lab hair all over it.  It did the job...the first 4 uses.  Then it stopped sucking up the fur - it just moved it around the carpet.  I called tech support, which was great (the women I spoke to were very nice).  We did some troubleshooting and I was told that they would send me a new belt JUST IN CASE that was the issue.  1 month later, I finally got the belt due to a backorder (naturally), and it still wont suck up the pet fur from the carpet.  Not only that, the red/green indicator showing that the filter needs rinsed went from green to red within 4 uses - and even though the filter is 100% clean as if it was just installed the indicator still shows it needs changed.  I am taking it to a repair shop (per customer service) to see if they can fix it because it is still under warranty, but it has been over a month since I've been able to clean my floors.  The sheer fact that it only took 4 uses to break does not leave me with warm and fuzzy feelings about this vacuum, no matter how pleased I was when I first got it.

rating 5.0. I love this vacuum I love this vacuum!  It is sort of horrifying to be able to easily see all the dirt and dog hair it picks up through the clear container, but I'm glad to know that it's in the vacuum and not on my floor.  And the retracting cord is a great feature that I really appreciate.  Cleaning is easy and I like not having to hunt down the right kind of bag at the store.  Have had it about six months and am really pleased with it.

rating 1.0. Avoid - Worst vacuum money can buy! Absolutely the worst money I have ever spent. I don't understand these positive reviews. Easy to maneuver? Nope. This thing is VERY difficult to push and pull on both hardwood and carpeting (not even deep carpeting) on all height settings. The vacuum barely picks up anything from those surfaces too, same with the attachments. I have to constantly clean out the main compartment, attachments, and filter, and still you'll only get a few swipes over your rug before it stops picking up again. We have two short hair cats and this thing does NOT make picking up their fur or loose litter easy. We absolutely dislike this machine and highly recommend against purchasing it.

rating 1.0. Ordered this January of this year. Thought I needed ... Ordered this January of this year. Thought I needed to replace my 5yr old Dyson. When I got it, it performed just as well as the Dyson. I kept it because of the attachements. ( I had lost a couple of my Dyson's )
Less than a year later, it has lost suction and I can tell the carpets are not as clean. I don't have any pets and it is just myself and my young son. So, or carpets are not that challenging. Really wish I could return this.

rating 3.0. it's only OK. nothing great, and don't expect high quality at this price. First, if you're interested in this machine, check out the ratings on the Hoover site: http://hoover.com/products/details/uh70935/windtunnel-3-pro-pet-bagless-upright/

it gets a 2.2 out of 5!

I am comparing this on its own as well as a replacement for a 15+ year old model. This one sucks! Well, much better than the old model, which was falling apart. Not sure why I got the "pet" model - we do have pets, but no carpeted stairs.

Pros:
o it has a lot of suction
o turning off the beater bar when the handled is locked upright is a great idea
o it's easy to empty - much easier than the old model
o it's very easy to change the vacuum height, and it works well
o being able to turn off the beater bar when cleaning hard flooring is great! Our old model didn't do this and it was useless cleaning hard flooring as it just shot out the back!
o it's fairly light and easy to manuever

Cons:
o it has so much suction, when you use hose, it pulls the vacuum towards you, or over - the hose pulls very tight and doesn't uncoil well
o the expandable tube is much to short when using any of the accessories
o other than the crevice tool, the other attachments seem worthless. We bought a brush tool that's much better
o the cord, if not pulled fully out, can get hot in the retractable canister - even though it has air vents; we always try to pull it completely out
o the cord is MUCH too short!
o it's loud, but not that bad

it's only been a couple of months, but it's just OK. Maybe we shouldn't have gotten the "pet" model. I'm sure there are better products that are close in price, however, my wife liked the previous model, so we stuck with his cheap one. You get what you pay for!
rating 1.0. One Star Does not pick up litter from carpet and over heats

rating 5.0. Great at sucking! Oh man, do I love this vacuum! Our apartment has nearly wall to wall carpet, which is not ideal. We also have one short hair kitty who sheds moderately. We had a crappy old Dirt Devil hand me down that was not doing the job so we ordered this. And I am so glad we did!

As soon as it arrived it put it together, which was super easy, and immediately started vacuuming our dining room. So, I am kind of a neat freak and our apartment is not dirty at all. This vacuum both horrified and elated me as it sucked a disgusting amount of dust and dirt out of our carpet. I was loving the clear canister as it allowed me to see the filth that is no longer festering under our feet. I filled that baby right up when I proceeded to vacuum the entire house. Even took a photo of all the dirt before I emptied it and sent it to the boyfriend (he accepts and loves my weirdness).

Quick break down of the pros and cons:

PROS:
- Easy set up - VERY effective - Canister is simple and not messy to empty
- Easy to switch from carpet to bare floor - Cord is plenty long - Maneuvers easily and isn't very heavy
- Get a sense of satisfaction and accomplishment watching the dirt fill up as you vacuum!

CONS:
- Spinning brush pet attachment is a little flimsy and powered only by suction so if you press down on the surface you're trying to vacuum, it slows down, or stops the spinning (depending on how hard you press).
- The attachment with three rubber things is great for brushing animal fur into giant clumps, but not the best at sucking it up. I just popped the attachment off and used the hose when needed, which worked great.

Overall, I would recommend this vacuum for anyone who has a lot of carpet (it's good on bare floors too). My carpets actually LOOK cleaner. When the boyfriend came home from work the first day I used it he asked if I had the carpets cleaned. "Nope babe! Our new vacuum is just great at sucking!"
rating 1.0. One Star This vac was a TOTAL WASTE OF MONEY!!!  Barely any suction power!!!
rating 2.0. Two Stars Its much bigger and heavier than expected
rating 2.0. Not so good It gets hot very easily!

rating 1.0. 1 Star well deserved Seriously... been using since around February 2015, so 3 month of use so far... it has just stop working completely.Stopped sucking up, then turned off, and i can't turn it back on... IM SO disappointed. I don't know what the heck is wrong with it and why it would even do it after only 3 month of use.

Update : Found out it was the belt. Be prepared to replace and buy a bunch of replacement belt because they will break off a lot with this vacuum. I was mislead by the ratings, like usual. I really don't like this vacuum anymore. And it's a pain to replace the belt too.
