rating 2.0. short-term investment Worked really well for about 4 months. Then it died. Cleaning lady said this is the second one she's seen that did the same thing.

rating 1.0. It does not perform to the level that the non-Pro ... It does not perform to the level that the non-Pro Windtunnel pet vacuum does.  I have had it for 2 months and it already needs to be replaced.  I have been averaging about 2 years with the other model and I have not changed my cleaning habits.

rating 1.0. I really wanted to like this. Bought this over a year ago and it's just awful. It overheats often and shuts down. Every time I empty the bag-less compartment I have to use my hands. It's disgusting. Now there's a clog somewhere and we have taken the whole damn thing apart and can't figure out where it is. If you have pets, stay away. Looking for a replacement.

rating 4.0. Great vacuum for concrete floors, medium duty use, and a smaller space. Had this vacuum for about a month, and it's doing great. We just moved into a loft with finished concrete floors and needed a good bare floor vacuum. After a lot of reading and researching, I decided on this one. We do not have pets, but this one was on sale, and cheaper than it's "non-pet" version. Therefore, I can't speak to it's ability to pick up pet hair. Although, I have found the attachment that has the smaller, suction powered roller very convenient for cleaning the furniture and pillows. You may consider it, too, even if you don't have pets.

It does really well on bare floors and area rugs. It was important to me to be able to control whether or not the brush was on (which this one allows) because if the brush can't turn off, sometimes it just flips the dirt and grit around. It is very lightweight, which wasn't an issue for us, but could definitely matter if you have to lug it up and down stairs or have mobility issues. I love having a filter that can be washed, and it even has a little indicator to tell you when it's getting dirty. The canister is easy to empty and clean, but it isn't super durable. Sometimes it requires a few light bangs with a hand to get all the lint and grit to fall into the trash can. If you bang too hard, you could crack the plastic. I didn't think the retractable cord would be that useful, but now I like it a lot.

A few things to point out:
1.) The hose is not long, and when you add the attachments, the suction makes the hose retract even more. If you're looking for a long hose with maneuverability, this may not be a good choice.
2.) The bristles and pick ups on both the main roller and the attachment roller are made of harder plastic--not plastic fibers. Be careful because turning these on when on the wrong types of floors could scratch them.
3.) The cord is not long. This isn't a problem for us because we have a small space, but it could be annoying if you are looking to not move the plug 15 times during vacuuming larger spaces.

Look for this on sale because you can get a good deal on it, which makes the price point and the features even out. Good for medium duty use in a smaller space.

rating 4.0. There is little dust generated and the dust container is easy to empty We purchased our Windtunnel 3 Pro Pet Bagless Upright Vacuum based on the reviews; specially cleaning hardwood floors. We concur with the reviews the Hoover Windtunnel 3 is powerful and safe for our hardwood floors. It has no problem picking up dirt and leaves that get tracked in. There is little dust generated and the dust container is easy to empty. The weight of the machine is not an issue ..consider it is a light workout. The vacuum rates 5 stars.

The short hose and attachments rate 3 stars. It is challenging to clean curtains, window ledges and doors without picking up the machine. Hoover should consider reengineering the length of the hose and attachments. Considering the options we are glad that we purchased the Windtunnel and recommend this vacuum to others looking to clean hardwoods.

rating 2.0. Limited and very poor attachments The pet attachments are not very rubberized.  The yellow strips on the pet hair attachment are almost hard like plastic so I'm not sure how well that really works.  There is no brush attachment and a brush is a must for any vacuum.  Purchasing a good separate brush attachment is difficult to find at Hoover because the hose on this model is not a standard diameter.  And the hose is also too short.  I bought two of these vacuums, they work fine, but attachments are very poor.  I don't see much advantage with this model for pet hair.

rating 4.0. We desperately needed a new vacuum cleaner that had great suction. We had borrowed a discontinued brand that ... We desperately needed a new vacuum cleaner that had great suction. We had borrowed a discontinued brand that was bagless and worked great from my parents. However they took it back. The Oreck vacuum we had from Costco and the Eureka commercial vacuum cleaner just couldn't cut it. The Eureka did have good suction, but horrible filtration which left my dust allergy-prone wife in fits. The Oreck couldn't suck a hair off a hard floor ...at least it sure seemed like it. Our house was being consumed by great dane hair, and we needed help.

I was looking hard at the Dyson V6 Pet vacuum, but I really couldn't justify the $500 price tag for less than 20 min runtime with 3.5 hour charge time. If it had double the run time, I might have snatched it up because it was so light weight and would replace all our vacuum cleaners.

I looked at the reviews on this Hoover model, and it looked promising and was on sale for $118. So I got this model, and so far I have been IMMENSELY pleased with it. I like how the attachments are well organized around the vacuum cleaner. The suction is absolutely fantastic, and we filled up the container immediately from our master bedroom which meant we were finally getting all that dust and pet hair up. We also have a entryway rug that the Oreck could never dent. This Hoover model tackled it like a pro, and it finally looks clean. I enjoy the fact that it can also clean our hardwood floors by being able to turn off the brush and lower the height. We are also getting a new area rug that must have the brush turned off as well for cleaning.

My only beef with this vacuum cleaner is that it weighs over 20 pounds, which means my wife in her condition can't lift it up to take it upstairs. I'm not sure my kids can either (maybe my oldest). My wife can however vacuum with it, once someone has carried it for her. I wish this vacuum was about 10-15 pounds lighter, so we can more easily use it upstairs where more carpet is located.

Overall, it's so nice to see our carpets and rugs clean again! I hope this model holds up well over time.

rating 5.0. Excellent for thick plush carpets, and pet hair on upholstery. We have 0.75" plush carpet. Our old Kenmore was unable to handle the carpet covered in pine needles after we took out our Christmas tree. I did a lot of research for vacuum cleaners that could handle thick pile plush carpet. We don't vacuum enough to warrant a $400 Dyson (or similar). This one was reasonably rated and priced, so I took a chance and ordered it with rush shipping. It was easy to assemble, and soon I was vacuuming up the pine needles. At first I didn't realize that the brush was set to off, and I thought it was a little hard to push, even though I had the height setting all the way up. Once I figured out the problem, it was easy to push and did a great job cleaning up the pine needles.

After finishing that, I tried out the pet upholstery tool, and it cleaned the cat fur right off the chair. The auto-rewind was a nice end to a pleasant and successful vacuuming experience. The canister was full of pine needles by this point; emptying it was simple, with no mess. All-in-all, I'm very pleased with this vacuum.

rating 4.0. while those who love it, swear by it So bought the Hoover WindTunnel 3 Pro Pet vacuum after reading too many reviews and taking too many polls. The Shark, all everyone does is compare it to the Dyson or with the response "...as good as the Dyson, but..." followed by either a love it or hate it. The Hoover got compared to both, costs as much as the Shark and generally reviewers have a more over-all sense of being at peace with it. "...It's not a Dyson or Shark but it's the best vacuum I've ever had..." After using the Hoover twice, all I can say is that it is absolutely disgusting how dirty my house is; and my dog should be as bald as I am with the amount of hair this thing picks up! rating 5.0. Works great years later - unlike FLIR ONE I purchased my SEEK Thermal camera through Amazon on November 19, 2015 and it still works fine.

I also purchased the FLIR ONE from Amazon September 22, 2016.
The FLIR ONE has a better interface/app and arguably better hardware. However it is not a reliable tool.
The FLIR ONE worked great for fall 2016 and winter 2017 and will no longer function, even with external power.
This makes the FLIR ONE the most expensive disposable camera ever.

Save your money and purchase the SEEK.

I have attached a photo shot with the SEEK checking the insulation I put up on our apartment terrace last night.

Given my experience with both IR cameras, I can easily endorse the SEEK to the Amazon community.

“Seek Thermal
Seek Thermal would like to communicate with the Seek Thermal, Inc. Thermal Camera”.

Yes, the company wanted to directly access my new camera, which apparently they consider to be still theirs. I tapped ‘ignore’ and hopefully that will take care of THAT little problem. rating 5.0. Read the specs online before you buy and you won't be surprised. Now I may be biased as I've been waiting for a price effective thermal camera for years.
But I don't need it enough to pay more than $300.

I searched the web thoroughly before I decided what to buy. I considered the Flir One and Therm-App and read all I could about them as well.
The first thing I noticed was that temperature range is way better on the Seek.
Thermal-App can't detect high and low temperatures (from 5°C to +90°C (41°F to +194°F)) so that limits the usefulness for me and the price is quite high (almost 6 times the Seek).
Flir doesn't go subzero at all (32 °F to 212 °F (0°C to 100°C))
So with (much) better range and a good price the choice was easy (I live way up north and intended to use it outside some of the time and it actually gets that cold here sometimes)

When I got the camera I liked the retail packaging. Feels as a good product even before you open it. (My seal was broken by Amazon for "inspection purposes" - I expect someone couldn't help themselves to try it out - I'm not complaining and I understand - hope you had fun).

The camera was much smaller than I had anticipated (I knew the specs but never thought of size as an issue). The carrying case was sturdy and watertight (I removed the camera and held the case under 2 feet of water for 45 minutes without any leaks - I floats by the way also with the camera inside).
The camera itself felt rough as well, nice magnesium body and very light.

There's no instructions what so ever other than to get the app and plug in the camera. The app has a reasonable size and I had downloaded it before I got the camera.

If you buy directly from Seek you will have to setup an account (I got cameras directly from Seek and from Amazon).
When you attach the camera you can setup another account (not the same as the one you setup for a buy).
And if you want to ask questions on their support site - you've guessed it - you have to setup a third account.
If you worry about privacy don't setup accounts just press an extra button when you start the app to accept the terms of use.

I use a wallet type case for my phone and had no problem fitting the Seek without the case getting in the way. It seated just fine. I realize that this may not be the case (no pun intended) with all case types but depends on design of your specific type of case.

First thing I realized using the camera was that 20° FOV (Field Of View) is very narrow.
I had to keep my distance from objects if I wanted an overview of things.
It was the cause of a little irritation to begin with but now I'm used to it and it works fine both indoors and outdoors.
It's not that I have to stand 20 feet away as other reviewers has claimed. I was able to take reasonable pictures in a small room 6 × 6 feet.

Next thing was the software. It's not intuitive at all. Often you have to look for the right button and while in changing settings you have to go through 2 steps to get back before you can take a picture or movie (not sound on the movies by the way - but you know that if you read the specs).
That is a constant source of irritation together with the fact that some controls switch places and gets mirrored when the camera (phone) tilts.
I hope an update will change that one day (updates can update your camera's firmware by the way).

The focus lens (not a zoom lens) works as expected. I've successfully focused on objects as close as 4 inches and as far as 1500 feet. On short ranges a picture can get very detailed because of the good resolution. On a distance it makes a difference as to how small and how far you can detect a heat source. Pictures from afar does not get sharp but you would know that if you researched just a little (you still "only" have 32,000 measuring points).

Focusing on most objects with just a little texture shows how accurate this camera is. It's possible to get very detailed pictures.
Most low resolution thermals produce blurry picture and Flir introduced the msx technology to outline the picture so you can see what it was you took a picture of, but in most cases the Seek pictures are very clear and objects are easily distinguished. Provided there's enough contrast in temperatures. Therm App (384 × 288 gives more than 110,000 temperature measurements in a picture) has a higher resolution than Seek but as mentioned the price is way up and temperature detection is much narrower than the Seek.

I bought an extension cable for my camera because I'm lazy and won't bother to get out of my car. I can hold the camera out the window and it works fine. It can be a little difficult to point but it does work. Only thing you have to take in to account is that the app orient itself according to the phones position and not the cameras. (You can take pictures that shows upside down on your computer, but even native programs on any Mac or pc can easily turn them).

I wrote to Seek regarding EXIF and thermal data in the pictures. There's no data stored with the pictures and what you see is what you get (other companies saves thermal data with pictures thus making it possible to process the pictures later on a computer - change the color scheme for instance).
Seek wrote to me that they might update firmware later to include this data witch would be a major improvement but not necessarily something all users need.

In the settings you can change what temperature scale to use (Fahrenheit, Celsius and Kelvin).
Aspect ratio can be set to 4:3 or 16:9. The first will utilize more of the thermal sensor and the latter fits better on modern screens (but be advised that what really happens is that the 4:3 picture is cut to fit)

It's possible to ad watermarks to your pictures. You'll find 3 options in the settings: "Date & Time", Location and Seek Logo. They can be chosen individually and the fact that you can turn off the Seek Logo is a plus (not possible on Flir cameras - don't know about Therm App)..
The date, time and location is taken from your phone so if the date or time is off so will the watermark be. If your location on the phone is turned off you won't be able to get a precise location on the picture (I don't know why I have to tell you this but apparently not all think about this)
The camera I heavily relying on the phones cpu and that's what keeps the price down.

The software allows you to change between 9 different color palettes: white, black, iron, cool, amber, indigo, tyrian, glory and envy.
They will each show temperatures in a mix of gradient and different colors.
I've found that the same motive can be more or less clear/obvious depending on the palette used.

it's possible to have the temperature shown on the picture either in spot mode (temperature measured and shown in the middle of the picture) and High/Low mode where highest and lowest temperature within the frame is shown.

There is a search mode where you can have the camera highlight temperatures higher, lower or exactly a given temperature.

In the thermal+ mode you can use the camera together with the phones camera and slide between the 2. If you double press on the normal picture you'll be able to zoom and pan the picture so it fits the thermal picture. With a little practice the 2 pictures can be lined up to almost perfection a feature that is very useful.
When you take a picture in this mode 2 pictures will be saved to the phone. An ordinary and a thermal. Within the app you can still slide between the two but that is not the case when you export the pictures or see them in your phones image viewer (goes without saying, but still saying it)
If you take the picture in one of the temperature modes the temperature will be shown on both pictures.

All in all I'm very satisfied with this camera.
I have both ios and android version and this review goes for both.
Only difference I've noted is that the ios version seems to fit firmer in the phone and can be set both ways in the phone.
For the android version, on a phone with the micro usb turned the "wrong" way a small cable works fine.
The narrow field of view can be irritating at first but when you get used to it it's not a problem.
I'd say that the fixed focus on the regular version would be a greater source of irritation to me.

Pros: price , temperature range (-40C to 330C), adjustable focus, size (smaller than expected), sturdy carrying case, build quality, app has many useful features, range of detection (from macro to long distance), ability to adjust normal and thermal picture to line up (thermal+ function)

Cons: flat photos (no exif or thermal data saved with the photos, thus no possibility of tweaking the photos on computer later), fov is very narrow, (20 degrees compared to regular versions 36), app is messy and not intuitive (some controls even move around on the screen when entering functions or tilting the unit),

NIUBEE 2nd Lightning Extender Dock For Lifeproof Otterbox Case, Male to Female Lightning Extension Charger Video Audio Adapter for iPhone 5/5s/5c/SE/6/6S/7 Plus/ iPad Air And More Connector (Black)
Sold by: Niu Bee

I have been using the Seek Thermal XR Imager with my iPad Mini 3. My eyes are opened again.

I am living in a 1450 square foot home, as it happens viewing the interior of the house with Seek, I find that the house leaks heat like a sieve.

Buy the Seek, be impressed.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Loads of Fun Experimenting When I read that the others had batteries that had to be charged after an hour's use, I knew the Seek would be better for me because it uses your phone's battery. I hiked for three hours and while it drained my iPhone 11's battery to about 30% by the end (I took a lot of photos), I was prepared with a portable charger just in case. It turns out I didn't need it.

I'm a herper (amateur herpetologist) and took the Seek into the desert to see if I could locate any snakes or lizards. They sometimes hide in crevices in between boulders in my area. I knew they did this because it holds heat. But until I took the Seek into the desert, I didn't realize just how much.

Posted are only two of many filters. You'll see I tested one of the filters on my desktop computer that was clearly heating up the door as it vented. One photo is of the living room window. You can clearly see which window had sun exposure that time of day. I was really quite surprised at how well the plant life holds the heat during the night. It was 46 degrees when I took the saguaro photo (not the crested saguaro). All of the shrubs around it were warm, as well.

Using a different filter, you can see where the crested saguaro held heat. The boulder photos were amazing to see. I knew they held heat. I could feel the temperature difference when I touched it, but to see it like this was very cool.

I can't wait to go back out and explore more!

I plugged the Seek into my phone and it immediately prompted me to download the software, so I did. I also watched the video Seek provided within the app. It was very helpful. I registered the product. It does ask for access to your photos, which is fine because I want the photos saved to my phone, which it does.

You can see from the photos that it's a very small device. It comes with a waterproof case and a key ring so you can attach the case to wherever you like. Ignore the hair tie that I tied to the key ring, I need get a carabiner for it. On a side note, it clicks at regular intervals as it's testing the temperature and setting the image, I presume. It didn't bother me at all, it's quiet.

I plan to experiment with the other filters. Well worth the money, in my opinion. It clearly has household uses, but for me, exploring nature with Seek is what's going to be fun. If you have kids, take them out with this and let them see nature in a way we don't normally get to see it. Science....let's find out!

rating 5.0. after FOLLOWING the instructions it works great  :P please READ THE INSTRUCTIONS!!!! Sorry for yelling caps but if you don't read and follow the instructions you will have a bad time with this product. AFTER following the instructions, I was in total amazement how well this product works. First find the stud beneath your floor (i was fortunate because my squeakiest floors have access and exposed joists from below), drill the pilot hole and use the adapter. The custom screw drives all the way flush to the adapter and literally snaps itself off. All that is left is a tiny 3/8" hole which can be easily filled but the squeak will be totally gone. Genius, i hope the creator of this product owns a private island and is sitting back sipping martinis. You might have to use more than 1 screw depending how bad the subfloor and joists are but you will get the hang of it. Good luck and hopefully no more floor squeaks

I triple checked to make sure I was hitting floor joists when screwing in. Especially after some negative reviews mentioned this didn't work for them. I had assumed that these people just didn't read the directions... Well 50 screws later in a 4ft by 8ft area and my floor squeaks as loud as ever. This kit did absolutely nothing to help with the squeaks. I can't return it either because all the screws are in the floor. Definitely disappointed with this product. rating 5.0. It Works! This product really does work. Once you’ve learned to use the joust locator, you’re home free. Only advice I’d offer is when you have a thick or very sturdy pad under the carpet. Using the alignment and depth control fixture might result in the screw from not penetrating the sub floor enough. Run a test in a relatively untrafficed area of your carpet. If the screw doesn’t go in far enough simply remove the alignment/depth fixture and use your drill to further drive it a bit more...I found that 1/4 inch  or so above the carpet seemed to be about right. One other thing... the alignment/depth fixture is plastic...while it is sufficient for the job of breaking off the screws, treat it with care.

rating 4.0. Works great if you can find the joists Had a very squeaky second floor hallway in a 30-yo house under thick carpet. So I bought one of those "deep scan" stud finders but that did not work. I resorted to drilling in 1" intervals over a 16" length with the joist-finder screw until I hit one and then ran a tape measure to mark out the rest. Fair warning--do NOT touch the stud-finder screw after drilling multiple holes as it will be hot enough to cause a blister if you touch it. While this may seem obvious, and indeed I had this thought as I was drilling multiple holes in my floor, I had a dumb moment and still went to touch it afterward and received a nasty little burn on my right forefinger and thumb. Totally my fault, but please take this into account.

After finding the joists, the largest squeak took 3 screws to silence and I sunk another two in every remaining joist in the hallway to completely silence it. The scored screws snapped off easily, and you can tell when they are deep enough because they will "cam out" when the bit reaches the bottom of the little three-pointed alignment jig. (This means you can drill these in and they will automatically stop once the bit meets the bottom of the jig.) Long term, I'd worry about the longevity of the square bit as camming out with any bit typically reduces its lifetime, but since you're probably only doing this a few times, it's probably ok. With any other driving bit, camming out is typically undesirable--with this kit though, this is definitely a feature. Just be forewarned, your bit won't last forever.

I'm super satisfied with this kit. It changed my upstairs hallway from seeming like a squeeky bounce-castle to silent, solid flooring. Now I can actually use the upstairs hallway while my wife is doing her online training, and we don't have to worry about waking my mother-in-law if we get up to use the bathroom in the middle of the night (and vice-verse).

Only reason for 4 stars and not 5 is because the things I mentioned here are things I had to learn on my own, and would have been nice to have been included in the manual or the video. It does work, but you have to learn it first.

Also, I have not tried this on hardwood although I intend to try, and I'll update this review when and if I decide to do so.

I wish I had searched for this solution sooner as we have struggled with squeaks on our second story forever, especially in our daughter's room. It wasn't until she just moved out that I decided I needed to do something and that's when I found this product. So here's the skinny ....

The kit works but it sure looks a lot easier on videos than when using it my first time. But after a while you'll get the hang of it. In short, you are given two joist finding bits and 50 break-away screws. The size and distance of your squeak will determine how man screws you have to use. I used about 15 on the biggest squeaks in our daughter's room, they were numerous and VERY loud.

Finding the joist is the biggest issue. I tried my stud find without much luck. I tried using a hammer to detect subtle differences in sound and sometimes that seemed to work 'okay' but not usually. Starting 9-10" away from a wall is usually a joist and finding that one helped immensely as most others should be 16" on center and that usually worked out well. Use the joist-finding bit to drill down 3" or so. If you find a joist you won't be able to pull the bit up at all or if you reverse your drill it will back out since it's in wood (the joist). If you can slide the bit up and down, you're through the sub floor and into space (not a joist).

After finding the joist, change your drill bit to the driver of the break-away screws, locate the squeak on that joist, put down the guide on the spot, lean into it to make sure a good deep connection is made and set the screw through the guide. Press firmly on your drill and drive the screw down until the guide stops you from drilling any more. At this point you can test to see if your squeak is gone. If so, use the side area on the guide tool to break away the screw top.

As I said before, you may need to use a lot of screws depending on the size of the squeak. If you have a large squeak and need to use multiple screws, I HIGHLY recommend leaving each screw exposed (don't break off) and go down the joist 3-5" and put in another one. Use a colored threat or twine to wrap each head as you drive it in to create a 'path' of sorts to help continue down the joist. It also helps to clearly see where your unbroken screws are. Let me me tell you, do NOT lose track of them because I brought me knee down on one and thought I broke my kneecap.

If you need to find a new joist, use your previous location +16" as a guide for where the next joist should be. I had problems finding consistent joists in the hallway and resorted to drilling hole after hole until I found the joists. The holes don't ruin your carpet as long as it's not berber. They have a special screw/procedure for berber.

One of my joist finding bits broke nearly the end, but they give you two thankfully. You might consider getting an extra 50 pack of screws. I went through more than expected but still have 15 left over from my 6 or so squeak areas.

It's not as easy as some make it look but you will get the hang of it and it's rewarding when done. It was more work than I expected and with one of the bits breaking I'm giving this four stars. I am going to purchase more screws for the future and would purchase this product again.

However, if people don't have easy access to view their sub-floor and joists, that's where I feel like having a top notch stud finder handy (I've seen some places say a $15-20 stud finder can detect floor joists easily) can make the world of difference between loving and hating this product.

rating 5.0. Worked so well I almost cried. TLDR: These work and are a life saver. As long as you follow directions and are careful where you drill.

My family moved recently into our first two story home. The only drawback to our dream home, was the extremely noisy second story floors. Upstairs we have 4 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms and a long hallway. No matter where you put your foot down the floors would squeak, yell, pop, groan and moan.

I didn’t want to to rip up carpet, and I didn’t want to have to deal with the subfloors. So after watching videos and reading reviews on this product, I decided to give it a try. After well over 500 screws later all I can say is this product WORKS!

Doing the bedrooms and hallway I felt was “safe”. Many other reviewers and “professionals” caution that you shouldn’t drill into your floors without being sure what’s underneath. This is why I decided to stay away from my bathrooms. The floors in the bedrooms and hallway I knew didn’t have any plumbing or wiring underneath.

Tips:
Read the directions!
Use the drill bits to find your studs.
Realize that you may have 2 subfloor boards meeting at the stud. So you might have to put a screw a little to the left and another a little to the right of the “center” of the stud.
Wear gloves!
Wear knee pads!
Get a magnetic bit holder for your drill! This will save you from constantly changing from drill bit to driver.

Cons:
More screws! More screws! More screws!
I found more of this screws at my local Home Depot, Menards and Lowes. (Yes I bought out everyone in my area)
Also both my stud finder drill bits broke. I think this was due to over heating going in and out of studs. They seemed to have “melted”. Luckily I found another of these kits at Lowes. However, I found the two bits in the pack from Lowes to not be exactly the same as the one I purchased from Amazon. One of the bits from Lowes was snagging and grabbing my carpet. Both of the Amazon bits did not do that.

Summary:
We lived 2 months with floors that drove us crazy. I spent 3 days of work putting well over 500 of these screws in my floor. I had no issue with the screws, either driving them into the floor or snapping the heads off. I have no holes or markings in my carpet. And now my floors are so quiet it makes me want to cry. Good luck and thanks for reading!

rating 5.0. Absolute miracle product I have never written a product review in my life but I felt compelled to for this product (I hate how fake that sounds but may the internet gods strike me down if I am lying, it is the truth). I am a light sleeper with an often-varying sleep schedule due to often-varying work hours, and I moved into a townhouse with a disturbingly loud and squeaky floor upstairs. This made it next to impossible for my fiancé to enter or move around the bedroom while I was sleeping without waking me up. After some online research we found this product.

I have very few handyman or home-improvement skills. Fortunately I have a close family member who does, and with just a little guidance from him and the lending of his power drill, I was able to master the use of this product. Just from following the instructions I would have probably been able to figure it out on my own.

The most difficult part - and based on some other review, the main source of other users' frustration - is locating the floor joists. This is an area where just a little bit of basic knowledge on home construction can go a long way. Knowing that in most homes joists are placed 16 inches apart, and that floor AC vents are usually located alongside a joist, can make a huge difference in narrowing down the search (I would not have known that, but fortunately I had some guidance). Also just taking a patient and methodical approach to locating the joists and not drilling a random constellation into the carpet helps a lot. I would recommend starting with an educated guess to where a joist might be based on factors in the room, and if wrong moving 1-2 inches laterally and trying again (and repeat in a straight line) until you hit one. Once you locate a joist, mark it with painter's tape to remember where it is, and measure 16 inches away while remaining parallel to the wall to find the next one.

Also worth noting, the breakaway screws can be a little difficult to break away with the tool included, but a set of pliers works just fine. Once broken away you'd never know they were there.

I have now put all 50 included screws into my floor, and my fiancé and I are stealthy ninjas when entering or exiting the bedroom. This is an absolute miracle to me after living with the squeaky floors for so long. I have already ordered a second set so I can start on the guest room (which is not as squeaky but might as well get it done) before I have to return the drill ;).

Great product, well designed, easy to use once you get the hang of it. Maybe it doesn't work for everyone but it did wonders for me. If the screws start popping out in a month I'll change this review, but so far the future looks bright :)

rating 5.0. These magnets demand respect!! I underestimated the strength these magnets have. One already chipped a corner from the sheer strength but that was completely my fault because the warning disclaimer made it obvious to avoid direct contact. I’ll be using these magnets as a tool to help locate floor joists for nailing squeaky hardwood floors (one person underneath the house and one on top nailing them down) because using a measuring tape is a hassle. I decided to wrap them in a Monkey Fist knot using 1/8” Nylon Rope to avoid another accident, so they lost a little strength but can still pull each other easily through 3/4” wood and max’s out around 1-1/2” separation. Great buy!

input: rating 5.0. FAST DELIVERY... I am stunned at how quickly I received this product! I have yet to use the actually product because the items I bought to apply it to have yet to arrive. I bought all products in the same 30 mins period, and the shipment was just so speedy on this it will be two more days before I get the products to apply it to! Thanks for the speedy service!

rating 4.0. Blinding white light! Ok, hopefully I will never know if this product helps me avoid being hit by a bloody cager, but I can say that I went home and tested it in the dark.  It's really cool that it is as black as black can be in regular light, then in the dark you shine some light on it (or flash camera you can see really good then) and it is an almost blinding white.  Cool stuff.

rating 5.0. Fantastic product; a lifesaver This is an outstanding product!  Works best on a dark fabric, as in daylight it basically appears black, but that would be a matter of taste.  But at night, when hit by light, it's like it has its own batteries!  The photo really gives you a good idea of its appearance in a headlight beam.  Very lightweight & flexible; sews on very easily.  I bought it for my teenage daughter's "messenger bag."  She didn't want a backpack with already sewn-on reflectors (e.g. LL Bean), and coats with reflective patches were out ("not cool," but I also wanted something she would use every day - well, she has to take her books, but doesn't always need a jacket).  I needed something that would blend in with the bag & strap material (teens are so conscious of appearance!) and not "stand out" when it wasn't needed, yet blaze out for safety.  We also wanted something "passive" that she wouldn't forget when running out of the house to catch the school bus or have to turn on.  This is PERFECT.  She's happy, we're happy; she's safe.  Outstanding product.  There's also enough that when this bag wears out, we have plenty more to do another bag (or two).  And, to top it off, the merchant was excellent.  Received it even faster than estimated, as advertised, well packed and in perfect condition.  If I needed more, I'd buy again in a second!

rating 5.0. Great stuff! I moved to Boston and now I can walk everywhere instead of driving. The downside is sometimes the cars don't see pedestrians until the very last moment. There's another review on here from a mother who sewed this tape on her daughter's messenger bag. That convinced me to give it a try. There's quite a lot of tape included in the package. I only used one strip for my bag, and I think the rest could easily be enough for 4 or 5 more items.

Sewing it was relatively easy. I don't have a sewing machine, so I did it by hand with a regular needle and thread. I put it all along the center of the cross-body strap. The strap is made of black seatbelt material, and the tape blends in with it perfectly during the daytime. This is a great solution for being visible. I can wear different coats, but I always have the same bag, so there's no additional vest or wrist strips or ankle strips to remember.

rating 5.0. bright the reflective cloth works great! comes in different widths so you have the ability to customize and not waste too much product. The strips light up so brightly when headlights hit it. A must have for night riding!

rating 4.0. Sew-on Reflective Cloth I've uploaded some pics so you can decide for yourself. I always hate hearing about how wonderful or awful a product is but the passionate reviewer couldn't simply upload a picture.
Anyway I did expect something a little more revolutionary, like solid deep opaque black that magically became blindingly reflective with any light source in a dark environment. Not exactly the case here. BUT, what I will say is that it's a good price point for the product and since nobody offers anything similar, really, nobody this is the best you'll get.
I guess from the description of sew-on tape I also imagined it would be a little more polished off(seamed/reinforced) edges, but that wasn't stated anywhere so that falls on me.
From the pictures uploaded you can see what it looks like out of the box and what it looks like in a darkened room with no light source and then with a flashlight directed at it from about 4 feet. Is it reflective, yes! As reflective as the plastic silver reflective tape with metallic flake, or as much as some name brands sports gear, not the kind I own. But again the whole appeal of this is to appear black and subtle during the day and give off visible reflectivity at night when headlights are present, and it does that. The market price for similar products is ridiculous so, short of buying reflective clothing, safety apparel, this is the best you're going to get at a good reasonable price.
I have not tested this through and number of washing yet so I will adjust/update if needed later.

rating 5.0. Exactly as expected This stuff is just like it's described. In normal daylight it looks black, but shine a light on it at night (or take a picture using the flash) and it lights right up.  I sewed it onto my black bike jacket and nobody could even tell it was there during the day, which is what I wanted.  But it really helped with visibility at night.

rating 5.0. Wow!!! This is honestly the brightest reflective cloth that I have ever seen.  It reflects just as advertised.  I will update after getting this product sewn onto my jackets and putting it through some use.
rating 4.0. Great product This is a grat product is you want to shut the military saftey nutjobs up.  Can't tell much durring the day but helps meet the military requirement to ride on base at night.

rating 5.0. Perfect for home made bicycle raincoat. This stuff is perfect if you live in the Great Pacific Northwest, are a bicycle commuter and don't want to look like one of those twerpicle yuppy bicyclists in flaming yellow.

What I did was buy a [[ASIN:B000QC8R2A Red Ledge Thunderlight Rain Jacket]] in black and some [[ASIN:B00063X38M 3M 08008 Black Super Weatherstrip Adhesive Tube - 5 oz.]] then I just cut and glued this tape to the jacket.

Works great. The only reason I would have given it a 4.5 instead of a 5 is I don't think it is quite as reflective as the 3M stuff but it works even better for my use.

For other little tidbits on biking especially in the Great Pacific Northwest see my list: http://www.amazon.com/lm/R399BQ6DGKYO4I/ref=cm_pdp_lm_title_1

rating 5.0. This stuff is amazing I had this sewn onto my club vest (black leather) and it blends in amazing during the day.  At night, you can literally see me from a mile away in almost all directions (I had it put in several places).  Visibility is the reason we're not usually seen and get hit -- this is one great way to help with it.  The picture of it "glowing" in light from another source really doesn't do it justice.... this stuff is BRIGHT when a light hits it.

rating 5.0. i used these in the army I hate those damn orange and those yellow vests the units try to force you to buy from the p.x. so I being good at adapting and over coming so bought this and a black leather vest from leatherup.com cut the strips to what I wanted ( one down the middle of the back and one across and one diagonal in each direction the put a big army seal right in the middle over the tapes) and took them to alterations with all the patches I wanted on it only got asked once if I hade a vest for at night but when I told the guard to stand ten feet behind the bike and shine his light on me he blinded him self so I will tell every one I meet its great and they should get it for them selves

rating 5.0. Very reflective and easy to sew I bought these to sew on to the outside of my black backpack. There were plenty of strips included and they are very reflective. Easy to cut and sew as well. They seem less messy then stick-on reflective tape. I would recommend!

rating 5.0. It Works! I am very pleased with this products. I had a friend sew it on the back of my black mesh jacket and it makes me shine at night with no unsightly stuff during the day.  I only wish I could find it in sheets rather than strips so we could create some neat designs.

rating 5.0. works well I placed this on my jeans jacket in small strips and across my black leather vest.  It shows up well at a great distance. I feel safer for using it.
rating 4.0. Its ok not the best I recomend the iron on stuff. this is ok but who likes to meticulously sew stuff on? not to mention each hole makes your jacket less resistant to tear.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. If you want to be seen at night, buy this! This reflective is SO bright. I was a bit skeptical when I pulled it out of the box, but then I put it in front of a headlight. WOW! It is not actually tape; it needs to be sewn on. It is strips of reflective material. I've hand sewn it, used a sewing machine on it and used material glue. All of the gear that I have used this on has held up just fine. It is not a deep black, it is subtly lighter than that, but that is because of the reflective properties. I've sewn it onto riding jackets and bags, both black. While you can see the strip, it basically looks like it's supposed to be there. And, it is a very non-invasive way (the complete opposite of a high viz vest, but just as noticeable) to add a huge margin of safety when riding at night. When I run out, I will definitely buy more (there is a TON of this stuff in the package!). I couldn't be happier with this stuff.

input: rating 1.0. Sprayers DO NOT SPRAY! Seemingly good product; two study bottles for a great price.  Until you realize they DO NOT SPRAY!  And yes, before you ask, I DID remove the plastic cap from the tubing, and yes I DID firmly attach the tubing to the spray bottle, and yes, I DID widely open the aperture to allow water to flow through.  Problem is, it just DOESN'T.  Filled a small tea cup of water and sprayed my kitchen sink so I could SEE what was happening . . . the water would not travel up through the tubing more than about 2 inches.  Total waste of money, regardless how inexpensive they are!

rating 5.0. Steel cord belting as it should be Very stiff belts, greatly improved 3D Print quality (less belt stretching during high accelerations). Fast shipping, modules work well .. and the PCB boards appear to be robust, in my prototyping, had to do some rework of pin in hole solder joints, .. no problem, no plated through hole pullouts of lands ripping off these boards, they are well made!

rating 1.0. Poor quality / Poor design Zero Stars - horrible burrs on die cut edges of male terminal blades (see my photo !!!) ... nearly impossible to fully mate, and certainly damages the mating connector .. had to file off those burrs to get male blade thickness down to 1mm standard thickness. Note, the burr side of these blades is the contact side that mates to the female connector contact (wire terminal side of female contact system) .. it's insult added to injury, a very poor product design and very poor quality control. For the low price, I didn't expect the best materials, but did expect a functional product .. AVOID this one.

After sanding parts, staining and then Shellac ... looked very much like wood. Warning:  the material does not completely soak up stain, the stain residue will prevent polyurethane from curing, so use Shellac instead, it will harden.

rating 5.0. Great for RepRap 3D printer Great for RepRap 3D printer, especially for higher power heatbeds ... was having a problem overheating and burning up RAMPS 1.4 boards, this relay removes that high current from the RAMPS board. I have run a 100+ hours of printing so far, works great, no issues. The wiring diagram here is a tad confusing .. this part is a high current switch, there are 4 terminals, you probably will not need to use 2 of them ... run power supply leads directly to your heatbed, on one of those wires, insert this relay "switch". The red/black relay wires go to the RAMPS 1.4 board where the heatbed wires formally connected.

input: rating 1.0. Horrendus Durability As someone who works in security, I can honestly say these gloves have some of the worst durability I've ever seen in a pair of nitrile medical gloves. It seems like 4 out of 5 gloves I put on wind up tearing at some point from simply moving my hands. I'd recommend getting a different brand especially if you are in Law Enforcement or in Emergency Services, unless you want to contract a disease from having fluids leak through the rips of your gloves.

rating 5.0. No Glove, No Love I used to work at a hair salon for almost 10 years, and we have gone through countless brands of gloves: latex, vinyl, etc - you name them, we probably used them.
Having to work on clients for hours on end, we needed gloves that could be tough but also wouldn’t leave our hands feeling like they’ve been suffocating.
One day I was in charge of ordering supplies for our 10+ locations across the country. Suffice to say, I received great feedback from all the locations. We had found our new gloves!
We’d order the big bulk box, of course. But I also found myself purchasing a single box item for my home use.
Useful for cleaning, cooking, painting, mechanical jobs, etc.

input:
rating 5.0. Just the fan sound remains, amazing! Upgrade was not hard. Took about an hour, half of it removing my existing printer mods, OctoPi, camera, etc., labeling some of the cables with a marker and taking plenty of photos along the way, just in case. I'd say much easier than the initial printer assembly.

I cannot believe how much smoother and quieter the steppers are on the new board. I was going to say whisper quiet, but I can hear a whisper. The only sound now is the fan.

Took noise measurements during print before (48db) and after (36db) to back my impressions up.

Besides the TMC driver upgrade, the new board comes with Marlin 1.1.8, includes thermal runaway protection and bootloader for easy firmware upgrades. I upgraded firmware on my previous 1.1.4 board (which might affect warranty), but see no reason to mess with the new one for quite a while.

I purchased my printer from Comgrow as well and have had a great experience with them. Including customer support interactions during my first 2 weeks of learning - they were always responsive and more than accommodating. Big thumbs up!

rating 5.0. Comgrow Creality 3D 1.1.5 Upgrade Mute Silent Mainboard I had no idea that you could make a printer so quiet. After installing the board and powering on, the first thing I did was auto home for an initial test and I knew this was supposed to be a silent board but for a second there was a weird disconnect between what my brain was expecting to hear and what I was seeing. I'm so used to the motor whine and there is absolutely no noise. It's ridiculously quiet.

Initial board inspection showed no issues with quality control. Connections are lebled. Just swapped everything over, powered on and good to go. I did have to calibrate the esteps but I just took note of my previous boards config, extruded 100m to confirm it was underextruding and set it to what the original board was and it was good.

Very pleased with this upgrade.

Update- Firmware 1.1.8 is installed and I have confirmed that thermal runaway protection is enabled as advertised.

rating 5.0. Great seller Excellent customer service. When I installled it on Ender 3 pro, I was hesitant that the LCD screen said Ender 3 only and did not show the pro, but the seller Cathy assured me that it’s the same software 1.1.8 for both printers. Very satisfied with the seller.

rating 5.0. This is the QUIET version Just installed the board and started a 20 hour print.  Unbelievable the difference.  Basically all I can hear is the fans.  I used to always know when a print stopped because I could hear it two rooms away.  Now, you have to visually look at it to know when it is done.  Worked perfect from the second I installed.

rating 5.0. Silent Printing It's amazing how quiet this board makes my printer. I had expected some noise reduction, but nothing like this. I usually sit with my back to the printer and now I feel I need to turn around every once in a while to assure myself that it's still printing. All I can hear is the fans.

It took about an hour to replace the old board with the new one. It would have probably gone quicker, but the old board's connections were hot glued in place and I had to break them loose. I plugged it back in, started it up, and it ran without a hitch. I think the display even looks a little clearer now - like they used a cleaner font.

I think it was a good investment for me because my printer is in my home office so reducing the noise made it a better environment for me.

rating 5.0. Easy to install, works like  a charm! It took me about an hour to remove the old board and swap out to this new one. It worked right away without any issues. I would highly recommend this board if you want to upgrade your Ender.
rating 5.0. Super quiet! When I first started printing after upgrading to this board, I had to check to make sure things were working properly because it was so quiet.  Works great!

rating 5.0. Must Have! The constant high pitch sound that comes from the stock stepper motors started to drive me insane after a few months. This board is an easy drop into place (about 45-60min install due to the insane amount of glue that comes on the stock board) and the TMC 2208 stepper drivers are silent. The fans are now the loudest thing on the machine and totally tolerable. Must have!
rating 5.0. Makes you printer completely silent!!! OMG!!!! I cannot believe how quiet my ender 3 is now!! You can only hear the part fan!!!

rating 2.0. No bootloader installed, in spite of Creality's claims. Arduino required to flash bootloader. Despite claims by Creality, mine arrived with no bootloader installed. Arduino required to flash bootloader.

Mine had no bootloader installed, and secondarily went into a boot-loop after 5-10 minutes of being powered up. I have since purchased another from a different vendor. It also had no bootloader installed but otherwise worked fine. Regarding the boot loop, I think I just got a random bad board when I purchased from Amazon. Probably a tiny problem that slipped past QC since it would only happen when the board got warmed up. No one's fault, just the way things go occasionally. Once I used a Arduino board to load a bootloader, I was able to flash firmware updates by USB, verify thermal runaway protection, use Octoprint, etc. For use on my Ender-5.

rating 1.0. bad product, bad support This was my second board from these people, i returned the first one because it would immediately stop extruding. I sent it back and got a replacement, when the second one did the same thing I emailed the company for tech support, their suggestion was to try a different SD card...  After some internet sleuthing i found out there was a conflict in the firmware with S3D. Ive been using Cura as a work around not being able to use my expensive slicer unless i flash the board with new firmware..  Then today on the very last day to send it back almost exactly 1 month after purchasing the board stopped working completely.  Very displeased.  I didnt even get a thank you from the company when i sent in the fix to the original Extrusion issue..  I ordered another board from Creality..  0 for 2 Comgrow.. ill be avoiding your products from now on and passing it on to my printing groups.

rating 1.0. Easy to Install - but doesn't work Installed wire for wire so it was pretty easy.
Unfortunately, it freaks out when I plug it into my computer and when I insert an SD card, it doesn't register it.

rating 5.0. Smooth an Easy to install and a must have. The wisper board is a great mix for the ender 3 hands down.

Now mix that with some TH3D software and firmware plus all the upgrades they have makes it 100 times better.

The only down fall with this board is the SD card slot broke in under 30 days.

But comgrow was very helpful with troubleshooting and emailing me directly to make things right with a replacement board.
Want to say thank you specially to Cathy for the assistance she provided.

Thats is, but amazon has sent me out another one to replace it. So everything is good.

Id say the wisper board does get a bit warm while using. Also the motors seemed like they got more warm but even after 16 hour prints they worked fine and heat was the same as a 1 hour print.

Starting with this board is good. It gives you a chance to learn. But then stepping up to TH3D upgrades is honest the direction my ender 3 will have more on.

No I dont work for creality nor TH3D my opinion is mine and wasnt paid or compensated for it in any way.

rating 3.0. Quiet, but skips steps - Trinamic drivers in Legacy, not UART mode The board was a drop-in replacement and went in without too much hassle. Also, it’s miraculously quiet compared to the stock board. The problem with the 1.1.5, though, is how the Trinamic drivers are connected. They’re in legacy mode, not UART. By default, they run in StealthChop mode, which really is quiet. But it’s also lower torque than the original drivers, so sometimes it skips steps. Out of the box, my 1.1.5 board was skipping steps on the Z axis and gave me a few weird layers on a small print. I think it was also skipping on the extruder. When the drivers are wired in UART, you can selectively switch them into SpreadCycle (louder, but better torque) and also tweak the drive current through the controls. But on the 1.1.5, they can’t be switched to SpreadCycle. Drive current can be adjusted, but this is manual on the board, not in software.

I returned the board. I probably could have adjusted the current higher to help with the Z and extruder skipping, but without the ability to switch out of StealthChop mode, it’s not a robust solution. I ended up getting an EZboard Lite from TH3D, and the thing is awesome - quiet, reliable, full support of the Trinamic features, easy firmware upgrades, and good general support. You get what you pay for.

The 1.1.5 is quiet, but with the way they connect the Trinamic drivers, they leave out a lot of the benefits I thought I was getting.
rating 1.0. Defective board Only worked for about 5 min and the magic smoke was released. Am reordering from another vendor

rating 3.0. Quieter at first but gets louder over time.. Go with another seller instead. I've purchased 2 of these boards, one from this seller and the same board from another. When I first installed it it was completely quiet just like my other motherboard upgrade. But now that I've had this board from this seller for about 2 months it's noticeably louder. For comparison, my other Ender 3 with the motherboard upgrade from another seller has been in use for 6 months and it's as quiet as it was day 1 of the upgrade.. I've run both printers at the same time printing the same file and this board is CLEARLY louder and whiner. It's noise level now is halfway between the stock board and upgraded motherboard(from another seller).  ANOTHER ISSUE is when I first installed this board, the LCD screen on my Ender 3 wouldn't work. I had to unplug the screen and reset the printer 3 times before it began working properly. Do yourself a favor and just purchase from another seller.

rating 2.0. It's quiet, but useless once the extruder stops working. Installed this board on an Ender 5, worked great for about three benchies. I was impressed by how much quieter it was compared to the stock board. In the middle of my fourth print, the extruder stopped working. Stepper motor and cabling tested out okay, leaving the board as the culprit. Disappointing to say the least. I'll replace with something else.

rating 4.0. AUGUST 2020 READ BEFORE PURCHASE The product that I received was NOT the v1.1.5 board, it was the "newer" v4.2.7 board that Creality is sending out instead of the v1.1.5 boards. Problem with this board is that it isn't set up out of the box to work with the Ender 3, and requires a different firmware to be flashed despite the description saying it should be plug and play with the Ender 3 specifically, which is the whole reason I bought it. It's easy enough to fix, but right now there is apparently questionable support for add-ons such as the BLTouch, so keep this in mind when purchasing. I have reached out to Creality directly for support with this, and will update if they're able to provide any insight.
rating 1.0. Did not have 1.1.8 Installed My board did not come with 1.1.8 installed but instead 1.0.1, will be returning.

rating 3.0. NOT A 1.1.5 BOARD! 32-Bit Boards are nowhere near the same thing. NOT A 1.1.5 BOARD!

I'm not sure why Creality decided to call this a 1.1.5 board. The 1.1.5 is 8-bit and uses the Arduino IDE for firmware flashing. The 4.2.7 that I received is a 32-bit board, which demands an entirely different firmware and needs a completely different environment for flashing.

At the moment, Crealitys own 32-bit firmware doesn't support BLTouch or inductive sensors, and the source code is nowhere to be found, meaning you're SOL if you've upgraded your printer with one of them. Vanilla Marlin 2.0 may work (I haven't tried, mainly because I'm on TH3D EZABL bed leveling).

3 stars because I appreciate the 32 bit upgrade. All boards are going to be this way in the future as the tech continues to evolve. I'm sure the board will be great when programming catches up. However, they SHOULD NOT be calling this a 1.1.5 board. They are not the same thing and can't even use the same firmware.

rating 1.0. Not a drop-in replacement for any printer Not a drop-in replacement for any printer due to EEPROM is disabled in the firmware.  Therefore you loose your setting when you turn off the power.

rating 1.0. I don't recommend for ender 3 After install and flashing the firmware (this is not plug and play for ender 3 if you have bed levelling) I am experiencing missed steps on the y axis and consistent failed prints. I cannot recommend this motherboard as a result.

rating 1.0. Bad quality control, wouldn’t recommend this board until they fix that. My product caught on fire a week into using it, everything was wired correctly and working, i own multiple printers and have had these boards replaced in half of them so now it draws for a big concern, quality control is clearly shotty so i can’t recommend, they work great when they’re not catching fire, i understand  this is always a possibility but a week in is just insane to me, hopefully i can get a return as it’s been close to month and i no longer have the box for it, but it also destroyed my BL touch in the process, so i got a bitter taste in my mouth, i really hope you guys can ensure these products wont do this consistently to others, thankfully i was home at the time and caught it pretty early in.

rating 5.0. I can print in my WFH office! I installed this for two reasons. 1) To update my ender 3s firmware and stop getting the fire hazard warning from octoprint 2) so my 3d printer wouldnt embarrass me on work calls with its serenading. The noise reduction is unbelievable, I have to check to make sure it started up or is still going. Install was easy with a yt video and flashing the firmware was too. Pleased with my purchase.

I ordered this 4.2.7 board specifically because the pictures show the RET6 cpu. The 4.2.7 board that I received today has the 256k RCT6 cpu, not the 512k RET6 cpu. Be careful and make sure you check out your parts.

rating 5.0. Epoxy Glue I've had three ceramic pieces from a grandmother that were broken by a house sitter's young daughter.  I was very disappointed to say the least.  I looked for an adhesive that would work to repair them.  Epoxy Glue has done the trick. I've got it all back together and since the Epoxy Glue is transparent and didn't bleed out of the seams, it looks almost new.

rating 3.0. Great stuff Works just as advertised.. I repaired a souvenir mug someone got me.. The handle broke into 3 pieces.. I got it all together.. Held for 1 min..

Because I was a bit messy, the handle is 'rough to the touch' I don't want to use any solvent in order to remove. I am afraid I could weaken the bond..

I didn't have an issue with clean up. But I will wear plastic gloves next time while using this. And I will watch the videos available online demonstrating this product.

UPDATE: 2 months later. The excess adhesive turned rust colored. I gently scrubbed off excess. I was left with some reddish cracks.

My mug held together for a few months.
The adhesive did NOT hold
Up to microwave use.

My take always are.
This product is not useful to repair a mug for kitchen use. I don’t know if the problem was dishwasher and microwave.
My feeling is it doesn’t like the microwave.

I will only use this product on ornamental items in the future.
Then again, superglue and other cheaper adhesives will also work just as well maybe?

input:
rating 5.0. Worth Every Penny I enlisted my friend to move a washer & dryer set, turns out the washer was VERY heavy. The job was so easy with these straps we didn't even take the time to load it into the pickup truck, it was easier to just carry it down the street the approx 8 houses down to its new home.
Cost - 5 Stars well worth it ~$40.00.
Ease of use - 5 Stars, it took about 2 mins to figure it out (Man style, we didn't read the directions)
Safe - 5 Stars at all times I felt we had the load under control, even moving down the stairs.
Overall 5 Stars, Before owning this product I worked way too hard moving stuff.

rating 3.0. Definitely helped to move larger items My husband purchased these when we moved our oldest daughter to her new apartment. She lives on the second floor with no elevator. She moved several hours away and it was just the three of us moving her stuff. The straps made it easier for my husband and I to move the heavier items like the queen bed, couch, and plasma tv up the stairs since I didn't have to hold everything with just my hands. So, they get three stars for that alone.

However, it would have been much easier if the straps came together in the back somehow for a more secure fit. I'm not real big and the straps kept slipping off my shoulders. I had to keep hunching my shoulders up to keep the straps in place. My husband had no such problem and so found this quite amusing. My daughter is shorter and smaller than I am and the straps wouldn't stay up on her at all.

Some people might think that the straps lighten the load somehow since the weight of the object being moved is on the moving strap and not actually in your arms. This is not true--if you can't lift the item without the straps you won't be able to lift it with them either! I'm just throwing that out there for anyone who might be misled about what the straps actually do. You must still use your muscles (legs especially) to move anything.

So, all in all, these would be great for two similar sized burly men to lift and move things all day long. They worked for what we needed them for but we won't be using them again any time soon!

rating 1.0. DOES NOT WORK. DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY...... DOES NOT WORK. DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY.........

rating 5.0. Makes moving things so easy! I bought these before I moved my family out of state. Worked absolutely great! Almost flawless! Easily moved washer and dryer up and down flights of stairs! Couches, dressers, mattresses, etc, there was practically nothing that challenged these straps (except the piano, don't even think about it). Theses put all the weight on your back, instead of using the strength of your arms to manipulate heavy furniture.

My advice: when sinching up the strap, squat as low as possible.

I wish it included some straps to around the sides of whatever you are moving to aid in keeping it upright and allow you to use your arms for easier support, but otherwise these are awesome!

rating 5.0. THE SHOULDER DOLLY IS AMAZING! I ordered the Shoulder Dolly as we were getting new hardwood floors put in.  My husband wanted to hire some kids to move the furniture but I'm a little picky so I decided to give this product a try.  I was AMAZED at how easy it was to move the furniture including tall dresser drawers, hutches, armoires and even a king mattress was a breeze!!!!  I didn't even flinch when it was time to move the furniture back in since I knew it was going to be easy peasy.  If you need to move big pieces of furniture around do not hesitate to purchase this item - the price is a steal at what you can do with it!

Cost - 5 Stars well worth it ~$40.00.
Ease of use - 5 Stars, it took about 2 mins to figure it out (Man style, we didn't read the directions)
Safe - 5 Stars at all times I felt we had the load under control, even moving down the stairs.

rating 5.0. Why didn't someone think of this sooner?? These things are amazing!  The appliance delivery guys from Lowe's used them and we just had to have them.  There is a bit of a learning curve so be sure to "practice" on something smaller before tackling a big ol bookcase.  Be sure to read the instructions thoroughly to ensure you put everything together properly.  Hubby and I are 5'7" and 5'9" and in our 50's and we moved some serious furniture.  They won't make you superman, you will be limited by how much you can lift with your legs, but those big, bulky, impossible to hang on to items will simply walk down the hallway. Between these and the furniture "sliders" we can move just about anything on any surface (stairs are always a bit tricky though).

rating 4.0. A 6 hour move only took 1 I just recently moved and I was dreading it. After reading the reviews of the Shoulder Dolly I thought, for the price, it wouldn't hurt to try it. The first piece of furniture we moved was a large wooden entertainment center. It would have been awful trying to move by hand. No place to get a handhold, it's extremely heavy, and it's awkward size equal into a nightmare. So, we went for the shoulder dolly. The entertainment center was out of the house and into the moving truck in minutes. We are impressed. I have held back one star, though, because of the same complaint many others have had - it becomes uncomfortable after a while. The nylon straps on your shoulders really start to dig in and scratch at your neck after a few pieces of furniture. Someone suggested wearing a collared shirt, which I forgot to do, but that would certainly help. We were close to improvising some padding with some soft towels and duct tape, but we were pretty much done, so we just manned up and finished. Hopefully, a future version of this product will address that and be within the same price range as this one. I don't recommend buying the $100+ version if you'll only be using this intermittently. Despite that, I highly recommend this!
rating 1.0. Not easy to use To carry items on flat areas it ok but takes time to readjust to each item and useless on stairs or large inclines.

rating 2.0. Buy some where else. I did not get what I paid for. If you look at the picture. You will see two harnesses, and one strap. I did not receive the strap. Needless to say I would never do business with this company again.

rating 4.0. Able to move Oak furniture My husband and I moved our bedroom furniture in preparation for laying a new floor and although I am not looking forward to moving it all back into the room, the shoulder dolly made it possible for us to do this on our own. Both of us have back issues that require us to be careful. I have also always hated moving thing since I am short and this seems a problem to others. The shoulder dolly does not rely on arm length or power so difference in height is a non issue. The shoulder dolly allow a quick move of a number of heavy pieces without injury or disagreement. Amazing! Not suitable for all moves. Stairs would be an issue. It is also tempting to carry a heavier load than perhaps you should. Well worth the price for our needs.

rating 2.0. Not convinced... I was sorry to find out that these straps did not worked for me.  I tried to move a big sub-zero fridge and the straps did slide and we could not adjust them to stay in position.  I have the other, simpler system with two independent straps and it works better.

rating 5.0. Truck owners' necessity I own a pick-up truck and have helped many of my friends and family move a dozen times the pass 10 years. The last time I helped a friend, I was getting pretty tired lifting the large furniture and took most of my weekend moving furniture. It, also, took my two days to recover from the sore muscles. A friend of mine told me about this "miracle" Shoulder Dolly. Pretty skeptical, I decide to purchase this dolly. About one month later I put it to the test by helping my sister move.

This dolly is GREAT. The Shoulder Dolly instructions were pretty simply on how to put it on and how to use it. With this dolly, I was able to move lots of heavy of furniture in less than half a day (about 5 hours) and didn't have any sore muscles from moving.

I highly recommend this product and wish I had purchased this product when I purchased my pick-up truck.

rating 5.0. They make the job so much easier I ordered these to first move a water heater--which I haven't yet done, but in the meantime I helped my son move a new chest freezer from our van through his garage, up three steps into his house, and down steps to a landing with a square corner bend in the stairs and  into the basement with another bend at the bottom. It was so much easier with the Shoulder Dolly because a freezer isn't made with carrying handles and we didn't need handles using the Shoulder Dolly. When we got to the landing we had to stand the freezer on end to make the corner, and that was quite doable because of these straps. We talked about taking their refrigerator, full of food, out of the house and carry it around awhile just because we could, but his wife wasn't excited about that idea.

rating 5.0. Squat together now Impressive - I am prior military and done my fair share of moves.  This saved time, plenty of time.  Not just the move of items, but because you can use your shoulders and only need to balance with your arms, you can carry more weight.  More weight means less boxing.  For example - I didn't box up clothes out of any of the bedroom dressers, just grabbed my brother and the straps and they all went into the truck full.  Computer desk and drawers still full.  Side by side spare fridge in the garage still had 2 of my 5 gallon beer kegs mostly full and all my home-brew ingredients in it - loaded in the truck.  Small chest freezer half full - in the truck (added 20 lbs of dry ice once in for the two days it would be in the truck.  Saved time on unloaded, boxing or reloading and repacking on arrival at the new house.  Just took it out of the truck and all my stuff was there where it was before the move.  This will not help you moving something like my new 1400 lb gun safe though.

rating 4.0. Shoulder Dolly tips My wife and I recently completed three moves in three days (3-bedroom and 4-bedroom houses and a 2-bedroom upstairs apartment). In the past, we hired professional movers at great expense because of the large, heavy items, but we decided to buy the Shoulder Dolly and try moving ourselves. We moved sofas, mattresses, recliners, dressers, bookcases, tables, and appliances. Nothing was dropped or damaged. The Shoulder Dolly worked flawlessly. We were still tired and sore from moving heavy boxes, but we would've been much worse without the Shoulder Dolly.

Some things that we learned:

- Buy it early and practice lifting with your moving partner. We chose a small but heavy, metal-framed recliner and lifted it off the floor. This was a huge confidence booster and we quickly determined whether we could complete the move ourselves.

- Be sure to route the strap properly through the buckle. When you lift, the weight of the item should pull on the bar causing it to clamp tighter against the strap. If your strap is slipping and pulling through, it is not routed properly through the buckle, pulling at the wrong angle (adjust length).

- Wear a shirt with a collar. Flip the collar over the shoulder straps to prevent them from cutting into your neck. Otherwise, just make sure that the straps are properly spaced on your shoulders before you lift.

- When moving fine furniture, the metal buckles can scratch and dig into the wood finish. If possible, wrap the piece in a moving blanket. If a blanket is not available and there's excess strap, wrap the excess strap around the buckle to act as padding. It would be nice if the buckles were dipped in vinyl or covered in rubber.

- When moving items up/down stairs, have the person on the lower end take up more slack to raise the item higher. The person's face will likely be against the item, but it's a small sacrifice for an easier move. The person on the higher end needs to raise the item high enough to clear the top step. You might want to place a moving blanket on the steps in case you need to set the item down and readjust the straps, but be careful not to trip on it.

- Place moving blankets at the stair landings in case you need to set the item down, reorient the item, and/or readjust the straps. Get plenty of moving blankets if you have lots of fine furniture.

When we had our new appliances delivered, we noticed that the delivery people used the Forearm Forklift and it seemed to work well. It seemed a little quicker to set up because they didn't need to untwist the harnesses and figure out how to put it on.

With puffy sofas and odd-shaped recliners, we would choose the Shoulder Dolly again over the Forearm Forklift. There were many times that we had to pivot items to clear doorways and handrails. Our arms and hands were free to guide the items and to hold onto the stair handrails if needed.

These straps make moving EVERYTHING I have come across extremely efficient with much less effort. They came promptly and in great condition! Thanks for saving my back!

rating 5.0. Now I can help my husband move things I used to HATE it when my husband would ask "can you help me move this sectional" because I couldn't -- BUT he is strong so just didn't understand when I said I couldn't. He bought these to help move an outdoor shed. It did that fine (no, I didn't get roped into that one) and now we own them for smaller projects -- like the twice a year move of the sectional so we have room for the Christmas Tree. The sectional feels like it weighs 33% of what it weighed before when we didn't have these. We have used them for dozens of things since and we both agree this was a GREAT purchase.

And I really don't get the above complaint about the center strap being too long sometimes. You make the strap as long as you need it and then wrap the loose ends around at the end so it doesn't drag on the ground and trip you. If it is that complicated, use a binder clip to hold the folded strap together.

rating 5.0. I wish I bought these before the first time I ever moved Probably one of my favorite purchases. The strap that goes across (between the 2 people) is a bit long for some things but if you're clever, you can find a way around that. My best friend helped me move a couple times using these. He's also had experience with the forearm straps. He said the harness ones are much better and more comfortable. We had my bed broken down and wrapped and carried it down 3 flights of stairs. On the way, we waited for my neighbors to move their couch (without straps). We stood there without strain and held the bed perfectly fine. The straps are very durable and withstood more than a few pieces of metal furniture being loaded onto them.

rating 5.0. Excellent lifting system but be careful I think the shoulder dolly is one of the best things ever made for lifting heavy furniture and appliances, but you must be careful and not get too overconfident. My son and I moved a set of double wall ovens and a fully loaded 27 cu in. refrigerator without too much problem, but I came close to injuring my back on the first move because I didn't adjust the straps correctly which caused me to hunch over too much. Make sure you read the directions and give the center strap enough slack so that you can stand up straight and lean back just a bit while you are carrying the item. The two people in the Ad. are a good reference. If you notice, they are both leaning backward just a bit. If you squat too low (ie. center strap too short) you risk injuring your back as you stand up.

The product itself is a "must have" tho, as we could have never moved these things as safely as we did. Highly recommended!
