rating 5.0. Good Product This system really works in the way the description promises.  You won't be able to lift more weight than you could normally, but it does allow you to lift your maximum without stress.  If you have to negotiate steps you will still have the most weight load going to the person on the low end of the load. Well worth the price, it is well made and saved me chiropractor bills! rating 1.0. Could cause more injury I wanted to move an upright piano from one corner to another, at first I was looking at the forearm straps but the reviewers were recommending the shoulder straps. So I bought it after reading all the positive reviews. After trying it out here are the caveats I found:

1. Keep in mind The straps does not make your stronger, it will NOT help you lift something you were not able to lift originally.
2. The only thing the straps does is provide better leverage for thing you can already lift. Instead of holding the washer with your arms and fingers the weight will be transferred to your shoulders.
3. You will need to know the basic concept of lifting with your legs or else you will break your back.
4. Very important: You and your helper will need to have similar strength and be at similar height.
5. Very Important: You and your helper will need to synchronize perfectly when lifting a heavy item from the squat position, if your helper is slower or weaker, you could potentially get hurt or vice versa.
6. Lifting with the straps is a bit awkward. You are lifting solely on your leg strength with no help from your arms (arms are used for stabilization as seen in the images). After it is lifted all weight will be on your shoulders, which could be good or bad, I prefer sharing the weight on my biceps and shoulders rather than only shoulders due to a bad lower back.
7. In all cases of lifting heavy items, you could potentially pop your knees or hurt your back when lifting from the squat position if the item is out of your legs/arm strength capacity. In this case, with all positive user reviews, marketing images and messaging behind these straps, one might feel that it will make lifting heavy items much easier thus causing one to attempt lifting things out of their leg/arm/back strength capacity which will result in injury. Make sure you read #1 again and again before buying or using these straps.

Overall, the item can provide slightly better leverage for thing you can already lift, but if you can lift it already then you will not need to get these straps.

rating 2.0. Not good for use on stairs These worked well carrying things from room to room, but these did not work at all on the stairs. They were just too unstable and we couldn't get the furniture and washer/dryer raised up high enough to clear the steps, no matter how short we made the straps. And one of the guys who helped us move was 6'6 inches tall and the other was 6'2 !! We ended up just using our regular moving dolly.

rating 5.0. Exactly as advertised I must say everyone was skeptical of my purchase, but it truly works and very well.  You must make sure it is positioned properly on your body (crisscross should fall between your shoulder blades).  I have never moved a full size refrigerator with such ease.  We placed the strap under the fridge, strapped ourselves up, stood up and walked over 20 feet into the garage.  This is ideally suited for larger items.  We played around a bit with smaller items and they were actually a bit difficult to move.  Can't wait to move the rest of our stuff, especially the king size mattress.  Makes a large, bulky, heavy item feel like you are carrying a small toddler.

I anticipate this helping a great deal with other solid objects like our other dressers, couch, and desks. When used with large, solid objects, it works like a charm.

rating 5.0. very usable for guys I like this . it helps in places a dolly cannot and makes it possible for two people to get the job done. However women with larger upper body will have problems with being uncomfortable. If you are used to squats on a bar though it is probably less uncomfortable except I cannot say about boobs cause I am a man :)

rating 4.0. Great Product! Easy to use! Almost Perfect! I saw the shoulder dolly on the TV show Pitchmen and thought it looked like a good idea. My girlfriend had to move out of her 3rd story walk-up apartment and we were going to hire some help for the big stuff but I decided to give the shoulder dolly a shot. Needless to say, we moved everything by ourselves! In regards to stairs, the shoulder dolly wasn't perfect, but it definitely helped. It worked great on narrow, solid items, like the dresser, desk, and bookshelves, but not so much on oversized items like the couch. Problem with her set up was that the stairs were narrow and you had to go down 1/2 a flight, turn at the landing, and go down the other 1/2 and that was 1 story; we had that times three!
When we got to her storage unit, they did not have carts, so the shoulder dolly was truly AWESOME there! We had a wide open 100 yard walk from the truck to the unit and the shoulder dolly was perfect for this situation. It worked with everything, even the couch! I recommend this product, it may not be perfect for every situation, but it worked for me!
One other note, this is the shoulder dolly "lite" which I didn't realize till I got it. It is not the exact same unit from Pitchmen, but it was just what I needed.

rating 5.0. Already used once, and will be used many times to come Bought this in anticipation for a big move from an apartment to a house (with stairs).  Had the opportunity to try it out moving a family member into a new apartment.  Worked like a charm.  Latch is somewhat difficult to get at first, and instruction manual is lacking, but we figured it out.  Moved a few larger pieces of furniture (tallboy and queen size bed) with very little effort.

rating 5.0. Look ma, No HANDS! Best product i have bought to move large items with.  Puts all the weight on your shoulders and legs leaving your hands free to ballance the item and not wearing you out.  I used this to move my household goods and it worked flawlessly, with both another adult and my 13 year old son.  Makes heavy lifting a breeze.  Moved a 65" Rear Projection TV with it as well and it was easy.  Recommend this to any self mover.

rating 4.0. "saved the day" We moved on a hot August Las Vegas Saturday and my helpers in their twenties used the shoulder dolly system for the first time.  "That saved the day" was the common expression.  It takes a little getting used to, but is stable and allows moving big unwieldy items such as a large antique buffet stand better than any other technique I know of.  I first observed this system when a refrigerator was delivered to the house.  It isn't magic, you can't lift more than you could otherwise, and it's is hard to move things a long distance (shuffle, shuffle), but we were well pleased.  The quality of manufacture seems excellent.

rating 5.0. Shoulder Dolly is a "back saver" I used the Shoulder Dolly system to help my son and his family move.  At first there was a lot of resistance to the new gadget, but by the 3rd heavy walnut chest of drawers, it was get the "strappie thing" and let's move this beast.  A simple system that works extremely well.  Lift with your legs/thighs and carry the weight evenly across your shoulders and not your arms and biceps.  Everyone who tried it said "where do I get one, I will not move heavy furniture without it ! "  Highly recommended.  Follow Up: (Sept '05) bought my second set, my son would not give up the set he used to move with.  I will not move furniture without one again !

rating 5.0. Greatest thing every invented! Purchased the Shoulder Dolly to move a new washer and dryer set into place. Had to go around a corner and into a tight spot with them. Never could have used a dolly for this because had to go round a tight corner. WOW! This thing worked great. Just took a little while and didn't have to fight everything. My neighbor helped me and said "I have to have one of those"
rating 1.0. Difficult to use Don't waste your money on this.  It is very difficult to use for anything that isn't a flat bottom and completely balanced.

rating 3.0. Works great, unless you are going down stairs. Worked great for walking on flat surfaces, but when we tried to remove a washer from the second floor of an apartment we had a lot of trouble. The washer would slide one the single bottom strap and touch the ground before we could get it down the first step. If there where two straps and the harness locking mechanism was higher up on your stomach it would have been a lot nicer.

rating 5.0. Great for Moving! (Updated at the bottom)

I recently used these to move a fair amount of furniture out of a second story apartment, and I was extremely pleased with how well these worked. They were fairly simple to figure out how to use, as you just have to throw the harness on and strap on the lifting strap while squatting.

Going down stairs can be a little tricky because one side needs to be higher than the other, but after we figured it out, it was a lot easier than using our hands and arms to lift the items. For some of the items, including couches, our legs and bodies were doing all of the lifting, and you could actually just use one hand to steady the couch. This made going down the stairs a lot more safe, since it left an arm free to hold the handrail.

I have the orange forearm lifts which I had used in the past: [[ASIN:B008ASBLJI Forearm Forklift Lifting and Moving Straps, Orange, Model L74995CN]], but those still require that you do the lifting with your arms. This shoulder dolly moves all of that weight off your arms and to your legs and back. (Be very careful when initially lifting, so that you don't injure yourself! - We practiced on a lighter mattress first, to get the hang of it).

I definitely recommend these if you have an upcoming move. I helped a friend move a few weeks ago, and I regretted not having these for that move.
(Note: if you have any hesitations on how well they work, or you want to get visual tips on how to best use these, there are YouTube videos that show people demonstrating their use).

Update 2/17/2014:

Just used these again this weekend for a move, and it is amazing how well this shoulder dolly works! Another person and I were able to lift and carry a huge and very heavy solid wooden bed with built-in drawers. We had moved this bed in the past, and it had always been the most difficult item to move and maneuver with (it had to go on carts, and it would always slip off the carts and bang the walls). This time, we just picked it up and walked with it. I am very happy that I have these straps and I will be hanging on to them for as long as they last!

rating 4.0. Great for lifting, not always practical I used this last week to move my house. We have a desk that you would think was made of depleted uranium, and it was upstairs. I bought this specifically to carry the desk, but used it for several other pieces as well.

In terms of actually picking up furniture, the fit, the ergonomics, this system works great. The two shoulder slings distribute the weight well over my shoulders, and the lifting strap seems very secure and very strong.

However we ended up switching to brute manpower for most of the heavier items, including the desk. Unfortunately while it helps to actually lift an item, the system is absolutely no help in tighter quarters such as doorways, narrow halls, or for items that need to go sideways (and thus are far off balance). As most moving jobs will require maneuvering through tight spaces, this product really has limited use, but if you are lucky enough to have small furniture or enough space to carry everything in upright, this is a great option.

rating 5.0. Back Saver We have used this system to move several stoves and refrigerators out of mobile homes and it is fantastic.  I used the forearm fork lift system before and this is far superior.  It is especially helpful if the two people are of different heights.  Makes taking a refrigerator up or down a set of steps a snap.

rating 5.0. Love this product I was surprised how easy it was to lift my washer and dryer (carried to my 2nd floor). There are instruction videos on youtube to lift heavy appliances, furniture etc on stairs. I wouldn't recommend this product for elderly people.

current_product_reviews: rating 4.0. Works great on a level surface, not so good for use in carrying large items up stairs These are sturdy, easy to figure out and use. They do a great job of weight distribution when an item is being carried on level ground. When you go up stairs these can present a challenge. For instance, my wife and I were carrying a large chest (all drawers removed) up steep stairs where the elevation of the top of the chest was at 4 feet above its top for the person on the bottom of the stairs. We ran out of strap length for the part that goes underneath the object being carried so we had to remove the straps when going up stairs and simply grasp the item by its base. So, these are useful when carrying on a level surface, they can sometimes be of little use when carrying a long object up a steep set of stairs as you run out of strap length for the strap that runs along the length of the item being carried.

input: rating 5.0. Easy to level and work. Needs torch. Needs thorough mixing. Needs warm air during application.  No more half gallon at a time per mixing bowl. Dont torch after 30 min. Thin on first coat with wood. 1 gallon covers roughly a 4 x 8 sheet of plywood at quarter inch coverage.

rating 5.0. Great product for a durable finish. This makes a great coating for a table or desktop. I would not use it in the kitchen or other areas where you may have high heat or hot pots/pans being placed on the surface. The table top in the picture above was done with a casting resin and this is NOT a casting resin. You CAN pour at about 1/4" increments to get to the total desired fill depth. I have done this on a tree limb with a lot of rot inside pouring 1/4" at a time and using a brush to help seal all the bug holes. It took perhaps 10 pours to get to depth and a good final sand and a flood coat to finish it to a smooth coat. Dont be afraid to sand between coats. 120 grit seems to work in the rougher stages and 360 to scuff for the final coats.

This material is temperature sensitive. 75F is the ideal temp and 70F and less will cure much more slowly. 8-F and higher really accelerates the pour. One pour on a table top was done at 84F and it was nearly a disaster. 10oz in a cup started to get really warm and you have to watch this. Anything above the ideal temp and the material sets exponentially faster. Mix it well and get it on the surface. Cooler temps are much more forgiving but may take up to 2 days or more depending on the temperature.

You will get air in the material when you mix it. I have not tried a vacuum chamber and it is not needed as the seal and flood coats are easy enough to pop the air bubbled with a torch. 2 or 3 passes waiting 15 or 20 min in between usually gets all the air bubbles but a few problem areas may develop. I usually try to get the seal coat done and solidly filled before the flood coat.

This is a great coating resin and keep in mind it is not a casting resin. You can pour up to 1/4" layers at a time which is handy is you are embedding/layering different items in the resin. I wont go into mold making but do suggest a plastic sheet under the project to catch overflow. I do make a 2x2 perimeter around the mold in case the mold leaks. Just the plastic sheet is sufficient if you are seal or flood coating.

The take away is mix well, watch your temp,watch your depth of pour, torch the air bubbles, scuff between coats, and practice. This is relatively inexpensive for the results you can get. This is my go to product for a coating resin.

rating 2.0. More issues than other brands Not the best, but not the worst. This has a slight yellow tinge, so I would only recommend using this for colored projects as it will show the yellow tones on white or clear projects. Easy to mix, easy to pour, but this epoxy produces more bubbles than any other brand I've used and I noticed the bubbles tend to have a hard time reaching the surface and leveling out. I had to run a heat gun several times to get them to level out, more so than I've had to with any other brand. I also noticed a lot of micro bubbles that just wouldn't pop no matter what. For thess reasons, I won't be purchasing again as I've used better products at a similar price range.
rating 2.0. Not satisfied Do not like that it comes out yellow!!!!!:/

rating 5.0. Great but tedious Be very careful and due your research. Do not poor more then 1/4 inch thick pours with 6 hours between them. And faster and your defiantly in for some major work or throwing away your project. Keep your room at 75 degrees (plus 3 minus 1) do not let room change temperature quickly. Slow stir for a while making sure to mix very very well, do not use drill(it adds more bubbles)

Example of good and bad pours.
Good: Hard as a rock 6 to 8 hours after final pour.

Bad: sticky tacky days after. Multiple pours with not enough curing time or lower room temperature, first pour 1/4" thick 6 hours later next pour 1/4" thick 4 hours later, 3rd pour 5/16" thick. Last pour will dry to quick causing middle pour to not dry at all, possible mixing of layers causing softer gel like to end up on top.

Bad: 2 inch thick pour. Grab a fire extinguisher and hope nothing catches on fire. Final product will dry in 30 min to an hour instead of 24 hours. Will crack everywhere while drying. Imagine throwing molten glass in a freezer thats in an ice cube tray. The glass sticks to the tray and cracks as everything cools/shrinks.

rating 5.0. Great for my Castings! I have purchased this brand a few times now. I’ve also purchased other brands. I ONLY do casting.
I usually get the 1 gallon but decided to start getting the 2 gallon. I have not noticed a yellow tint to any of the bottles I have received.
I do notice bubbles, but I don’t see any more than in other brands I’ve purchased. With this brand, I am able to take a little bit longer to do what I do than when I’ve used other brands....not sure why.
I live in Texas where it’s always warm or hot- but because of medical reasons...my house temperature is *never* over 68 degrees. Never.
I do mix in a slightly different manner than I’ve read how others do, and from what is suggested.
I mix my resin epoxy in 10 ounce cups. I’ve used larger but I feel the 10 ounce or smaller cups work better, even if I have to use 3 or 4 cups for a larger project. I stir with a tongue depressor for 5 minutes, then I let it sit for 5-10 minutes. During that 5-10 minutes...I torch the bubbles, usually 3 times, sometimes just 2. Right before I go to pour- I torch it again. This is when I add colorant (mica and/or alcohol ink) and/or glitter - or other cool things. I lightly torch one more time and I do hold the torch about 6 inches above the cup so the cup doesn’t melt. When I start pouring...the resin is clear and there are hardly any bubbles.
After pouring is done- IF there are any bubbles, I use an acupuncture needle to pop them. I very rarely use a torch once the resin is in the mold. I’ve ruined a few molds doing so, (when I first began casting) so I stopped.
I check my molds every 30 minutes and if there are no bubbles or any dust dropping in, after 2 hours...I cover and let it cure.
My items are usually hard within 8 hours. When I make my candle panels- I take it out of the mold then because it is hard enough that it doesn’t lose its shape, and nothing is ‘drippy’, and is pliable enough for me to put a curved shape to it. My husband and I made something that I use to shape my panels.

My only “complaint” is this stuff smells like stinky feet. 😳 seriously, I double mask, I open windows and have fans going near where I work. My husband says I have a sensitive sense of smell because he can’t smell it...but you can never be too careful using resin epoxy. I like that the Hardener is like water and the resin is super thick...it helps me make sure I don’t use double of one, which I have done before.

My experience with this particular brand is great. I’ve not had any negative issues. I feel like the price for a 2 gallon kit is worth it.
I’ll post some photos in a few days of things I’ve made (I had to put everything away as we had our windows replaced).

So I did a lot of calling around town about citric acid & came up with no good deals. 4.00 for 5-7ounces of the stuff. rating 5.0. Crystal Clear Dishes- Whiter whites I have been looking for a better solution to my hard water problem. Dealing with powdery residue left on dishes and in washing machine combined with expensive small 4 oz size containers at local grocery store.  I researched and found Citric Acid is the main ingredient in those tiny 4 oz bottles.  Shipping was extremely FAST.  It wasn't due for another week (shipping option I choose to save $)  So delighted it came early, as I needed to start using it right away.  I immediate dumped the first of two 5 lbs bags into air tight container that was easy to open/use daily and tucked under sink. the second is stored for later in pantry. I am actually glad it didn't come in ONE huge 10 pound bag- but in two 5 lb bags.  My dishes are sparkling crystal clear once again for a fraction of the cost. Our washing machine no longer has white film on the inside, and I use less soap with each load. My whites are finally bright and white again.  Towels are softer. Cant wait to see what else this product does to help me clean around the house. I will keep re-ordering citric acid from this seller!

rating 5.0. To clean the toilet I usually use about 1/3 cup of citric acid dissolved in some hot water and I pour it in the cleaned toilet t Received as two 5 pound bags as advertised.  I use this for a variety of uses - from canning tomatoes (which doesn't take much citric acid), to cleaning the brown stains at the bottom of your toilet that no amount of brush scrubbing seems to remove.  To clean the toilet I usually use about 1/3 cup of citric acid dissolved in some hot water and I pour it in the cleaned toilet that has the brown stains (that look like p**p) and let it sit overnight.  The next morning you just need to brush around and the stains lift up and come off.  Amazingly easy with no need for elbow grease.  I need to look for other ways to use it as a cleaner.  People say use vinegar in cleaning, but I hate having things smell like vinegar.  Maybe the citric acid will work as an odorless alternative.

input:
rating 4.0. Good filament, but don't be afraid to raise your temp just because its PLA It works very well, I get pretty clean and reasonably strong prints! I have to print it pretty hot at 225-230C, which certainly seems a little strange compared to my Hatchbox PLA, but it works nicely.

EDIT: for some large prints and/or for higher speeds, I needed to push my temps even higher (235-240C sometimes, which is a little iffy with the unstable PID on my Marlin firmware). It's still good filament, but you'll have to push your temps up for larger prints or those with pillars

rating 4.0. Great filament but more translucent than expected I have been using this filament for a couple weeks and it has been great. Pretty easy to print and have yet to have a snag or quality issue. It is translucent which was not in the description.

rating 2.0. requires extra heat. Revised This filament requires unusually high temperatures for pla to get good layer adhesion. I had to print at 230 which is my normal abs temp.I havent tried any prints that require the cooling fan but im certain this will effect print quality.

Edit: I cut my rating in half. The more I use this filament the more I hate it. Even at 230 there are layer adhesion problem on thiner walls. The final straw during a print I discovered that filament had been poorly wrapped on the roll with the inner layers overlapping outer layers causing it to bind and stop extruding.

rating 3.0. This filament is a beautiful deep blue This filament is a beautiful deep blue, but it's not opaque like it appears in the photo of the spool. Other reviewers are right, you have to print this at 230 or higher to get the layers to adhere. So not quite what I expected, but I can make it work.

rating 4.0. Inland White PLA So far has printed nicely, though I ordered this blue filiment but received white filiment. At first I tried printing at standard PLA temp 190-210 C but have ended up printing at 230-235 C. I'm pretty sure that there is something besides PLA in the filiment evidenced by the higher than typical print temp.

rating 5.0. My prints all came out fine printed at 215C on a 60C bed After seeing the other reviews, I am not sure what I expected. It doesn't seem translucent to me, but my parts have been really high density so far. My prints all came out fine printed at 215C on a 60C bed. I print at slower speeds though. Either way, it works well in my Maker Select, and results in some really good looking parts. It doesn't seem brittle, before or after printing either. I have made a few chip clips with it, and they seem to flex well without breaking. I would certainly recommend it.

Edit: As I worked with this, it became clear that I was printing MUCH too hot. I am now printing just a bit under 200C on a regular basis, and have improved my results quite a bit.
rating 2.0. Not very strong quit using it and went back to ... Not very strong  quit using it and went back to other brands
rating 1.0. One Star Color is inconsistent. Changes from blue to teal and back to blue in a single print.

rating 4.0. ok for non detail work prints well at 205 but very tough to remove supports also this color is a bit dark to show off details well its great for stuff that you don't need detail on like repair parts but molding and prop making I'd advise a lighter color

rating 1.0. Strange Tempature Filament.... It's cheap and that it what you're paying for. I just opened the filament yesterday and this junk PLA will not stick to the 3D bed at 195/55 temps. Had a friend, with his 3D printer even try it and would not work. Just missed sending this junk back yesterday, so it's just going into the recycling trash. So again, don't waste your money on this filament. It doesn't work....

UPDATE: After leaving this review above. I read other reviews and thought I might as well try the much higher temps. I made a toothpaste key and it wasn't too bad. I did make it with a raft and at the temps of 235/65. I really don't like running at those higher temps. Making a Frame Brace at the moment and will see how the brace turns out. So with this update, I went from a 1 star to a 3. Don't think I will ever buy filament from Inland again, but will use what I have at the moment. Cheers.....

UPDATE AGAIN: Tried using this filament again after using some other makers filament. Used the same 235/65 and I was very disappointed at the quality of the print. Ive added 2 pictures for you to see how bad this filament is. Its not that I had my setting at warp speed, but they was set for high quality. So set my star at 1 and wish I could go lower but it is what it is. Do yourself a favor, and stay away from this cheap junk filament.

rating 3.0. I love the color of this blue as there is a ... I love the color of this blue as there is a slight translucency to it. It doesn't print consistently like Hatchbox does and requires it's own set of retraction settings. Once you get that dialed in it will print.

rating 4.0. Decent PLA filament, very satisfying hue. A very pretty color that varies a bit dependent on the temp it's printed at. Had some trouble getting the temp dialed in just right to stop some excessive stringing, but otherwise prints just fine.

rating 3.0. but I would not recommend if you are looking for somthing high quality The color was much MUCH darker than it appeared in th picture, and has a kind of translucency to it. my first print with it ended up breaking, but I guess I don't know what I expected from a $17 filament. In the end it gets the job done, but I would not recommend if you are looking for somthing high quality.

rating 1.0. This stuff is wound incorrectly. Filament is wound incorrectly and causes the extruder to snap it because it cant pull any more filament. You will have to babysit your print jobs until you use up all the filament or toss it out.

Yes i have heard others say they don't have this issue, but when you have dozen people reporting this specific color having issues, then you know a bad batch was put onto the market.

rating 5.0. The way this blue printed is just beautiful. There is a slight sparkling/shimmering quality to it Got this in yesterday, in true Prime fashion. Other PLA ordered has typically taken a week or so for delivery. Try it out immediately on our Monoprice Select Mini. The template was a TARDIS pen holder for my desk at work. The way this blue printed is just beautiful. There is a slight sparkling/shimmering quality to it. It was the perfect filament for this print job. [[ASIN:B01FL49VZE Monoprice Select Mini 3D Printer with Heated Build Plate, Includes Micro SD Card and Sample PLA Filament - 115365]]

rating 1.0. Wasted and failed prints Of all the filaments I run through my MP Select V2 this is the only one that jams on extremely regular basis. I also regularly get inconsistent extrusions leading to failed prints.

rating 5.0. It almost looks like it has a metallic under coat WOW!!! I was expecting a solid blue for a project I am working so I ordered this to try it out.When I loaded it and printed my temp tower then my tree stump that I use for color samples. WOW I was surprised that it was not a solid color but a more of a translucent blue. It almost looks like it has a metallic under coat. I posted a pic but with the lighting it does not do it justice. If you are looking for a solid color do not get this filament but if you need something cool looking then I highly recommend it. My project is for the computer I built for my fish tank., I wanted blue but what I got was so much better.

rating 5.0. Good PLA This is good PLA but it's dark blue. I'd say it's noticeably darker than in the picture. Often that's what you want, but if you are looking for a lighter blue, look elsewhere. It's also slightly transparent.

It stuck hard to my bed. That could have had something to do with what I was printing and how much glue I used, but it does seem like some PLA sticks harder than others.
rating 1.0. One Star Tends to clog and warp better off with a roll of hatchbox
rating 1.0. One Star Jams occur frequently with this filament.
rating 2.0. This is not PLA This is not PLA. It's clearly ABS. If you treat it like PLA, you will not get good results.

current_product_reviews: rating 2.0. I love Inland filament, but.... I love Inland filament, it is my 'daily driver' as it were.  However... they changed the blue color.  Egyptian Blue has changed.  These rolls will say they are Egyptian Blue on the side, but it is no longer the royal blue color that it once was, but now a darker almost purple blue.  REALLY disappointed, since I bought 2 rolls of the stuff for a project as my other, older Egyptian Blue is hours from running out.  Now I'm going to have to scrap 72 hours of printing simply because they changed it.  It is NOT the color in the picture shown here on Amazon.  The image I've attached, the printed part is what this blue USED to be... the spool is what it NOW is.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Works great! Using to turn on a heated bed on ... Works great!  Using to turn on a heated bed on a 3D printer

input: rating 5.0. Worked well Heavy and feel like good quality. Used it to smooth concrete for resurfacing and it lasted for the porch, sidewalk and 3 squares on driveway. Not sure if still usable, because my husband did the work and he hasn’t used the second one we ordered yet to finish driveway.  As far as I know he can still use first one to continue what he started (still have 4 large areas and 2 smaller areas on driveway to complete). Price is really good and he really likes them. He knew by using them on concrete that it would wear the one out so we ordered the second one for when he will need it.  Worth the money.
rating 5.0. Wow Grinds down concrete edges FAST.  Just make sure you have a mask or vacuum hooked up (lots of expected dust).

rating 2.0. One wheel did not fit The 4" wheel did not have enough space to accommodate my locking flange rendering the wheel useless to me. The 4.5" wheel did however fully lock onto my arbor and thus was usable. It does fairly well grinding a cinderblock wall. However the amount of diamonds on the wheel seemed pretty sparse. Have not used it enough to determine the life span. NONE of the wheels listed on Amazon have adequate dimensional data to allow one to choose a proper fitting wheel. There needs to be more technical data shone. My grinder is a Hitachi (now Metabo) model G12SS if this helps anyone.

rating 5.0. Great! The 4 inch Turbo wheel is pretty much standard to others I've bought on Amazon. But the 4 1/2 inch Double Row is a beast. Very similar to the RIGID wheel I usually buy (at about triple the price). The Double Row one has lots of meat and a high diamond content. Heavy steel core. I use these to grind concrete slabs, and go through wheels like crazy. This wheel lasts longer than the more expensive name brand ones from big box home improvement stores. Also comes with new washers.

rating 5.0. Heck yea!  Worked like a charm We needed to grind off quite a bit of thinset after removing some tile and this worked like a charm on a grinder.  Bewarned,  you will want to attach the grinder to a good dust extractor!!  Using this without a dust extractor is not a good time - at all.

rating 5.0. Much better than expected I didn't have high hopes, but it was cheap and claimed to be able to do the job, so I gave it a try. Our foundation had been extended and so we had around 25 feet by 8 inches wide  that was between half an inch and an inch high which had been fine until we were looking to finish the basement. I went with this two pack figuring that between the two of them I'd get a good idea on how many of this type of grinding wheel I was going to need. Well I ground the whole thing down using only 1 wheel and it still looks nearly new, I didn't really notice any major difference in the grinding efficiency from the beginning to the end. Make sure to wear safety glasses as it definitely occasionally throws some bits. I'd also recommend you keep it wet and even keeping it wet a dust mask isn't a bad idea.  It isn't quick, my project took me a little over 2.5 hours  with a Ryobi 18V cordless grinder, a high amp electric would probably go faster. The end result was a nice smooth surface that I could easily lay a subfloor over

rating 5.0. It performed exactly as needed. I had a paver walkway that I needed to extend but the pavers were old and I could not find any. I decided to pour a concrete path and shaped them with a trowel. The result was a very coarse unprofessional looking mess. I bought this grinding wheel to smooth it out. It worked very well but I ended up with a bare metal wheel when I was finished. That is fine with me because it did the job I needed it to and I don’t believe that I will ever need to do it again. I never needed to use the second finer grinding wheel so I still have it for smaller projects if necessary. I have included before and after photos so you can see the results.

rating 5.0. great on cleaning surfaces and trimming These cut concrete quickly. I needed to recycle tiles for a floor and these made quick work on cleaning thinset off the back and cutting new grooves.

rating 5.0. Survived 30 hours of concrete floor grinding This thing was great for the money.
It survived preparing a 40 x 30 concrete floor for epoxy, it outlived one grinder...

rating 5.0. Amazing tool. Removed old tile motar. Took out 50 year old pink floor tile in bathroom with sledge hammer and hammer drill.  While the tile came out, much of the mortar remained creating a rough surface.  Definitely did not want to pour a floor leveler and raise the floor any higher plus the cost and time.  Tried these on a Dewalt battery powered angle grinder.  The mortar came off so easily I could not believe it.  The old concrete was harder than the mortar and the grinder went right down to the concrete and made a level surface suitable for tiling.  Did about 25 sq ft in about 20-30 minutes.  I wet the floor to hold down dust.  Used one wheel and wore down slightly less than half of the thickness of the diamond grinding capacity. Highly, highly recommend for such an application.
rating 5.0. Works great Took 3/8” high spot of concrete down to level with now problem, 1’x3’ area

rating 2.0. Arbor hole not centered and does not fit The arbor hole is out of center on the 4" wheel. Also my locking flange of 1 5/8" will not fit the 4" wheel it only has maximum space for about a 1 3/8" flange.

Everything is fine on the 4 1/2" wheel.
So basically I got one usable wheel.

rating 3.0. Which wheel is aggressive and which isn't? I found the wheel with fewer vanes was less aggressive. Maybe that should have been easy to figure out but I didn't without using them both first. Marking them or instructions noting that would have been nice. Wore out the aggressive one grinding down speedset in a 3'x4' patch trying to remove high spots that were 1/4" or less in areas, not the entire space. Creates a lot of dust so best to have a fitting around your grinder to connect your shop vac to with a strong filter to prevent it going out the exhaust and all over your work area, IE kitchen!

rating 2.0. Didn't work as intended Disappointed. Please note, that not is to say it didn't work for what it was designed, just not what I was using it... stump grinder hack I saw online. I eventually removed the stump with a sharp axe and chain saw.

rating 5.0. Easy to use, great results I needed to bring off 5 layers of paint on our porch. I tried all options before I bought these. I spent s hours and hours scrapping with minimal results. Once I got these it took less than an hour to remove all the paint layers. You have to keep it flat or it will dig in but I found it easy to use.
rating 5.0. Fantastic The wheel was just what I needed and actually worked better than I expected.....

current_product_reviews: rating 1.0. No QC from this manufacturer! these grinders are off center by 3mm- 1.46mm between the two disc and vibrant so bad that it will destroy your angle grinder and your hands arms and neck. just spend a few more buck and get some from a name brand company.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. dual run!! you never know how important this is till it's 118°F!!
rating 5.0. Quality high quality bar of aluminum. shipping takes a bit of time.

input: rating 4.0. It works Cleaned the dirt out of my coils, and smelled alright. Wish it would have more of a disinfecting property, better smell, and made the coils shiny. It removed dirt well, however did not remove mold. I recommend using a pressurized sprayer (like one you would use for pesticide or weed killer) to rinse out the coils after you spray this on.

rating 5.0. I recommend this product to anyone looking to clean coils Worked perfectly.  Spray coils, let it set for 20-30 minutes, and spray with water.  Coils look brand new.  I recommend this product to anyone looking to clean coils.
rating 5.0. Five Stars Keeps your coils nice and clean!

rating 5.0. I have a window a/c that wasn't cooling like it used to I have a window a/c that wasn't cooling like it used to. I took the cover off , sprayed the coils with this and rinsed with water. Now it blows out 48 degree air and cools as good as ever.

rating 3.0. It works good, but it produces strong fumes and you should ... It works good, but it produces strong fumes and you should make sure that the ventilation is sufficient if you use it inside the a home. It also took awhile to wash off the residue afterwards despite diluting 5:1 as recommended.
rating 5.0. It can be used on other things. I am trying this on my own. It cleans as described. I'm not disappointed at all
rating 5.0. COMSTAR 90-298 COIL CLEANER USED ON MY HEAT PUMP COILS
rating 5.0. A Product that Works This product works great and is easy to use. Would recommend to anyone.
rating 1.0. Ask questions This one for cleaning inside coil and pipes or outside coils?

rating 5.0. Simple to use appears to work well. Easy to use cleaned the coils on my RV’s air conditioners. I need to try it on my home air conditioner. It works fast I used a pump spray it only took a few minutes. A lot of dirt came off the coils I repeated it a second time. Did not get as much dirt off the coils on second try but it does look much better.

rating 5.0. Bang for the buck and works well It cleaned up the condenser coils (outside unit) very well. Used a dilution of 1:2 (coil safe : water). Tons of sand and dirt came out. I unscrewed the top fan grate so I could get at the coils from both sides. After a couple applications and letting this stuff sit for a little while, I rinsed the coils off a couple times with the flat setting on a garden hose (do not use a pressure cleaner! The high pressure could destroy the formation of the coils). I rinsed it off until there wasn't as much grit coming off in the water. For the price, this product can't be beat.

Have yet to try on my AC unit's evaporator coils (inside). Will update this review when I try it out.

rating 1.0. Seller Listing Of Product Use Description Is Misleading! For AC Evaporator Not AC Condenser! I'm NOT happy. Listing for this says it cleans ac evaporators AND condensers. No it doesn't as I found out after using it per the directions of 1 to 5 ratio. Before I started I cleaned off what I could with my hand and a soft condenser brush. I used it the first time and was like that's it? So I waited two hours and did the whole process again and between the two cleanings it hardly did anything. Frustrated I looked over the entire bottle word for word and it says evaporator cleaner but nothing about condenser cleaner. I looked up the Comstar website and it says evaporator cleaner and not condenser cleaner. Comstar offers a condenser cleaner that's yellow in color that formulated for cleaning ac condensers and not evaporators which I'm sure has a much higher price. This one is formulated for cleaning the inside ac evaporator coil and is not strong enough to clean the outside condenser coil.

I'm sure Comstar coil safe cleans evaporator coils good because that is what it's formulated to do. But the seller list this as a cleaner for both which it is not. The seller is capitalizing on those of us who are looking for a cheaper option and lying about what its uses are to make the sale. I was going to buy the blue Nu-Calgon condenser cleaner but at $37+ plus tax . I thought with the Comstar Coil Safe I can save it will do the same thing for much less money, WRONG! I'll be getting a refund because of the fraudulent listing the seller has put on this product. It was a lot of work taking my Trane condenser fan and panels apart only to go back and do it all over again.

rating 4.0. Woked pretty good This product worked pretty good on evaporator coils and okay on condenser coils. It took 4-5 applications on both units to get good results. Nu-brite works better on outdoor condenser coils, but is less safe to use than this product.

rating 5.0. Easy to use and great results. I usually clean my AC outdoor unit at least once a year. I usually use some rather toxic stuff that isn't safe for anyone let alone pets. Since last time, i gained a fury child and needed to be careful on what I used. For the amount of product this makes and cost along with how well this works, it is a great buy. I used a pressure sprayer that I use for weed control and that worked fine. I let it sit for a good 10 minutes and washed the dirt off with a pressure nozzle on my water hose. I was amazed at how well the coils were cleaned and how much dirt was removed. I hadn't seen this must debris removed before. I must say that my AC cools faster and is much colder than before. I will definitely buy this cleaner again.

rating 5.0. Best product you will find for cleaning air conditioner coils! I diluted per instructions into hand sprayer and applied product to heavy mold coated air conditioner coils. The mold washed completely away with a light sprayer of water from my water hose. By gar exceeded my expectations. Ordering more to have on hand.

rating 5.0. This stuff REALLY works! I have only window units in my home and the fins were nasty. I sent away for this. It came with a stiff bristle brush and a metal 'comb'. You just mix the concentrated liquid as directed, brush off any loose dirt from the fins and then spray it on the fins, let it sit for a couple of minutes and then 'comb' the fins with the metal tool. No rinsing! My window unit cools so much better and I believe that this is the reason my power bill went down by almost $50!
rating 1.0. I Donat work on heavy duty coils. It don’t work for heavy duty dirt on the coils .
rating 2.0. Did not foam up Didn’t foam as expected. Won’t be buying again. Spend the little extra money on the good stuff.

rating 3.0. Helps but not as aggressive as  had hoped. It certainly beats just plain water but doesn't clean as well as I had hoped.  I used this one as it is safe for powdercoat/paint but next time I will buy a more aggressive cleaner and remove or mask the more delicate surfaces. Probably ideal for inside coils as the solution has very low odor and isn't tooooo aggressive.

rating 5.0. this has sold me! it works and no odor or fumes! doesn't take much either! Used this to clean my electronic air cleaner element's for my furnace and the filter for my orik air purifier I bought used years ago. I have never been able to get either clean, I have used dawn and hot water as well as even gave steam cleaning a shot but always had dirt stuck on my filters for my furnace ( these are aluminum so plz do not try to clean paper or any other filter media with this, it is not designed to work on anything else other then metal type filters ), my air purifier filter is also aluminum and when I got it the filter was stained with what I have to assume is tar from cigarettes. I have tried multiple cleaners and none have worked on it either. I put ( in a clean spray bottle ) 1/4 bottle of cleaner with 1/2 bottle of hot water in old windex bottle and sprayed the purifier filter, the junk stuck on there that didn't want to come off, just started running off like using a garden hose to spray mudd off your truck before washing. Side of bottle says to let sit for 5 min after spraying, I let it sit for maybe 2 to see how well it cleaned stuff and then used my shower head to spray the filters down with hot water. I have not seen the dirt run off these filters like this before. I even rinsed the dirt off to start with using the pressure spray on my shower head before spraying the cleaner and I still got double the amount of dirt to come off. When I rinsed the filters the 2nd time, the water rinsed off as much dirt as it did when I first rinsed the filters before spraying them!  The air purifier filter, I had chunks coming off of this junk/ tar when I rinsed it. The odor, there is none! I'm serious, there is no odor! This stuff is about as much as the spray calgon I believe is the brand cleaner that claimed to be odorless, but wasn't. This is cheaper and is alot better since there is no fumes or odors!
rating 5.0. WORKS GREAT! see photo, you be the judge ;) quality cleaner. pleasant scent throughout building for an hour or so, then goes away. great buy

rating 1.0. ZERO DIFFERENCE IN MY COIL Applied it twice and it only changed the smell once the unit was back on.

rating 1.0. Not the best. Look for alternatives. Doesn't do as advertised. Beware.

rating 5.0. Worked Well! I recently purchased a battery powered weed sprayer and decided to use that with this. I mixed 3:1 water, waited 5-10 min and rinsed. I used a small brush to remove dirt and a shop vac to suck up the dirt and water. Turns out I had a lot of dirt underneath the unit since I blasted the fan base probably 5-6 times and I still had some dirt coming out from underneath. Overall it looks almost like new. The base has algae growth so I'll look into an eco way of cleaning that.

tl;dr
Get a garden sprayer, long handle brush, hose with moderate strength sprayer, algae spray and shop vac and you'll leave your condenser looking like new.

rating 5.0. Works good 👍 Whoever is saying this isn't for condensers has no idea what they are talking about. Works perfectly well, undilluted, on this mess of a 27yo Unit. Not as good as the foam stuff, but it will clean 😀
rating 1.0. Very toxic Heavy fumes I wouldn't recommend spraying this in your home. Very toxic
rating 5.0. Works great, Price great. This solution made black moldy and dirty coil shiny silver. 2nd time ive bought this product, so yeah, i am pleased with it.

rating 3.0. Be Careful, The rinse can kill your plants Diluted as recommended and cleaned the coils great. I used a spray bottle from top to bottom. Then some of the rinse water puddled around a Carolina jasmine, and I did not realize or pay enough attention to this. So, within 4 days, most of it dried out and turned brown. I'll update the end results after another month or so of (diluting) watering the plants.
update: The contaminated dirt will have to be removed and replaced if you expect to grow anything in the future.
rating 2.0. Doesn’t work Doesn’t work what I expected!

rating 5.0. Super concentrated I washed my AC coil after spraying down my AC with this using a backpack sprayer, and I left it for about 15 min. Came out to hose it off, and the amount of suds that came out was crazy!! The coils look better, and it may be my brain, but the air coming out of the AC was cooler too!! I will use it at least twice a year.

AC guys usually take off the top of the AC to clean, I did not, and I still saw a significant difference in coils!!
rating 5.0. Great ,will go for more Worked as needed, one hint: do not rush it, give it time to act in the coil

rating 5.0. Cleaned my outside Condenser I.used on my outside condenser coils (Trane Heat Pump). I removed the fan from the top and sprayed the mix on the coils (3 parts water, 1 part Coil Safe), then sprayed the outside of the coils. Waited 10 min and then used the water hose to rinse from the inside to outside. Coil Safe did a great job!

input:
rating 1.0. 14 gal bags slide down into the bottom; leaves a 4" gap and MESS! WASTE OF MONEY...WHAT A MESS!
Does not hold a 14 gallon trash bag in place. It is 16 gallon in the base, so when the garbage gets half full and heavy enough, it slides down into the bottom, leaving a 4" space between the edge and the top of the bag. It creates a big mess! I regret buying this garbage can on looks alone. It looks great collecting dust in the corner of my garage. Will be going into my yard sale.
rating 5.0. Swing top for the win! Super stylish. Super clean. And tall enough for my 1 year old not to fully get into. I've never had a swing top trash can but it's super convenient!

rating 2.0. Very toxic fumes..... I just pulled this out of the box and I have to say that it is horribly toxic smelling -  within two minutes I had a headache and I had to put it outside to gas off. It is also a bit larger than what I thought by looking at the photos on Amazon. I think it will work for what our need is but it is a little larger as i said -  I thought the circumference would be smaller. I would not purchase it again because clearly it is a toxic product and I do not want to support that at all. I would return it but it is far too much hassle with the size of the box that it came in.  Hopefully leaving it outside will help gas off the obvious chemicals that are leeching out of this product. I guess I’m not very happy with this product.  I will update if that changes. It is nice looking but at this point I am really feeling ill - headache and nausea.
rating 1.0. cracked The bin was cracked-no option for refund have to send back which is a total pain

rating 4.0. Very Lightweight & Sharp Looking!!!! Definitely Recommend This Product & Seller 👍 This is a pretty steep price for being plastic. It looks great. Holds a 13 gallon garbage bag well. But for $35.00 you can throw in another $20.00 and get an automatic opening one. It's lightweight. It's pretty tall. The pictures do the trash can total justice because it does reach as tall as my countertop. I have waited YEARS to find the "perfect" garbage can for my kitchen. Seriously mine was bought from a Family Dollar back in 2011. It's cracked and broken but I wasn't about to settle for the ones I've seen in certain department stores. They'd have either a $200.00 can that needs special bags and opens automatically or you're settling for a big ugly brown, dull, plastic can that none of the tops fit.
Amazon has quite a few nice ones (of course my favorite being the pink or rose gold colors) but I can't spend over $50.00 on a garbage can. Even though my $7.00 Family Dollar can lasted me eight years, it was falling apart. It ripped every bag where the plastic was cracked.

The flip-floppy lid doesn't line up perfectly BUT if you look at the pictures.... That's how it's manufactured.
I'm very happy with my purchase.
I just thing I would LOVE if it were $10.00 cheaper.
The peuter color goes well with any decor.

rating 5.0. In my case, best for recyclables Very attractive and sturdy, I love how it looks and operates (I have it in the kitchen). Whether you use this for regular trash or recyclables will depend on your household. I intended to use it for regular trash, but for me that didn't work. The lid doesn't seal, and the bin holds so much that things that quickly smell (like chicken bones) begin to smell long before the bag is even half full. If you have a large family and fill the trash can more quickly this may not apply to you. For me I found it perfect for recyclables- paper, cardboard, rinsed cans, etc. which tends to be the lion's share of my trash, for one thing, but even if I don't fill it quickly the smelly stuff tends to go in the regular trash. I have another bin I use for that (originally a large dry dog food bin), purchased at Albertson's, which keeps smells in and ants/flies out.

BTW, through trial and error I found heavy-duty bags that fit it perfectly: Signature Select drawstring lawn & leaf bags, 39 gallon, 1.0 MIL, 2'9" X 3'5"- (green box)-18 bags for $3.99, sometimes on sale for 2 for $7 at Von's and Albertson's.

I really like this bin, looks sleek and classy, the burnished silver ring and lid coordinates nicely with my appliances and swings easily & soundlessly. Easy to empty, replace the bag, and clean. Perfect. Highly recommended.

rating 5.0. Feeling defeated in your trash can search? I used to think dedicated trash drawers were a coveted kitchen feature solely due to the minimalist/clean look of hiding a trash can and space saving. Now I know the truth.

It was time to buy a new trash can. A household item that I somehow managed to avoid purchasing my entire life thanks to roommates already having one and then leaving behind. The one I had was simply too narrow, ripping the bag of it got too full as you fought against it to take out the trash. And it was short enough that our 1 year old dog was able to nose the lid and get into it.

I began my search thinking it would be far more straightforward and less time consuming than when I was in the market for a cat tower. Then an hour passed and I was feeling less confident but still hopeful the search would end soon. Another hour passed and all hope was lost.

It reminds me of a 30 Rock episode where they are tasked with creating “a giant, flimsy microwave.” If you’re not privileged enough to be able to snag a simple can because you don’t have enclosed spot to contain smells and keep away pets, you’re faced with reality of having to drop well over $50 for a TRASH CAN - most of which seem to be $80+ - that is far too lightly to break within 6 months. If you’re lucky, you get a good year out of a place to store TRASH. That doesn’t even include the likelihood that the one you buy will simply rip the bag to shreds, assuming it lets you take the bag out at all. And I saw many that were delivered damaged to begin with.

So I gave up my search that day. The next morning, feeling refreshed and determined, I decided to change priorities.

This trash can was tall enough that our dog cannot nose her way into the lid. Although the size makes our remaining 13 gallon bags slip down when it gets too heavy, there is zero opportunity for the can to cause a rip in the bag. The swing lid is such a basic engineering concept, there is virtually no chance it will wear out and stop working for years to come. The depth will be difficult for my arms to reach into and clean when the time comes, and yet it will still be far easier to thoroughly clean than my previous one as it’s the equivalent of washing a cup the size of a 4 or 5 year old. And it doesn’t look cheap. The charcoal/grey color is actually very appealing.

I was worried on how difficult it would be to use while cooking, but there are two parts to the lid. So if ease of access is necessary, I can take off the swing lid and the bag is still set in place by the ring. And finally, the size, although larger than what I originally set out to get and somewhat odd for available trash bag sizes, is a game changer. Imagine getting full use out of your trash bag instead of pulling it out and realizing how much more you could fit. The swing top doesn’t restrict the size of items you’re tossing as much as I anticipated, either, and so far we haven’t had any bad smell issues.

If you have found yourself just as frustrated and hopeless in your trash can search as I was, this is where your search stops. Save that extra $30+ you would spend on other models. This one is worth it.

I built a power distribution panel and used the heat-shrink on the weather pack style connectors to help ensure the connections remain secure for a lifetime. rating 5.0. USE IT THE RIGHT WAY AND YOU'LL BE AMAZED! I loved this silicone tape since the first production of it which I believe was called F1 tape or something military. Had a stripe on it. With this brand I was skeptical but now I see it exceeds my expectation and works exactly the same if not better. Now to the part why people rate this product with low stars; using it wrong!

How to properly use it; okay so if your trying to fix something leaking water...first start off with drying any side of the pipe because the first roll around the pipe has to be clean from water and debri. After that you can stretch it around the leak snug, keep going at least an inch below the leak and come back to the middle and stretch it and put pressure on the end and stick it on the tape. I've used this on everything radiator hoses, house water leaks, hose leaks and it's never let me down. START WITH A GOOD CLEAN WRAP AROUND SO THE REST YOU CAN STRECH OVER THE LEAK(can be wet and dirty it's just that first wrap around that's important) AND END ON A CLEAN SURFACE! Use alcohol or just a clean rag to dry or clean the starting point. This stuff is a hero for a handyman/DIYer.

rating 5.0. Best repair tape! I've tried many of the cheaper imitations. None of them stick like Rescue Tape. Wrap it stretch it and it sticks. Other tapes I've had to try over and over and it's touch and go. It works about 1/3 of the time.

rating 5.0. Worked for me! worked great. All I had was a simple leak from a drain pipe that leaked after a lot of use from thanksgiving. Make sure you have clearance around the entire pipe...you are supposed to pull it as tight as you can around the pipe. go slow and make sure to not twist and overlap half on pipe half on previous tape while you continually stretch.

rating 5.0. Handy to have around Great tape. I use it all over the place. Amazing what you can find a use for it. Just make sure you stretch it tight if you are trying to make a repair on something like a water hose.

rating 1.0. Wouldn't seal air leak Tried to repair 1/4 inch air hose with 50 psi pressure.  Rescue Tape does not stick to anything other than itself, so the air leak travels under the tape and comes out at the end of the tape repair.  The stretchy Rescue Tape also stretched and bulged at the location of the leak. Ordinary electrical tape that sticks to the air hose worked perfectly.  Because Rescue tape does not stick to whatever is being taped, it's hard to imagine how it could ever seal a leak.  Where did all those 5 star ratings come from?

rating 5.0. Rescue Tape should be in everyone's repair box I have always had a roll or two of this tape around because you can rescue yourself with Rescue Tape. Kept it on my boat and when I blew a coolant hose in the Puget Sound RT allowed me to do the repair and make my way back to my ramp AND a new hose. Instructions are simple and if followed will almost guarantee a successful mend. I have repaired coolers, copper pipes, steel pipes, exhaust hoses, radiator hoses and never had a failure. Pull the tape to ensure a very tight bond. The more you stretch it the more strength the repair will have.

rating 4.0. Works as advertised! Amazing product, worked as advertised. I'm using it to connect some panniers that I made from some charcoal caddies to my bicycle. So far I haven't traveled very far, the main thing I'm concerned with is compression over time, I'm basically wrapping the pipes until they are big enough for a 1/2 inch pipe strap will work, so it will have the panniers pressing down on it. I'll update it as soon as I get some miles with my panniers.

Anyway, that is a special application. I messed up on wrapping one and had to remove it, which was easy, and after it was off I tried to pull the layers apart but it is basically one piece of silicone now, which is what is is supposed to do.

I took a star off because it is pretty expensive compared to other manufactures of silicone tape. I bought some Tommy Tape, a 6 pack overstock set, and after shipping it turned out to be about $3.60 a role, 10 foot vs 12 foot for rescue, but still lots cheaper. They will be random colors but even if they are all pink what I am using it for on my bicycle it won't matter as it all ends up covered anyway.

rating 5.0. Not a sticky tape This tape is designed to adhere to itself.  It works well in wet situations where you can repeatedly wrap the tape against itself.  It does not hold when used in strips.  If wound round and round itself it can stand up to water and pretty good water pressure if the puncture is cleaned and the wrap is tight to prevent any air pockets or bleed paths.  Close to a miracle, maybe, in certain wet situations, but not quite miraculous.

rating 4.0. Great product for superficial suturing As a dermatologist treating skin cancer patients wanted to find a practice kit for training residents/fellows. For practicing interrupted suture, running suture, matress (Donati) and other types of "epidermal" surface are excellent! The nylon sutures run a bit more "stuck" than in real skin. Silk runs well.
For practicing deep dermal suture I think needs improvement since the "dermal" component is very stiff and no undermining is possible; hence, is hard to approximate the "dermis".

Bottom line:
I would totally use this to train first-year dermatology residents on skin suturing but it's not realistic for deep sutures.
rating 5.0. Great! This kit is Great! I am able to practice sutures over and over again. Highly recommend.

rating 5.0. Sutures, Instruments, and Life-Like Skin Pad! I’m a student in a Surgical First Assistant program, and I bought this kit for the lab classes. I searched Amazon high and low for suture kits, and this is the one I bought. This kit is an excellent value! By far, I felt that I got the most sutures, nice instruments, and a life-like gel suture pad from this kit. I love the variety of cuts and slits in the gel pad, which should cover all the types of wounds I’ll encounter. It even features the different layers of skin. Great product, and I would recommend it to any surgical student.

rating 5.0. Realistic feel, great for practice, great price I most enjoyed the included pre-threaded sutures as well as the tool kit that has everything you could ever want for practicing. This product would be most useful for people who already have some experience, but beginners may also use this kit after watching a few YouTube tutorials. The only addition I would like to see is possibly an instruction kit for those people who are beginners and an advanced knot tying instruction set for people who want to learn more techniques. Overall I think anyone interested in a field where suturing is a daily task should try out this product, the pricing is excellent for everything that comes with the kit!

rating 4.0. Cool suture kit, a little expensive This is a nice suture practice kit. The pre-made incisions are interesting since most skin pads don't come like that. I think it would make a pretty fun little gift for a new medical student who wants to get a leg up on the competition and practice with some YouTube videos. Probably less useful for a 3rd or 4th year medical student or end of 1st year PA student as those students usually have learned suturing by that point.

rating 4.0. Almost perfect, minus one thing This is overall a good tool for medical students. It comes with a lot of suture, new tools (needle driver, Adson's, etc), and various types of suture (monofilament, braided, silk, 3-0, 4-0, etc). The rubber does not quite feel exactly like human skin, but it does feel like any other practice pad out there. It comes with various types of incisions (laparoscopic, wide, linear, etc) and a scalpel to make any modifications. My main complaint (and why I didn't give it 5 stars) was that it does not have a good representation of the dermal-epidermal junction, which is crucial when trying to practice laporoscopic and continues sutures. Most pads don't have it, but if I find a pad that does, it will get 5 stars.

rating 2.0. Suture Material Is Very Poor Quality The skin provided is much more rubbery than anticipated. The needle sticks as it passes through and this gives a very unrealistic experience. This may help in longevity, but I cannot comment on that thus far.
The real problem with this kit is the extremely cheap needles on the sutures. Most sutures have a pointed needle with a flat portion in the middle so the needle driver can properly grip onto it. These are completely round and make it impossible to throw a comfortable stitch. I cannot understate how disappointing this is. Basically, if you want to practice properly, you have to purchase separate sutures.
To end on a positive note, the needle drivers and forceps are of decent quality. They store nicely in the case.

rating 5.0. 3rd Year Med Student - Exactly what I needed I'm a 3rd year medical student, and I bought this during an OBGYN rotation, just prior to a surgery rotation. Suturing was required for both, and this practice block was the exactly what I needed. It's been infinitely better than anything else I've come across. The pre-made cuts are appropriate to the openings you will actually be suturing on, and the depth and tensile strength of the material are also great. There is room to make some of them deeper if you want, so you can practice closing multiple tissue layers. The number of sutures the kit comes with will also give you quite a bit of practice.

Relative to other practice materials, pigs feet seem to be the gold standard, but those are not super convenient. It can be hard to find them in stores, and then you have to deal with the realities of having a greasy rotting pig leg in your refrigerator. Also, dead refrigerated tissue doesn't act like live tissue. I would argue the "realness" advantage over a synthetic block like this is pretty minimal, and comes with a lot of hassle. Beyond that, I have also used a couple other synthetic tissues in the past. The texture on one was far too stiff, like sewing through cartilage, and the others were all too soft. They also tend to acquire a veneer of dust and hair over time, as well as their own oily texture. It's gross. For whatever reason, this one doesn't seem to be doing that.

Overall, I'm very impressed with this kit, and I would absolutely buy it again.

rating 1.0. Not Good The suture practice kit has a ton of great reviews, which is why I bought it. I am trying to practice surgical sutures and the material keeps ripping. This is not worth $45 for something that is not even going to hold up the the things it is supposed to be used for.

rating 1.0. OK for throws, but TERRIBLE for needle placement Good set of tools, tons of sutures, just fine for practicing throws.

But this suture skin is TERRIBLE. Not even sure how surgical "residents" or "techs" are saying this is good for practice?! I am also skeptical if any of the positive comments below have ever even seen or felt a human being under anesthesia in the OR. It's not even close to how a needle inserts into skin-- which is the most important part when suturing.

I am a third year medical student on clinical rotations and currently rotating through general surgery. My attendings wouldn't even want me to practice on this because I think it would in fact WORSEN my technique.

Yes-- it is good for practicing throws if you've already have the needle and suture set up in the skin. But the most difficult part about suturing are not the throws themselves, but how the needle is inserted into skin.. It's about correct and identical proportions!

I can't even perform half of the techniques I've been taught in my general surgery rotation on this skin without it ripping. Skin, fat, and muscle, are malleable and move with your placement-- this material doesn't thus ripping every single time.

Definitely returning. Not even worth it for the kit and extra sutures.

rating 5.0. A nice, strong but light-weight cart and hand-truck. After I lost my traditional hand truck I decided to try this nylon truck/cart combination, and so far it has been a great choice. Because it is nylon it is lighter weight than a steel hand truck, and is much lighter than a steel convertible hand truck/ 4-wheeled car. It is very easy to lift into and out of the trunk of the car. The square beam construction with reinforcing ribs makes it very stiff, so don't worry about that. I have easily carried 120 lbs on it as an upright hand cart and 260 lbs using it as a 4-wheeled cart and it could have easily carried more weight. In my case the limiting factor was size. This is about 2/3 the size of a normal cart, so moving a lot of boxes will take more trips. It easily handles three 16x12x12 moving boxes when used as a hand truck and six when used as a cart.

The transition from hand cart to wheeled cart could not be simpler - just pull out the yellow handle and insert it 90 degrees upright. When used as a hand cart the handle length is adjustable. The cart in vertical or horizontal mode is wide enough to be stable when pulled so I had no problem with tipping, and when used as a 4-wheeled cart the swiveling front casters are large enough for easy rolling over door jambs.

The base plate is steel and is larger than my old cart, making the cart easier to tip back without having the load fall off the front - a good thing. The steel base plate puts most of the weight near the ground so the cart doesn't have the tendency to fall over backwards when empty like many all-steel carts do. The only negative about this cart is that the rubber wheels are really plastic but have a rubber ring around the edge. I guess that way they could say it has rubber wheels. So although the part of the wheel that touches the ground is rubber, it is clearly not going to be very durable. That's not a huge problem as better wheels can be purchased at any hardware outlet. But I will keep using these until they wear out.

The cart comes partly assembled and I only had to attach the base plate (wrenches and/or pliers needed), insert the axle, put on the wheels and drop in the handle. It took all of five minutes. With its black body and yellow handle it is a very attractive cart.

The packaging is cardboard with no styrofoam or plastic packaging, so everything except the bag the small parts came in is recyclable.

There is a pin that goes in to hold the wheels in place and you have to bend the metal back to lock it into place. I did it with a flat screwdriver but would not recommend that. Get this if you're going to be using it more than twice!

It's pretty small in size, so don't expect to move furniture or appliances with this. It says it can handle up to 400 pounds, but anything that heavy is big and clunky, and this hand truck just couldn't handle that (think refrigerator or larger furniture). Transitioning between modes is clunky and not smooth at all. I had a different brand cart years ago that was much smoother when it came to transitioning between modes, and it didn't require that I completely remove three pieces, potentially losing two small pins, in order to do it.

Like I said, it's nice for a small hand truck, but if you want something for jobs bigger than moving small things, this isn't for you.

Many of the holes did not line up exactly and the bolts / axle had to be wrestled and forced into place. I sliced my finger open on a razor-sharp seam edge (it was so sharp that it cut me like a knife!). The attachment for the yellow handle to the body of the dolly relies on two metal pins that have to be forced through holes in the body and the handle and then snapped into place...

All in all, this isn't a bad product, but there's nothing about it that impresses me. The fit and finish leave much to be desired.

rating 5.0. Excellent Value Before I purchased this two wheeler, I ordered a Dayton convertible two wheeler from a competing website (after this incident, I will probably never order from them again). The Dayton was about $60, and I became suspicious when I saw the same unit elsewhere for $452! I called the vendor and was assured that there was no mistake. What I actually got was a single replacement wheel! I eventually sorted out the error and got my money back. I mention this because some folk who purchased this Harper have complained about quality. If you intend to traverse multiple city blocks of uneven sidewalk with a couple hundred pounds loaded on one of these, you will be disappointed. I recommend the $452 Dayton for such use, knowing that just one wheel on the Dayton is more money than this entire Harper! That is, if you intend a commercial duty cycle, step up to a commercial hand truck. For household use, this thing is great! Quick, easy assembly, a very solid steel plate to lift heavy objects, a handle that is easily adjustable for three different heights as a two wheeler, and then upright as a four wheeler. I have stood on this thing in four wheel cart configuration (I weigh 240 lb), and no hint of sag. As a home user, I am confident it will serve my needs for a very long time. And, at only $37, I paid just a little more that an eight piece family meal (with drinks) at KFC. The value proposition is undeniable, If your expectations are realistic, you will not be disappointed. I absolutely am convinced that you will never find a better value hand truck! And, it is made in the USA!

rating 4.0. Easy to make it work for me... UPDATE
A couple of years later and my opinion has not changed. In fact, I bought a second one to use outside. Instead of taking the load off the one I use inside, moving it up/down the steps, and reloading it, to take things to and from the car, or for taking out the trash or recycling to the curb, etc... Well, you get the picture. I use one inside and keep the other in the garage.
~~~~~~~ original review ~~~~~~~~~
This is a great tool. I bought it mostly for the flatbed in conversion mode. I am partially disabled, meaning I have 'bum' leg. I can still lift with my arms most necessary things in the household, but I can't carry weight and walk too. I use the cart to move heavy or awkward loads, like groceries in, trash out, laundry and supplies inside - from one end of the house to the other. It was delivered promptly and was easy to assemble. There is a help line for questions or complaints listed with the instructions. The surprise was, it was actually answered by a native-English-speaking American.

I would have given this cart 5 stars except for one thing. The flatbed of the cart has only the two crossbars - no real bed. I had to rig a bed, with extra-stiff cardboard that I happened to have, to keep smaller items from falling through/off the cart for lack of support underneath. Larger items - no problem - but if I need to move several smaller items, or even just one item too heavy to carry, I had to find something to lay across the bed or balance carefully - and that balance usually didn't last through all the turns required to get to the back door or the laundry room. If that one thing was different I would probably have been singing a much more enthusiastic song. I'm sure this was designed with something entirely different in mind, but that's how it effects me.

rating 1.0. Milky water and black spots, and TERRIBLE customer service I bought this pan to use as a water pan for my dogs. After using it only for a few days, I noticed that the water would get "milky" after it sat for awhile in the pan. So, I washed it down again using nothing more than some mild dish soap and a sponge. Again, the water looked milky after sitting awhile. Then I started to notice black spots appearing on the finish. At that point, I didn't want to risk my dogs' health further so I stopped using the pan. I sent Behrens and email asking about the problems, but got no response. Then I tried calling (after having to search around Behrens' website for the number), and got a voice mail. No return call by someone from Behrens. Owning a $10 pan shouldn't be this much trouble, so it's going back to Amazon or it's going to get tossed into the garage and used there. My dogs will be getting stainless steel pans!
