rating 5.0. Awesome 0 to 3 in mic set For the money I think everybody should have one of these, I wish I could have found a zero to 6 in set. The fit and finish your excellent and the only negative thing I can say is that the heat shield is some thin cheap plastic that is screwed versus bonded to the micrometer frame. Of course just my luck the first time I used the 1-in mic I ended up sliding it off the edge of my bench on accident and it missed my rubber floor mat by about an inch and ended up chipping the plastic on the side. The good news is that even after smacking the concrete fairly hard damage to the plastic heat shield with the only thing I can discern wrong with it I immediately checked it against the standard supplied with the set and it came back in perfect calibration. The fit and finish is awesome on these and would be a great addition to any toolbox that come with standards and calibration instructions. They have a ratcheting thimble that seems to be a little bit loose for life to res urrect as tight as it needs to be to read zero but that may just be the need to slightly correct the calibration. Overall very happy wish I could find a 0 to 6-in set of these.

rating 5.0. Cheap and connectors that will work in a pin All things considered these are fairly reasonable cheap n connectors. When tested up to 900 MHz I didn't really see any impedance bumps caused by the connectors. The biggest attractor I would say is the crimp on furel it's just a little bit too small for rg8, RG 213, lmr400 type cables. You can capture a little bit of braid and get it where it should be and crimp it on if you really really work at it with a pair of channel locks and a hammer, don't ask! And you can successfully get the cable put together. a couple of them had bad threads so check for that before you go to all the effort of putting a cable on it. Oh yeah, and don't forget to slide your heat shrink onto the cable first.

rating 5.0. Very versatile hand come along that is worth more than its purchase price. This is a great little hand come along that has plenty strong for a tremendous amount of tasks. At Wheat State welding LLC and Derby Kansas, I carry two of the small ones and two of the big ones on my truck all the time, and I can't believe how useful they are I've never had a problem with these and they have plenty of features that you don't find in a come along at this price point. I wouldn't hesitate to recommend this to anyone. It's priced in size right for the home user, and heavy duty enough for the commercial user to get great reliable service on a daily basis with.

rating 5.0. The perfect caster for large projects or gate wheels over rough ground. This tire lives up to its description and then some. This is my go-to wheel on large gates at car dealerships etc. I install these pretty often at Wheat State welding LLC in Derby Kansas. This is a great product that a wholeheartedly recommend.

rating 5.0. Excellent Live center for the money This Live center absolutely rocks on down the road! It has great bearings, a great design and will probably Outlast the owner. I use it in a commercial fabrication shop, Wheat State welding LLC in Derby Kansas. It's very accurate with very little total indicated run out. the only thing I wish it had was a little stub on the back so that you could eject it from your tailstock by running the tailstock all the way back. I think you could tap some threads into the back of it because there's a hole that's almost perfectly sized for 3/8 threads and put a bolt in there like a socket head cap screw or maybe just a stud and that would solve that problem.

rating 5.0. Excellent economy priced carbide Burr, I will buy tons of these in the future These are excellent carbide burrs for the price. My first day I put them to use cutting steel and aluminum in my welding shop. these are good quality carbide burrs that are likely to give years of service. When using with aluminum a little bit of beeswax keeps them from loading up badly with material.

rating 2.0. You get what you pay for nothing more Although these four steps work for minor hobby use, the fit in Finnish on these is horrible. There looks to be a lot of hand filing, and very rough machine work on them. They grip extremely well, but tend to pop open at the most inopportune time if you put them under any load at all. The larger ones are okay but the smaller ones are junk.
rating 5.0. The best flap wheels in the world benchmark abrasives are the only thing you'll find in my shop because they work better than Norton and cost less than everything else

rating 5.0. Handy little test indicator good value for the money This is a great little test indicator if it's fairly rugged. I use it everyday in my shop at least a welding LLC.,  and it has proven to be a workhorse on my Mill.

rating 5.0. The only flap disc you'll find in my welding shop. Wait State welding LLC I live benchmark abrasives and every way their quality is outstanding and the value for the dollar spent will always make them welcome in my shop! as their product line expands I cannot wait to try their new offerings. This is an outstanding product! Wheat State welding LLC.

rating 5.0. High quality core for amateur radio and power supply use. This is a well-made product clearly intended for use in a gigantic switch mode power supply. I use it as an RFI choke and balun core for amateur radio HF use. I have not found any issues with quality or saturation up to 2 kilowatts and beyond.the only thing that could make this better would be some technical documentation that tells you what frequency range these cores are appropriate for. In order to check that for your application
In order to check that for your application I would suggest making a 10 turn primary and a second term primary transformer wound on the core in bifeller arrangement. Connect a secondary to a dummy load and the primary to your signal generator. the frequency ranges that you see good power transfer and low SWR are the frequency ranges this is appropriate for as a balun. The frequency ranges that it's not appropriate for are the ones you want to use it as an RFI choke. As I gather more data I will update this review.

rating 5.0. These are great value in hard steel crush washers. in spite of what the name says these washers do not crush in any way shape or form. They are made out of high-quality hard steel. They are however very good good quality crush washers. I used mine for clocking a muzzle brake correctly. You may need to lap yours as I did mine on a whetstone in order to get it to the perfect thickness so that your muzzle brake will clock correctly. Very high quality

rating 5.0. Tremendous value in heat shrink tubing! This is an incredible value for heat shrink tubing. I bought two of these and I'm pretty sure I may never need to purchase it again in my life. You get a ton of heat shrink tubing for the little amount of money you spend on it. Most of this has to do one ratio shrink tubing, and none of it has a glue on the inside. Even with that in mind this is still a tremendous value for anyone that regularly needs small diameter heat shrink tubing for electronics applications.

rating 5.0. You get what you pay for. Good bottles for short-term use but don't expect nalgene quality The squeeze bottle seem to work okay in the short-term without any issues, but they have a real sketchy feel to them when you're using them. They seem to be made out of a different resin or something else difference between them and for example a nalgene bottle. I've only used them for a couple of months and they seem to be working fine. I know for certain they're resistant to acetone, I believe in me k as well, gasoline, oil, alcohol. I have doubts about how they're going to hold up long term.
rating 4.0. Good performing integrated circuit voltage regulator These regulator ic work well. Not much else to say

rating 3.0. This is one of the few affordable 10 amp variable voltage bench supplies out there. For the price this is been a very good 10 amp bench supply. Although it seems kind of cheaply made I have two of these supplies and they serve yeoman's duty day in and day out doing everything from charging lithium iron phosphate batteries to power in industrial equipment for lab testing. I have never had a problem out of either one of these supplies and I'm sure I will get years of good usage out of them. These are switch mode design power supplies. I don't see any filtering in these, so I'm guessing there will be a fair amount of RF Birdies out there when used with sensitive RF test equipment. I will do further testing and update the review.

rating 5.0. This is a good versatile test indicator stem. I bought two of these because my son and I both work in the same machine shop and after he hit his test indicator with a dead blow hammer and broke his old stem I figured I should probably have a spare too. This stem exceeded my every expectation, and was a million times better than the OEM equipment on most test indicators. I would suggest buying a couple of these to replace the factory one they come with your indicator. I wish I would have bought one of these 10 years ago before I needed it. Overall great product!

rating 5.0. This is a great compact easy to hold deburring tool. This is my go-to deburring tool for lots of projects in the shop. The hex shaped handle keeps it from rolling off the bench. The only downside is I'm like plastic body tools this one gets hot in summer, and freezing cold in the winter if you don't have a heated and cooled shop environment. Otherwise I love this deburring tool in every way oh, and use it several times a week. I'm convinced it will last at least 10 or 15 years, or until one of the other guys in the shop needs it more than I do stop. They're cheap enough I plan on giving more away as Christmas presents.

input:
rating 5.0. A good upgrade for the Ender 3 I recently switched to direct drive, putting a bit more weight on the x axis with the stepper and added parts. I decided to look into what options were available for dual z for added rigidity to take on the extra weight. This product was one of the only ones with Prime shipping but with lack of any reviews I was skeptic.

The overall quality is good. All parts were included but it does lack directions, though in one of the above pictures they tell you what search term to use to view their assembly/instruction video. Some advice just to look more professional, I would include a card with QR code or something that links to the install video or a pdf with instructions.

The z lead screws were straight and good quality. No visible bend or warping.

The only gripe I really have is that the screws are not Hex. I would have really liked it if they stuck with the same screw type as the stock Ender 3 screws so when maintenance is needed, I can use the same Hex wrenches on everything.

Overall, the solution works and is of good quality.

If I encounter any problems after testing I will update.

*Update*

After about 2 months of use I have decided to update and give a full 5 starts. This product does what it is meant if installed correctly and I have no problems. Some upgrades cause more problems than they solve but this product works great at a price point better than most other similar solutions.

rating 5.0. A great upgrade that is easy to install. I wish the ender 3/pro had come from the factory like this. I'd noticed that my x gantry didn't always stay level. Even after messing with it several times it just didn't always move perfectly. This fixed all of that.

The hardest part is making sure your gantry is level before tightening everything down.

One final note, you may want to print a power supply relocation bracket before installing since you will need to move your power supply from the stock location.

rating 1.0. Belt is too small, makes kit unable to be used. [[VIDEOID:a9619f41259147f43974abfddc225b7c]] Belt is FAR TOO TIGHT and makes the kit pretty much useless. Even in the install video, they skip over the part where they struggle to install the second pulley using editing.  Great idea, poor execution.  It's so tight that there's no way that the stepper motor can overcome the load added.

rating 1.0. Belt is too small, makes kit unable to be used. [[VIDEOID:a9619f41259147f43974abfddc225b7c]] Belt is FAR TOO TIGHT and makes the kit pretty much useless. Even in the install video, they skip over the part where they struggle to install the second pulley using editing.  Great idea, poor execution.  It's so tight that there's no way that the stepper motor can overcome the load added.

rating 5.0. Super service from the supplier The belt supplied is around 4 teeth short.

Definitely not made for the ender 3.

Edit: The supplier has returned with a solution and for that I will give 5 stars

rating 2.0. Belt too short Like a few of the other reviewers mentioned, the belt I was supplied is just too tight for the printer to function. The tensioner is not even installed. The belt installed per the video is as tight as a drum. I'll order a belt that slightly longer and put the tensioner to use. Seems like a good kit other than the belt.

rating 5.0. Updated Review: Had a issue, but resolved very well. New Review >> Dec-2020, I wrote an "ok" review for this product, the seller reached out to me and we discussed the issue. Overall, very simple - the initial belt supplied was a bit too short. Because it was short, when assembling it, I had to pull everything inboard slightly to get it to fit and that caused some binding. Long story short, the seller was in constant contact and has provided a slightly longer (2mm) belt. After removing the old one and making the kit"square" like it should have been from day 1, I popped the longer belt on and it worked flawlessly. Was able to get it to travel full up and down with no binding. Very pleased that the seller stood behind his product and went what I will say is the extra mile (KM) to earn my business. I would not hesitate to get this kit (with the longer 618mm belt).

Old review>>> I've had this and have installed it and kept past my return period - so I have time to write an honest review....

The online instructions are great - after you find them - there needs to be a link or mention of this INSIDE of the packaging. The bag of parts you get is spooky and scary until you watch the video - then it all makes sense.

The belt - there is a lot of talk about the belt being too shore and the vendor stating that it is not - well, the belt works - BUT it is too short - let me tell you why. The belt forces you to draw the rollers at the top of the shafts together to get it on - by doing this the lead screws come out of alignment - so this means the carriage binds up when it travels upwards. If you print only on the bottom 1/3 of your Z distance - it works great - but the misalignment causes issues as the gantry travels upwards. Simply adding 2 or 3 additional teeth AND a idler pulley that could accomodate would solve this issue.

I would be happy to revise this review if the manufacturer were to reach out to me and provide some updated parts to try. I am presently using it as I cannot return it, but have to consider larger prints as something I have to do extra steps to to make work properly.

rating 1.0. Belt too short.  Binds the Z Axis when it gets near the top.  Cant print tall objects As others have said, the belt is too short.  The vendor says they are shipping a longer one.  Well their not.  I just got it today and installed it (9/10/2020).  The Z Axis shafts are closer together at the top then they are at the bottom.  It would work for shorter prints both nothing tall.  I sent the vendor a message about it to see if they would send me a longer belt to fix the issue.  If I get the longer belt I will edit this review to indicate that.  Till that happens I would definitely pass on this.
rating 2.0. Belt is too tight It’s a good idea but the belt is too tight… Needs one or two extra teeth or a couple extra millimeters

rating 5.0. Fantastic upgrade for your 3D printer I purchased this kit to use with a highly modified Creality Ender3. My X gantry is now 400mm wide, and dual Z was highly desirable for keeping things nice and level. Some folks use dual motor kits, but that requires recalibration of the motors on a regular basis. Mechanical dual-Z seemed like a much better option, and this kit did not disappoint. I should add that I also have 420mm of Z travel now, so I HAD to do something one-off.

I bought this kit along with some extra idler wheels, 700mm z-screw rods, and an 1140mm belt. Everything was found on Amazon. Once I had all the parts, installation was easy, and fast. This kit has flat plates that mount on top of the riser legs, but it comes with longer screws to do so. Initial level adjustment was quick, and it has been dead-on level since. A little synthetic grease with PTFE took out all squeaks/noise in the drive screws, and travel is super smooth. I could not be happier with this kit, and think any Ender3 (or similar printer) of any size will greatly benefit from this reliable, mechanical dual-Z upgrade kit.

Jose

rating 3.0. Works, overpriced. Ive run this mod for 20hrs print time now..
So, it all works as advertised, assembly is a pain, but your bolting it into an ender 3, so you should have expected that coming.. If you got your machine together right the first time, levelling all the bits correctly, then you will get this done as well. If ya get all haphazard with it, I could see how it would bind, but that has to do with your printer as well.

The belt is fine, the 2nd z axis is left floating here, but ive had no issue with that, and plan to slap another motor on it anyways.

My complaints lie with the screws, and the cost.. I mean, I've filled my room with cheap china stuff, but these have to be the cheapest screws I've got on hand.. There are extras, I can only assume because if they are removed again, you will need them. And $60.. Woah.. I was drinking when I bought this thing, and thats my only excuse. If your sober, you could save a bit by just getting the parts you need tracked down yourself. They could of tossed in a 24v stepper for that, IMO.

I dont have an issue with the product, it is gonna stay on my machine, and is working as advertised, but...

rating 4.0. Power supply grounding The product requires you to relocate the power supply not a problem for me however please note the power supply needs to be grounded to the printer chassis. I did it by taking  a wire off the power supply case and onto the screen mounting bolt it's taky but it works.

rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY THIS This item is a complete scam and low quality product sold on Amazon. The bearings were way too small that you could not fit the z axis rod into the bearing, literally could not even force it. Wrong parts! Belt was obviously
Too small, the bearing for the center guide was missing an insert sleeve. Worst product ever, I am 100% certain any of these positive reviews are fake or paid for.

rating 4.0. Solid product worth buying I noticed the added stability and accuracy immediately. I have been printing cable chains and when the print head would zig zag quickly over a narrow space, the X axis would shake a tiny amount - I could see and hear it. The prints still came out great, but I knew I could get better and ditch the annoying shaking sound. This product was the solution. Your printer and prints will really benefit from this upgrade.

Two things keep this product from getting a 5 star review.

1) No instructions, just a video on YouTube they refer you to. That sucks, give us instructions - it's not rocket science, but paper instructions would really be nice and speed up the installation process.

2) The quality of the hardware is garbage... Way too soft, strips and bends easily. Be careful! You get a set of hardware with a phillips head and a set of hardware with a hex head. I had everything installed before I realized this... I had the phillips head hardware installed and hated it. I had to start over and replace with the hex head hardware. Just ditch the crappy phillips head hardware and make the hex head standard.

The belt fits! Yes, it's tight. I have the tensioner set to about 10% out - it didn't need to be any tighter. Don't tighten down the top brackets all the way before you install the belt. With a little pressure, it pops right on. Overall, great upgrade. Watch the video one time through first - organize all of the parts and hardware in separate piles - then take your time installing. You'll be happy with the results.

rating 4.0. A must have upgrade if you add direct drive. This is a must have if you changed your ender 3 to direct drive.  The print quality is much better.  I will add one warning that is not the fault of the people that make this kit.  The top brackets don't necessarily align with the servo position.  They align with the center of the vertical supports.  The problem is that the creality printers aren't built to exact specs so the left hand lift screw does not set exactly in the center of the vertical supports.  The solution is simple.  File the holes in the top bracket so it can be adjusted in the X direction one way or the other.  I tried the aluminum blocks that can be ordered separately but they also have the same problem.  There can be a small alignment error in the Y direction with them.  Its easier to file the holes on the supplied brackets than shim the aluminum block.

rating 3.0. Made a big improvement...but hard to recommend. First, let me say that this Z axis mod made the single biggest improvement in printed objects that I've done so far. Layer to layer is very much improved due to stability of the extruder. But there were two problems that prevented the kit from even working at all as delivered.
The first item has been mentioned before and is entirely unforgivable. Without a collar under the bearing on the new drive screw....it will allow the pulley to climb up above the bearing distorting the belt path and NOT driving the screw in sync with the one on the stepper. BAD!
The second issue was the spacer under the tensioner bearings. It was WAY (by about 6mm) too tall and nearly above the belt.
Once I made a collar for the added drive screw and locked it under the bearing, and shortened the bushing it worked great. And I'm very happy with the results. Fixing those two issues makes it all worth while.

rating 5.0. A must, even more so if you go direct drive extruder. I would say this is a must for an Ender 3. From what I can read online, they all have sag on the right side of the gantry. Installing this kit will completely eliminate that sag, in turn making your bed and prints that much more leveled out. After install, don't forget to level the gantry one last time. Am easy way to do this, on the right side worm gear, loosen the set screws for the belt, and at the same time raise the gantry. Then tighten the set screws back. See my pics for what I mean. Doing that leveled out my gantry within a 1/16th. Also, the belt seems short but it isn't, you need to wrap the belt around 1 gear, then take the other gear(while it's not attached to the worm drive) and stretch it onto the worm drive, then tighten the set screws.

rating 1.0. Missing parts. I did not receive all of the parts in my kit. The bearings were missing. Both z lead bearings and the 2 belt tension bearings were not in my kit. Other than that the kit seems great.

rating 5.0. My printer is not only level, it's square. Not only is my bed actually level with this modification, it's actually square!

All Cartesian 3d printers (Ender 3, CR-10, etc.) that only have one side of the x-gantry being pushed up are going to suffer from some sag on the side without the lead screw. This means that no matter what, when you level your bed, even if you level it perfectly, it won't actually be square along the axis. All your prints will have be slightly leaning left. Squares won't be square, they'll be a very slight rhombus. This kit fixes that and you don't have to wire up a new stepper motor.

rating 4.0. Definintely helps with print quality but no install instructions! Being a noob to the 3D print world, you keep reading about what mods/upgrades are suggested for better quality prints as well as what will make calibrating your machine or printing easier. It was suggested that for the Ender 3 V2 to install a dual z axis kit and after watching a "billion" youtube videos over and over I decided on the kit with the belt instead of a second motor. My decision was really based off of 2 things.... I didn't want to have to worry about another motor and with the belt version there was less chance of the 2 sides getting out of sync.

There are 2 things that were frustrating about this kit.

1) No instructions. The are some good YouTube videos so it wasn't too bad of an install. Just watch the videos a few times each before attempting the install for any fellow noobs reading this review. PS.. the belt is the right size.

2) When I got to the assembly of the bearing for the lead screw there was too much paint on the threads where you needed to secure the bearing to the bracket. So it took forever to slowly turn the screw in a bit and then back out over and over, little by little, until the screw was all the way in. What sucked is that the phillips head on the screws were almost stripped because I need to use so much force and a lot if back and forth motion. Eventually you will get it through, just be patient and don't force it. If you have a way to clear the paint first that might be easier, otherwise this will be the most time consuming part of the install.

The good thing about this kit was it came with a set of hex head screws for everything. The phillips screws are ok but they can be stripped easily if you don't use the right size phillips screwdriver. So I used the hex head screws for the final assembly.

After I got everything installed and calibrated/adjusted.... I must say it does make a difference. Is it 100% necessary? Probably not, unless you are one of those persons that needs super quality prints. Just take your time with the assembly and remember to add a little "super lube" to the lead screws.
rating 1.0. Belt is Short My belt was way too short and ruined this item as well as all prints.

rating 5.0. A good addition! This has proven to be a good addition to my Voxelab Aquila 3D printer. The install wasn't to bad if you follow the video. My prints have been much better. I also added the two spring loaded anti-backlash screw nuts.

current_product_reviews:
rating 3.0. Seriously under-engineered, the driven lead screw is allowed to float up and down. I wanted a belt drive and not a second stepper motor as the latter can get out of sync.
This kit doesn't do anything to hold the right side lead screw fixed allowing it to float up or down, thereby making it a useless mod. The left side is held captive by the stepper motor bearings and can't move.
I needed to add a collar to the new lead screw below the upper bearing and along with two plastic collars that were in the kit, I clamped it with the the pulley, a bit rube-goldberg but it works.
The belt is just long enough to make it between the two screw centers so no tensioner was necessary.
I also found it necessary to swap out the 8mm nuts with brass anti-backlash parts to get rid of all the play. A 8mm handwheel makes it easy to manually position the Z axis.
My machine is an Ender 3V2.
other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. I like that the extruder is 90 deg to the carriage. This bracket allows the extruder to be placed at a right angle to the hot end which makes it easier to see and feed the filament. I also bought the double gear extruder drive show in the picture but not included, the two  are a great inexpensive upgrade to a virtual direct drive setup.
rating 4.0. I got the right part but no set screws My hand wheel didn’t include any set screws, I used some screws I had on hand.

rating 5.0. We had one of these when I was a kid and that was 50+ years ago. When I was a kid growing up in Brooklyn in the 1960's we had one of these for the front porch.
I bought this because I wanted a mat that would not allow water to puddle underneath it, the open weave does that nicely.
It's actually soft underfoot, the tire tread sections have some give to them and it does a good job knocking the goose poop off our shoes from the yard 😉
It rolls up fairly well, but lays out nice and flat.
Highly Recommend.
rating 3.0. My part had a bend in it The piece I received had a bend in it, it is still usable for me, but it shouldn't have been bent.

rating 5.0. Love Superlube, this can is a bargain. I had bought this in the past in the tube, the can is much more economical and doesn't get lost so easily.
Being a silicone base its fine for rubber and I use it to lube spark plug boots.
Its also food grade so it can be used to relube the gearboxes on stand mixers.
I use it for bearings on bicycles and other electro mechanical devices.
Some folks said that the cans they received were short on product but mine measured 453 grams which is fine, 400 for the grease and 53 for the can.

rating 5.0. Works well with particle board I needed to attach some slides to a particle board (Melamine) cabinet and these worked very well. I used a 3/8 bit for the whole and a step drill to make the recess for the top of the insert. I used 5 minute epoxy to make sure they stay in tight, so far so good.

rating 5.0. Fixed my snow blower In most of the reviews that I saw on-line, people generally use this belt as a replacement for a table saw or some other stationary constant speed application.

I had an old Simplicity snow blower with a 4L V belt that was used to engage the auger. I ordered the belt before I had read on-line that these belts shouldn't be used for clutching applications, at least that is the recommendation for the similar red colored version of this belt made by Fenner.

I saw no such warning from Jason Industrial the manufacturer of this belt so I decided to give it a try.

So far it worked well during a relatively light snow fall we had this week.
The last belt snapped ripping the top of the belt with the cords in two, and it was a farm duty Kevlar cord belt.
Time will tell, how long this will last, but if it does break again, I have spare segments to replace any that get damaged.
The other thing that I like about these is that the cord runs through each segment so it may be stronger then a standard belt, again we will see.

I've used these belts before on my woodworking power tools and had good luck with them, they do reduce vibration significantly and I've had no failures with them.
Update:
A couple of snow falls later and the belt is still holding it's own. The last snow involved some heavy slush at the end of the driveway it it powered through it.
rating 5.0. Work as they should These inexpensive plastic fittings work well, none failed on me.
rating 5.0. great It's a diode, worked for what i needed, quick delivery, great price

input:
rating 4.0. Need instruction on how to assemble it. These small fishing magnet does not come with any instruction which seller definately need to include one.
I save everyone the trouble and google proper way to assemble these. The main screw goes through the bottom of the magnet, on top put on the flat washer, then the lock washer follow by the loop bolt. You will need a Philip screw driver to tighten it.
The magnet itself works fine. Not sure it holds 75# but works for my need.

rating 5.0. Not falling off the fridge! My wife likes to put pictures and calendars and notepads for shopping lists on the fridge and they fall off all the time. These will definitely do the trick and keep anything you stick on the fridge secure.

rating 5.0. Small and Strong Don't let the size fool you. These are six strong magnets. I ordered them to help locate nails in the soil. Using the provided eye bolt, I tied each magnet to string and dragged it through dirt I reclaimed from a dry pond and used to level depressions in my yard. Apparently there were rusting nails and other pieces of iron in the soil which presented a challenge to my mower's tires. By systematically dragging these magnets over the soil I was able to remove any rusting iron objects. PS I used 75 lb pull-force because anything more would have been difficult to remove from the magnet. Very effective.

rating 5.0. Very Small but Very Strong The material in these magnets is new to me and seems to be as strong as a magnet of a much larger size. I am very pleased with this purchase and will do business with this seller again.

rating 5.0. Incredibly useful in my camper van I recently bought a "blank slate" mini cargo van with the cargo area being almost completely bare metal - floor, sides, and ceiling. I wanted to outfit it as a camping vehicle without spending a small fortune to have it professionally converted. (Nor do I have a shop or the skills to do a fancy conversion myself.) I watched a DIY camper van video where the owner made extensive use of rare earth magnets. Wow, what a brilliant idea! I initially ordered a package of six of these "fishing magnets" and immediately realized I should have more. I ordered two more packs and already wish I had ordered three. Having the rings attached makes it reasonably easy to detach and move the magnets and also means nylon rope or carabiners can be attached to them. If you've never used rare earth magnets before, I would suggest buying this size before assuming you need something bigger. Many thanks to the previous reviewer who provided photos for how to assemble these magnets - each one has 5 parts! But they go together quickly when you know what you're doing. One last thing: I ordered the first set on a Sunday and received them the next day - which was Memorial Day! I would give 6 stars if I could.
rating 5.0. Super strong and well built. Worked perfectly for what I needed them for.
rating 5.0. Strong magnets The magnets were plenty strong for my use.  I used them to hang up a projection cloth screen over steel storage cabinets.  They held strong with no worries.
rating 3.0. . Tested on dumbbells. These are 30lb magnets sold as 75lbs

rating 5.0. Want to check to see if you accidentally threw a steel can into the aluminum bin?  Here you go. Tied some parachute cord onto one of these & found that the Weruva cat food cans are steel after going “fishing” in my aluminum bin.  Another one is glommed onto my Kitchenaid mixer where it lets me check items for steel content.  They are versatile & will positively secure almost anything to your refrigerator.  Buy them now - you can’t go wrong unless you drop one on your old cassette tapes.

rating 5.0. This stuff lasts I fixed a handle of a Dishwasher and glued the front mirror of my Jeep Wrangler. In both cases - after 6 months - no issues.

rating 5.0. it sticks! This epoxy is amazing! I used it to bond glass to rubber and it adhered with a strong and tight seal. It took about 5-10 minutes to set as advertised, and it was about 24 hours until it fully cured (although I was comfortable moving my project inside after about an hour without worrying about the adhesive failing). The smell is about as strong as any epoxy, just be sure to use it outside or a well-ventilated area when working, and use gloves if you can. It definitely dries clear as well, since I couldn't even tell there was glue there through the glass! Definitely a great buy.

rating 5.0. Holds Extremely Well Under Wide Temperature Swings I used this to hold the suction mount for my navigation to the hard plastic part of my dashboard.  Normally, I don't like epoxy mix glues because (1) they are messy, (2) they don't dry properly if not mixed exactly right and (3) they take a long time to set & dry.  However, after trying and failing with super glue and silicone glue, I tried this after reading the many reviews.  GREAT STUFF!  First of all, it comes out mixed in the correct proportions - just make sure you keep the plungers in sync while pushing them down.  I applied it to the suction cup of the navigation mount, pressed the mount onto the dash and held it in place with duct tape while the glue was curing.  I left my sunroof tilted open to allow the fumes to vent.  Soon after applying the glue, the temperature dropped significantly, to as low as 40 degrees. I was worried that would prevent the glue from curing correctly.  But, no problem.  I removed the duct tape after a full day, tested the bond by applying increasing pressure to try to remove it.  Since I was only mounting a GPS unit, I didn't exert extreme force, but far more than the GPS would place on the mount.  It held tight, so I connected the GPS (a 7" Garmin).  It has now been mounted for several weeks. During this time, I have driven over some rough roads (and my car has a very still suspension) and the temperature in the car has varied from 100 degrees to 40. The mount has held tight and firm.  The glue did give off some odor while curing, which lingered in my car for about a week, but nothing extreme.
rating 1.0. Didnt work The product did not work and this is the second J-B weld product that did not work.  Great marketing on how it works on everything but in reality it does not.

rating 2.0. Laundry Basket Fail... I am a big JBWeld fan and have used their other products for years. This stuff was a major disappointment. I bought it specifically to fix the handle on my wife's laundry baskets. I tried a simple glue joint and it couldn't even support the weight of an empty basket. Fine, whatever. I assumed the reason it didn't stick was due to the smooth surface of the plastic so I hit it with some 150 grit sand paper and cleaned it up with alcohol then applied a very liberal amount of adhesive to the joint and feathered it out and let it set up for a day. That repair lasted about a week. I'm sure it is good for something and plastic can be challenging to repair. Knowing this, I turned to the experts, JBWeld. Fail. If I find something it works on, I will update this post. Two stars because I have hope...

rating 5.0. Worked for Kintsugi I’ve used this for a modern ‘Kintsugi’ style porcelain repair, with satisfactory results.
I wanted to repair a chipped old bowl to reach a Kintsugi style repair while using modern accessible materials. I decided to try the combination of epoxy and golden colored mica powder.
I mixed some powder with the epoxy and applied it in small quantity on the chipped part. I had to factor in the epoxy viscosity, gravity and the relatively quick setting time to get a nicely rounded and smooth patch. At first I didn’t like the gold effect produced by the mix of mica powder with the clear epoxy, so I decided to sprinkle more powder on the surface of the epoxy patch a few minutes into the process and after it started setting. This gave it the desired finish I was looking for.
For this type of application I would recommend this product.

rating 5.0. Fixed my Iron Arch with Ease One of the finials on my iron archway rusted through. I tried fixing it with E6000, which lasted one season. rating 2.0. Downsized plunger system makes it harder to measure equal amounts ClearWeld always worked well for me and I assume it's still a good formula, but J-B Weld has downsized it so small (for the same price, of course) that a Hershey bar -- the old downsized cliche -- looks massive in comparison. The problem is that the plunger-driven openings are so small, it's hard to see whether you're squeezing out an equal amount of epoxy and hardener. That was always something of a problem because you'd usually have to press one side harder than the other -- they never seemed to come out equally. But you could eyeball what you were squeezing out much more easily than you can with the new baby sized version. J-B Weld should just put the material in separate tubes, like a lot of other products.

rating 4.0. Easy to mix and does not set in 1 hour I see a few reviews complaining about the container being leaky and others about the cap welding shut.

It's a syringe.

Both of those problems are solved by pulling the syringe back up after you push out what you need. The remaining liquid is drawn back in and does not mix. I honestly think their packaging is fine and nifty, but I have to agree that perhaps they need to improve it if a large portion of their consumer base has difficulties with it. Additionally, as long as you have applied pressure to the center or entirety of the syringe, equal amounts have to come out of both sides, even if one side comes out first because it is more fluid. I suspect some people pulled the syringe away before it was finished releasing. One side is clear and the other is yellowish, and they settle into semi-circles so you can eyeball the amount if you really feel you need to. In summary, I didn't have issues with mixing at all.

The epoxy definitely set around 5 minutes. It is hard to get a very small amount out of the syringe, but the smallest amount I could get out was enough for what I needed it for. It set quickly enough that I hardly needed tape to hold the pieces of broken porcelain together that I was adhering. The downside was I didn't get to wipe off some of the excess because it set in the time it took for me to finagle some of the final pieces in. For a porcelain/ceramics job, it works well. I can't speak to any other material, but I do believe the mixing is going to be the same across the board. I think it is important to highlight, like a couple of others have, that the epoxy is still tacky after several hours and should be probably be left to cure for a good 24 hours.

rating 2.0. No better than cheap super glue. Maybe worse. Very disappointed. It worked significantly less well than super glue. rating 5.0. Prints well! I only printed a few small prints for benchmarking. It didn't fail once but it does require more exposure time.
On my Phrozen Mini I set the layer exposure time to 4s and first 3 layer at 20s.
I need to print some functional prints, I'll update my review accordingly.

rating 5.0. It's just perfectly splendid! Other than build materials, I have nothing really bad to say about this printer.
I don't mind the flat top building plate, it takes seconds to drain it.

It prints well and it prints fast.
I've had no issues so far printing Siraya Tech Simple clear and Elegoo water washable clear green and gray resins.

Biggest advantage of a mono screen is very short exposure times that increases the LCD's life significantly.
Exposure times are shorter because it's easier for UV light to pass through a monochromatic LCD compared to RGB LCD.

I wish the vat was polished. It's pretty rough and cleaning it with a paper towels pulls hard to remove fibers.
Plastic cover is very thin and feels a bit fragile.

FHD screen isn't as bad as one might think, prints are coming out very detailed and layer lines are rarely noticeable.
I will add some close up shots soon.

General settings for rapid curing resins (0.05mm):
- 2-4 bottom layers
- 12-16 seconds per bottom layer
- 1.8-3 seconds normal layer
- 7 seconds light off delay
- 0 seconds bottom light off delay
- 6mm lifting distance for both
- 60mm/min lifting speed for both
- 180mm/min retraction

Start on the low end and move. up until you achieve optimal quality.

rating 5.0. A Great FEP I've changed my FEP for the first time with one of these. It was super easy and it feels very comparable to the original on my vat (Sonic Mini).
The scrapers that comes with these are excellent, much better than the one that came with my printer. They're sharper and bigger.
Highly recommended!

rating 5.0. Excellent! A super glue that lasts.
The cap design is genius and miraculously prolongs the glue's life significantly.
I've never had a super glue that haven't dried up in just 6 months or less.

Totally worth the extra bang compared to the cheapy ones. It's nice having a super glue ready when you need it.

rating 5.0. Absolutely great It prints great, adheres great, easy to clean and cures super fast.
I have the Phrozen Sonic Mini. This review is for the Ceramic Gray resin.

My optimized settings for this resin at 0.05mm layer height are:
- 2 bottom layers
- 15 seconds each bottom layer exposure
- 2.6-2.8 seconds normal layer exposure (lower times work too but prints don't come out as detailed)
- 8 seconds light of delay for both
- 8mm bottom lifting distance
- 6mm normal lifting distance
- 60mm/min lifting speed for both
- 120mm/min retraction.

It smells a bit stronger than my Siraya Tech .Simple.

rating 1.0. Wouldn't print There's a chance the resin I got is just from a bad batch, I couldn't get even one successfull print out of it.
9 prints out of 9 failed so far no matter what settings I use.
I have a Phrozen Sonic Mini and before using this resin, I've never experienced failed prints.
Only the first 30-50 layers stay on the building plate, then the prints detaches and sticks to the FEP.

I've never experienced this issue with Elegoo water washable or SirayaTech Simple resins.
My settings for those are 2s normal, 15s two bottom layers, 60mm/min lifting speeds and 150mm/min retraction speed.

rating 5.0. An Amazing Vacuum Update 5/18/20:
Phil still works great, latest update breaks our home into rooms letting me vacuum each room individually. The biggest improvement for me is the ability to save maps. Now I can vacuum multiple floors (we have 3) and have my virtual walls saved.
In order to have maps of each floor saved, I'd have to move Phil's charger as well. I wish I didn't have to, I don't see the point in doing that.

Original review:
We had a Coredy dumb robot vacuum (Rosie) that did a great job cleaning our 750sqft apartment but it took it close to 2 hours to clean our whole apartment creeping around randomly.
Our Roborock S5 (Phil, after the French maid minion from Despicable Me movies) does it in 30 minutes and has virtual no go zones and borders, parts are very cheap, dust bin is bigger and filters are washable. It is loud. Loud enough to be irritating. On "Quiet" mode it's still fairely louder than our old Rosie but it's just so much smarter and faster.
rating 5.0. Prints great, looks great 205/60, 60mm/s on my Ender 3. Prints come out perfect with minimal stringing. Beautiful color. I will add pictures soon.

rating 5.0. Prints great! I've used it with my Ender 3 printing at 60mm/s, 205°/60°, 5mm retraction.
No issues, minimal stringing and beautiful color. A fantastic filament.

rating 4.0. Just like the one that came with my printer I just received it today and found a two issues.
First, the thermistor's wires seem squashed, hopefully they're not badly damaged.
Second, the filament guiding tube's hole is off center (just like the original that came with my Ender 3) making it useless to me, I won't even bother trying to use it.

I ended up installing the hot end with my original thermistor and a capricorn tube. Works great.

rating 5.0. Prints Great, Indestructible It doesn't print like TPU, more like PLA, but an extremely durable and somewhat flexible PLA.
Color is very similar to item's pictures. I got the green and it's a very soft nice looking green.
I found it prints best at 205-210°c, 75° bed, 6mm retraction. I printed at 40mm/s but I think I could go faster.
Stringing isn't too bad for a TPU filament, much better than softer TPUs.
It wouldn't stick to my bed without applying some glue stick to it.
I wanted to print durable containers and casings out of it, I hoped it will be stiffer but it's still softer than I would like.
It can't be stretched at all. Other reviewer mentioned its flexibility is similar to a Pyrex cover and I agree. If you print it thin, 3mm thick and lower, it's pretty bendy.
I wish it was stiffer but I still like it and find it more usable (for me) compared to softer TPUs.
6 months later, still prints great. By far my favorite filament.

rating 1.0. The worst filament I have I bought this filament in a lightning deal for $12.74.
It's extremely brittle, do NOT leave it in your printer overnight because it will fall apart in your extruder. When it prints, no matter what setting I've tried, it's always underextruding. Prints come out just okay with very rough top layer.
For PLA, it requires way too much tweaking. I wouldn't recommend this filament.

rating 5.0. Excellent filament Set it to 210-220/60 to get a nice shiny more metallic look. Just note that at these temperatures overhang might sag a bit without support.
I printed Gustav the turtle at 60% scale, 0.15 layer height, 0% infill, 3 layers on top bottom and sides, 80mm/s, 210/60 with just a tiny sag at the chin.
It's nicely packaged and spooled as well.
Bulbasaur was printed at 205/60°c, 0.15mm layer height, 6mm retraction with Cura's new tree support (works great).

rating 3.0. So so quality Paint job is pretty bad, some springs are exposed in some spots, 3 were stuck together with paint.
These springs didn't improve at all the bed stability on my Ender 3 3D printer. Actually they are very comparable to the original springs length and force wise.
Mine came from China, so it took about a month to arrive here.

rating 3.0. Great filament! Prints great, little to no stringing, bright white. It's packaged in a very Chinese style packaging but it is vaccum sealed with a desiccant and so far it's been printing great.
Prints better than my pastel blue Solutech and black LabelPros filaments and its rolled perfectly with no tangles (unlike the LabelPros one).

Update 10/8/19:
I updated my rating from 5 to 3 stars.
This filament is highly sensitive to moisture (more brittle, stringy, poor layer adhesion), much more than all my other filaments. I had to dry 2 times in the past 4 months.

rating 4.0. A Bit Harder To Print I use it with my Ender 3 at 200/60 degrees.
It's good most of the time but I feel like it's a bit harder to print compared to my black LabelPros spool. It also doesn't stick to my hair spray covered glass bed as easily.
Print qualiy is good, layer adhesion is just okay, wounding is very good.
I managed to reduced stringing to a minimum tinkering with my slicing app. The benchy boat was my first print with this filament thus the stringing.

Update 8/9/18:
Okay, after printing Sir Snake I can definitely say this filament is lacking. Stringing is way worse than my LabelPros black filament even at 185 degrees and 2mm retraction. It seems layer adhesion isn't good, all the snake's ribs (100% infill) snap easily.
I don't know why, but the dog wistle came out much better with similar settings.
Something about this filament is inconsistent. I didn't have these issues with any other filaments I have.
I gave this filament 2 stars just because some prints did come out good.
I would say use this filament only for decorative pieces, nothing with thin sections. Super glue seems to hold it together very well though.

I can't recommend this filament.

Update 1/3/19:
Bumping up retraction to 8mm helped a lot to minimize stringing.

rating 5.0. Prints Great I got the two yellow 0.5kg pack. I'd prefer if it was opaque.
I use it with my new Ender 3 (my first) printer. This is my first TPU filament but so far I had only successful prints.
I print at 220-230/60 degrees, 40mm/s and it comes out good. 2mm retraction seems to help a bit reducing stringing.
Make sure it flows smoothly through your nozzle before each print.
Very durable, impossible to break prints. It doesn't stretch much and it has hard as TPU phone case when printed at the same thickness.
My first print was a benchy boat and other than some minor stringing and a defect on the boat's front bottom it came out pretty good.
I added a picture of cover cap for M365 scooter charger plug.

input:
rating 5.0. Best borescope/endoscope I have used with my phone. This is my third endoscope. By far this is the best. It has the right amount of stiffness for most jobs. My other units would typically need me to tape stiff wire along the cable to get stiffness.

It is a bit futsy connecting to the wifi, and if the unit turns off you have to reconnect to the wifi. But there are advantages that I think make it worth it. For one thing, both my son and I could connect at the same time. He was fishing the endoscope through, and I was watching as it moved. I was able to spot things that he was missing as he concentrated on the mechanics of moving. I can also see where it would be handy if you wanted to monitor a situation. For example, set it up at a spot in the pipe and then go and turn on water. Or you could position it in a crevice and use your phone to monitor for rodents.

As someone else noted, with the light on full the battery only lasts a few minutes. But there are a couple of offsetting factors. One is that you probably will not run the lights at full power, even if you are probing in total darkness. Full brightness tends to wash out the detail The other is that you can hook up any external battery pack you would use for a cell phone. Or you can even plug it into a wall charger. The unit will run as long as it is getting external power.

The tools come in a little container. That is very nice. It is also good that they screw on securely instead of snapping on. I have lost tools because they snapped off in a drain. The tools are not perfect. They are all plastic. I am used to long, thin metal hooks. This one is shorter than the focal length; I would like it to be at least as long as the focal length. It is also plastic and seems flimsy. I do like to keep the hook attached as the default. I find that in a lot of cases it helps guide the scope and gives a frame of reference.

The instructions say to change the resolution. I ignored that at first, but once I reset the scope's resolution to match my phone the picture quality and apparent depth of field improved dramatically.

rating 5.0. Actually saved hours worth of work Okay. I told this thing that if it got me out of the mess I was in I would write it a positive review. And a deal's a deal. I had been working on my vehicle; replacing intake manifold gaskets. The engine intakes had been carefully covered with a towel to keep anything from dropping down into them, and I was just beginning re-assembly when I noticed that the mating surfaces were kinda grungy. I moved the towel off of one of the intake ports and spritzed a little solvent onto a rag. On taking my finger off the spray nozzle, it slowly lifted up off of the can, rotated gently in mid-air a few times and then dropped straight into the *only* uncovered opening. Nothing but net. I could not have made a better shot using a funnel. But there it was. Fortunately, this was not one of those pencil-eraser sized sprayers but looked more like what's on a can of shaving cream. Problem was, I could see it (sorta; in the right light and standing on my head) but I just couldn't get to it. I tried with my finger, and I'm sure we all know how helpful that was. I tried one of the little flex grabbers and that just pushed it down further. I even tried using a shop vac to drag it out of there, and I'd swear there was someone holding it from the other side. I mean, I had enough suction that I was pulling oil out of the crankcase but this thing wouldn't budge. So while in my resigned, peaceful Zen-like meditation of deciding whether to ponder the meaning of life or simply let out a blast of language that would keep the neighborhood kids in therapy for years, I recalled having used a fiber-optic scope with a hook on the end of it many, many moons ago. And where does one go to find something like that these days? Amazon. In looking over various options, I decided on this little guy as the best fit between price, function and features. It arrived on time and in a box that was significantly smaller than I had expected. Setup was relatively painless and the app is pretty simple to use. I initially tested the camera with my Android phone but soon realized that, for my use case, I was going to want something a bit larger and easier to see without having to hold. I installed the app onto an old tablet running Android Kit-Kat, which took off and worked just as well as my current-version phone. The moment of truth had arrived. I threaded the hook attachment onto the front of the camera (for those that may not notice, there is a trim ring at the front of the camera that must be removed in order to use any of the extra, supplied tools). I also noticed some people report experiencing issues with the camera staying connected. I found, as a few others suggested, that if you turn off your cellular data the connection stays solid. My guess is that the WiFi transmitter just isn't intended to let the neighbors down the street tune in and live-stream the show. So if it drifts a bit, and your phone is set to auto-toggle between WiFi and Cell to maintain the best reception, you're liable to get knocked off. If you're seeing a connectivity problem, try turning off the Cell data and see if it clears up. Anyway, I have to admit that it took me about ten minutes to get my bearings on how to orient this round camera in a square hole 3D environment. My first foray knocked the little devil down about another half-inch or so. That might not sound like much, but when you're already panicked it seemed like it had fallen off a cliff. So after a few minutes of out-of-the-scary-place practice, I was ready to try again. Though I wouldn't really call it easy, I did find that as I used it, and varied the light level to minimize reflections coming off the polished metal sections, it became more apparent how I needed to position the camera for optimum grab. And after about two minutes of rotating and lifting and corner turning, I saw that the cap was moving as I began to withdraw camera. Very carefully, very slowly, and offering prayers to pretty much every deity I could think of (in fact, I think I still need to sacrifice a cactus under a full moon while wearing orange parachute pants for one of those guys), I had cleared the housing and had the cap in my hand! After a few moments of jubilant dancing and high-fiving everything around me (there's one very confused squirrel at my place, now, BTW) I then threw it and the can it had come from as far and as hard as I could toward the back of the house. About half-way through its arc, I truly expected to see it take a hook turn into one of the windows. But it apparently decided it had done enough damage for one lifetime and simply bounced off into the weeds. And as far as this little camera goes - it did what I needed it to do, for a good price, with only a moderate learning curve and plenty of extra cable had I needed to find something further down. I, for one, am quite satisfied with both the product and the outcome for which it was purchased. It saved me more than its cost just in aggravation, not to mention the additional parts that would have been required had I had to dismantle the top of the motor. I give it a job well done.

rating 5.0. Doesn't get better than this Doesn't get better than this. Be aware that you do have to pay for lab test results. Duh! Of COURSE you have to pay for lab tests. This is the kit that puts it together and explains how to do it. There isn't a little botanist in the box to give you free lab results. We signed up on-line for email results, paid for expedited shipping to Florida, and expedited test results. The whole process took maybe 3 days. By submitting the nasty results to our landlord, they were all of a sudden eager to let us out of the lease. Without this, our landlord was claiming we were "going overboard," and "it's just mildew." Wrong. The results showed which mold species we're dealing with, and confirmed a medical diagnosis of sinus mold infection. You have two choices with this kit. You can take a sample out of your vacume cleaner bag and have them do a spore count, (I think they also tell you which species is included in the count) or you can take a sample of the actual mold you're concerned about and find out what species of mold you're dealing with. Or buy two kits and get them both, which is what we're doing. Among the concerns of living with mold is allergic reaction (stuffed up nose and other symptoms), mold GROWING in your body such as your lungs and sinuses, and mytotoxins that some molds give off which can have permanent health consequences. Find out which mold you're dealing with, then get a antigen blood test to see if THAT particular mold is impacting your health. Lots of steps, this mold kit is step one.

I did catch some mold spores in our bedroom, but we really didn't notice anything in 48 hours, except a couple of tiny, gray dots. We knew we had mold. I let it sit about a week (too long for the test), and there's 5 kinds of scary-looking mold. But if you want to know what mold you might have, you have to mail it in with $40. Not that anxious to spend the extra money, and I didn't realize there was that sizeable fee to get specific results. We just invested in a good dehumidifier, and our mold greatly diminished.

rating 1.0. Hire a real professional Do not buy these home mold tests. If you are truly worried about a mold problem, please hire a professional. I bought this and used it as directed, which resulted in various mold spores growing in the petri dish. It naturally freaked me out and I thought I had an extensive mold problem. After some consideration, I hired a professional to examine my home and test the air quality for mold spores...results were there was no mold overgrowth issue and my home was fine and had the "normal" amount of mold in the air (nothing more than what was collected and tested outdoors). The problem with the home mold air tests is that they only test what may land on the petri dish, which is not a valid test for what may be actually be in there air all around the house (because things may land but there is no overgrowth of mold, or maybe nothing lands on there but there is an overgrowth..both are possible). The professional I hired used a device to suck in the air for five minutes, adequately gathering samples throughout the air, which allowed for a more reliable test.

Long story short, I highly recommend using a professional for these types of issues, since they not only test air quality, but use equipment to accurately measure humidity levels, areas where moisture is coming in and pooling, any signs of mold growth on walls that may not be visible to us, etc. These home mold kits don't really tell you anything about the true nature of mold in homes. In my opinion.

rating 4.0. This kit works as advertised I was concerned that the discoloration behind the wallpaper in the bedroom might be mold.  I ordered three kits, although the company only recommends two.  I wanted to be doubly sure so I placed 2 in the bedroom, and 1 outside as a control as proscribed.  Both inside kits showed about the same amount of mold growth so I believed those results to be accurate.  I then compared both inside kits to the control outside and saw similar molds and similar amounts.  There is always mold present to some degree both inside and outside.  Based on these results I believed that I did not have a mold problem.  However, still wanting to be sure that the mold present wasn't harmful, I paid the required fee and submitted the inside sample to the company for analysis.  They responded within the designated time frame and identified the strains of mold present, the level of mold present, and the fact that they were among the most common strains and basically harmless.  I am satisfied with this purchase

rating 5.0. Great DIY Mold Test Kit I purchased this test kit to see if we had a mold problem in one of the buildings in our church that we wanted to remodel.  For quite some time we had noticed a moldy smell in the building and were concerned about any health issues that might arise from a high level of mold being present in the building.  I followed the directions and sent off the kit with the supplied envelope. (It didn't take long to see that there was mold growing in the petri dishes that came with the kit after they were exposed to the air in the building.) The results were emailed to me and I was pleased with the report they supplied.  It indicated that we did have mold (which is pretty normal), but that there was no sign of any of the more harmful types of mold. The remodel seemed to verify the results we got.  We found no signs of "black" mold during our remodel.

rating 4.0. happy with this kit! i know that there was and is mold in my apartment, i've seen it and this place has had a lot of water damage. i just didn't know how harmfull mold could be until i started getting sick.  now i know i have been getting sick since i moved in here... which was 6 years ago.  i had a cough recently that wouldn't stop, had a chest x ray that showed severe inflamation, have had swollen lymph glands, spots in my throat, memory loss, hair falling out.. you name it.  a friend of mine said to me one day about 2 months ago.. "didn't you say you had mold in that apartment? you know that stuff can make you very sick and even kill you".  i talked to my doctor and she said if i knew what kind of mold it was they could test my blood.  so i got this kit.  it took a long time to actually get there after i did the test and sent it,  i don't know why.  i sent it early november and they didn't get it until the 18th.  i had the results on the 30th and i had the mold that i suspicioned. i looked them all up (there were 3).  the report failed to say how toxic these molds can really be.  they say they are rarely reported as pathogens but i think that means the symptoms are diagnosed as something else.  i asked that question and no one would answer me.  from what i have read and studied the past several months.. yes there is mold everywhere but there is toxic mold in most of our public buildings, schools and homes.  and it's attributed to other things cause god forbid that we have to tear down millions of buildings because they are killing us all.  watch "dr andrew campbell  and the effects of mycotoxins on the human body".  it's on utube and it will scare the hell out of you. do you know that in the late 90s a study was done in this country on people that had chronic respiratory issues and over 90 percent of the them were from mold toxicity?  do you know that canada saw this study and retrained ALL of their health care providers to be aware and educated and to look for mold toxicity in patients with symptoms and NOT blow it off as something else?  what happened in the us where the study was done?  hidden from the public and NO training or education to ANY medical professionals. we are all sick form this crap and no one talks about it, there are only a few states in the us that have any laws in place to protect renters that live in mold infested dwellings. in my state, the department that dealt with indoor air quality and mold was closed due to budget cuts.  if you want anything done, you do it yourself, landlords don't even have to disclose and they don't have to clean it up.  i have never been sick in my life until i moved here. people should be outraged and demand that this issue be a top priority in this country!  if this stuff is in my blood (which i'm sure it is) i am looking for a lawyer.  my landlord has known for a very long time that his buildings have mold in them and he has done nothing.  as far as this kit goes.  it tells you clearly that you will have to have a lab test the mold.  if you had a true self test kit it would have to include a microscope and a a book that you would have to read and educate yourself thoroughly on and it would probably cost thousands of dollars,  get real.  how else do you think you are going to get a result?  those cheap self test kits tell you you have mold, they don't tell you what it is.  a lab needs to do that.  all and all i was very pleased other than the time frame.  i'm sure they are extremely busy since more and more people are finding out how dangerous this stuff can be.  i did the research ahead of time and they were recommended as the best home kit on the market.  crappy customer service doesn't mean a crappy product.

rating 5.0. It works The instructions on the kit were very specific and I  cultured several areas.  I had a terrible skin reaction that the drs couldn't find the reason for. If you are having a problem and think a mold is the cause, I highly recommend this. it was worth the money for me.

rating 4.0. Know what you are getting a do a little research This is not a true false test. People ranting about "false positives" need to read and understand mold is all over. I ordered these to see if I had a mold problem in my bathroom. I put one in the bathroom, a few other rooms and then OUTSIDE as the control. If the same or less growth as the one outside you probably do not have a problem. It is also important the type as well. If you see different types you will want to get that checked out by a lab. This is not a professional result but it does have the lab option which is nice. If you think you might have a serious mold problem this is not the kit for you. Get a professional in there to do things right. There is a reason real professional mold tests cost hundreds of dollars. It suited my needs and I got exactly what I was looking for. You can't be the price.

rating 4.0. good value for the price and able to handle great loads I came across this product and decided to buy it, even though at the time I didn't have an existing need to use it. I figured it will come handy at one point and I liked the price. I recently had to move some heavy packages around and I used the cart to move a few heavy boxes weighing around 200 pounds. I used the cart position which handled the weight nicely. A little bit difficult to maneuver, but that was due to quite a heavyweight.

The cart has two shortcomings that I observed:
1. When opening it from a closed position, it seems you have to apply a bit of pressure to the handles to release them from the plastic. The first time I did this, the plastic broke and I had to replace the item.
2. In cart form, if you have small items, they may fall, as the surface of the cart contain gaps - I wish it came with a net of flat panel of some sort.

Overall, good value for the price and useful

rating 4.0. This is a great little light duty hand truck for the $ This is a great little light duty hand truck for the $.  Key words, "light duty" and "for the $".  I got this in a hurry because I have a dryer delivery coming tomorrow and my usual go to guy for borrowing a heavy duty hand truck was out of town.  This thing isn't super heavy duty but easily handled my old 1980s era Kenmore dryer that is admittedly light as these things go.  Tomorrow will be more of a test since the new one is significantly heavier.  I marked it down 1 star mostly because the lifting platform angle is weird.  When you pull back on the handle to lift an object the lifting platform doesn't correspond at first until it comes up on its stop.  So you really need to grab ahold of the object and tilt it back mostly manually.  Not a deal breaker but not ideal.  This might be fixable with a simple modification.  And many many thanks for all the reviews that mentioned the snap trap wheels, so I was able to avoid that hard lesson.  My fingers are intact, but I can see how your fingers could be pinched or hammered pretty bad.  Be careful!

rating 5.0. Follow the instructions and know the limitations and adaptations and you will be fine It does what it is designed to do. I read a lot of the reviews and so far I have avoided being pinched by the collapsing wheels. My pet peeve, probably due to my lack of being mechanically inclined, was trying to get the large wheels to drop down properly as I brought up the handle to open the cart. I watched several videos, but none of them brought this point up. Now that I finally got the hang of it, and I’m trying to repeat the same mistake, of course it doesn’t happen. I am half blind, so trying to see the tiny images of the instructions on the handles was very difficult. The trick to getting the wheels to properly drop down is to grip the handle with four fingers, while placing the thumb on the plastic retainer and pushing down. Continue raising the handle to the upright position while raising the blade that scoops up the boxes at the same time. This should keep your fingers safely out of the way of the snapping wheels as they go into position. (Someone mentioned always getting caught by the wheels.) The part of pushing down the retainer with your thumb and raising the handle is actually in the illustration but I didn’t notice it when I first tried to interpret what I was looking at.
I also bought the suggested replacement wheels and a couple of wrenches for when the time comes to replace the weak rear wheels.
As for the handle being short, it was probably designed that way for a reason since the cart’s weight capacity is limited to 300 pounds. It is suitable for my height of 5‘8“.
I don’t have a scale to weigh the boxes that I have moved, but it works perfectly so far. If I ever use it outside on the sidewalk, or have to go over sliding door tracks that would chew up the wheels, hopefully I will have a plastic runner underneath to keep the wear off of the wheels.

rating 1.0. Inspect very carefully upon receiving. Twice now I have ordered this.  In both cases, the end of the box had been damaged and as that is exactly where the small wheels sit when the dolly is folded for storage, both times the wheels were damaged.  I could hear the ball bearings rolling around in the box before I opened the first one and one of the wheels had been bent over enough that there were no ball bearings left.  This was after there was a delay of a couple of days in shipping, so I assumed there had been a problem of some sort.  Easy enough return, immediately ordered a replacement.  The replacement came very quickly but.....there was the same damage to the box although not as severe.  I shook the box and, sure enough, I could hear something rolling around in there.  Upon opening the box I found that once again the small wheels had been damaged.  The problem is that there is nothing between the wheels and the outside world other that the cardboard box.  Making the shipping container longer and putting some padding in there will probably fix the problem.  The hand truck appears to be well made and I am a fan of Cosco products, having owned one of their adjustable ladders for many years.

rating 3.0. Sturdy but Caution when Closing This product is sturdy and durable. I would recommend caution when folding because it will snap close on your fingers when closing. I really think this is a safety hazard because it is a very powerful snap that can cause serious injury to the unsuspecting. After getting caught a few time, I was cautioning a friend who was helping me to fold the dolly when it snapped his fingers resulting in broken skin and a large bruise.

rating 4.0. THOSE DAMN TINY WHEELS Pretty awesome. I did a recent move with lots of boxes. This thing was incredibly useful.

As almost everyone has mentioned, the small wheels need to DEFINITELY be changed out but unfortunately they are using tamper proof nut lugs. I looked at ALL the reviews to find the best ways to get rid of the wheels. Here are some of the suggestions
- use a flame/blowtorch (!) to expand the nut
- use a can opener/twisty thing to clamp it down while you use an opposite force (wrench) to unscrew.
- use some type of pinching tool ie clamp it down ie oil filter while you use an opposite force (wrench) to unscrew.

As I only had 2 wrenches at that time and wanted to get it done that night, I found out after much sweat and effort, that if I separate the metal that encloses the "tiny friction beads" and then pulled out the beads. It would give me a place to "grab" the center rod. Then I used an opposite force, the wrench, to remove the nut. Personally it required a lot of brute force so if you can found a simpler way, please make a video to help others. The can opener/twisty thing sounds like it might be the way to go and it was done by a lady but there was no video to clarify how it was done.

I only give 4 stars because of the cheap wheels. Cosco, PLZ, upgrade the wheels. Probably cost you like $5 to change them.

rating 5.0. Compact, strong and easy to use! I am a specialty food vendor and have to carry 2-3 LARGE coolers (150 quarts) filled with glass jars everytime i have to set up my booth. rating 3.0. Overall good with several fixes Overall pretty good with a few minor changes it has potential to be perfect.
1) Stock back wheels, they suck. I replaced them with better quality casters as pictured (see info below) +2x $9 to cost.
2) When in dolly mode the handle is annoyingly a tad too short (~31"), and I'm average height (5'11"). Another 6" I wouldn't notice it, an additional 12" would be ideal. The height is perfect in hand truck mode, so 6" could be a good compromise. I'm contemplating jerry rigging an attachment to make it a less awkward height, an adjustable handle would be good for Cosco to add.
3) Not a gripe, but a fair warning... I thought that this would be common sense. This is folding, watch your fingers!
4) The bottom is not at a 90 degree angle when in the hand truck configuration! Causes boxes to fall off, careless design flaw, added spacers (coins) on bottlm for fix.

Replacement Casters: Shepherd Hardware 3265 3-Inch Threaded Stem TPR Caster, 110-lb Load Capacity

rating 2.0. Only for very light duty. Platform is not at right angle. I bought it to move 50 gallon water heaters which weigh about 200 pounds. So I'm well below the max 300 pounds weight limit.
The main problem is that the bottom platform does not sit at a 90 degree angle with the frame. So when you load a box it is only sitting on the edge closest to the frame and the load is not distributed properly. If I try to lift the box it acts as a lever and pushes the load away from me. I either have to strap it to the frame or lean the box back before I pull back on the dollie.
Having to use ratchet straps on every load is annoying. This is definitely going back if they'll accept it without the box since I'd already disposed of it thinking I was getting a quality product.

If you do get the stack tipped, you have to keep the handle really low to keep the load from trying to pull away from you. It's just bad. Maybe not all have this issue.
