rating 3.0. Nice Unit with Room for Improvement UPDATE: My wife liked the idea of the cart, that is until she used it. Despite my warning of watching her fingers when she collapsed the unit, it caught her fingers like a mouse trap. Unfortunately, she has ordered the unit out of the house because a child could get seriously hurt. Although I don't think that would happen, it can mash a finger pretty badly (happened to me too). So, it looked good leaning against the wall while it lasted😕

ORIG REVIEW: If you're a serious DIYer or Contractor, this truck will seen like a toy. It's not a Magliner and not meant to be. However, even for that same crowd, this Cosco unit has its place around the house (especially if you have nice hardwood floors) or on a rescue run (kids, family, neighbors, ... that need a hand). Others have commented on the uses. Here's a list of suggested improvements:

- the pinch from slamming wheels upon closing are completely avoidable with a slight design modification. At a minimum, put a warning sticker where to never put your hands.
- the handle is too short for tall people and could use an extension feature (or just make it longer).
- the nose plate angle, appearing to be about 95 degrees, makes little sense. Make it 90 or 89 degrees.
- the smaller plastic wheels are simply cheap and make the device feel cheaper than it is. Put better wheels on it.
- the handle unlock process isn't intuitive and somewhat odd.

Otherwise, a clever design that is very good for light to medium duty moving.

current_product_reviews: rating 3.0. I guess I feel like it kinda sucks... Ack! I was so disappointed with this! I was really excited to have a cart that could be easily stored and then quickly unfolded, but this was more headache than helpful. They're not good for steps or grass (or even sidewalk cracks) WHATSOEVER. Second, the metal part that folds out for the dolly setting is too small/shallow. Not very helpful. The cart itself is just not good for anything remotely heavy duty or for any uneven terrain. It also kept getting stuck in the folded position. Yes, I read the instructions, and I'm a competent person. The thing was stuck. But it does fold up to be very compact, so there's that! I saw so many rave reviews, so I guess I'm confused!

rating 5.0. Hey, it's a slim trash can There isn't all that much to say about it - it's a trash can.

-Plastic is quite thick, what I'd consider 'commercial' grade. So it'll stand the test of time.
-This is pretty darn big trash can - 23 gallons.
-The 'vents channels' on the sides do serve a purpose, they do seem to work - they keep the bag from creating a vacuum between the sides of the can and making it impossible to get the bag out. Useful actually.
-If you want the beige version (or are OK with it) your local home depot sells it for the same price (or less). I couldn't find the grey at HD.
- This matches my rubbermaid recycling can that is the 'slim' version.
- This is very good for a 'closet' pantry - you can fit recycling and trash side by side in good volume.

-If I were you, I'd get the lid for this too - See here [[ASIN:B005KD3AJU Rubbermaid Commercial FG267360GRAY Slim Jim Rectangular Trash Can Swing Lid, Gray]] - seems like kind of a ripoff, but hey might as well get the whole package and have the lid to keep the smell of trash inside.

rating 5.0. Worked great on my ceramic item Great item. Used it to fix a ceramic syrup pourer. Don't use super glue, use this stuff. Epoxy will hold better and not discolor the ceramic. I can't even see where the handle had broken off in 3 places. The item I fixed looked like a coffee mug and the handle had been broken off.

While you can put epoxy repaired ceramics in the dishwasher, it's safer to wash by hand so I have not put my repaired item in the dishwasher.

rating 5.0. Stronger than metal. My copper pipe broke before the epoxy did If used properly, this stuff makes an incredibly strong bond. I used it to attach a copper pipe to an aluminum pipe on a DIY project. I realized that I needed to make changes and get the copper pipe off. Nope I broke the copper before I could get it to budge, Which I then had to hack saw it up and break parts off with pliers to get the copper off so I can re do it. As an adhesive, thats pretty much a 10/10. My intent was ot make my 2 pipes one, and this did that pretty perfectly.

If you use this wrong the bond wont be great, but used well its amazing. Bonding 2 flat surfaces together isnt nearly as good. If you rough the surfaces up it helps, but all it needs is for one edge to cope up a tiny bit and it can be popped off. But if its a 3 dimensional joint, so that theres adhesive stopping it from moving being able to pull off, its amazing. When properly used, everything I try to take off breaks before the epoxy goes. So as a permanent solution I couldn't ask for more.

And for ease of use, its very easy to use. I just squeeze a bit onto a piece of cardboard with packing tape on it to make a water resistant surface. Mix them with a paper clip. Apply it. Wait. Note that a hot environment will set faster.

Try not to get it on your hands, its sticky and doesnt come off well, even with dish soap. But its not like super glue so it wont just instantly stick your skin together. so it gets points over glue for that.

Also this is incredibly cheap and non toxic when cured. Meaning it should be safe for most applications. I cant really ask for more.
rating 4.0. Good for what was needed Works as advertised, but can't be used for everything
rating 5.0. Five Stars Just what I needed for my project.

rating 3.0. Mixed Epoxy is Very Viscous - Long Time to Final Cure The mixed components are very viscous.  Do not expect the mixed material to flow into a crevice.  I used the epoxy to repair a wooden child's scooter.  Fortunately I could brush the epoxy onto the two surfaces (wooden dowel and drill hole) and push them together.  In retrospect, a clamp would have helped to push the dowel firmly into the opening socket since the viscous epoxy does not flow easily.  The material starts curing in a short (2-3 minutes, room temperature) time but does not fully cure for several hours.  It remains tacky (gummy) during the long cure cycle.  I recommend clamping the surfaces with the applied epoxy until the epoxy fully cures.

rating 4.0. Dries crystal clear... It is somewhat hard to work with especialy if you use it before it starts to cure. It starts out thin and slippery. You need to wait about three minutes before you apply it or it goes on way to thin. Keep checking it for the right consistency. You will probably need to use tape to hold the pieces until dry. It's not like super glue and you can just hold it until it's hard.  If you have several pieces to glue together it will be challenging, I would do one piece at a time. It cleans up with rubbing alcohol before it dries. So, if you mess up don't pannic, clean up and start over. For glass repair I think it is amazing beacuse of its clear drying.

rating 5.0. What the pro's use I found out about  PC Clear Epoxy a few years ago after discovering Lakeside Pottery, a ceramic and sculpture repair studio. I highly recommend visiting their website before you toss out that broken heirloom piece that seems unsalvagable.  The before and after pics will amaze you. If you're like me and just want to know how to DIY a repair or use the right tools and adhesives they have lots of detailed videos that show the process start to finish. That's how I got my introduction to PC Clear Epoxy and several other products. This stuff really bonds well and has a few minutes working window before it starts to set. rating 5.0. Works as described I have used this product on multiple surfaces including plastic and ceramic. So far, it has held incredibly well. rating 5.0. It's glue! Good stuff. Easy to use and easy to put the cap back on and store for future use. Did an awesome job of fixing my broken decorate plate. A little tip about fixing a plate or similar item - after you mix the glue let it sit for maybe about 60-120 seconds before applying. This allows it to stiffen up a little, makes it less liquid and easier to work with. Then when you press the parts of what you're gluing together they will stay better and won't move around as much. Keep in mind that the glue has a "working time" of 5 minutes so definitely don't wait too long especially if you have to apply glue over a large area which may take longer. Use your noggin. Wear gloves!

Once the glue sets and the thing you're gluing is relatively stable in 5-10 minutes you can clean up excess glue with acetone or rubbing alcohol although I found acetone works better. If it's a large glob of glue use a scalpel, plastic card (like old library car), or similar device to scrape it off as long as you get it early.

rating 5.0. Did a Great Job with Porcelain Toilet Tank Cover We had a broken toilet tank cover and the company no longer makes replacements, so the only option was to repair it. We initially tried Gorilla Glue, but the result was ugly and didn't hold. The second time around, I researched repairing porcelain and found this as a recommendation.

I had a hard time identifying how much I would need, so I ended up ordering two one-ounce packages. My tank cover was in three pieces and needed a significant amount of repair, but I still only used maybe 3/4 of one. Overall, I found the epoxy incredibly easy to work with and it dried clear. The cracks are still noticeable and you can still see the glue marks... I think the image of a glass is a bit misleading. You couldn't glue a glass together and have it look new. However, it did the trick for my project and it came out better than I expected, plus is holding way better than the Gorilla Glue did, so I'm happy with it and will use it again.

rating 4.0. Not really a 4-minute epoxy... I mixed a small portion of the glue, trying to see how fast it cures. I mixed it up and left the mixing stick in the pool. As advertised, I expected the glue to set after 4 minutes. After 15 minutes I were still able to move the stick around, and I couldn't anymore until 20-minute mark. I've used Double-Bubble (by Hardman) a lot of times, which is a 3-5 minute epoxy, and the stick wouldn't be moved after 3-5 minutes as advertised. So I don't know the claim of 4-minute time is based on what stage in the curing process... Double the initial viscosity, maybe? I'm a bit disappointed since it doesn't really set in 4 minutes as stated. But for the price, I'm keeping it.

Update: Now I looked at the label again, and it says "work time 4 minutes". So it's not the time that it'd cure (or can't be moved around anymore) but just the time you'll have to set the pieces in place before it starts 'gelling'. So if this glue was to be labeled in the same way as other epoxies, then it'd be '20-minute epoxy'.

rating 5.0. These work!! Worked well for me, convenient to have on hand for when dehydrating apples or other foods. I've found that keeping the opened bag of O2 absorbers in a glass jar with lid and rim works beautifully for preserving these long term (they've lasted a few years using this method). The thickness of mylar was just fine for my needs. Thanks!

Oh and if you want a phenomenal mid-winter treat for yourself or kiddos, thinly slice some apples (honey crisp are amazing but galas and jonathans or jonagolds work well too), soak in water with a heaping teaspoon of ascorbic acid (can also use lemon). Drain the water and then dry using your fav dehydrator. Mine is the cheap round one with four plastic drying things (things? You know, trays - sheesh, middle aged brain here). Anyway, I can usually get about four apples' worth on the trays. Sprinkle with cinnamon (OK, I do put a hint, just a hint, of sugar in), dry the apples for 12 hours or so, enjoy the amazing apple-cinnamon aroma as the apples are dehydrating. Once they've reached the desired crispness, let them cool off for a few minutes, then place in your handy dandy mylar bag, pop in an oxygen absorber, crank up your iron and seal two inches or so with the iron. And then save bag for a rainy day, or a cold wintery day when the kids are whining and you are desperate for a little joy.

rating 5.0. Buy this product I highly recommend this product.  They seal easily with an iron on a low setting, #2 on my iron. No need to buy a special sealing device. Oxygen decreases self life.  Flour type products contain more oxygen therefore need a 500cc packet.  For additional insurance/protection, I only fill the 1gallon bag half full.  I'm on my second set of 60 bags and am most pleased.

rating 5.0. These are a great size and absolute blast to use for storing our ... These are a great size and absolute blast to use for storing our food stock. We have used them to store three varieties of rice (no punctures) and three different varieties of beans. To do this we filled the bags about 2/3-3/4 of the way full then threw in (2) packs of the included oxygen scavengers (one pack is sufficient as long as your pack extremely tight) and then packed the rice or beans as tight as possible and folded the free end down . (We folded the free end of the bag down along the top of the bean filled bag pushing towards the open end to expel all of the air.)--If you do this carefully, the static of the bag itself will basically hold it shut and you can rolled/pin the free end against the side of the bag. Once pinned, you can fold the end over twice (about a 1/4 of an inch at the extreme open end of the bag). This folded edge was pinched with our fingers and the bag stretched out over a metal cookie sheet/pan.--When the bag was stretched out the rice will not move and you will see a vacuum form from stretch it out. On the wool setting (setting 5 on our iron) we ironed the folded edge for a few seconds, let it cool and then unrolled the sealed end and ironed it for around 10 seconds. Finally, we fluffed up the contents so that they filled the bag completely. Within an hour the bag had collapsed on itself and you could feel the warmth of the oxygen scavenger through the bag during that first hour.

I know these directions are a little hard to follow (someday i will take pictures or videos and post them) but if you are careful you can pull a pretty large vacuum just by tightly rolling the air out of the free end when packing the food in the bag. If you are quick and careful, you can fold over and seal this free end before you end iron. Two folds seemed to do it each time. Word of caution.... If when you stretch out the bag and rice, beans, etc. end up by the folded over free end, you don't actually have a seal. It's not a big deal as the oxygen scavenger should still do the job (as long as the bag isn't too full of air) but I would just scrap the bag and start over. Moral of the story, once the bag is sealed, it should be quite difficult to get the contents to the edges of the bag due to a good seal and lack of air. If it is easy to move the contents to the corners of the bag after sealing, you can easily get more air out before sealing.--If you have a good vacuum on the bag after an hour, I probably wouldn't worry too much, but perfection will only make your food last longer.

If you do decide to use a vacuum sealer, you will have to put a cardboard piece or slotted piece of plastic in the bag opening as a baffle. The mylar is so slippery and static-y that the machine will probably not be able to pull a vacuum. See videos on Y-tube for this.

rating 5.0. DON'T OVER STUFF THEM I bought both the 1 gallon mylar bags and the 5 gallon mylar bags. The best and most convenient way to store whatever may be needed at another time. Just make sure before you seal them up, you first put in one or two oxygen absorbers. Then slowly squeeze out as much air as possible by rolling up the bag towards the opening. Fold the opening of the bag over once or twice, and press them shut with an iron. That's it. LOCKED IN FOR GOOD. The more air you are able to get out of the bag before sealing it up, the longer shelf live the food you have stored in them will last. Definitely the way to go

rating 5.0. Accept No Substitutes The Good: This is a fantastic mat - love the texture for grabbing prints, love the flex for removing prints, good strong magnetic grab to the build plate. Absolutely no complaints.

The Bad: If you get your first layer too close, it will stick -very- hard...hard enough to damage the mat when removing it. Also, wait for the mat to cool before removing prints to avoid damage as well - it's tougher when it's cooled down. It will wear over time as some have pointed out but so will any build surface this good.

The Ugly: There's a knock-off mat for 5% off out there using the stock image. DO NOT FALL FOR IT. It does not grab the plastic like the original, and it has a weaker magnet and is prone to sliding.

The original is the best mat.

rating 2.0. Functional but NOT durable. Pros:
- It definitely makes removing a print much easier.
- Good adhesiveness.

Cons:
- Installing it is tricky.
- Sometimes it doesn’t line up magnetically perfect. This requires removing the bed and adjusting it.
- IT CREASES TOO EASILY. You have to be careful you’re not bulking the bed off a piece at too much of an angle or it will leave a permanent crease in the bed. This may affect some bed leveling sensor.
- Only really recommend for PLA. Printing anything that requires a higher temp like PETG will damage the bed.
- Bed isn’t sold separately if you damage or crease it. You have to buy the whole set.

FINAL RECOMMENDATION: Don’t Buy. You’re better off with a spring steel bed and PEI on it.

rating 5.0. Perfect Replacement for the Ender-3 Pro Magnetic Bed It wasn't entirely clear whether this is the same thing that came with my Ender-3 Pro that I received as a Christmas gift on December 25, 2018.

I can report that it is the identical item.

What's more, I can also report that having a new build surface made an absolutely HUGE DIFFERENCE in how well my prints stick. Even though I attempted to keep my old build surface clean, and even though I frequently re-calibrate and re-level my bed, I was having a tougher and tougher time getting prints to stick.

All I did was peel off the old surface (I didn't bother to replace the bottom surface, even though that part is included with this purchase), put the new surface on, re-level, and then printed.

Given what a huge difference this made, and given the modest price, I will now consider this to be a "consumable," like filament, nozzles, and Capricorn tubing.

So, if your prints aren't sticking, buy this.

rating 2.0. Adhesion on the one I recieved is terrible, regret purchasing This is the same size and shape that comes with the stock ender 5, and it attaches just the same way. The only difference is that the logo is centered and larger. The problem is that adhesion is terrible, even after leveling several times and washing. I was hoping it would work the same as the one that came with my printer but even heated to 60 degrees and using 215 c PLA things just don't stick. Slightly depressed in the center, it was shipped in an envelope with a metal tool rather than a box. Easy to install and remove but mine refuses to stick anything except large flat pieces, even then there's issues because the center is slightly shallower than all the edges.

Tested adhesion on this vs. the one that came with my Ender 5 and this one is definitely worse, parts print on the original can't stick to this one. I've tried 2 brands of my usual PLA (one prints at 205, the other prints at 215) and they both have some issues.

Update: after comparing the magnetic surface received with my Creality printer vs. this magnetic bed I notice that the texture of the bed is much finer on the original than it is one this one. The original is matte black, this one is shinier due to the less detailed surface (think ultra fine grit sandpaper vs. coarse sandpaper). That likely has an affect on adhesion, as there's less surface volume for the PLA to grip and melt into on a microscopic level.

rating 5.0. Basic but Adequate For Specific Needs I bought this inexpensive little meter to make some measurements of road noise in my car and, as it turned out, all around the house too. It's simple, functional, and accurate enough to make basic comparisons. I found the build quality to be good, except for the fact that the base is slightly rounded so that the device will hardly ever stand on its own. Fortunately, there is a tripod mount on the back that's made all the difference. The interface is simple and easy to manage but very basic, so keep that in mind when selecting your device. Also, bear in mind that the device samples two times per second. Practically, this means that continuous, near-constant level noises are the best candidates for measurements with this device. Finally, the device uses an A-weighting scale. This is the most commonly used curve when relating measurements to the relative loudness perceived by humans.

So, overall this device is inexpensive and optimized for a small set of specific requirements. I would not recommend it for anything beyond that.

rating 5.0. Excellent product! Worth the small investment to know what things weigh! I liked the packaging and presentation of the product. The users manual was easy to understand and I was set up to start weighing in just a few minutes. Mine came with batteries and only needed the hanging hook/u-bolt mounted to the scale to be fully operational.
I ride at a local state park that attracts large crowds from the serious rider and racer to the kids and families just looking to have fun. I put a rope in a tree at the park (no harm done to the tree) and offered to weight peoples bikes or gear for free. It was quite the crowd draw! The kids enjoyed guessing what the bike might weigh before we had the results. My best "serious" racer wanted to weigh the bike, his protective gear and his hydro backpack.
After the day at the park I hung it in my shop to weigh anything from my body armor to the bikes and gear. I have tested the results by weighing items and comparing to another scale in the house as well as weighting 5 lb and 25 lb dumbbells. Great accuracy.
It is nice to know that your back pack weigh ##pounds an the bike you thought was light actually weight more. I follow the manufactures guidelines and while I leave it hanging in the garage I do not leave any weight hanging from the bottom of the unit. This allows me to power it up with the push of a button and hang the bottom hook then lift the item to be weighed onto the hook. I have found that some items require a long zip tie (48") to provide a point that you can hook onto that item. Get creative and you will find yourself wanting to know the weight of all kinds of items.
Seems well built and the case/box it came in makes for a nice storage case as well. I would recommend to anyone.
GOD BLESS
john

rating 5.0. Handy simple to use scale with one annoying "flaw" So far this scale works fine and I appreciate the fact it runs on readily available common AAA batteries vs some coin style lithium battery. Also, the batteries are easy to remove when storing the scales for sometimes months before the next use so you don't come back to a product destroyed by leaking batteries.

Bought it to replace a 300lb capacity traditional spring scale we use when weighing deer after a recent boar hog took the indicator 15-20 lbs past the 300 mark. Recently used this scale to weigh another boar and there was no guessing about the weight, 221 lbs. Have not had time to verify the ultimate accuracy by weighing multiple items of known weights, but have no worries this scale will be accurate and expect it to remain so. I recommend this scale and would buy one again unless time reveals some durability problem. Gave it a 4 on "Durability" on hopes it will be durable, but then who can really honestly answer such a question after less than 2 weeks of ownership?? Duh!!

I rated "Easy of use" at only 3 because of following thing I consider a "flaw". I would change how the hook attaches to the lower aspect of the unit, way to easy to fall out the way it is bent, it should fasten as securely as the hanging clevis fastens. Will probably use a zip tie to hold it in place, but honestly doing so should not be necessary. When shooting hogs for eradication I haul my scales on the tractor to weigh before disposal so trying to keep up with the hook that is not securely attached is a PITA, add in gloves etc in cold weather for deer hunting and it gets annoying. Will I give these scales up for this reason, NO WAY, I will find my own solution.

rating 3.0. Better options now available I got this to weigh a wheelchair patient using an overhead lift and sling. Works like a charm! So much easier to use and cheaper than a roll-on wheelchair platform scale. If you use a Hoyer lift or overhead lift, get one of these. My only issue is the LCD readout is hard to read when looking up at it at a large viewing angle, so I have to use a step stool to get up more level with it. I have no issues with accuracy or coming back to zero after the weighing.

I have to edit this review after two years of use to change my rating from 5 stars to 3. The on/off button failed, which was disappointing but not surprising for this type of membrane switch. But when I started to order a replacement, I discovered there is another brand which is much better and similarly priced as of this writing. The RoMech Crane Scale looks very similar but has a much better display with a wide viewing angle so I can easily read it even when it is near the ceiling, several feet over my head. It also displays the weight to 0.01 lb vs 0.2 lb for this one, has a metal housing, and uses three AA batteries instead of two. Does any of that matter? I don’t know, but my experience with strain gauge scales tells me higher voltage usually results in greater precision. But I would not expect greater accuracy than about 0.1% of the applied load (0.2 lb for a 200 lb load), no matter how many digits are displayed. The main reason for lowering the rating is the much better display on the RoMech.

rating 3.0. An aesthetic I wish could be reliably printed and safely put into use! I bought this filament after reading through the previous reviews, so I knew that I was likely in for a wild ride.

The filament was packaged as one would expect with a stiff, resealable plastic bag. There didn't seem to be anything wrong with it at first glance. The filament looked similar to other 'silk' style filaments that I have used in the past, though it felt 'light' to the touch. It's hard to explain, but I have become accustomed to a very solid feeling filament and this felt somehow lighter.

The original purpose of this filament was to reproduce a gold angel for a customer without having to sand/refine/paint the pieces. Due to the size requested and the build space of my Monoprice Maker Select, I had to cut it into several pieces to fit it all. I felt confident that I could simply attach everything once it was printed.

When the filament printed correctly, it printed beautifully. The yellow mustard color mixed with the silk finish definitely gave an eye-popping result that the customer loved. Unfortunately, that lustre was won through great difficulty and a ton of wasted time and filament. It is, as others have stated, very brittle. I found that anything less than a grid support would simply snap off midway through printing, leaving pieces' success up to fate. This often resulted in malformed, hole-ridden and indecipherable pieces, since the layer adhesion was very, very poor. The grid supports mitigated the issues but left horrible scarring on the pieces that ruined the lustre completely. However it is that they get the 'silk' finish does not translate into the filament, resulting in very dull, ugly spots where supports have touched or where one might sand it. Similarly, when used in a 3D pen, the resulting extrusion is not 'silk' finish at all, making spots where you might use it to weld pieces together dull.

I do think it worth mentioning that once the head, arm and horn of the commissioned figure were put together, it got knocked a scant foot from my hand and shattered at the wrist, neck and mouthpiece. The places that I would've expected it to break, such as at the 3D pen welded seams, were not where it broke, but instead at random layers in the print, regardless of thickness or infill. This is the first time I've ever dropped a piece, especially at such a low height, and had it explode into chunks. I was able to heat-smooth the layers with the 3D pen's tip but it left ugly, dull lines that stood out like a sore thumb.

Other reviews have mentioned that the filament is great once you 'dial it in' but I was never able to find that secret temperature combination that would keep things running smoothly. Nothing seemed to help make things lay down smoother, join stronger or feed better. In the end, it jammed both my nozzle and my 3D pen, the latter of which was a casualty of the effort, since it could not be taken apart at the heating element without breaking apart the plastic body and desoldering the board.

Since I purchased 1KG of this filament, it will stay in my rotation primarily as a 'draft' filament to check the size and shape of my models. I find that it prints okay when the pieces have a broad, flat base that tapers upward and requires no supports, though the previous caveats remain. Anything that is thin or requires bridging or steep overhangs will suffer. Any thin pieces will be very likely to snap off. It responds poorly to ironing, bubbling up strangely in some spots and showing through in others, even with 1.5mm in top layers.

I wish the quality of this filament were better or that another company would make a 'silk' filament in a nice mustard color like this. It hurts me to say that I will never trust it to a final piece, and that I cannot, in good conscience, recommend it to anyone else looking for a lovely 'gold' straight off the bed.

rating 4.0. Solid gold color. Even if not solid gold :) I'm usually too lazy to upload photos but since I find tremendous value when other buyers do it, I thought I'd pay it forward for karma. Just got a 3D printer and have been fanatically exploring all the fun filaments and projects I have time for. Bought this Gold Filament based on the photos people uploaded. I know there were a few issues for some people, probably got a bad batch or 9/10 user error. I popped the filament into the printer and changed nothing and it worked fine. Nozzle was 190f to 200f and bed was 50f to 60f ish. Anyway, first print came out beautiful. Slightly yellower than real gold so don't expect the "deep metallic" gold look. However with a splash or two of oil based polyurethane you can amber the color  up a bit and brighten to the point where it looks pretty damn good. I reckon with really thin layer height it would REALLY stand out. I printed this at .2mm and it's about 120 mm tall. Anyway, have a look. Definitely a cool product for messing around and making things look like gold lol. Special shout out to all the amazon buyers who leave reviews w/images. You are fantastic help. Wishing you good karma in the future. -A

rating 2.0. Bad dimensional tolerance This filament looks great, but that's about it. The dimension tolerance is so bad (+- .15mm) that I can only get a half dozen layers down before the printer jams. I threw this in one the workhorse printers at my job and have yet to get a successful print. Disappointing.

rating 2.0. Works if you get a good batch, can be tricky to work with. I've purchased this plastic twice now, and have had good results with one of the two spools I received. When this filament prints successfully it looks fantastic, but it can be difficult to work with.

The first spool I purchased produced great looking parts right out of the package without me having to change any temp/speed settings from previous projects that used "normal" PLA filament.

The second spool I received was terrible, and I was constantly changing temp and speed settings to get the filament to extrude at an even pace. I experienced clogs with the bad roll, as well as each print coming out with the first layer welded so firmly to the glass that I had to destroy the prints to remove them from the print bed. Even after I was able to get the filament to extrude evenly, my prints would delaminate easily creating very brittle and unusable parts.

This seller has an inconsistent product and keep that in mind when purchasing. I have had both good and bad experiences with this seller. I did not contact the company when having issues with their filament, it is possible that I received a bad batch and they may have shipped me a better roll of filament if I had contacted them. Still, with a 50% success rate I will not buy from these guys again! go check out Hello3d's silk gold filament, it's $10 cheaper and works much better.

rating 1.0. Too brittle I would love to give this a 5 star rating. The parts printed with this filiment look very good. But the issue is getting it to actually print.
The product is very brittle in filament form and much harder to push through the nozzle. This causes the drive wheel to grind away at the filament and lose drive power.
Nothing quite like a 20 our part failing after 14 hours.
Wasnt going to review it because I thought maybe it was my fault but I have tried everything.
Best option is running it over 220 degrees. It wont insure a part but you get a better chance of actually getting a part printed

rating 3.0. Beautiful, but very brittle, bad layer adhesion This has a gorgeous look, and simple prints came out fine. Prints with finer parts though, broke very easily when removing them from my PEI bed, often at a layer boundary. Overall prints ok, not too hard, however the layer adhesion problem is killer.

rating 5.0. I Love This Brand! This is quite possibly my favorite color! A non-abrasive gold filament that simply shines. I can't say much more than that except buy yourself a roll and then kick yourself for not buying one earlier.

rating 1.0. Shiny Gold garbage PLA Worst PLA i have ever bought on Amazon. Very stringy, won't adhere to previously laid filament. Going to perform 1 more test tonight to see if Heat is the issue to it not adhearing properly. If it doesn't work ill be returning.
rating 5.0. Good buy Prints great and looks great
rating 5.0. Fitted results with polished look Produce beautiful results with higher tolerances .02 to. 04 mm nozzles

rating 5.0. Great quality, beautiful, consistent filament It's beautiful, with an incredible metallic lustre right off the machine.

I'm printing here with standard Prusa PLA settings at 20um layer thickness (215C hot end, 60C bed). @julienmakes on Instagram if you're curious about these models.
rating 5.0. BRILLIANT! What more can I say...

rating 5.0. WOW! This filament is amazing. It is easily the most gold-like filament I have seen. And I've seen... like 3.

Anyways, the plastic performs pretty similarly to Hatchbox stuff, good stiffness and yield strength. Seems to break cleanly when it does snap, indicating it has a lower ultimate strength, probably due to the additives. It likes to expand a little bit but shrinks back down quickly, I think this property helps provide a more even surface finish on the sides of prints, and definitely doesn't negatively impact the printing process.

The underlying filament color seems to be an orangish-tan blend. This color is visible when your hotend is drooling, or when you try to sand the final print.

On that note, this filament is non-sandable. The gold is a surface quality and does not spread throughout the plastic. This gives it a much stronger and more metallic finish but you can only really sand tiny defects on your model without it being noticeable.

Absolutely recommended for any parts you want to look like gold.

rating 3.0. Very easy to get great prints. Very easy to get great prints but the color is not what I expected.  The color of this filament in person looks more like a slightly shimmering mustard yellow.  It is actually pretty ugly and I am amazed no one else has said this in the reviews.  The pictures are the right color but it just looks better in them than it does in person for some reason.

rating 4.0. Beautiful finish but not for utility Disclaimer: I'm fairly new to 3d printing.

I do like this filament overall. The finish on it is really stunning. I would say it is more brittle than some PLAs I've used so I wouldn't use this for utility type prints. For vases and statues and figures tho it really looks great with no polishing or anything needed.

I have a Wanhao duplicator i3 clone and the settings I've found work best for me was extruder at 205c and bed at 65c and fan off for the first couple layers then bed at 60c and fan at 75% there after. Those setting have worked well for me for decent sized prints but on a large vase I just made there was some marks towards the top so not sure what's up there.

Conclusion: this is great filament for figures and vases but go with something more sturdy for your holders and tools etc.

rating 5.0. Absolutely beautiful gold filament but has gotten too expensive and is no longer Prime available This is some very nice gold color filament, actually the best gold color I have yet to use. It takes a little work to get it printing nicely but once you do it is amazing. I would LOVE to see other colors come from this company such as copper, bronze, steel, etc. If they could get those colors to look this good I would be hooked.

Unfortunately they have decided to place a big increase on the price of this filament. I'm sorry, but this filament is nice but not worth $30 a roll. If it was Prime available I might spend that much but they have since decided not to use Prime so they have lost me as a customer.

rating 5.0. Once you get the settings right, this is GOLD I wasn't sure about trying this filament when I first found it, but I'm happy I did. Once you get all your settings right on your printer and slicer (I use the Mono Mini) this stuff makes fabulous, beautiful prints! They are smooth, incredibly shiny/metallic and show texture very well. I love it! I am surprised by how incredibly metallic it is, even in less faceted prints. If you're looking for something that looks closer to a GOLD/METALLIC filament, this stuff hits home. Don't mind the gaps in the one printing - those are my slicer's issues. Not the filament's.
rating 5.0. Beutiful This stuff is worth the money

rating 1.0. I wanted to love this stuff but.... I had read all of the negative reviews and took a gamble based on the positive ones.  It turns out it was a bad bet.  As stated by a few others the PLA comes out of the nozzle then quickly expands (like insulating foam) and then contracts back to the small size with in seconds.  It is almost like watching something alive.  I am hoping I just got an old bad batch because every other PLA I've ever had prints amazingly on my  machine.  I spent at least 20 hours trying to dial in this filament (temp range from 195c to 235c) and even went so far as to buy new PTFE hot end tubing, replacing nozzles, buying a new fan for the heat block but nothing worked.  3 or 4 layers is all I would get and then it would start to MASSIVELY underextrude.  Not sure what I will do with the roll at this point.  I wish it worked it is some pretty gold filament.
rating 5.0. Perfect look So thrilled with this! Looked at so many and hoped this would have the gleam I needed, perfect for my project!

rating 2.0. This filament was purchased due to the great reviews. The best thing about the filament is ... The seller of this material was prompt and friendly in regards to addressing the issues encountered. For this reason the review gained one more star. Updated due to further printing resulting in multiple clogged nozzles within 15 meters of filament. Also started smoking (yes blue headache inducing smoke and not white steam steam) at lower temps and extruding erratically due to gas pockets / voids in the filament. See pictures for void and examples.This filament was purchased due to the great reviews. The best thing about the filament is that it does print a beautiful gold color. It has some quirks compared to other filaments though. First it is grossly undersized, measuring only 1.60mm in diameter average and down to 1.50mm in places. Secondly it is not as dense as other filaments meaning it takes much more adjustment (extruder flow) than normal. Running 130% just to prevent porous prints. More to get good layer adhesion. Lastly it acts very weird unlike any other filament (pla) i am aware of, it shrinks vigorously upon exiting the extruder. This can be observed if you purge your extruder, a long skinny string will almost instantly shorten 3x in length. This makes explains the very exaggerated extrusion flow rates needed (beyond the dimensional compensation for smaller diameter). If you work with it, you can make very good looking prints. If you need a solid print however be sure to check for air / water tight properties on a test print first. This filament can not be trusted for any type of structural component due to voids in the filament causing extrusion issues. I may have received a single bad roll out of a batch, Due to the smoke emission and quality issues I have contacted the seller and await a response 3-7-18.
rating 5.0. Make sure you get the right temp for your printer to get good adhesion Very good the color came out great

input:
rating 4.0. Very similar to Carpet Tape .
This appears to be the same as XFasten’s Carpet Tape, except it’s 3/4” wide instead of 2-inches wide.

The packaging lists exactly the same features and properties, and it’s the same thickness:

- .0135” with backer, .009” after backer is removed. That’s .23mm. (This is twice the thickness of blue painter’s tape.)

As with carpet tape, it gums up your scissors and it’s gooey and hard to handle after the backer is removed. So stick it to one of your surfaces before removing the backer.

~~~ Comments & questions welcome ~~~

rating 5.0. Great tape for certain woodworking situations. We love it. This tape is accurately described. We use it in the wood shop in various precision applications to temporary mount and remove after machining process. This tape holds rock solid and is fast and easy to remove afterwards. The roll feels a little tacky on the sides, but its the nature of the beast, just like duct tape but unlike duct tape it does not leave any residue. It helps us that the tape is white and not clear like some others. With the contrasting color you see quickly if the tape is where it needs to be, and also the removal afterwards is easier than with clear tape that is sometimes hard to see. Last but not least, the tape cost less than other tapes we used before.

We ordered more tape after testing it, and XFasten tape has secured its place in our shop.

rating 1.0. Not good for putting up sound absorption panels I bought this to put lightweight sound absorber panels on my studio walls. With two strips of tape they should have easily stuck to the wall. After putting up 30 of them, I left the room for half an hour. I came back to finish and every single 12x12 panel was lying on the floor. This tape proved useless for my application.

I am going back to the 3M command strips. It only takes one of those to hold each panel, although I use two -- one on each side of the panel -- to avoid them curving over time.

rating 5.0. Don't judge a book by its cover. I bought it thinking it was regular double sided tape. Look when I got this I was pretty upset. I was in a rush, totally my fault and bought the wrong stuff. I threw it out in the garage and went to the local Hardware stores. They didn't have regular double sided tape either. So back to the house, I ended up using some screws for my project and just was refusing to use this tape. But then screw wouldn't work. And I was messing with clamps and that wasn't working and I had no choice but to use this tape. I thought it would be too sticky, too thick etc to be used for a wood routing template. But it was perfect!! No residue after leaving it on for about 2 hours, cam off and the hold is awesome. Had to really work to get it off but that's what you want. Not some weak tape. Now I have used ALL of the first roll on other wood routing projects as well as random stuff around the house. Definitely worked better than I thought it would and am very happy with this purchase.
rating 1.0. One Star This tape is complete garbage. It’s only good for paper.

rating 5.0. Awesome double sided tape This is an awesome tape, i used it to tape this track to my windows for my Christmas lights and it worked great, im about to finish my second roll, I will be buying it again!
rating 5.0. Amazing how great this stuff is Amazing how great this stuff is! It helped me keep my shelf liner in place without having to buy adhesive liner.

rating 2.0. Really messy adhesive sides. Tough to take off. ... Really messy adhesive sides. Tough to take off.  Have to have a large piece to be able to pull the whole thing off.  Small pieces result in gooey adhesive that is difficult to hold on to.  I specifically bought these for sticking foam board onto the wall.  Doesn't stay put.  Not sure what I will be using this for then.

rating 2.0. Don't use for router work. Bought this for router work. Usually I use spectape but this was cheaper so decided to give it a shot. I'll figure out a use for it but I wouldn't recommend for router projects.

rating 3.0. It's thin and looks like electrical tape This tape came in handy when a notice went out through our school that we could no longer staple items to the wall. It's thin and looks like electrical tape. Very easy to use and peel. Took off 2 stars because it's supposed to be removable (safely) but it tore the paint off my wall.

rating 2.0. Won't work on all painted walls. I'm an amateur photographer and I bought this tape to attach 8X10 matted photos [  Not with wooden or metal frames, just matting] to my painted walls.  I also attached a panoramic 36 X 12 photo [not matted or framed]. Within 2 hours they all fell off. I attached a photo to plastic siding on my enclosed porch and it's working great. I also attached a large mutli page calendar to the back of a semi gloss painted door and it's doing fine too.  I was really hoping I had discovered an inexpensive product to hang my photos.  It was advertised as "Adheres to smooth, rough as well as uneven surfaces".  It didn't for me as I had expected.

rating 5.0. Holds up light weight foam padded insulation. great price for the amount that you get.  working great so far.  The double sided is sticky enough to hold foam packing material to a heavy door that was getting too hot in the sun (burn your hand if you touched for more than 5 seconds) and it holds up to the extra heat so fare.
rating 1.0. One Star Stuff I got was not sticky at all.

rating 5.0. This Stuff is Amazing! This stuff is amazing! I just got my tape today, and used it on my first project – cutting 4 identical cabinet corner clamp blocks on a band saw. I only used a single strip on each 5" x 3/4" plywood triangle. I was able to cut 2 right angle cutouts on the long side of the taped together triangles with 4 band saw cuts. I then took them to the drill press, drilled two 1/4" pilot holes and finished with a 3/4" HSS bit all the way through the stack. The tape held up to the entire operation, and then I separated the triangles and removed the tape easily – without a trace of adhesive left behind.

If you need strong, but removable double-sided tape – then look no further!
rating 2.0. Two Stars Doesn't stick all that well. Ended up buying 3M tape
rating 5.0. so I put one strip on top of another and it was great. Good product A little thin, so I put one strip on top of another and it was great. Good product!

rating 5.0. Excellent as template tape I am using this as template tape for routing identical pieces of MDF board with a profile bit.  The tape has a very strong hold that could be used to permanently mount items but it also breaks apart easily (with some careful, patient effort) without damaging the two pieces and leaves absolutely no glue residue  behind.  Once the two pieces are separated I find that rolling the tape off of the other piece rather than peeling it works best.  For the price I don't think you will find a better product to use for template tape or any application where you want to be able to remove the tape later.

rating 4.0. Super sticky, does the trick Bought this for a DIY project adhering photos to a wall (no, I didn't care about the paint underneath but it doesn't actually look like the tape is harming the paint). It is really sticky. It sticks right to the photo backs, and adheres tightly to the wall. I created a fun photo montage in an otherwise colorless tiny little room, and I'm actually quite pleased with the whole bit. My only beef is that it is so very sticky that it gums up your scissors as you try to cut the tape to specific lengths. I ended up having five sets of scissors going at once as I wiped them down with alcohol to un-gum them. In the end, though, if you want something that lays flat and tightly adheres, this tape will do it.

rating 2.0. Not really for wood work. Moves and shifts. Tape sticks pretty well to wood. But is mentioned or appears as advertised for holding router inlay jigs. Has too much creap and lets jigs and forms shift. Not a stiff firm hold for precision work.

rating 5.0. Effective Double Sided Tape that is removable The removable 3/4 Inch XFasten Double Sided Tape works well to keep the Neato boundary markers and recharger where I place them.  My Neato Connected Vacbot  will come up to the charger and the boundary markers and then goes around them without moving them out of place.  So far I really like this tape and I am very glad I bought it.  I recommend this tape as it holds the item in place on my carpet which is exactly what I want.  http://amazon.com/dp/B01LT9K06Y/ref=cm_cr_ryp_prd_ttl_sol_8

rating 5.0. Great double sided tape. I've started using it for near everything from scrapbooking to light wall hangings. Easy to work with, easy to cut to size and strong. I will say another nice thing is that, even though it is pretty darned good at keeping stuff where you put it, if you misplace it then it's easy to peel back up from a corner, but if you aren't peeling it off something via a corner then it stays put. Not sure if these was an intended feature or a happy accident but it's quite nice either way. Also it's not a bulky tape nor is it some flimsy thin thing. I find the bulk of it perfect so you know where to press and it fills in any air gaps without leaving a bump in whatever you're mounting.
rating 5.0. Five Stars It is a very good product.

rating 5.0. Great for woodworking I use this for taping two pieces of wood together one as a pattern the other to be routed to match. This tape holds very well. It also comes off without leaving a residue nor tearing. the only thing to watch is that the roll of tape has very sticky sides and will accumulate a lot of sawdust/chips etc.. I put some wax paper on both sides when not in use.
rating 3.0. Not strong for pasting wallpaper on the wall: Not strong for pasting wallpaper on the wall  :||

rating 4.0. Sticks well, messy to tear. This comes highly recommended by Amazon reviewers, and it works well. I use it to temporarily attach a template to wood parts that are cut on a router table. It sticks like glue, but can be peeled right off when you remove the template. It's sticky enough to use twice, which is nice.
What's not so nice is that it can be a pain to tear. The tape has fibers in it, like mesh. That can make it sometimes difficult to tear, sometimes easy. Forget cutting it with scissors, the tape is too gummy/sticky. When it doesn't tear cleanly then then end won't lie as flat as it should. It's not a huge hassle, but constant enough for me to remove one star. If it tore or cut more easily, it would be perfect.

rating 5.0. CLEAN the surfaces and this stuff works great. When used right, this stuff is super strong, like I have to use a tool to pry the pieces apart.  I use for securing router templates to wood for building guitar bodies.  IMPORTANT!!! - You MUST clean BOTH sides before adding the tape.  Any dust or dirt or oil will keep this tape from working like you want.  I also use a far amount of tape. don't be cheap, also, run the tape in DIFFERENT directions, I've found this really helps in making a tighter bind.

rating 5.0. glad I have 3 rolls I used this to tape templates to wooden projects for use with a router and pattern cutting bit. Worked perfectly. Not too adhesive that it pulls fibers but  the pattern was held very well and the end results were good.

rating 4.0. Much better than what I had been using Much better than what I had been using.  The old stuff was just to strong and to difficult to remove the backing.  This has the right amount of stickiness and is easy to prepare.

rating 5.0. works great but they should sell a dedicated dispenser to cut the tape. I was using this to keep underpads on an invalid's bed. (on top of the sheets, so I wouldn't have to remake the entire bed every day).  It worked extremely well for that purpose without ruining the bedding.  My only issue was trying to cut the 3-inch pieces cleanly without it sticking to scissors and utility knives.  I wound up using a packing tape dispenser, but had to make sure the cutter was extra sharp, as a dull one would only pull the tape out of shape.  So, the tape is great, very sticky.  Removable from fabric fairly easily.  Would love if they sold this in pre-cut rolls.  Very economical compared to other double-sided tapes I tried.
rating 1.0. One Star too thin to pile out

rating 5.0. XFasten Double Sided Tape that really does Work! I have tried other double sided tape but they all failed to impress me.

BUT, at long last I have found a brand that actually works, in fact it works perfectly. I use it when I need to make a part from a template on my router table. I am very impressed with how well a small amount holds the pieces together and what's more, I can even use it several times before the parts no longer stay together.

When it's time to remove the tape, it comes off Extremely Easy and there is no residue left on the work pieces.

If you need Doubled Sided Tape, You Need XFasten. It really is worth the money. Once you use it you won't buy any other brand.

So Just to recap, it can be used several times before you need to change it, it is so easy to remove and there is no residue left on your workpiece.

rating 5.0. If you have an X-Carve or other CNC Router, this is THE TAPE FOR YOU! If you have an X-Carve, listen up. This is the tape to use when you are making inlay lettering. I'm not kidding. Just make sure you have one strip under each line of letters and you are golden. I've tried several double sided tapes. I've tried all the clamps in the shop. This TAPE is the ONLY TAPE you need.

To recap. If you are using an X-Carve, and you want to hold the work piece down, use this tape. Look at the picture. I secured the cedar (12mm thick) and the maple inlay (3mm thick) with this tape. It held fast and true. It didn't gum up my bit, none of the letters flew off and the work piece came loose without unnecessary stress. It's a perfect tape. I'm in my shop more often making more projects because of how awesome this tape is. Look at the walnut New River Gorge bridge. I secured it with the tape. (Look how thin some of the piece is. This tape did the job! You don't need to look anywhere else.

rating 5.0. Great Product I have only used it once so far, but it worked really well. Once you stick one side to the wood, it is easy to peel off the other side. When you are finished with your project, the tape removes easily without leaving a sticky surface. I will be using it more.

rating 5.0. Strong bond with zero residue There is a lot of substandard double sided tape out there.  Either too week to be useful for anything but paper or so strong and leave a residue difficult to remove.  This tape is the best of both worlds.  Strong to the point it takes significant strength to remove two taped services yet leave no residue. This will be great for templates. I cannot use solvents on acrylic so I cannot have residue.

rating 2.0. Careful I received a DOA chip My first chip was DOA. Instead of dealing with a return I just bought another, instead of spending the 5$ in gas to drop-off at kohls.

Otherwise the new chip is working great on my CoreXY printer running the BTT Octopus 1.1 Main boars with Klipper firmware. Tuned my resonances and it gives consistent readings. Make your own harness with CAT6 or 22AWG Silicone wire to avoid noise.

rating 5.0. Works with Raspberry Pi, great for Klipper Input Shaper If you're looking for an ADXL345 to connect to your Raspberry Pi for use with Klipper Input Shaper, the HiLetgo GY-291 ADXL345 probably is the correct part for you.

The HiLetgo GY-291 ADXL345 is a 3.3 volt part with an SPI interface and can be connected directly to the Raspberry Pi or a Pi 2040 and used for Klipper.

If you're attempting to connect to a 5volt MCU you are likely looking for a different part but if you're doing that, you're not the common Klipper User and probably already know what you are looking for.
rating 1.0. DOA Product was dead on arrival, after running multiple tests and checking connections I borrowed another one and it worked. Buyer beware

rating 4.0. 0 Bells and wistles but more bang for the buck This machine doesn't have the convinces of other resin printers, no WiFi, limited slicing software, no wiper, no auto filler, no peel mechanism. rating 5.0. Great printer for the money. I've had this dude for over a year and it's still printing strong. In a year i've had to replace one thermocouple that went haywire.  Any other problems I've had you would have on any FDM style printer.

rating 5.0. Works well for crafting Item exactly what I expected based on prior reviews. The handle itself is as expected, fit "ok" for blades, does not compare with the $12 name brand blade for precise fit and ease of blade insertion, but the blades eventually do click in. A little WD40 assists in ease of insertion. The blades themselves are acceptable. The tips are not very sharp, the flat portion is glide-easy sharp. However, the tips can still do some serious damage to your skin if caution is not exercised. They work well for crafts. For precision skin-related and cosmetic purposes, look elsewhere and pay the price for name brand blades and handles.

rating 5.0. Great little product! Love them! Just when all my glued-on door hooks finally fell off, I found these to replace them. They are great!  I've purchased a total of 6 so far, because they fit our doors. They don't fit every door, but most of them. Amazing that such a simple product can be so convenient and necessary. I especially like the sturdy metal and being coated with protective vinyl. And if your doors accept them, they stay put and things remain hanging on them, unlike other styles which wiggle around and clothes fall off them.  And unlike those that are glued or nailed on, you can move these to wherever you need them.  Thus, if you need portable hooks and your doors are the right thickness with enough space above them and the door frame, you would like these.  And, no, I did not write this for a free gift. I never do that. My reviews are just my honest views and experiences.

rating 2.0. Lightweight, best for semi-permanent hanging I purchased these hooks based on their Amazon Best-Seller status but was disappointed. While they are very well made, and probably perfect for hanging shoe racks and other items that won't often be removed from the door, I wanted to use them in my bathroom to hang robes or extra towels over the door. I found that the hook was so light-weight that every time I lifted the towel or robe off the hook, the hook lifted off the door along with the towel/robe. Then I'd have to reach up and put the hook back onto the door. I'll keep them around the house, for if I ever need to easily and semi-permanently attach something over the door. But I don't like using these hooks to hold items that often get taken down and put back up.

rating 5.0. Great over the door hooks! I've been looking for over the door hooks for a long time. Three years to be exact. Something to hold up the 31 organizers I bought. This was perfect! I'm so thrilled to finally be able to hang up my organizers and use them.

rating 5.0. simple, affordable, and effective hooks -- a staple of my household I've been a fan of these hooks for many years -- I am fairly certain that I bought my first set of them at a discount store about 15 years ago before I went to college. One hung over my dorm room door and was a good catch-all for my jackets and tote bags, and the other hook hung over my bathroom door and held my big towel.

I was really glad I had these particular hooks because I knew the thicker plastic or heavier metal over-the-door hooks wouldn't have fit. My dorm room had those heavy metal doors with no real gap between the door and the doorframe. These hooks have a very low profile and don't obstruct the closing of any door that I've ever put them on. Without these hooks, I would've had to resort to 3M Command hooks in my dorm room since we weren't allowed to do any drilling, but Command hooks probably wouldn't have held my heaviest items as well as these. Plus, the installation of these hooks is so much more simple and foolproof, obviously.

These metal hooks look thin and they're very lightweight, but they are sturdy and strong and never bend even under heavy loads (winter coats, loaded tote bags, you name it.) The metal reminds me of airplane-grade aluminum. Also, the rubbery white coating grips quite effectively. Even slippery nylon jackets stay on the hook well, regardless of whether you're opening and closing the door a lot. I've also never had the coating crack or slide off.

Over the years I've taken these hooks with me to numerous houses and apartments. I've used them for clothing and towels, of course, but also they've worked to hang an ironing board behind my laundry room door and to suspend shoe organizers from my closet door.

The one con of this product? They're really easy to leave behind when you move (you just forget they are hanging there because they are so inconspicuous and almost feel like a fixture of the house) so I've had to buy a few more sets here and there. After a recent move I accidentally left my set of hooks behind with my old roommate, and since she loved them too, I told her I would just buy more for myself.

Alas, after looking at my regular discount store sources, the hooks were nowhere to be found, but Amazon came to the rescue! I was excited to find that these were the same exact hooks I've been buying for 15 years. No frills -- just a sturdy hook that fits on pretty much any standard door and, like I said, it actually works to hold your clothing and other items (unlike some of the fancy decorative hooks you might screw into the wall where smooth items just slide right off.) They aren't fancy, but they really work, and they're so versatile to have in your home that I bet you won't care about the lack of fancy.
rating 1.0. Not what was pictured I ordered a simple over the door hook. What I got was a hook that sticks out and is short

rating 4.0. Good hook This is a good hook and you can't go wrong with the pricing. If you go to Home Depot or a similar place, just one of these heavier-duty hooks will cost the same as this Amazon pack of 2 metal door hooks.

Door is able to shut with the hook on it, and the white lining does not extend to cover where the door closes (otherwise it wouldn't shut). The hook is able to hold my massive collection of scarves on a Ikea hanger without bending.

Thanks!

rating 5.0. Towel Hamper Hanger My boyfriend and I recently moved to a new apartment that had some different challenges than our old place, mainly the space. Our bedroom walk in closet is much smaller, but we have more coat closets throughout the new apartment. This led to me having to get creative with my items (like old bridesmaid dresses towel hamper and wrapping paper) needing hung or stored. I was looking all over amazon for a product that hangs over the door for these uses.When I found these I fell in love. When I received them I was even more in love. They are packaged in one wrapping and kinda "stuck" together at first, so you have to wiggle them apart. This wasn't too much of a hassle though. The white 'silicone' that is on the curved end and the straight end make them item amazing. You don't hear any sliding around of the metal because this keeps them from sliding. The white worked great for me personally because my doors at the new apartment are white, so it's like the items I hang on them are hanging on air because they blend in so well. I have one of them holding my bathroom towel hamper and it is amazing how much weight it holds (up to 15 pounds so far). I would recommend this product for anyone needing to get creative with space like I needed to, or if you just like to hang things over your door!
