input: rating 5.0. Great. Purple is better than the maroon This is my second one of these vacuums.  My first one (maroon one) would tend to flip over easily, but the purple one has larger wheels and does not seem to have that problem. I prefer a bagged vacuum over baggless. Much easier to just throw out the bag rather than emptying and cleaning it. The bags aren’t very expensive and last a very long time.  Their customer service is great too. I thought mine was missing the crevice tool so I called and they sent one right away. However, unlike the maroon one, the crevice tool was actually underneath the vacuum.  Very lightweight, very powerful, and expensive. Great little vacuum cleaner.

rating 5.0. A great deal, a great little vac I have been pleasantly surprised by this purchase. This little vac works better than other more pricey full size vacuums I have had in the past. You will find yourself wishing the cord was just a bit longer because you have gotten into a satisfying cleaning groove! I use it for the few non-carpeted rooms in our house and it makes clean up a breeze. If your entire house is hard floor, this may not be for you. One complaint is that the extension comes off of the suction hose, I just make it a point to hold one hand on the extension and one on the hose handle and it is no big deal. But for the price, I am very thankful and satisfied with this purchase.

rating 5.0. great vac for the money regarding the problem reported by others who complained about the hose and floor wand coming apart, easy fix for that...just wrap a single layer of painter's tape around the end of the hose, then insert into the wand. Stays together until you pull apart, tape easily removes if desired.

rating 1.0. Don't waste your money It's too late to return this vacuum cleaner or else I would. I kept hoping that the hose would hold in the insert, but no such luck. Now that I can't return it, I'll have to duck tape the hose to the unit. No matter how hard I push the hose to hold, it comes out during vacuuming. Very frustrating. Don't purchase this vacuum cleaner. Spend a little more money and get a decent one.

rating 1.0. STAY AWAY horrible I bought one of these before. The first one was great ...for a while... then the brush attachment broke off the plastic piece that attaches to the rod. So I contacted Bissell just to buy the rod and the brush attachment but when I saw that those 2 pieces cost over $30 and the whole vacuum cost $60.. I thought it’d be more economical to buy the whole vacuum a second time. So I used only the pieces from the new vacuum that I needed for my old one that was still working great. 1. Even though the vacuums were identical, the pieces never really fit right, the pieces would constantly fall off in the middle of vacuuming. 2. The brush on the second vacuum broke in the end anyway just like the first one... so now I have 2 working vacuums (one of them still brand new in the box)
So now I have 2of these machines and they both work but I can’t use either one because I don’t have a functioning brush head.
They both broke. This is really disappointing and I’ve always associated the Bissell name with unbelievable quality. Not anymore.
I’ve bought vacuums from Walmart that we’re better than this one. After reading some of these negative reviews I don’t feel confident contacting Bissell about this issue as I feel I’ll get the same “oh so sorry about that but there’s nothing we can do, good luck with everything” response.
I’ll be sure to let everyone I know to stay away from this one.

rating 4.0. Good for the price I don't love this vacuum. Other reviews covered the things I don't like - but they are worth repeating. 1) The cord is stupidly short, 2) it is not great on carpet that has any length to it, 3) it is so light (also a +) that it does flip over easily. All that said, I am going to keep it for the price - if for no other reason than it did a killer job on my blinds with the little round brush tool and the extender tool was long enough to vacuum my 10' high ceiling vent. It is also good on my VERY short area rug, but totally useless on my shaggier runners. However on the short rugs, I do like the brush in the main vacuum tool because I have cats and it does a great job of collecting the hair there and it cleans easily back into the vacuum. I got my Hoover upright serviced and it is great on the thicker rugs.
rating 2.0. Parts missing The product was what I expected for the price but there were parts like the crevice tool missing and as a specialty item for this vaccum don't think I can find a replacement
rating 1.0. Not recommended Get what you pay for. Sent it back. Weak. Did not do the job

rating 2.0. Love bissell in general but not this product Unimpressed with the vacuum. I kept it because it’s easier than returning but the bissell featherweight for $22 works much better and is less cumbersome... if this was more powerful then it would be worth dealing with the short cord and stiff hose, etc..

rating 1.0. RUN AWAY FROM THIS VACUUM If I could give zero stars overall, this would be more accurate. This is The single worst vacuum I’ve ever owned. Attachments constantly fall off, regardless of how firmly they’re put on. Extremely short cord; can’t complete a single room without relocating the plug. So light that it flips over every time it’s tugged on. DON’T WASTE YOUR MONEY!!!

rating 4.0. Excellent vacuum for the cost! The main selling point of this vacuum should be the variable suction power, which might suit you very well if you want to use high suction to suck every bit of dirt and dust off the floor , and use the low setting to gently vacuum off any lint or debris off your bed without sucking up the blankets or covers.

Second selling point is the price, usually around $50-60. Very worth the price, the only downside is buying more bags. (Between you and me, if you can't be arsed to buy more bags, you could always shake it out in the trash bin with precaution. BUT - Since the bag itself acts like a filter, you can't get away with doing this for more than a couple of months.)

Coming to the point with this vacuum, this thing SUCKS. In a good way! :) Could suck off the socks from my feet! Accidentally sucks up some objects up that I thought I vacuum couldn't, that's why you get an option to knock the suction down a few notches.

Oh yeah the tools, comes with everything you should need. A crevice tool, nice. A brush tool, sweet. And the regular floor tool, awesome! Nothing special to note about these, they work like any other vacuum attachment. But the only thing I should note that is unfortunate. No clasping/securing method. Just pressure-fitted. If you had a bad history with your attachments prying off your hose, look elsewhere. For me? Never seemed to happen to me.

rating 5.0. Inexpensive, but works great for bare floors and dusting. Compact, light weight, convenient. I loved this little vac so much, I bought my adult daughter the same one, and she loves it. We both have dogs and cats, and noncarpeted floors. We like how the vac picks up pet hair, especially in places other than the floor. The telescoping pole is handy. One surprise bonus of that is it makes the vac instantly child-sized, so my grandkids can play with it and really sweep up dirt.
Other reviewers mentioned how the pole separates from the handle during use. It does, and here is my quick fix: wrap a piece of duct tape around the hose fitting - not enough to overlap. This gives the connection just enough grip to stay put.

The two included attachments, a brush attachment and a crevice tool, are okay, considering how inexpensive this vasc is. The crevice tool is super short, so sometimes I just use a longer one I have leftover from a previous vacuum ckeaner.
Do not buy this for sweeping carpet. It isn't great for that, because there isn't a beater brush.
Also, I haven't yet found replacement bags locally, so I order them on Amazon. Not an isdue for me, but just wanted potential buyers to know.

rating 4.0. Best buy for under $100 I bought this to keep upstairs to vacuum the bedrooms, hallway, bathrooms and the stairs, which are all hardwood or tile. It has great suction and is very lightweight.  It seems to be well made, even if the hose feels a little cheap. I've had both and I prefer  the bag to the bagless, as I don't like the mess and dust from emptying a bagless dust container.  It's small head gets under and around furniture with ease. And it sucks up dust bunnies super fast. I read a lot of the reviews and I think the people who gave it poor ratings had too high expectations.  Let's be real, it's not a $1200 Electrolux. It's a $59 vacuum cleaner.  You get what you pay for, and here, surprisingly you get a little more. The tubes do come apart if you vacuum CARPET and you pull the handle to vacuum on the reverse stroke, but that's because it has a lot of suction.  Just pick the head up to break the suction before pulling it back toward you. The upside to this is, if you need to remove the head to get into a corner or a tight spot, you can place your foot on the head and easily pull the tube out without having to bend down. It works just fine on tile and hardwood floors - no tubes and hoses falling apart all over the place. Besides, it doesn't have a beater bar in the head, so it doesn't do such a great job on carpet anyway. It does an okay job on the area rugs we have in the bedrooms. It has rubber wheels and an indicator to let you know when the bag is full. With regular use,  I fully expect this vacuum to last at least 5 years, maybe more. All in all, it's probably one of the best vacuums you can buy for less than $100. And at $59, that makes it a bargain in my book.

rating 3.0. You get what you pay for You get what you pay for,Powerful little motor but not put together very well,Basically use it as you would a sweeper if you’re looking for a true vacuum cleaner look elsewhere

rating 1.0. Look elsewhere Does not work well on high pile carpet, at all.  The hose does not lock into the head attachment, comes apart constantly and the cheap little plastic piece that holds the hose while not in use broke after the first use.  I guess you get what you pay for.

rating 4.0. Just right for our RV I was looking for a small, but powerful vac for our RV.  This one absolutely fits the bill.  I would have given it 5 stars, but the floor brush is almost worthless.  Fortunately, I have an old one that I can use.  Anyway, love the vac!

rating 2.0. You get what you pay for I bought this because it was cheap.  My long-lived Kenmore died.  I'll be moving relatively soon, but I don't know what my floor situation will be, so I got this with the idea that it would be temporary.  So, given that my expectations weren't high, I can't complain too much, but I'm amazed that this vacuum gets so many good ratings.  I would say that this would suffice as a primary vacuum machine only for someone living in a small efficiency apartment with no carpet.  It would do fine for that.  For me there are three problems with it.  As others have cited, the metal wand pieces pull apart while you're vacuuming and, relative speaking, they're heavy - I've had them come apart and land on my foot, which is not pleasant.  What's a bigger pain (pun intended) for me, though, is that although I have a very small house, the cord is so short that I have to plug this in in three different places just to vacuum the first floor  (about 500 sf).  My Kenmore would do the whole first floor while plugged in in one place.  Less important is that it is remarkably loud for a small vacuum.

rating 5.0. Great suction Great buy WOW for a really cheap vacuum, this is great.  We have a Dyson bagless cordless which works for about 10 minutes with good suction.  I plug this in and vacuum everything with great suction, then the cord zips inside.  Fits in the cabinet. Great buy.
rating 1.0. Does not work on rugs/carpet Says it works on rugs/carpets, but really does not

rating 4.0. Great vacuum for the money I'm pleased with this little vacuum so far. Got it yesterday and used it twice so far. This is my first canister vac so I'm sure there is a learning curve. I bought it in anticipation of installing hardwood floors throughout and it came highly recommended for that. The reviews and recommendations were correct. It is fantastic on hard floors. Not so much on carpet.

Before I get into some critiques let me say I would definitely buy this again for hard floors and recommend it to a friend. One just needs to keep in mind you have purchased a $49 vacuum not an expensive luxury model.

I'm a bit disappointed that the cord is so short and so is the hose. They work but would definitely be more convenient if they were both a bit longer. I don't like the pressure fit for the handle and accessories to the hose and there is no onboard storage for the little dust brush that came with it. It would be nicer if the handle and accessories had some sort of click to keep them in place and aligned correctly. I put on on backwards the first time LOL.

Overall a great little vacuum with tons of suction. Perfect for hard surface but carpet not so much.

rating 2.0. So-so Ok it has great suction. But come on they  could have made the hose and extension longer I'm 5'9 and I have to bend down to get it to clean thick carpet. And the wheels could have been made a tad bigger doesn't really roll great over thick carpet. But it does have excellent suction

rating 2.0. Disappointed for use on carpet/rug I bought this to use on 2 rugs and 3 bedroom floors. It is very lightweight, easy to put together and has great suction. I assumed since it was good for carpets it had a beater but it's actually a brush that just drops down on the front of the floor attachment. This prevents you from pushing it forward. Therefore you lift it up set it down away from you and pull towards yourself. At first I thought it would just be an annoying thing to get used to but after a few pulls the parts came apart. The handle does not lock into the telescoping wand and the wand does not lock into the floor attachment. The hose is also made out of a cheap plastic that kinked on me cutting off the suction. I did not try it on my vinyl plank floors.

rating 2.0. It's just a large dust buster Fisher Price makes more substantive vacuums. Mine arrived in a box with loose cat/dog treats. Gross. Hate it, but too much of a pain to box it back up and send it back. Will try to sell at a yard sale.

rating 1.0. Not worth it I was recommended to buy this from a friend. I bought one from my account for me and from another account for my cousin. Both of them didn’t resist more then 2 months. It doesn’t work anymore. Even when was working the suction wasn’t good, I had to go over and over to the carpet and floor to clean it. I don’t recommend this product. They better offer me a new one that it’s not ready to break in the first uses.
rating 1.0. Junk This is the worse junk possible Reminds me of a child’s toy they would have for playing house. Useless!!!

rating 2.0. Toy vacuum cleaner Piece of junk that has good suction - so far. Cheap vacuum - hose keeps coming apart, has no beater bar so don't even bother if you need for rugs. I suppose it would be ok as an extra vac to use on quick cleaning of hardwood and vinyl flooring as long as you glue or tape the annoying hose parts together so they don't keep coming apart.
Otherwise, don't waste your money.

rating 2.0. Meh Pros:
- easy process of replacing vacuum bag
- button for retracting cord

Cons:
- No lock mechanism to stop the metal part of the wand from falling off the tube (I've scratched my floor and also given myself a number of bruises because of this)
- Dust/hair gets stuck on the brush part of the vacuum

Conclusion: you get what you paid for
rating 5.0. Five Stars Very reasonable price and outstanding customer care and support.

rating 2.0. Dont Buy The only reason I am giving this a 2 is because it does have great suction.  But the handle does not stay on and the crevice tool and dust tool were not with it.  Just got it today and the hose comes apart everytime i pull it backwards.

rating 5.0. Great lil sucker I am just blown away with some of the negative reviews. It took longer to open the box than it did to put together.
Assembly was one of the complaints. Another was suction. Broken plastic wand (the wand is metal). One reviewer was just getting down on the 2154A purple vacuum and even sent in a picture to make her point. Picture was of a completely different (green looking vacuum cleaner). If you gotta rant be right.
My wife of 57 years loves this vac and that's all the review I need. Sixty bucks and a happy wife. Can't beat it!!!

rating 1.0. problems with hose & handle staying together & plastic broken that holds hose to brushes. This vacuum hose has already broken in one place ( where the hose is supposed to connect to the floor brush attachment - both the plastic on the hose and the plastic on the brush)  and the hose will not stay together. where the metal meets the hose.  I've owned it less than 5 mos.  How do I  fix this?  Duct tape?  Is there any warranty for hose breaking and not fitting in less than 5 months?  I live in a small 1 bdrm apt and vacuum only small area rugs and tile floors.  Help!  So disappointing.

rating 1.0. Bad product.  Buttons to turn on and off/and power cord rewind do not work after 1 use! I currently own a Miele and Sebo.  I wanted a vacuum just for my wood landing on 2nd floor.  After one use, the on/off button would not keep power on.  I had to hold it down continually to keep power on.  Same thing on the button to do auto cord.  Spend your money on something else.  This is a terrible product.
rating 1.0. Not for carpets This vac is only good for hard wood floors, doesn't work well on carpets at all. Disappointed in this.

rating 5.0. Fabulous 5 star vacuum and I am hard to please My precious Dirt Devil finally broke down after 3 decades and I needed a new hand held all around vacuum to take care of my hardwood floors and the carpet on my stairs and attic. After much study I decided on this Bessel Zing model. I absolutely love everything about it. I am writing my review to share my love with anyone who reads this review. You should feel fearless and hesitation free in buying this product. I have 3 cats and a long haired dog and this Bessel Zing picks up ever they lay down for me. It also gets all the kitty litter, all the food particles and dirt I drag in from the outside. I always hated the way no vacuum cleaner cord would stay wrapped around well when I would try to put it away but this Zing has a cord feature where you push the button and it auto winds away-you never have to wrap the cord up! It’s amazing and it works easily. I fully and thrillingly recommend this product. You will be very glad you chose it.

rating 5.0. Excellent on floors! I have finally come to the harsh realization that there is no such thing as a vacuum that cleans every surface well; however, I think I've found a way to get my floor a lot cleaner and using two vacuums is worth it. This vacuum cleans my wood floors beautifully! It's been two days since I vacuumed and I'm still amazed how much cleaner my floors look than when I did them with my upright. I do have area rugs and I use the upright on them as I have four dogs and it's only sorta good about vacuuming the hair - but this vacuum is so much easier to do under the tables, furniture and just all around.

All in all, I love this vacuum and if I have to use this one and follow up with my upright on the rugs, then that's okay with me!

rating 1.0. Retractable cord needs improvement! Poor suction! Things I dislike about this vacuum:
the retractable cord. It is VERY difficult to retract the cord. Disappointing.

This vacuum has poor suction.

The cord needs to be longer.

Pluses: nice color, lightweight.

Every time I use this vacuum, I like it less. I am 10 days past the day I can send it back for a refund. Wish I had paid attention to how long the window of return was.

rating 2.0. Terrible on carpet I didn't realize that this would be awful on carpet.  There's no power nozzle or brush roller to get into the nap.  It's cumbersome to push and doesn't clean carpet at all.  It might be ok for hard floors only, but the floor attachment is so narrow that it would take a while to do an average sized space.  Sadly, we didn't try it out until just outside the 30 day window, so we are stuck with it.

rating 1.0. Not worth the time or money My shop vac works better on my hard floors and this vacuum doesn't work AT ALL on carpet, filter kept popping out, attachments were missing, overall very poor product
rating 2.0. Not for carpet!!!! Works well on wood floor and other hard surfaces.  DOES NOT WORK GOOD ON CARPET!!! Do not buy if you are going to use on carpet!!

rating 1.0. Cute but not as effective as I hoped. I have some things I really liked about the cleaner. It's super light weight which makes moving it much easier. As far as suction power this definitely has good suction for its size. So the flaws I have is that the hoses I cannot get together properly due to lack of strength or something and they keep falling apart while I'm trying to vacuum causing a huge irritation. Secondly this does not have a beater bar it has a stationary brush like a handheld vacuum and a squeegee on the back side with the suction in the middle making the brush head pretty much useless as it just flings anything out of it's way. They said on board attachment holder but it only holds the crevice tool and not the tiny handheld brush head. (I knew the vacuum main head wouldn't have room on it.) Over all I am not happy with this product and do not recommend it unless you are wanting it to use the crevice tool on a small area but not a small room.

rating 2.0. Not Good on carpet or stairs The suction is not powerful and it’s difficult to keep the head on the end of the hose.  The end does not have a clip to hold the attachment in place.  The attachment only slides on.

rating 5.0. Zing Works Great and Relatively Quiet Needed this to supplement an upright vac in a second home.  I hate using an upright vac for tile, hardwood, stairs, car interiors, wall art, window blinds, furniture, etc., etc.  To me, uprights are only good for carpet.  This inexpensive vac does a great job.  It has excellent suction and it is relatively quiet (or more accurately, way less loud) than my Eureka The Boss Mightymite.  The Mightymite has me going to my gun safe for hearing protection.  I haven't used Zing much yet, but so far I'm favorably impressed!
rating 5.0. Good Great lightweight high suction vacuum for bare floors, doesn't do so well on carpet, I like the retractable cord

rating 2.0. HOSE DOES NOT STAY IN Everything seemed fine with this little canister but the hose not staying in the wand is a deal breaker.  there is no locking device of any kind so it falls apart with use. We thought about duct taping it which would work but didnt seem reasonable for a brand new product. They should charge $10 more and put a lock on the hose. We returned it.

rating 4.0. light weight with good  suction this works, especially for a small one bedroom apartment fully carpeted living room and bedroom. I purchased this for the parimeter of the apartment, where my upright could not reach and i trashed it. There is no motor in the want, but, it picks up and for the kitchen and bathroom it works and isn't clunky. It will get into the nooks and crannies and window sills and the bottom of cupboards. Now you are going to think it's a toy at first, but give it a chance. I would still like to have an upright for the middle of the room, but that's a lazy comment.

rating 2.0. Accessories missing Was not to impressed being I ordered two and they sent one vacuum with no accessories other than hose where as the other came with bag floor nozzle and crevices tool and a  Upholstery nozzle and the hose

rating 4.0. Good for the price I like this vac. It does a good job with suction and cleaning.. The only issue I have with it is that it for flips over easily and won't stay on it's wheels and the hose detaches from the floor extension too easily..

rating 2.0. Short changed I just received the purple model 2154A. There was no crevice tool or any extra bags included. I read many of the reviews on both models, but did notice a lot of reviews for a free product given to reviewer. These types of reviews are biased and only serve to raise the average thus giving a false impression to those who may purchase the product. I used the vacuum for a few minutes and it seemed to do the job even though it gets hot quickly. The biggest disappointment is that none of the attachments from a Bissel upright will fit including the crevice tool that I need.

I relied heavily on reviews on of the maroon one and thought they were simply different colors since they were listed on same page, They are different models not different colors of same model. Several buyers of the maroon model did state that tools from other vacs did fit that model. The wand on the purple model is larger so the tools from the upright will not slide on the end. Since I did not receive a much needed crevice tool, I am boxing it up and returning it. Also when others are saying they received a crevice tool and extra bags, then I expect the same from Bissell.

rating 4.0. Never judge anything by it's size This little guy packs a frickn' PUNCH!.
The suction of this thing is amazing it literally blew me away.
I previously had a dirt devil bagged vacuum and i thought that suction was powerful and it costed more.
I love the ability to press a button to have the cord put back into place, with my older vacuum i had to wrap it around the back about 10 times? since the cord was long. WIth this little guy, I just press the button and its back in place.
Since it's so small it's easy to store it anywhere. The only issue with it is vacuuming carpets, I had a hard time with vacuuming my baby bros carpet. I was able to with lightly pressing it on the carpet so it doesn't get sucked into the vacuum. Other than that this thing is a beast, glad I purchased

rating 1.0. Does not pickup carpet mess This vacuum was Sergio. I’ve used it three times. It’s just sitting in the closet. The vacuum is loud and doesn’t pick up much of anything. The hose does not stay attached . Horrible buy . I should have known not to buy a bissell! This is a complete waste of money. The vacuum is bulky and Isn’t easy to maneuver either. Advertises that it picks up carpet and hardwood but doesn’t!

current_product_reviews:
rating 4.0. It's OK. But designated bags DO NOT FIT I have 3 Bissell Zing vacuums. They are inexpensive and do the job well, although suction could be a little better. This most recent one is a newer model. It takes the same bags as the older models... But its bag holder does not easily accept these bags -- they are too tight to properly fit into the side rails in the new vacuum's bag holder. The only thing I can do is peel off one of the three plies of cardboard on the bag's side tabs -- and then carefully fit it into the side rails. Even then I sometimes have to TAPE the bag onto the side rails so it doesn't fall apart when being used. WHY? If I could, I would expand those side rails to accept the cardboard tabs of the bags-- but the side rails are plastic and I don't want to break them.
As for suction, I needed to vacuum small piles of fine dirt on my balcony -- I had to remove the wand and use the curved part of the upper hose to get adequate suction .
The floor attachment is just...O.K. Nothing more than than ho hum suction.
Since I'm on a budget I have to take what I can afford. Once I can afford to buy a better one, these Bissells will be history.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Works perfectly for me!! I use this to hang things from the top of the corrugated wall in my rental garage-- I use metal hooks that don't mar the corrugated, and the chain slips right into the hook. I hang medium weight, not heavy tools and other car things from it-- and it suits my needs perfectly

The price is great -- MUCH cheaper than the same type of chain at my local hardware store.

Nice color-- although any color would have worked for me.

As for UV protection, the garage is dark until I open up the door-- so that's no concern to me.

rating 2.0. Extremely powerful, Great holding power These have great holding power for their size. Very impressive and VERY powerful. But even with the plastic spacers I had a very hard time trying to separate the magnets from one another. I used four magnets -- and they suited my purpose perfectly. But I have been unable to separate the other eight remaining magnets, so I am using them (all eight) to hold a metal bookend on a metal bookcase-- to keep the books from making the bookend slide.

rating 5.0. I actually use them as planters on my balcony Rubbermaid is my Brand Du Choice, after many years of using their products. They are sturdy, inexpensive and Look Good and are easy to clean.

For the price they cannot be beat!! I actually use these as planters on my balcony and they do a great job withstanding the elements.. I'm happy, my liddle trees and flowers have more than enough soil to sink their roots into.

Had I decided to use these as garbage and recycling receptacles I could not have made a better choice.

rating 5.0. It sucks-- WONDERFULLY!!! I bought two-- the price was GREAT!!! This little thing is a mighty mite!! I use one of them on my balcony -- it sucks up dried leaves and other detritus and asks for more. Powerful suction. Plenty of attachments -- BUT I DO regarding that little black plastic band that covers the hole just above the place where the hose attaches-- I wish it was tighter-- I had to use rubber bands to keep it from sliding up ALL TOO OFTEN, and interrupting the suction effect.

rating 5.0. I use them in a most oddball way These Urinal blocks are a Godsend to me. I have a smoker who smokes like a chimney-- right across from my apartment. The fumes are awful at times, fill the hallway (even the UPS guy can't stand it) and cascade right into my apartment every time I open my door. SO-- I got a CUTE door wreath -- I put it on my entrance door facing The Smoker's apartment -- and behind the wreath I fastened a net bag into which I put TWO of these blue godsends. A simple spritz of water every morning and evening brings out the scent of these blocks and they do a VERY good job of killing the cigarette odor!!
I am FLUSHED with success!!!!

rating 5.0. Re-soled my shoe with this The whole sole came off and we're speaking of Vibram. I smeared Goop on the bottom part of the shoe -- stuck the sole on, wrapped the shoe so that there was constant pressure on the sole while it was curing -- and the sole STUCK and stayed on for YEARS.

Goop has many other uses. when I apply it to small areas or large, I wet my finger and run it over the glue that seeps out -- to smooth it-- it looks good, has countless uses and the price is very right.

rating 5.0. Keeps  my wheel chocks together Drilled hole in each chock, inserted heavy duty eye bolt, added two washers each, plus extra nut. Insert paracord, tie to each eye bolt to keep chocks organized.
rating 3.0. Well..... It seems to work well but the finished product/result looks like orange popcorn or Styrofoam.. How long this will last and keep the mice at bay will be seen..........

rating 3.0. clogged after one day Used it for tincture if green soap. The tip clogged up after one day, useless. Will use for something else. Waste of my money.
UPDATE -- OK I gave it two more stars. I just CUT the opening a little larger and if it gets clogged I open it with a Q Tip. now I'm happy (er).

rating 5.0. Just what I need I keep stuff under my desk, or in the car-- these black bags do a great job of making things harder to see (like personal stuff, food or car parts that I am about to install but want to store inside the car until I can get to them).

It beats having to go to the liquor store to buy something just to get a black bag.

Excellente.

rating 3.0. I have some issues with the second order The first order I received in July were fine-- nicely wrapped, solid, and did not break. The SECOND order of the same item is awful -- some of them split right down the middle -- others are jagged on the edges. Was quality control on vacation when these were made??? There may not be a third order.
rating 5.0. Just in case I misplace one..... I have a plethora of glue tubes that are waiting for me. Very handy to use, tiny tip or larger opening.

rating 5.0. MANY uses Well, it does what it's supposed to do and I also find it GREAT to cover the cracks on the tips of my fingers with-- I put on a little drop n the crack, smooth it with a spitty fingertip and let it dry -- no more screaming when I accidentally touch something with a painful cracked fingertip -- the cracks eventually heal and I am happy, But then I get them on another finger, so I do this also over again. No big deal- glad it works.

rating 4.0. Glues nicely, but I hate the blue cap It is too hard to take off and too hard to put back on, when you have an item you just super-glued and you need to position fast before everything sets.

Then a forbidden drop of glue gets into the blue cap and welds it to the white glue dispenser under it -- there goes the ability to pin-point exactly where the glue should go. Unscrewing everything and dispensing from the round opening is a waste of glue.

input: rating 5.0. Works well. I've tried it with double cotton fabric with interface, and it works well.

rating 5.0. Easy to Use I know this isn’t the least expensive grommet tool on Amazon, but it’s worth the extra $$.

Super easy to use.

rating 2.0. Works sort of The alignment of the crimp head was far enough off that I took it apart and did my best to realign the two halves. Even with that effort, the heads do not align correctly and the resulting crimps are only semi-satisfactory. The punch feature is only useful for thin material. I've been manually punching the holes then inserting the grommets and then crimping to achieve better success. Would I purchase this model again. No.

rating 4.0. for lightweight fabrics This product was simple to use, however you need to practice first and limit your layers of fabric or keep your fabric to a thin weight, otherwise the grommets don't have enough slack to fold over and seal well. I ran into this problem and some of the grommets came out in the wash. They were easy to re-do though. The overall process does take some muscle though, you will need to push it until you feel it give. I made some curtains with bed sheets and the process went well.
rating 5.0. Try it you’ll like it! 😀 I love this product it was easy to use I used it on my cloth plant pots at the edge for plant training I was able to tie the ties on the grommets. Thank you.
rating 3.0. The gnomes started to rust in less than a month. For a Costco car cover.

rating 2.0. Cheap Works on thinner materials. Will not punch through nylon or poly. Not very well made. Dies do not line up. There are several screws that give the appearance of adjustability but the dies are welded on. You get what you pay for.

rating 1.0. Looks like great equipment I bought this to add new grommets to my 10x20’ storage tent. The covering is the same as a tarp you’d buy at a hardware store.

This is not Powerful enough to penetrate a single layer and set a grommet so it doesn’t immediately fall out

Useless

rating 5.0. Best grommet tool ever. This thing is the bomb!!!  It worked even beter than i had hoped.  I read the poorest reviews looking for common complaints  before I bought the product.  They complained about it not working well and not going through enough layers of material.  I used it the day I got it on approximately 100 grommets.  It worked like a charm  it went through 4 layers of duct tape and the tarp material.  Unmitigated success.  Buy it with confidence.
rating 2.0. Difficult to squeeze handles. Not for anyone with arthritis. Requires a lot of hand strength. Even with two hands I couldn’t do it easily.
rating 2.0. Might work on very light fabrics or ripstop. Does not work on doubled over canvas. Does not cut evenly, not sturdy enough for canvas.

rating 4.0. Good, easy grommet tool Tool works well.  I installed 120 grommets on some heavy duty Poly tarp pieces. The tool punches the hole and secures the grommet in one action. No problems at all. No adjustments required.  I did have a couple installation failures. Not a big deal.  The grommets are aluminum and not really heavy duty but for general use, no issues.  You get 500 grommet sets. Be careful to make sure you separate the flat disc part of the grommet set as they stick together and you might be trying to install one top and two discs.  That won't work.
rating 5.0. Great grommet pliers Sets grommets very well, I was punching through denim, and tent material layered together, and it didn't even flinch. Requires a firm hand

rating 1.0. A disaster It looks great.  We tried using it, and the grommet it produced had a break in the circle.  I then examined the tool, and the hammer and receiving end are off-set.  Meaning that all grommets would have cracks in them and be weak.  The tool looks good, but is so poorly made that it is useless.
rating 3.0. This will NOT punch through nylon webbing or strapping Purchased to put grommets in nylon webbing. It would not punch through.
rating 5.0. Great purchase Easy to use with one hand which is why I purchased.

rating 4.0. Great product I upgraded to this from my old hammer and wood method, and I am extremely pleased. I had some worries about how it would work, but did great! I used it to add grommets to 16 mil plastic rolled into 2 layers, and it ate through it like butter! Would buy again!

rating 5.0. Don't waste your time with the cheep ones I bought one of the cheep grommet tools you need a hammer and a block of wood to use and sent it back. rating 2.0. Didn't work For me it didn't work. The grommets never made a full seal with the ring leaving a unsecured joint that came apart. You get a lot of grommets but the tool didn't work.

rating 5.0. Works as advertised right out of the box With this BIZOEPRO Handheld Grommet tool I made a tarp for my dump trailer which has 3 foot sidewalls. I took heavy commercial vinyl from a 12 X24 foot billboard wrap and cut to fit. I doubled this heavy vinyl and have put grommets all around this six foot wide by fourteen footlong trailer. With the doubling of this vinyl it is heavy and tick. The tool Worked perfectly which allows me to cover the trailer from the elements and loss of cargo while traveling down the highway at 70 mph . You will find this a joy to use and now begin lots of tasks you have been wishing to complete. John

rating 1.0. Total waste of money...do not buy Grommets are worthless.....cheap and good for nothing

rating 1.0. couldn't punch a hole in wet paper bag if you tried all it does is squash the rivet on the fabric. rating 5.0. Awesome Grommet Punch I purchased a Shelterlogic for my new tractor. The back wall has way too much movement. I thought using grommets with bungee cords to tighten up the wall. It work out perfectly.

rating 2.0. Mostly ok… maybe The punch itself seemed sturdy. However the grommets were a hit or miss. Some did well and some just pulled apart very easily. Probably a 2/3 success rate. So not horribly good.

rating 2.0. Heavy and stiff Really heavy and stiff. Need a mighty strong hand to use this thing and I needed it for a big job so returned and bought the one that has a base and stands independently, much happier with that even though it takes up more space.

rating 5.0. Does the job well Used it to put grommets around the bottom edge of the shrink wrap material on my boat. went on easy and seems to be holding up well. I put them through 4 layers of shrink wrap with no issues.

rating 5.0. More impressive than I thought. This is exactly what I was hoping it would be. Living in Texas, the sun gets hot. Our little slice of paradise is great, except for two months. We needed shade! So I wanted to put something up that would keep the blazing sun to a mild roar. I didn’t feel that it was worth the extra money to have the factory made grommeted shading, so DIY man came to the rescue. I looked everywhere for a grommet tool, nothing was speaking to me that was less than $75 and I didn’t really expect to grommet a whole bunch. When I came across this tool however, it was basically what I was looking for, nothing too spectacular, just another tool for the box. When I got it in, I had a sneaky suspicion that it was going to be cheap, difficult to use and some sort of pain. I was thoroughly impressed, it worked exactly how I was hoping, very easy to press. I had one incident out of the 50+ grommets I did. More operator error than anything else. When I finished I got comments that it looked like it was something that the stores sold. Very much worth the money.
rating 1.0. Review This was extremely hard to press in and when I did it was destroying the gommets. Not good at all

It claims to have a magnetic seat for the grommets to sit in so that you can use it at any angle.  The magnet was so weak that the grommets fell out even at a slight angle.  I tried it out on a sheet of paper, just to see how it operated.  With a great deal of force, it was able to punch a hole in the paper, but it did not seal the grommet completely, resulting is very sharp edges exposed.  I'm sending it back.
rating 5.0. Takes some hand strength Arrived on time as described, worked good putting gromits on our pool winter cover.  Easy to use.

rating 4.0. Does the job and price is right Grommets are stainless steel and quality.  My wife used this to put plastic on her greenhouse and this fit the bill.  The tool itself is not made to be outdoors and will rust but the grommets are designed for weather and are stainless steel.  If you have a project or two then this is good but if you want to continue using the tool, you need to oil it and keep it out of the weather or it will rust.  I think that is acceptable for the great price.

rating 5.0. Five Stars My experience with tools like this, at a price like this, is that they are usually junk. Not this one. Nicely made and comes with lots of grommets, a nice touch. Makes great grommets when installed. Recommended.
rating 5.0. Wow. This is actully really good. Good value, works, seems well made. Problem free so far. Works well with thin to medium duty fabrics. Also worked with vynal banners.

rating 2.0. Does not perform as advertised Bought this to add shade curtains to our gazebo. I pressed the machine so hard it crunched the metal grommets but still did not mesh them all around completely.  The machine does not meet the grommets squarely so you don't get a good seal.  I would not waste my money on this.  If you can put the grommets in the old fashion way with a punch then go that way.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Worth the money! Due to unexpected cold snap in Central Florida we had to scramble to protect our tropical plants. Making "shower curtains" out of visqueen and duct tape to wrap around our potting shed was a priority. Once this done we needed holes to thread wire through in order to hang snugly. This grommet set was just the ticket. Instead of having to hammer each grommet with a punch this hand held grommet punch saved us so much time! We consider this more an investment than an expense. I just used it to repair an older tarp to add a few extra holes near corners. I'll be using it on our shade cloth next Spring for sure. Very handy! I will update this review if necessary.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Very useful and sturdy I use these bags for everything! The size is just right for organizing earrings with necklaces together. I use these for sorting seeds collected from my plants as well as keeping dried herbs handy. I use them for sorting beads, broken jewelry. Good for sorting daily vitamins and prescriptions when packing for vacation. Multitudes of uses.

These little packages are easy to label with a sharply pen and the ziplock top is easy to close AND reopen without tearing apart as some of the cheaper bags do. I would highly recommend and will purchase again.

rating 5.0. More of a gel than a liquid I am just like everyone else writing a review, I love it! It works! I recommend it!

A little goes a long way don't over apply for several reasons 1) no needs to waste the glue by applying too much 2) It will set up more quickly and which point you can join the two items you are trying to join. 3) it will dry or cure more quickly if you don't over use the product. 4) you won't get as much on you or on the surface you are working on if you apply sparingly. It is more of a gel than a thin liquid which I like also, it stays where you put it and is less messy to use.

I have used Gorilla Super Glue on things that get washed on a regular basis, on glass, wood, plastic, metal, just about any surface you can imagine. It has been holding my bathroom vanity together now for about a year. Be sure to put the lid on tightly when you are finished and it will be ready for the next time. This is a much improved product over the original super glue.

input:
rating 3.0. Worked great for about 2 months attached to my 3d printer Worked great for about 2 months attached to my 3d printer, then stopped. Bad connection inside the switch.

On the plus side, mine showed up with a fuse already installed, and was fairly simple to wire up, even with mediocre soldering skills.

rating 5.0. Perfect for a hobby cabinet, and comes with fuse. Cost is 2x the shipped-from-China ones that are identical, but this one came with a fuse and is Prime-eligible. Worth every extra penny.

Perfect little switch. Bought some 4.8mm crimp-on terminals, soldered them onto some 16AWG wire, and used some dual-wall heat shrink to protect from possible shock. Used these to make two jumpers from the socket to the switch. Cut the end off my power strip / surge protector and soldered 4.8mm terminals with dual-wall heat shrink. Wired the green wire to the bottom pin of the socket and the black and white wires to the switch output.

Now I have a quick external computer-cable hookup on my cabinet, and a surge protector inside the cabinet with an external switch. Makes for a very clean installation.

FYI - this came with a fuse.
rating 1.0. cheap. Quality meets the price. One piece fell ... cheap. Quality meets the price. One piece fell off from the switch. I will ask the seller to replace the item.

rating 1.0. FIRE RISK! After two weeks of continuous operation the switch contacts burned out and melted the switch casing, almost causing a fire. The switch and fuse states it is rated for 10 Amps but I had it supplying less than 5.

I will not return the switch due to the hassle. I simply bypassed the switch portion to continue using the plug and fuse.

rating 1.0. Melted The plastic around the terminals melted after 3 weeks while the fuse stayed intact. I was using this to switch a 250 watt heat lamp. This switch was far removed from the heat source in ambient temps of 76-80 degrees.
not very happy and missed my return date by the time I got around to it.

rating 4.0. Bit of misleading picture... I don't understand why you have create your own jumper wire to go from the power cord connection to the rocker switch... I can't help but wonder why the Mfg didn't do that particular connection themselves... Maybe it's just me, but the frontal view / picture of this product "seems" to be very misleading... You really have to pay attention by looking at the "rear" picture... The fact that the description said "7 prongs" is what raised my eyebrow and made me look deeper... I may just be over thinking this due to my intended use of this product and was looking for a bit more simplicity... Anyway... Lesson learned when it comes to taking a product at its face value (as far as the pictures are concerned)...

rating 1.0. Melted after 4 months - Fire Hazard The switch component sparked and melted after about 4 months of regular use. Peak load was only around 3.3 Amps 120V, with the average being closer to 1 amp, so nowhere even close to the supposed rating of the fuse. I really liked the concept of having a plug, fuse, and illuminated switch all in one compact package, but much like some of the other reviews have stated, this unit is a fire hazard.

rating 1.0. I could easily see how this device could fail more catastrophically as ... Almost immediately burned out the provided fuse when connected to a 5V 12A DC Power Supply with an AC current draw of 2A. I could easily see how this device could fail more catastrophically as others have described. I would highly suggest buying a more reputable product.

rating 2.0. The first time I bought these they looked just like the photo and worked perfectly The first time I bought these they looked just like the photo and worked perfectly,  we cut up a bunch of enclosures to fit these and simply riveted them into place.  The second time I purchased them they did not have the side mounting flanges with holes instead they had small side clips that did not hold very well and we could not mount them tightly to our enclosures.  The second set also did not come with fuses which I did not realize until I had my units all wired up and tried to turn them on? What's the deal now I have to go out and find some small fuses that work with these  :(

rating 5.0. great item and this one had a fuse with it while others on Amazon were reportedly hit and miss. This came with the fuse which was great. I ordered this same item from another seller 2 weeks prior but was waiting for the China Post ETA of June 6 - June 23rd and decided I didn't want to wait that long to finish my project. I ordered this one as it was a prime item, received it in 2 days and was happy to find it had the fuse, while the other one (arrived early) had no fuse. :)
Photo is the switch/socket installed on my car audio Boombox I just finished lastnight.

Thanks again for the great product, works perfectly.

rating 4.0. Used in arcade, worked well. Used this for power into an arcade machine.

Worked well. A wiring diagram and cutout diagram would make this nicer.

If you don't care about the switch light, you can run just hot to the switch terminals and neutral directly to power supply. But if you want the switch to light up when ON you will have to wire both hot and neutral to the switch.

I crimped my wires, they went on easy ... but I did notice the ground fell off ... there isn't much room between all the spade connectors (it's tight!), so it can get tricky with all the wires in there. May be better to solder...but I like crimping.
rating 2.0. A bit on the melty side Bought two of these.. One works okay, the other's internals melted and the switch broke.

rating 1.0. Do you smell something burning? I've gone through two of these switches-- both have had their top switched terminal melt and charred the plastic and crimp connector I had attached. Both were at under 150W load (1.28A at 117V measured RMS) which is way less than the stated 10A. One melts? Well that could just be a fluke or a poor connection on my part. Two melt in the exact same place? These are accidents waiting to happen. Both of mine lasted a little under a year, but I imagine at their rated capacity they would burn out in no time at all.

rating 5.0. Good Upgrade for 3D Printers Used this on my chinese Prusa i3 3D printer to give it a longer cord and an on/off switch. You'll just need jumper wires and female blade connectors to wire it up, then some way to protect the back from being exposed when you have it plugged in. Don't let your fingers touch two hot terminals while it's plugged in though, ask me how I know.

rating 3.0. The plastic melted way to easily even under low heat solder The fuse connection setup leaves much to be desired. Does not stay in place very well and the cover did not fit over the fuse. Ended up not using the fuse because of this. The plastic melted way to easily even under low heat solder.

rating 5.0. Works great, but no wiring diagram. I finally got around to mounting this onto my 3D printer, and it works great so far. Luckily, I was able to find a wiring diagram somewhere else online, because the seller didn't provide one and it was surprisingly difficult to find. It's awesome to be able to just flip a switch to turn my printer on or off instead of having to unplug it each time. Some of the other reviews have been saying that the fuse inside blows easily, but I haven't experienced this yet. If it does, I'll update this review to say so, but so far, I'm super happy with this plug and I would totally recommend it if this is something you need for a project.
rating 4.0. Four Stars Works as intended. Just wish the package included wiring instructions. I looked the schematic up online.

rating 5.0. Very clean finish Wanted a clean look on the back of my arcade machine. Cut the plug off a power strip. Hooked it to the inside of this plug. Check other reviews or search online to see exactly how to wire it.

rating 5.0. Standard IEC inlet This seems to be a fairly standard part and everything went together well.  I use it as the mains switch/fuse for 30A 12V power supplies and it works well.  My only note is to remember to be safe and use sheathed crimp terminals or heat shrink on the contacts and I used a piece of electrical tape to cover the bus connector on the L side where it goes from the plug to the fuse.

rating 5.0. Excellent Switched Power Pass Through Excellent power switches for replacement or your custom build. I use these for a pass through and switch on video arcade machines. Perfect for anything that needs a pass through with switch and accepts a standard PC power cord.

input:
rating 5.0. Creality has impressed yet again I’ve purchased a number of printers from amazon, and the Creality line never disappoints. My main printer is an Ender 3 Pro, and when they announced the V2, I figured it was worth trying out. I have to say that I’m very impressed. The additions they made like the color screen, low profile power supply, Gates belts, and tensioners do certainly make the difference from
The V1.

In my opinion, if you like to tinker and do mods, go for the V1, but if you want a really ready to go printer out of the box, the V2 is a great option.

rating 5.0. Worth every penny [[VIDEOID:64dae1365c56575b5923c8ebb932e365]] The v2 has several improvements over the original. The silent board is a great improvement. The unit heats up much faster then the original as well.

rating 3.0. Started great but ended up returning printer The prints were great after leveling the bed and all.
Went to print something and the bed stopped heating up (Thermal Runaway Warning).
I emailed customer service but haven't received a response.
I checked for a loose connection or a burnt wire but found nothing.
I asked reddit and facebook groups with zero luck.
So I just ended up returning it.
rating 4.0. Love my 3d printer Definitely get what it’s worth I love it already!!!
rating 5.0. Out of the box Second one of the 3V2. Working great almost right out of the box!

rating 1.0. Should be called Crapality RANT TIME!!!
I'm just going to put the results first....the details you can read through if you're bored.....in short after weeks of troubleshooting with creality "support" they finally said they'd send me a new main board....i'm like ok that would be great as that's kind of what i was suspecting all along, i then waited and got a response saying i needed to pay for shipping with a invoice for shipping....while it's only $9.66 USD, I still say that's a bunch of BS....I buy a brand new product, and from the very start it doesn't work, they waste a ton of my time doing meaningless troubleshooting, and then want to charge me shipping for a part that may (or may not) fix the issue....oh and literally while i was typing this i get another email saying they think it the "T screw", they are talking about the Z rod, i mean WTF......they don't have a clue what is going on.......so at this point it isn't repaired, it isn't done, but mentally i'm done, i hate this thing and so sorry i every bought it.....
I'm sure people who love creality will probably bash me (my care button is stuck in the off position at this point), but i'll just say i'm extremely extremely disappointed with the creality printers.
Not sure if it's been used before, but "Crapality" is what i will from now on refer to this brand....even if the product is decent, the service deserves the name....Crapality it is........

On with the details:
So i tried to make contact with creality service, with me being in the US i typically got a response about 3 AM in the morning, i'd respond with results, then wait until next day (or a few days) to get a response....this went on for weeks.
During this time, even though the issue i was having you could duplicate from the printer menu which i explained on numerous occasions, they would have me tweak slicer settings, check bed level, check everything for square, etc. etc..... ..the latest was they sent me a gcode that literally did nothing but set z steps to 800....i mean really, the result was pretty stupid, raising to 5mm from control panel it actually raised z by nearly 10mm.....i laughed.....so i got a response back saying they would issue a new main board....and then got a response that they think it's the T screw (z screw)......at this point i'm pretty much done with it, if i decide to pay for the stupid shipping and it fixes the printer it's getting sold i can't take this kind of BS, i've totally learned my lesson buying cheap crap from china.
rating 2.0. Damaged rollers and defective card reader. The rollers on the Y axis were scratched and damaged causing uneven printing. Also, the card reader does not work.

rating 5.0. Worked as expected after initial set up Had to recheck the leveling as I had not accurately secured the Z-axis limit switch. So consequently I did scratch the build plate. However, after that issue I printed my 3mm high test square and it came out perfect. I then printed a couple of PPE face mask holders, followed by a 3 hour print of an in place gear mechanism. See pictures. Everything came out perfect. Th gear latch mechanism worked right off the plate, This is my third 3D printer and is by far the quietest one. I have test printed from the SD card as well as connected it to my iMac and once again everything worked as expected.
If this is your first time in 3D printing setting up the plate level for the first print is key. Use a simple design with possibly long runs to make sure you get "stiction" across the plate. The 3mm cube I use is only 20mm x 20mm but with the outline printed first it gives you a good indication of how well you have set up that first layer.

rating 5.0. V2 is definitely a WIN I haven’t used tech support so I cannot comment on that. This is not my first 3D printer, everything just works like a dream. Octoprint recognized it and worked without a bunch of BS firmware altering. Just put it together and go. Definitely a pleasant experience after putting a Bigtree Tech board in that requires massive editing for anything to work correctly.

rating 5.0. Works great [[VIDEOID:ab6c6712f2308ad6e375ff592587d9f7]] Got this 2 weeks a go and have been useing it a lot. I love how well it works and the kind of quality you get for the money. It was easy to setup once you watch a couple YouTube videos. I really don't know how I went so long without one of these. I will buy it again hopeing soon so i can double the fun!!!
rating 5.0. Great quality prints for a very good price Easy to assemble, with amazing print quality.

rating 1.0. Defective X-Axis motor driver Very impressed with how quite the stepper motors and fans are. But the X-Axis motor driver on the circuit board was broken right out of the box. I verified the X-Axis driver was bad by taking the Z-Axis cable and plugging it into the X stepper motor.

I also checked the connection the the circuit board, it is fit properly and glued from factory.

Only reason I am giving 1 star. I will order again and update to 5 stars if it works....
rating 5.0. Best bang for buck Perfect printer for starting out.

rating 2.0. Don't believe the hype Lot of issues with this printer. TLDR; Its a cheap printer, will require a lot of tweaking to get it setup right and a lot of learning to understand firmware settings to get the most value out of your spend on this machine.

Issues:
1. Board versions is a lucky draw. The latest version is v4.27. I got a v4.22 board as sold by "Elysia U". 4.27 has newer drivers and will support new features in updated firmware better. Unfortunately, there doesn't seem to be a way to tell which you will get when buying an Ender 3 V2.
2. Full Assembly means YMMV with how good you are with assembly. Minor differences in assembly like the stiffness of rollers, aligning the gantry, aligning the bed will yield vastly different results in print quality. I had to constantly tweak in the first 2 weeks to get this to a perfect setup.
3. Stock firmware is pretty useless. Fortunately I installed a BLtouch 3 (it is compatible with this) and the custom firmware from Smith3D which has a much better feature-set than stock firmware even without the Auto-Bed-Leveling parts.
4. Support on Creality site is a hit and miss. Firmwares are not labelled properly. Instructions are very basic and unhelpful. You have to figure out yourself which board version you have (requires opening it up) to get the right firmware.
5. The carborundum glass surface does not adhere well. It will also get scratched easily. Much better to get a magnetic surface.
rating 5.0. Great product I am in love with this printer. I have found great support online and have been able to print some great products. If you are looking for a reliable printer this is the one
rating 5.0. Great product I am in love with this printer. I have found great support online and have been able to print some great products. If you are looking for a reliable printer this is the one

rating 1.0. Not great design Idk if the springs are supposed to be different sizes but order the upgraded springs, they're recommended anyway and I could not see how you could level the bed with the stock springs. The bed also runs into the motor unless it's raised nearly all the way in the rear side. Makes adjustments difficult when you only have so much room to begin with. Have not been able to start printing bc the stock design has so many seemingly obvious flaws. Hopefully I can get it running without having to do a complete refund and go with a CR10 or a different brand entirely. Little dissappointed so far.

rating 5.0. Great starter printer right out the box. I bought this printer not knowing anything about being able to do 3D prints. When I say I gained so much knowledge from YouTube and just researching the sample filament they send you is the best. I wish it came with more make sure you adjust setting depending on the filament you do buy it plays a major role. 10/10 recommend to any beginners who don’t know a lot or to experts who want a quite printer with minimal fan noise I can run it at night and sleep.

current_product_reviews:
rating 1.0. Stuff The Pipe Man, one star. My wife convinced me to purchase a 3D Printer. She is an adventurous woman, and was seeking a bedroom toy that would fill her up to the brim.

I found the “model” on the line. I bought some dark black filament, and went to work putting this together.

The instructions are awful. The book is awful. The printer is awful.

I finally got this wiz bang stupid assembled. Then, I started printing the baby’s arm. The kickstand. The third leg.

Man, this thing is BIG! My wife giggled with excitement as she saw layer after layer of dark plastic being out into a cylindrical shape.

She enjoys a veiny member.

The issues started when the shaft was done, but the family jewels were crooked and stupid looking.

My wife is into ball play, but this is ridiculous.

Secretly i was hoping the machine jammed so she would have to have the white meat.

Anyway, I sent this thing back. It sucks.

rating 4.0. By hook or by crook Man, 4 stars. There are hooks in this pack to hang your hooks on. Lots of hooks. Crazy looking hooks, maybe from the orient. I’m not sure.

Anyway, if you need to hang some stuff, these hooks got you.

input:
rating 2.0. Would not dare use the unit to its rated design These pulleys are good looking and appear well manufactured, but the specification that gives them a breaking strength of over 3,000 lb is, optimistic.

The pin from which the entire assembly hangs is hardly 1/4", less actually it would give the pins (before any corrosion) only some 1200 lb.

If they are special steel, one might get more.

We bought these to use in an onboard crane, it lifts boats on and off the main ship and is rated at 5 toms. We never put over 1.5 tons on it, so thought these would be fine.

We simply wouldn't dare to use such a flimsy item for such a task, never mind just pulling in trawler nets.

Pay your money, take your chance, but stick to using these for lanyards or small jibs on up to (maybe) 35' sailboats.

Two stars because I think that in such an application, they'd be fine. Amazon appears to have them VASTLY overrated.

We wouldn't use them to even simply lift our stainless booms, never mind before outting additional weight on them.

rating 5.0. Good value for High quality. Have a couple of these. Exposed to the elements year round and they hold up quite well. Good value for money
rating 5.0. Worth the money for the serious boater Great pulley ! Always used galvanize pulleys for ware I dock my boat , this lasted 10 yrs instead of the metal only lasting 2

rating 4.0. Perfect for cable exercises! These work perfectly to create and viable cable and pulley solution for working out. They are quiet and smooth. The weight capacity far exceeds what I could ever lift.

(For reference, I’m using 1/8 diameter cable) Also be aware, these pulleys don’t come apart, so make sure you measure and assemble your cable correctly the first time otherwise you’ll have to cut out and redo it. input: rating 5.0. Feels like modeling clay, but with powerful results I've used this on only one item -- to repair a small leak in a hard-shell kayak. It worked for me, also worked for a kayak-owning friend who used it for the same purpose. Used the directions to roll this out like modeling clay, and then applied it on both the inside and outside of the boat where the leak occurred. Last time I checked, the Sugru Web site didn't include kayak repair in the intended uses, but it works there, too. Seems like a small amount of product for the money, but it's tough to argue with such positive results.

rating 5.0. Makes an awesome stamp! This stuff is awesome! And I could totally see it being addictive. Bought it to make my own wax seal stamp for some packaging since having a custom one made in metal was soooo pricey. Just rolled it in a ball and squished it on a bottle end and carved my design in with a clay carving tool I had around the studio. Now I'm wondering what else I could do with this!

rating 5.0. Absolutely incredible product that saved me like $70 to get a new charger My Macbook Pro charger wire was starting to break so I got some Sugru to mend the damage. Absolutely incredible product that saved me like $70 to get a new charger. The only downside is each package is not resealable. Luckily I also had an iPhone cable and Apple earphones to reinforce. So basically, Apple, get your s*** together and pack some Sugru with your products.

rating 5.0. Sugru is always great, though I hope they're improving it even more Sugru is always great, though I hope they're improving it even more. :) White is definitely the best choice, because the other colors can bleed a little.
rating 5.0. Can't Go Wrong with Sugru! Love this stuff! We use it to doc our Dropcam to the wall outside to spy on our hummingbird babies.

rating 5.0. Wonderful product for making stamps! I love love love this product.  I wanted to make my own sealing wax stamp, and didn't want to go through the process and mess of making a plaster mold, etc etc.  The end product is slightly pliable (i.e., it's not rock hard) but it works perfectly for the sealing wax stamp.  I use a sealing wax glue gun to fill my stamp, and then applied it to paper -- and everyone raved.  I may buy more and then sell the wax stamp (it looks similar to the Phantom of the Opera skull stamp, but smaller  - just awesome!)

rating 5.0. Wonderful Product -- Would Not Want to Be Without It! Good stuff. Used it with magnets to hold the door on our broiler (at bottom of our range) in place. Also fixed the plastic housing on an iron that broke. I really love this stuff. When you use it with disk magnets, the possibilities multiply.

One problem has always been fixing rigid plastic. Although this stuff shows (it is applied like a putty), it works well even on plastic!

rating 5.0. It's expensive, but it works! If you've never used it, it takes a little time and patience, but it is great for my ceramics. If a lid is too loose, I roll out a coil of it and slowly press it to my pottery and thin it out as I go. Eventually, it spreads and I get a snug fit. It's permanent and washable.

rating 5.0. Incredibly Useful! I have started using Sugru Moldable Glue for all sorts of projects.  I first got it to attach an ornament onto my mailbox so people could find my house among my subdivision look-a likes.  Worked great!  It is easy to work with, is very forgiving, and holds any two objects firmly together.  It's a little pricy, but you don't need a lot for most things and it really holds.  You can also fashion useful custom fixes out of it, too...cord restraints, notched stands for small items, etc.  If you change your mind later you can always remove it with an Xacto knife.  Quite a handy find!

rating 2.0. Pretty good. Ive used 8 rolls of this over the past month (I print a lot). Ordered another roll, and I'm assuming it will be all good as the other 7 were perfect.

*UPDATE* The new roll has Major issues with quality, and clogging. I switched to a higher end roll of CF PLA and it printed with no issues, when I switched back it was clogging again. The filament was dryer and still had these issues after. I won't be buying this again any time soon. Quality control needs to be more strict.

Settings:

Bed Temp: 55
Nozzle temp: 195
Print speed: 60mm/s
Retraction 7mm at 90mm/s
Retraction prime 6mm at 60mm/s

Also make sure this filament is dry, it will degrade print quality after about 10 hours. Keep it bagged until you use it and store it in a drying container.

rating 4.0. Excellent quality filament All I have to say is "Wow!" This is some good filament. However my .4mm nozzle didn't like it. But the .5mm nozzle is perfect. The Oni Mask is printed at .2mm layer height, .5mm Vanadium nozzle, 10% infill, with 70 degree supports, and 220c print temp.
I didn't have any build plate adhesion issues. Didn't have much stringing. However my .4mm Vanadium nozzle did not do so well. I kept getting clogging. To be fair it has gone through about 15 other spools of cabon fiber, nylon, and glowing filaments.

rating 2.0. Lowest Quality Caliper I've Ever Used As a college student who has tried a variety options of research and have used some high-end calipers to your mediocre and manual calipers, this caliper is definitely the lowest quality caliper I have ever used. For example, while doing research, I had the luxury of using the Mitutoyo 500-196-30CAL calipers, and I will highlight the differences and why this one is such low quality. I bought this pair to have a pair of my own when needed, and I needed to make some measurements on certain bike parts.

Pros
- This does give a consistent inconsistent readout for me at least
- Was just accurate enough for me to get the job done

Cons
- It ultimately has inconsistencies in its measurements
- Sliding movement feels loose and toy-like; does not have a smooth sliding action.

The only reason that I'm giving it 2 stars instead of 1 is that at least the inconsistency in my caliper was consistent and the case with an extra battery were a nice touch. After some trial and error with things that I knew the measurements of, I discovered that mine would be off by -.07mm for about every 15mm that the slider was moved. Eg, moving it out 15mm then moving it back to zero, I'd consistently get a measurement of -.14mm. So with this knowledge, I made the measurements necessary for replacing a bottom bracket on my old Raleigh Technium and it fit perfectly.

However, it feels wrong that I have to ultimately still be guessing the correct measurement when the tool itself is made for the purpose of providing an exact measurement and taking the guesswork out of things...

I would not recommend this product and if you need high precision, look into the more expensive calipers. If you're okay with being 1-2mm off your measurements, then this caliper will get the job done.

rating 5.0. Has held up fine for me, retained zero at 0.00 for 100s uses Have had mine a few months, and it's been reliable. It has the essentials, inner/outer calipers, metric/standard, a ruler to roughly calibrate it, zero, case, tightener, thumb roller etc. It all works well. I like that it turns on/off automatically. Also has maintained its 0.00 zero despite a few hundred measurements.

If I was using it daily for wide varying measurements I might want something sturdier but for occasional use for hobbies and projects it's a great value. People take for granted living in a world where tools like this are the cost of about 1 hour's average wages and clicking a button. Also should use some common sense and keep it in the case, avoid doing things like ramming the slide back and forth too quickly or with the screw tightened down.

rating 2.0. Worse, this could happen multiple times in a row Someone else had the same problem - this was consistently inconsistent.

It would regularly (but not always) add ~5-55 mm to a reading, and I would have to re-zero it. Worse, this could happen multiple times in a row. Very frustrating, and not worth the hassle.

rating 5.0. Good value for the price! I purchased this caliper to use with my 3D printing endeavors. I needed something to accurately measure when designing 3D models to print. For the money, I'm pleased with this caliper. There is a little bit of slack in the tool so if extremely low tolerances are important to you, this is not the caliper for you. But I'm not designing surgical instruments so a 0.5mm or so tolerance is quite acceptable for my needs.

The photos in this review include measurement of a printed puzzle that was 20mm wide, and filament at 1.75mm. Both measured exactly as expected! The caliper comes in a sturdy case which I appreciate! A spare battery is also provided, which is a nice bonus!

I try to write helpful, concise reviews for all of my Amazon purchases. If you found this to be helpful, please let me know by clicking “yes” below. Thank you!

rating 3.0. Jury is still out. / Jury is in now - the caliper works just fine. The jury is still out. I received a box labelled for the caliper. The package actually contained a collection of six spools of colored wire which had come loose from the box during shipping. See pictures.

If and when Amazon makes good, I'll modify the review.

Update - A week later I have the correct product. I am now rating it three stars. It would have been four, but for initially sending a mis-labelled box of colored wire. The caliper looks just like the picture in the listing. On initial try out it seems to work as it should. The LCD read out comes on as soon as the slide moves, even a little bit. There is also an "off/on" button. The slide feels a little rough to move, but there is a small knurled wheel that is helpful to control small precise movements. the pieces feel like they are precision fitted, no loose play at all.

A word of warning - the "Operating Instructions" were written by someone with a very limited command of the English language, and more than half of the instruction sheet is about some other model, not this product. Nevertheless, with care, they are intelligible, and operating the caliper seems straightforward, even to this novice user of calipers.

rating 1.0. The case is better than the instrument I hate to give out 1 star reviews. And I often will still give 5 stars to a product that may not be the best available, but the value (price vs performance) is excellent. With that said, this product earns a solid 1 star.

Here are the reasons:

1. It arrived with a dead battery. This could be excused of that were the only problem. But I'm sure the reason it was dead is because and slight movement to the device and the power comes on. It was designed this way. So by carrying it around in my tool box I can expect to need a new battery every time I use it.

2. Build quality is horrible. I think it's metal but it feels plastic.

3. This is actually part of the bad build quality. The thumb knob seems to grind on the side of the rule. That's because it's machined with two wheels that rare spaced just a pinch too far apart (a pinch is a measurement we refer to when we don't have decent calipers to measure!). Sometimes the wheels will ride on top of the rule and other times they straddle it and grind against the sides.

The whole purpose of digital calipers is to take a reasonably exact measurement. The design choices made by this manufacturer make this a no go for me.

But if you want to take inconsistent measurements with a dead battery and a rule that grinds on itself, you've found the calipers you need!

There is no corrosion or bend to prevent movement so I chalk it up as cheap S, threw it in the garbage and bought a $45 caliper. I can't imagine what went wrong with the thing.
